label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
There is a straight seaweed
There is white reed
I am still whiter, dear brothers
Go to the corner, young men
To the dark pipe-caps
My brother is coming to fence
Kenakand is coming to twist
Umisking is coming to sweep.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
There is a straight seaweed
There is white reed
I am still whiter, dear brothers
Go to the corner, young men
To the dark pipe-caps
My brother is coming to fence
Kenakand is coming to twist
Umisking is coming to sweep.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Lastelaulud
translation:
My bride was a little virgin  
Still swinging in the cradle  
Hanging on the weak bow  
She had three swings  
One was under the barn by the house  
Another out in the meadow on the hill  
The third down in the hayfield  
The one under the barn by the house  
Was for smoking smoke  
The one out in the meadow on the hill  
Was for moving birds  
The one down in the hayfield  
Was for swinging the wind  
Oh, oh, my bride  
Let the smoke grow bigger  
Grow a little stronger  
A bit longer in length  
As tall as a straw  
As long as a drop of milk  
As heavy as a grain of sand.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Lastelaulud
translation:
My bride was a little virgin  
Still swinging in the cradle  
Hanging on the weak bow  
She had three swings  
One was under the barn by the house  
Another out in the meadow on the hill  
The third down in the hayfield  
The one under the barn by the house  
Was for smoking smoke  
The one out in the meadow on the hill  
Was for moving birds  
The one down in the hayfield  
Was for swinging the wind  
Oh, oh, my bride  
Let the smoke grow bigger  
Grow a little stronger  
A bit longer in length  
As tall as a straw  
As long as a drop of milk  
As heavy as a grain of sand.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
The room was left empty by the daughter  
The yard was left to guess the clothes  
Support was given to those who were not recognized  
Help to those who were not counted  
Help to those long-lashed  
Support to those on busy days.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
The village is heading to cumin oil  
The streets to a star reef  
The whips of time to the rack.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
You nursed, you pampered
You raised, you hugged
You thought to be of help
You thought to come as support
Support was given to the one you did not know
Help was given to the one you did not count on
The stove remained unused as help
The room remained unused as support
The spinning wheel stretched out its arm
When the daughter was taken home
Then beauty was taken from the house
The beauty, however, was beneath the bloom outside
The neat jug from the chamber
The room remained crying with sobs
The stove remained begging to be lit
The bundle remained crying with sobs
The kettle was rinsing gold.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Where, where, dear brothers  
Where must I go  
Where must I lay my head  
To give way beneath my feet  
To turn my body around  
I lay my head towards Perila  
My feet give way towards Anija  
My body turns towards Kehra  
There, there, dear brothers  
There I must go  
There I must lay my head  
To give way beneath my feet  
To turn my body around  
The boys of Perila, Parila  
The boys of Raiküla, Ravila  
The boys of Pikva and Penija  
The boys of Kiviloo and Kehra  
They make a ball for you  
Uuhti-rallalla.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Tavern bench and card table,  
This is my father, this is my mother.  
This is my true teacher  
This is my dear caretaker.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I did grandmother's work in the barn  
I did grandfather's work in the barn  
Up to the shoulders, I did the sisters' work  
Up to the waist, I was busy with the brother's work  
I think about helping to donate  
To tend the herd  
Then came the giving to the barn  
Tending the herd  
From the minister I was given the barn  
From the ring I was given the tending of the herd  
To me, the beginning was a gift  
I was given a loincloth to wear  
I was given thoughts about the mother  
I was given a heartfelt injection  
From the yard, I got an upper bandage  
From the gate, I got whipped rods  
That too I had to take secretly  
To pass through time  
Without grandmother's knowledge  
Without grandfather's wise awareness  
Without the elder's eyes seeing  
Without sister's ears hearing  
Without brother's eyes noticing  
Ai-rui-raa and ai-rui-raa.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Where must I go  
Where must I lay my head  
I will lay my head in Piibe's shop  
My hair on the spruce's root  
My neck on the birch stump  
My eyes in the ant hill.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud sõjast ja nekrutist
translation:
Oh, I, poor despised child  
Father despised me, mother despised me  
The manor elders despised me  
All the stewards, overseers, and granary keepers  
Were angry with me  
I was taken to town to be punished by whipping  
There my head was shaved  
And my hair was singed.  
Then the brothers came to see me  
I sent my hair home to my mother  
Letters to my dear bride  
Then the bride wept bitterly  
Oh, my dear wife  
This bread was made for you  
You eat it with great sorrow.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud sõjast ja nekrutist
translation:
Oh, I, poor despised child  
Father despised me, mother despised me  
The manor elders despised me  
All the stewards, overseers, and granary keepers  
Were angry with me  
I was taken to town to be punished by whipping  
There my head was shaved  
And my hair was singed.  
Then the brothers came to see me  
I sent my hair home to my mother  
Letters to my dear bride  
Then the bride wept bitterly  
Oh, my dear wife  
This bread was made for you  
You eat it with great sorrow.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Neither work tires me  
Nor does the field weary me  
Thigh bones do not age  
Sickle handles do not shorten  
Empty talks are bothersome  
False tongues age  
That make stories to me  
Empty talks to a girl  
False tongues to a poor child  
Oh, my dear old woman  
Did you make death when you were born  
So that I would not be born a sinner  
To grow up a cause of harm  
You tied me in apron strings  
Bound me in shirt collars.

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, poor me, why did I go
To a drunken man
A drunkard will hunger
Can no longer go back
Comes from the tavern, takes a fiddle
Pulls the bow on the strings
On the strings and crookedly
Oh, my father, oh, my mother
It would have been better to put me in the water
The water could have rolled me
And raised me on the riverbank.

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, poor me, why did I go
To a drunken man
A drunkard will hunger
Can no longer go back
Comes from the tavern, takes a fiddle
Pulls the bow on the strings
On the strings and crookedly
Oh, my father, oh, my mother
It would have been better to put me in the water
The water could have rolled me
And raised me on the riverbank.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Whose village does this seem to be  
This seems to be Kagavere village  
This golden one indeed gleams  
The silver ridge turns green  
There are golden well covers  
Silver barn doors  
From the yard ran beer pipes  
From the gate, the drinking of spirits.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Other big clans aside  
Proud sisters aside  
Dashing five brothers aside  
Who am I poor for?  
I have no kin in that land  
No sister in my village  
My kin is in Finland  
Father, mother in Hiiumaa  
Half brothers in Põhjala  
And real brothers in Pärnumaa  
My father speaks Hiiumaa dialect  
Real brothers speak Pärnu dialect  
Half brothers speak Põhjala dialect  
I myself speak Estonian  
Wait-wait sisters, well-well brothers  
Let me prepare the room  
New homes are being knocked on  
New houses are being painted  
Then I will make happiness outside from barley  
Useful rye for the roadside  
Sheets linen under the loom  
Beans will be placed on the stove  
Then I will manage to feed the kin  
Welcoming the young forest  
Sisters to suckle the piglets  
Brothers to raise the herd.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
My dear old granny  
Why did you come to get me  
To get me, to create me  
Into this world of wonders  
To become a fool of the world  
Could have thrown me away  
Before you drew the bow  
Could have blown me away in the snowstorm  
Before you stormed inside  
Could have turned into a stump  
Before you hid in the corner  
A rush of water would have rolled  
A puff of smoke would have blown  
Rocking in the stump’s corner  
To the wicked you brought a slave  
To the jealous herdsman  
To the rude a servant  
The angry do not know how to thank  
The wicked do not praise the slave’s child  
The jealous praise the herdsman  
The rude praise the servant.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Milla, milla, old woman,  
When will I repay the old woman’s labor,  
The old woman’s labor, the precious milk,  
Near the mouth, the sucking,  
On the hand, the carrying,  
How many nights were sleepless,  
How many cigarettes without enjoyment,  
How many lunches without a lunch,  
How many mornings sleepless,  
The fire did not go out in the room,  
The fire burned on the fireplace,  
No spark from the bed’s hearthstones,  
Woman mending clothes,  
Horse harnessing the gray horse,  
Looking for the child's lullaby,  
Taking away the young one's crying,  
Cannot find the child's lullaby,  
Cannot take away the young one's crying,  
Despite, despite, old woman,  
Despite, despite, old man,  
I screamed despite it all,  
I sweetened hatred with bitterness,  
Let me tell it again,  
Let me sing it back again,  
Why do you girls smile,  
And laugh across the yard,  
Oh you crazy son of a whore,  
I am certainly not a son of a whore,  
I am a son of a simple man,  
A simple man in spirit,  
Under the village and in the grove,  
Below the manor and in the hayfield,  
Wait, wait, wait, wait, no, no, no, no,  
Let me grow up, I will repay,  
I will repay the maiden's laughs,  
The village’s mockery.

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, poor me, why did I marry  
a drunkard man?  
A drunkard may go hungry,  
there's no turning back.  
He goes to the tavern, eats and drinks,  
comes home and beats me.  
He takes a belt, hits me  
all along my waist.  
Oh my father and my mother,  
why did you raise me?  
You could have taken me to the river,  
maybe to the riverbank.  
There the water would have rolled,  
the riverbank would have nurtured me.  
There I could have found a husband in the forest,  
a companion by the pot’s edge.  
A maiden’s life is golden,  
she lives without worries.  
Oh, poor me, why did I marry  
a drunkard man?

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, poor me, why did I marry  
a drunkard man?  
A drunkard may go hungry,  
there's no turning back.  
He goes to the tavern, eats and drinks,  
comes home and beats me.  
He takes a belt, hits me  
all along my waist.  
Oh my father and my mother,  
why did you raise me?  
You could have taken me to the river,  
maybe to the riverbank.  
There the water would have rolled,  
the riverbank would have nurtured me.  
There I could have found a husband in the forest,  
a companion by the pot’s edge.  
A maiden’s life is golden,  
she lives without worries.  
Oh, poor me, why did I marry  
a drunkard man?

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Where must I go  
Where must I lay my head  
It was bad to go to the gray place  
It was nasty to drown in the lake  
Father despised me from the knees down  
Mother despised me from the sleeves up  
Sisters shunned me from the yard  
Brothers from the field with the grain  
The parish from the church path  
Where must I go  
Where must I lay my head  
To give way under my feet  
To wither in the middle of my body  
I will go as a mole into the soil  
As a rat into the grass nest  
I will go as a mole digging mole hills  
As a rat making rat holes  
As a mouse the lady of beauty  
The mole brought me a black hat  
The rat brought a hat made of grass  
The mouse adorned me with jewelry  
Siim dressed me in silk clothes  
Then I go under the village swing  
A ring shines on my finger  
A necklace glimmers round my neck  
All these Germans pondered  
Lords marveled  
Whose beautiful lady is this  
Whose pretty maiden  
I am the bad child of the old woman  
I am the bad child of the old man  
Let the story end, to be told again.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Feed me, brother Toomas  
If you do not feed me, then let me die  
Take me to the swamp, to the moss  
To the edge of Kalma river  
Finally, take me to the village  
If not before, then in autumn  
When the sleigh rides come  
The dancing of the sled runners.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I worked but was not thanked  
I struggled, then was cursed  
I worked despite others  
For my own honor afterwards  
I thought of donating to the manor  
To entrust the herd  
But I was given an empty barn  
An empty barn, an empty stable  
The barn was empty of grain as well  
The stable was empty of sheep  
From the yard I got only chaff for bedding  
From the gate, I was given a twisted whip  
Even that I got by my daughter's work  
I became a stepchild through hardship.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
When do I repay grandma's effort  
Good mother's milk's effort  
Carrying with both hands  
Took me to the field on an apron  
Put me between the bulls  
A pawful of forest lingonberries  
Where no wind came  
Nor hillsides saw rain.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Whose village does this seem to be
Seems to be Vaste village
That seems to be hungry
There are sprouts even in the barn
Firewood in the shed too
The sprouts make sour beer.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Toomas, brother, feed me  
If you don't feed me, then let me die,  
Take me to the swamp to the hummocks,  
To the shores of the fish lake.  
I will be buried there,  
If not before, then in the autumn.  
Let the time of sleigh rides come,  
The time for the sleigh runners to rise,  
The harness reacts in the Riga tongues,  
The sleigh shafts in the Harju tongues,  
The spokes in the time of Kolimu.

label: Murelaulud  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I did not have a father
Nor a mother when I came into the world
God knows who made me
Whether it was a capercaillie
Or a little duckling
Or laid by a black bird
Or hatched by a little magpie
Father called me noble
Mother called me premature
Brothers called me a Russian man
Sisters called me a straw stalk
The family said I was to die
Brothers to drown in the water
Sisters to fall into a well
Not the elders die
Not the loved ones perish
My kind does not die
Does not fall under harm.

label: Murelaulud  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I did not have a father
Nor a mother when I came into the world
God knows who made me
Whether it was a capercaillie
Or a little duckling
Or laid by a black bird
Or hatched by a little magpie
Father called me noble
Mother called me premature
Brothers called me a Russian man
Sisters called me a straw stalk
The family said I was to die
Brothers to drown in the water
Sisters to fall into a well
Not the elders die
Not the loved ones perish
My kind does not die
Does not fall under harm.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Oh, I poor despised child
My father despised me, my mother despised me
The manor elders despised me
All the bailiffs, stewards, barn helpers
All were angry with me
I had no father being
No mother no arrival
I have been cursed by black birds
Old crows have hooted
A wolf has bitten me
A bear has carried me into captivity.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Oh, I poor despised child
My father despised me, my mother despised me
The manor elders despised me
All the bailiffs, stewards, barn helpers
All were angry with me
I had no father being
No mother no arrival
I have been cursed by black birds
Old crows have hooted
A wolf has bitten me
A bear has carried me into captivity.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I went to a beautiful hayfield  
Marjukene to other lands  
A beauty under the arching sky  
The village called me a swan  
The people thought I was an anek  
The manor men considered me a vahak  
I hear, I respond:  
I am the daughter of Ol' Memme Viimik  
A slender nestling  
I am also a lovely little pine marten

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
Oh, I poor spinner  
Spinner, spinner  
Blue-winged swallow  
I must not go away  
Do not, dear, disappear  
To live under the grouse's hill  
I must leave the duck pair  
Where must I go  
Where must I lay my head  
Give way from under the feet  
Turn the body in the middle  
So let me say it again  
Let me sing the words back  
I will go to be a mole in the mound  
A rat in the grass nest  
A frog in the caves  
The mole brought me a black hat  
The frog brought six rounds  
The rat brought a rosy apron  
Then I went to be a brother's stranger  
A sister's neighbor  
Hello, brother, know your brother  
Cannot know, cannot guess  
The girls played on the hill  
I played behind the hill  
I did not have the courage to go closer  
Nor the heart to take in my lap  
Nor the hand to reach the neck  
I took courage from the wolf  
Seven minds from the fox  
Then I had the courage to go closer  
Then I had the heart to take in my lap  
The hand to reach the neck.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I had a wealthy godfather  
He took me to Riga for baptism  
To give me the name Tallinn  
He gave me a gold tooth as a gift.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I was bad, standing sad  
I came in uncertain times  
For the autumn's chaff cutter  
For the winter snow shower  
There was no water to wash with  
No water to rinse the rag  
Men brought water from the sea  
Women from the gray of Narva  
Girls from the Türi river  
Then there was water to wash with  
To rinse the rag after all

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, poor me, why did I go  
To a drunken man  
A drunkard can starve  
Cannot go back anymore  
Dear mother, dear little woman  
You could have taken me to the water  
To grow up by the riverbank

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, poor me, why did I go  
To a drunken man  
A drunkard can starve  
Cannot go back anymore  
Dear mother, dear little woman  
You could have taken me to the water  
To grow up by the riverbank

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Dear gentle mother  
When you raised me  
You raised me, hugged me  
Took me in your arms, cooed to me  
Put me down, played with me  
You thought I would grow twofold  
Nine times upward  
Twenty times more  
Dear gentle mother  
You could have whipped me  
Hung me every Friday  
Given me a slap every Saturday  
Hit me every day  
I could have become a German man  
A merchant's servant in the colony  
A carpenter’s son  
Now I have become a pauper  
Rolling under the whips.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Listen, our old father  
Why has your beard turned gray  
Your butt has stretched long  
That you have many daughters  
One of them cries into her lap  
Another shouts to the kitchen stove  
The third wants to go to Taalder  
I, the poor one, have nothing to give

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
My father tended the sacred forest  
Behind the room, the oak forest  
Under the yard, the apple trees  
At the gate, the maples  
He waited for the boys to grow  
The boys grew stubborn  
They rolled under the pegs  
No, no son, wait, wait son  
Tomorrow I’ll go to the town of Paide  
From there we will get silk shirts for ourselves  
To wear a beaded necklace around the neck

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Already long ago  
Long ago many days  
When we came home  
The moon stayed above the kitchen at home  
Stars above the barn ridge  
When I go back home  
The moon shone from the Kuura river  
From the gray Pärnu  
The moon weaves fabric of golden destiny  
The bird weaves fabric of wreath destiny  
Destiny of nourishment, destiny of God

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, me, poor old woman’s slave  
Old woman’s slave, old man’s slave  
Unfortunate sister’s servant  
Wretched brother’s employee  
Not given even a gift  
Even those, faintly refused  
Sworn to them by oath  
Stirred up through fighting.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, oh, oh, oh, little old lady!  
Oh, oh, oh, oh, little old man  
From me grows a small marigold  
A little rose of the herb garden  
Beautiful like a little blossom.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
The daughter at home paid with gold  
The daughter in the house paid with berries,  
Now that land does not pay  
That lies under the side  
Between the toes  
In the twist of the heel.  
I grew up like a chicken at home  
I sat in my father's room  
I rolled among my brothers  
Neither the slave suffered a blow  
Nor did the father suffer a blow  
Nor could the keeper manage  
The brother sheared wool from the sheep  
The sister a silver ring  
The father struck the bird cherry flower  
Now that land does not pay  
That lies under the side  
Between the toes  
In the twist of the heel.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I grew up like a chicken in the henhouse  
I sat in my father's room  
I rolled between the brothers  
I was like an apple falling outside  
Outside on the silver apron  
No egg spoiled the grass  
No chicken spoiled the nest  
An apple cannot be held back  
The egg made a grassy nest outside  
The chicken made a neat nest  
The apple made a proper nest outside  
I was not hit by lice from the girl  
Nor was I hit by lice from the old man  
Nor could the spirit handle it  
The old man struck the thistle flower  
The brother struck the wool blanket  
The sister struck the silver ring  
When I became beautiful and went further  
I gained a berry from other lands  
Now it is not worth the land  
That lies below the hill  
Between the little toes  
The heel twisted in turning.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
The salmon roars in the river  
The fish calls from the shore  
The crayfish twists on the outside  
There is no breaker of salmon  
No splitter of fish  
No remover of crayfish from outside  
Who breaks the salmons in the sea?  
Who chops the fish from the shore?  
Who catches the crayfish from outside?  
I have only one brother  
He stands in front of seven lakes  
He wears a hat of two lakes  
He breaks the salmons in the sea  
He chops the fish from the shore  
He catches the crayfish from outside

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Dear gentle grandmother  
When you raised me  
You raised me, hugged me  
Took me in your arms, soothed me  
Put me down, played with me  
Carried me on your hip in the field  
Held me on your arm  
Placed me between the pigs  
In your arms from early childhood  
When will I repay grandmother’s trouble  
Mother's tender care  
Grandfather's loving upbringing  
Warming me by the mouth  
Let me grow bigger  
Let me grow stronger  
Then I will repay grandmother’s trouble  
Mother's tender care  
Warming me by the mouth  
Grandfather's loving upbringing  
Then I will bring a gift of bouquet  
From the town road I will bring lilacs  
From the cranberry field I will bring cranberry berries  
Then I will repay grandmother’s trouble  
Mother's tender care  
Grandfather's loving upbringing

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I don’t praise the old woman  
Better is the old man  
Who took me on his lap straight  
Led me by the apron strings in the field  
He put thistle in my pocket  
Burdock made me dance

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I did the work, but was not thanked  
I toiled, yet was sworn at  
Who will thank my work  
A scoundrel will thank my work.

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I was once a young man
I wore striped shirts
Two- or three-buttoned
Now I took a young woman,
Now I get things done
My body still shines brightly
My body looks healthy
The fire in the hearth doesn’t go out
The spark doesn’t die down
The young woman doesn’t dress poorly
The gray horse isn’t harnessed
The child seeks a storyteller
Takes in the weak cry.

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I was once a young man
I wore striped shirts
Two- or three-buttoned
Now I took a young woman,
Now I get things done
My body still shines brightly
My body looks healthy
The fire in the hearth doesn’t go out
The spark doesn’t die down
The young woman doesn’t dress poorly
The gray horse isn’t harnessed
The child seeks a storyteller
Takes in the weak cry.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Father called me recent,
Mother called me premature,
Sisters called me a stray outside,
Brothers called me a thief,
A recent one can be rich without,
A low one can become wise as the land.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
The old woman should know,  
The old man should know,  
That my stomach is empty,  
Hunger squeezes my belt;  
The old man would bring confirmation from the chest,  
From under the lid would bring suffering.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Where must I go,  
Where must I lay my head,  
To kingdoms, to realms,  
To the forest's tall reed,  
To the pine I must go,  
To the pine I must lay my head,  
To the pine I must make a nest,  
To hatch pine's young.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, my dear little granny,  
When you raised me,  
Raised me, endured me,  
Bounced me upside down,  
Danced me against the sky:  
Took me in your lap, soothed me,  
Put me down, played with me;  
You thought I would grow quickly,  
Nine years or so.  
I turned stubborn again,  
Rolled under my brothers,  
Ended up alone on the saddlecloth.  
Oh, my dear little granny.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Old man, old man  
Put my horse in the harness  
A dappled stallion in the harness  
Gray in the silver saddle  
Ears in the earring  
Hay eyes in the rings  
You put the boats in the rowers  
Tin eyes in the wicks  
A dappled stallion to the lantern  
A saddle for the dappled stallion  
In the saddle, German honey  
To the German man, a hat  
On the hat, a golden ribbon  
To the golden ribbon, a weaver  
To the weaver, golden honey.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
When do I repay grandmother's effort  
Grandmother's effort, or milk  
Grandmother's care  
Whispers by the mouth  
Being carried in the arms  
How many nights oil without sleep  
How many mornings without rising  
How many countless smokes  
How many lunches without a break  
The fire in the room did not end  
A spark on the bedpost  
The blaze beneath the hearthstone  
The horse without a gray mane  
The woman without young clothes  
Looking for a child's speaker  
For a young child's weeper  
Promised to a sheep bleater  
To a goat crying caller.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
When I endure the grandmother's trouble,
The grandmother's milk trouble;
With both hands carrying
She took me to the field,
Placed me among the best animals.
Among the domestic animals
In the middle of the turmoil,
So that no wind came.
So that no rain fell.
The grandmother saw a great death,
Great death's loud trouble.
Many nights were sleepless.
Many days without lunch,
The fire did not go out in the stove.
No spark in the fireplace.
The woman did not dress the young ones.
Did not harness Obu Alli.
Alli was taken to the doctor.
They sought a spell for the child.
To silence the crying
To stop the young one's weeping.
The grandmother gave the charm to the doctor.
Gave it to the sheep healer,
To the goat's crying stoker,
To the wielder of the variegated verses.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Raven, humble little bird  
Silent in our maple grove  
Tail long in the rowan tree  
Waited for the horse to die  
Old in the ear of the thunder  
I had wise stable boys  
Diligent were the stable lads  
They made the stable into heaven  
Lanterns close to the ceiling  
Where German dogs could not come  
Where the lord’s hounds could not reach  
Raven waited long  
But finally went flying away.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Raven, humble little bird  
Silent in our maple grove  
Tail long in the rowan tree  
Waited for the horse to die  
Old in the ear of the thunder  
I had wise stable boys  
Diligent were the stable lads  
They made the stable into heaven  
Lanterns close to the ceiling  
Where German dogs could not come  
Where the lord’s hounds could not reach  
Raven waited long  
But finally went flying away.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Eidekene, ellakene  
Little honeyberry granny  
Do not force me in front of the grown-ups  
In front of the young men of Noomi  
Do not force me over the head  
Do not lead me by the hand  
Teach me over the shoulder.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
The old woman despised me, the young woman despised me  
The old woman despised me even from the bosom  
The young woman despised me even from the knees. -  
Where must I go  
Where must I lay my head  
To give way from beneath my feet  
To give up from the middle of my body. -  
I went to be a mole in the mound  
To be a rat in the bush  
To be a hedgehog in the anthill. -  
The mole brought me a black apron  
The bear brought crooked claws  
The hedgehog brought a silk-striped one.  
I went to the young woman to be a cousin-in-law  
I went to the old woman to be a cousin-in-law  
The young woman comes out humming  
She does not recognize the young daughter  
The old woman comes out humming  
She does not recognize the young daughter  
I hear and answer  
Whose is this lovely mistress  
Whose lovely young maiden?  
I am the old woman’s bad child  
And the despised daughter of the young woman.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
We still have to walk,  
Walk at night, walk by day  
Walk at night in the nightshade,  
Walk by day in the daytime,  
By the beautiful war of siblings,  
And at night by the moonlight.  
But home we are awaited,  
At the gate we are welcomed.  
To bring water from the well at home,  
The canoe waits before the shore,  
The bucket on the well path,  
The milking pail beneath the cow. –  
What do I push to the canoe,  
Or what do I pull to the bucket?  
I push my shoulder to the canoe,  
I pull with my hands to the bucket,  
Or what do I press to the milking pail,  
I press my fingers to the milking pail.  
May my shoulder be strong,  
Oh, may my hands be tough,  
May my fingers stand firm.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh my dear Ella grandmother  
Little honeybee grandma  
You nurtured, you embraced  
You picked up, you soothed  
You put down, you played with  
How many nights were sleepless  
How many mornings helpless  
How many days without lunch  
How many smokes without a break  
The fire in the chest never ended  
The spark in the pillow never faded  
The gray horse was never harnessed  
The woman never dressed the young  
You sought a spell for the child  
For the barren, for the sufferer  
You promised to the sheep spell-caster  
To the goat tongue-beater  
To the verse healer  
You thought to come as support  
You believed to be helpful  
Help came to the one you didn’t expect  
Support came to the one you didn’t know  
Support came to the mute  
Help came to the long-silent  
The room remained my support  
Longer in the jaw's grip. –  
Long ago, half a cow’s jaw  
In a bustling fair  
Milking the woman by the hip  
In the earless hat  
In the three-holed dog's jaw  
Through the whip gate.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Enemies grow in the field  
When two grow, one decreases  
Enemies in the furrow  
Poorly laments the bad  
Ugly falls the sad  
Enemies in the furrow  
I stand in bad sadness  
I am the son of bad Anne  
I am of the black swine of others  
Dark among others, sister  
From my brother’s face I frown  
Because my family torments me  
Father’s brothers hunt  
So they cannot feed the family  
To feed the family, to water the family  
To keep the family great  
Brothers cannot stack the barn  
Brothers’ children at the table’s edge  
So wait wait family no no family  
I will bark family loudly  
Let me make luck with barley outside  
On the roof I will grow useful oats  
In the field, mighty wheat  
Then I will manage to feed the family  
To feed the family, to water the family  
To keep the family great  
Brothers’ children at the table’s edge.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Why am I a little blueberry  
Compared to my other sister  
Because I was the hay-gathering time  
In the early autumn  
When Martin was angry  
The water cannot wash me away  
Nor rinse me out of the mud  
I told Andres to fetch water  
But Andres did not go to get water  
So I went myself, quite late  
Along the road, very small

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Mother Ella dear grandma,  
When you raised me,  
Raised me, hugged me,  
Nursed me, supported me,  
Took me in your arms, soothed me,  
Laid me down to play,  
Laid me among toys,  
In the lap at the middle of the day,  
Placed a thistle for me,  
Rocked the rose hip,  
Silenced the berry stem;  
The thistle did not rock me,  
The rose hip did not rock me,  
The berry stem did not silence me,  
You took me in your arms, soothed me,  
Laid me down to play,  
Thought to come as support,  
Thought to be assistance.  
The support belonged to the one who did not come,  
The help belonged to the one who did not count,  
The room remained your support,  
The stove remained your help,  
The threshold remained longer unlifted.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
When I cry on the hills  
I cry on the long stretches  
For a lifetime's harvest  
From my dear golden home  
A good father placed  
A modest brother’s little yard:  
Where I grew up dear  
But Mari rose up bravely  
I raised the little flower. -

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
The foolish one sings sadly,  
The ugly one sings sadly,  
Patiently towards enemies.  
For I am foolish, I stand sadly,  
For I am the child of a foolish old woman,  
For I am the child of a foolish room,  
Among sisters, there was a truly foolish one,  
Under the brothers' hatred,  
That I was for the foolish time,  
That I was for the sad month,  
For the light spring,  
For the frosty autumn,  
For the snowy winter,  
No water has beaten me;  
No rinsing from a basin.  
Men brought water from the sea,  
Women from the Narva spring,  
Boys brought it from the red river.  
Then water could beat me,  
Rinsing could cleanse me from the basin.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, the beauty of the sacred grove’s bark,  
Dear little birch forest bark,  
Here the plants are scant, poor:  
The daughters of the old woman are beautiful,  
The daughters of the room are calm.  
Neither has the old woman struck them:  
nor has she struck the daughters of the room,  
Nor does she know how to harm the sister,  
Nor to cause trouble for the brother.  
The old woman struck the straw roof,  
The room struck the linden cluster roof,  
The brother threw it with wool.  
He tried with a bundle of linden.  
Quickly the tester jumped up,  
Feared to frighten the old woman,  
Feared to alarm the dear one,  
To hurt the dear sister.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, the grains of our sea,
The lineage of the long rowan,
The shoots of the white maple,
The offspring of the wise oak,
We were to be tracked to Järva,
Thrown into the debris of Petepuri.
We were to be given to Harjumaa,
Sent to Läänemaa,
Loaded onto a Swedish ship,
Shaken into Pskov.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
My mother is like fresh morning light,  
My father like cold well water,  
My sister like an ear of grain in the garden,  
My brother like a gust of rainy wind.  
I myself am a little beauty,  
I roll like an egg on the grass.  
I turn like a ball on the lawn,  
A little apple down outside.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Who else has seen a great death,
A great death with bright suffering.
My mother has seen a great death,
A great death, bright suffering.
My mother bought me for the world,
Redeemed me for the flesh and bone.
My father bought me with barley flour,
Got me for the spring cleaning.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Three went to the Sámi village,  
In the valley was the herdsmen’s village,  
From there came the ringing of bells,  
From there came the clattering of hooves.  
Below the village was the herd’s village,  
From there came the squealing of pigs,  
From there came the rustling of rakes.  
On the hill was the girls’ village,  
From there came the jingling of beads,  
The ringing of gold chains.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Three were certainly at home,  
One was rich at the hearth,  
The second stood tall outside,  
The barn is rich at the hearth.  
The dog stood tall outside,  
The cat is a fiery troublemaker inside.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
One from one family,  
Another from another family.  
Our gates face each other,  
The wells face each other,  
One does not know the other's name,  
One does not call the other by name.  
Calls "dear," calls "precious."  
Calls "dear" like a friend,  
Calls "precious" like a treasure,  
Names [her] a little girl,  
Digs [her] like a pea seed.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I worked without thanks.  
I struggled to swear.  
I worked harder than others.  
I did go among the young ones.  
I think about helping.  
To turn over to the herd.  
Aid was given to the young one.  
The herd was turned to the young ones.  
Grain was given to me,  
Heart’s origin.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I keep grandmother's sheep  
I protect grandfather's herd.  
At the mouth of the river, at the mouth of the lake.  
Into the five gray fold,  
To the sixfold pasture.  
Grandmother makes me a new sixfold,  
A felt sixfold of white wool.  
Grandfather makes a hairy coat.  
Patches from flags,  
Legs from sheepskin.  
A wolf came from under the island,  
With wide paws from under the forest.  
Five forests took the lamb,  
At grandmother's farm, the lamb's resting place.  
I sharpen stakes for the garden,  
I pour copper tipped rods.  
Golden stake, copper rod,  
Silver time-twigs.  
So that the wolf cannot enter,  
Nor the bear attack the sheep.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
For I thank the old grandmother!  
For I thank the old grandfather!  
For having raised me beautifully,  
For having taught me rightly,  
Forced me to greater work,  
Ordered me to quicker work,  
Used me for wiser tasks.  
Kept me safe from stumbling,  
So that I did not break my breastbone.  
Did break my neck bones,  
Smashed my kneecap,  
It was no harm to my face,  
Not to my full face.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I have poor kin children  
Fatherless, helpless children  
I have no kin of this kind  
Nor sister in my village.  
My kin are from Finnish land,  
Father's brothers from Hiiumaa,  
Half brothers from Põhja land.  
Full brothers from Pärnu land,  
My brothers from the Russian side.  
Kin speak Finnish language,  
Father's brothers speak Hiiumaa language,  
Half brothers speak Põhja language,  
Full brothers speak Pärnu language,  
My brothers speak Russian words.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I am a poor stolen animal!  
A premature little soul.  
I have a field though I have a father,  
I do not have a mother while living.  
Whoever wants it, pursues it,  
Whoever cares, strikes,  
Whoever reaches, runs after it.  
There is no one to forbid,  
Nor to stop in front,  
No one to grant mercy.  
Where must I go?  
Where must I lay my head,  
Give way under my feet,  
Bend my hands at the ends.  
It is deep to go into the sea,  
Wretched to let go into the waves,  
High to jump into the well,  
Steep to leap into the lake.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I was grandmother Ella’s daughter!  
The room plant was calm;  
I opened the barn doors,  
Lifted the lid of the chest,  
Pulled open the cupboard door,  
Untied the butter pot,  
Peeped into the milk pot,  
Tipped over the cream churn,  
Ate grandmother’s pork there,  
Touched the chicken eggs,  
Who was blamed then?  
Who was accused?  
The mice were blamed,  
The cat was accused.  
I myself was grandmother’s little mouse,  
A two-legged little cat.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
My Ella grandmother,  
when will I repay grandmother’s effort,  
grandmother’s  
closing at the mouth,  
twisting on the head,  
grandmother was bought by an angel  
bought me with barley dough,  
saved me from palm wiping.  
Grandmother is fleshy to the town,  
my room has cold well cake,  
brother is a fierce northern wind  
sister a warm sauna steam.  
My Ella grandmother,  
how many nights were sleepless,  
how many meals uneaten,  
how many lunches without lunch,  
how much sauce without fish.  
When you looked after me  
the fire in the room did not go out,  
a woman dressed young clothes,  
a horse harnessed the gray;  
Despite me to the wedge roof  
through tears I smiled.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
My Ella grandmother,  
when will I repay grandmother’s effort,  
grandmother’s  
closing at the mouth,  
twisting on the head,  
grandmother was bought by an angel  
bought me with barley dough,  
saved me from palm wiping.  
Grandmother is fleshy to the town,  
my room has cold well cake,  
brother is a fierce northern wind  
sister a warm sauna steam.  
My Ella grandmother,  
how many nights were sleepless,  
how many meals uneaten,  
how many lunches without lunch,  
how much sauce without fish.  
When you looked after me  
the fire in the room did not go out,  
a woman dressed young clothes,  
a horse harnessed the gray;  
Despite me to the wedge roof  
through tears I smiled.

label: Pulmalaulud  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Devil take the suitor’s eyes  
Boys’ eyes be cursed  
Let the wedding guests’ feet be scorched  
They took away the belt weaver  
The belt weaver’s son  
Reader of a large book  
Writer of a cipher  
The room remained empty from the daughter  
The barn remained without clothes  
The place became ugly from weaving  
The stable became bare of sheep  
When the daughter was taken away  
Then the belongings were taken from the house  
A beautiful arch by the meadow  
Proper on the pasture field  
There was noise in the village until  
The daughter was taken away  
When the daughter was taken away  
The noise in the village stopped  
To the men’s camp in the birch grove  
When the daughter was taken away  
Then beauty was taken from the house  
The village’s noise ceased  
From the men’s camp in the birch grove  
I raised daughters  
I raised and cherished them  
I nursed and cared for them  
I took them in my lap, soothed them  
Laid them down, played with them  
I thought to come as support  
I thought to offer help  
Support comes to those unknown  
Help to those unthought of  
The room remains my support  
The stove remains my aid  
The forge opens wider  
Support remains for those in need  
Help for the long-suffering  
Mother, dear granny  
When will I repay granny’s efforts  
Grandmother’s efforts, mother’s efforts  
Grandfather’s efforts, dear efforts  
Grandmother’s and grandchildren’s  
Carrying in arms and on shoulders  
Took me to the meadow under the apron  
On the wrist, out to the meadow  
Thistle made the grandmother wail  
Rosehip rocked her  
Thistle did not wail for me  
Rosehip did not rock me  
Berry bushes did not calm me  
Oh, what a poor child’s play  
There were playpens under the hand  
I had three playpens  
Five were weak to bow  
One was under the meadow  
Another by the garden field  
The third by the house on the edge  
What was under the meadow  
Was for moving birds  
What was by the garden field  
Was for swinging the wind  
What was by the house on the edge  
Was for swinging smoke  
It was granny’s rocking.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
The home is ravaged by a vicious dog  
Iron bars on the gate  
What we had in the yard has been taken away  
The goose was taken from the yard path  
The chicken was taken from the digging path  
The sheep even from the market  
The daughter from the empty room.

label: Kalendrilaulud  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Family daughter, a young maiden  
Farm daughter, a little plant  
Blow, fire, into the stove  
Blaze the bellows into a flame  

If there is no mirror in the house  
Pull shards from the wall  
If there are no shards in the wall  
Take a branch from the room’s ceiling  

If there is no branch in the room’s ceiling  
Take straw from the edge of the eaves  
If there is no straw at the edge of the eaves  
Bring my firewood into the stove  
Light the wick on fire  

The fire must be in the room  
The blaze must be in the bellows  
The fire in the room must not go out  
The spark must fly to the pillow’s edge  

A young woman does not dress  
If the firewood is not put in order  
I seek a child’s whisper  
And the weak sound of crying.

label: Kalendrilaulud  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Family daughter, a young maiden  
Farm daughter, a little plant  
Blow, fire, into the stove  
Blaze the bellows into a flame  

If there is no mirror in the house  
Pull shards from the wall  
If there are no shards in the wall  
Take a branch from the room’s ceiling  

If there is no branch in the room’s ceiling  
Take straw from the edge of the eaves  
If there is no straw at the edge of the eaves  
Bring my firewood into the stove  
Light the wick on fire  

The fire must be in the room  
The blaze must be in the bellows  
The fire in the room must not go out  
The spark must fly to the pillow’s edge  

A young woman does not dress  
If the firewood is not put in order  
I seek a child’s whisper  
And the weak sound of crying.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
When do I repay grandmother's trouble  
Grandmother's trouble, mother's trouble  
Grandfather's trouble, dear trouble  
But when you raised me  
Lifted me up, sat me down  
Danced me against the sky  
Rocked me on three knees  
One was down in the hayfield  
Another out in the field  
The third under the home by the duck  
One was for moving the bird  
The second for rocking in the wind  
The third for rocking in the smoke.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Alone I am the grandmother's subordinate  
The grandmother's subordinate, the grandfather's subordinate  
The enemy of the brothers  
Father despised me from the knee down  
Mother despised me even in the womb  
Alone, alone live the brothers  
Alone I remained without gifts  
A chicken chick to crow  
A linden bird to mourn  
An oat grain to sigh  
A barley grain to chatter  
A flax seed to move.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
Berry brings the berry from the hill  
The berry bringer is a herdsman  
Arukas is driving the oxen  
From swamp to swamp tending the meadows  
Mari brought berries from other lands  
Lingonberries from across the river  
But the crane stayed to listen  
The poor crow stayed watching  
Arukas to reason  
If only God would give daughters  
Daughters are taken to husband  
Then the ox is slaughtered  
The cow is milked in the barn  
Then the crane gets a mouthful  
Arukas gets a handful of food  
The old crow gets a bellyful.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
Berry brings the berry from the hill  
The berry bringer is a herdsman  
Arukas is driving the oxen  
From swamp to swamp tending the meadows  
Mari brought berries from other lands  
Lingonberries from across the river  
But the crane stayed to listen  
The poor crow stayed watching  
Arukas to reason  
If only God would give daughters  
Daughters are taken to husband  
Then the ox is slaughtered  
The cow is milked in the barn  
Then the crane gets a mouthful  
Arukas gets a handful of food  
The old crow gets a bellyful.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
When I suffer the grandmother's trouble  
Grandmother's trouble, the room's trouble  
Grandmother's life's milk trouble  
The room's generous teachings  
How many nights were sleepless  
How many days without rest  
You wrapped, you played  
You raised, you endured  
You hushed by the mouth  
Laid down, wiped  
Lifted up, carried  
Rocked on three arms  
Put between the knees  
Sitting by the edge of the barley  
You hoped to have a black goose  
A black one was born to bring sorrow  
Oh, the black one was born a culprit  
Became the maker of damage  
Rolled under the whirligig.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
Our village like that town  
A little bit back from Tallinn  
No dung under the yard  
On the Kalajärve well road  
Fish swim home to us  
Peat comes into the house  
Roaches go to the stove heat  
Bleaks under the edge of the bed  
Burbots under the stove hearth  
Large pikes at the edges of the ponds  
Breams with colorful sides  
Large bleaks with broad backs  
Large burbots with broad jaws

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
Our village like that town  
A little bit back from Tallinn  
No dung under the yard  
On the Kalajärve well road  
Fish swim home to us  
Peat comes into the house  
Roaches go to the stove heat  
Bleaks under the edge of the bed  
Burbots under the stove hearth  
Large pikes at the edges of the ponds  
Breams with colorful sides  
Large bleaks with broad backs  
Large burbots with broad jaws

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh little grandmother, dear old woman  
Little wise woman  
When you conceived me  
You grew bed linens  
Wrapped me, played with me  
Whispered by my mouth  
Held me by the shoulder  
In your arms you carried me  
Then you saw the great death  
The great death’s harsh struggle  
The fire would not go out  
The spark would not settle  
The blaze would not stop  
Hidden under the ashes.  
The grey ones rode to the doctor  
Thick boats to Paide road  
Sheets to the town road  
Ladies to the church road  
You searched for a child’s speaker  
For a young one to be soothed with crying  
The child could not find a speaker  
The young one could not be soothed with crying.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Chest, little chest sister  
I made the chest at the old woman’s work  
I made the chest at the room’s work  
From the shoulders of the sisters’ work  
I thought to give to the barn  
To be handed over to the herd  
Who will give the barn to me  
Will hand it over to the herd’s daughter  
The barn was given to the departing one  
To the calves to be handed over to the herd  
Oh, I was given the barn’s back  
The herd’s shed’s back  
What I got to take secretly  
From the yard I got an oat binder  
From the gate I got a bending twig  
From behind the room a killing twig  
I got this from my father  
I got this from my mother  
Without the old woman’s knowledge  
Without the parent’s observation  
Without the sister’s hearing  
I got to take this secretly  
To send it through time  
To scatter it through the haystack.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Would be would be existence  
Secondly my doing  
I would tie to the stable under the sky  
Into the stable a ladder  
On the ladder a stepping stallion  
On the stallion a nice saddle.  
On the saddle a German boy.  
To the boy a nice hat  
On the hat a silk ribbon  
On the silk ribbon golden dots.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Under whose yard is the bearberry?
Under Aru's yard is the bearberry.
(Whose cows have bell collars?)
Kensa's cows have bell collars.
(Whose chickens have hobnailed feet?)
Krassi's chickens have hobnailed feet.
(Whose rooster has a golden beard?)
Koplimäe's rooster has a golden beard.
(Who is groaning to the suitor?)
Kuoli is groaning to the suitor.
(Under whose yard are berry bushes?)
Under Matte's yard are berry bushes.
(Under whose yard are apple trees?)
Under Sillaotsa's yard are apple trees.
(Who had leaf plates?)
Lepik had leaf plates.
(Who had a hell-hole?)
Põhjakal had a hell-hole.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
My dear grandmother  
When you raised me  
You raised and hugged me  
You took me in your arms and soothed me  
You bent down to take my hand.  
How many nights were sleepless  
How many days without rest  
How many smokes without a break.  
You thought there would be help  
You expected support to come  
Help for those who do not think  
Support for those who do not feel  
Help for the miserable  
Support for the needy.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
When Do I Repay Grandmother's Efforts  
Grandmother's efforts, oh efforts  
At the mouth’s slipping  
At carrying on the hand.  
How many nights were sleepless  
How many times without a smoke  
How many little things to know.  
The fire did not go out  
The spark remained in the bedpost  
The woman young from her clothes  
Apple from the rack.  
Looked for child speakers  
Young tear takers  
Promised to the lamb’s speaker  
To the goat’s chatterer  
To the dragger of the dung stallion.  
But I, in spite, screamed out  
Smiled without reason.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Who is seen as a villager  
Who is seen as a man of Raka village  
He is seen as such a man  
To Kolka, to the Kolka people  
To Aganala, to the Al´lend people.  
Aganala's barn doors  
Swaying barn windows  
Moss-covered hayloft doors  
Bread cabinets discolored  
Sprat bowls rusted.  
He is seen as a hungry man.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
You recognize the village from Mue
Between the Munde parishes
In the center of the parish.
When they go to town
They come from Lasnamäe;
They have fences with broad logs
Clotheslines with stretched rods
Attached to the tops of the fences.-
You recognize the village from Kuru
Between the Munde parishes
In the center of the parish.
When they go to town
They leave from Lasnamäe
They have fences just as the Creator made
Clotheslines like coins hung
Fence tops unbent.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The daughter in the morning is crying!  
The daughter is not crying for nothing  
The daughter has a quarrel coming  
And the poles are being examined.  
The fire in the rooms does not go out  
Nor the sparkle in the bed posts  
Nor the woman from young clothes  
Nor the horse from the gray harness  
Nor the sparkle in the bed posts.  
Illu-tillu is pregnant with gray  
In our yard there are many stables  
In each stable seven horses  
They neigh and make a fuss  
They all wanted payments  
Settlements on the oat hill  
Servings on the golden comb  
Delicate aitings  
I could not pay them  
Nor settle on the oat hill  
Nor serve on the golden comb  
Nor treat them more delicately.  
Then they started whipping with a whip  
Pulling with a tarred rope  
But I could get away poor  
Wandering across the field  
Then they caught me with a rope  
And chased me mercilessly  
Look, the rope was a thousand fathoms long  
And there were a hundred knots in the rope.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
The daughter comes home crying,  
Blaming her father’s farm. –  
"Oh, my Ella grandmother,  
Oh, my wise uncle!  
Grandmother was nursing  
And was wise at dancing,  
Nursing and supporting,  
Raising and nurturing.  
You took me in your arms, gave me food.  
Put me down and played with me,  
Picked me up and lifted me.  
Put me in a cradle and rocked me.  
I grew healthy and sturdy,  
Very plump and rosy.  
There were no scars on my face,  
Nor blisters on my toes,  
No wounds on my head;  
No blemishes in my appearance.  
Oh, my Ella grandmother,  
And my wise uncle,  
You gave me to a bad man,  
A cruel, whiny man,  
An angry, grumbling man.  
A very bad mother-in-law,  
A very bad father-in-law,  
A little bit angry.  
I'm cold in my bones.  
The field became fallow,  
The barn became crooked,  
The flax became sandy.  
Why must I wash the laundry?  
Cold took me five miles to the village,  
Father-in-law put a pot in the pot-house,  
Grandfather took five gilded crooks.  
So don’t cry, young daughter,  
Go back laughing instead.  
There is cold water under the village,  
There is plenty of water under the limestone,  
Wash your apron clean,  
Your shawl white,  
Make the crook golden-haired."

label: Laulud laulust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Where I sound my clarion  
There I break into the forest  
Tall trees stand like pins  
Logs are stacked in piles  
Without a young man  
Without a sharp scythe  
Without a hat  
Without a copper belt, white  
Without a teacher's belt, correct  
Without boots, straight  
Without a pouch, tall.  
Very, very young men  
In our village, in many villages  
There is the clinking of bells.  
On the meadow is the cattle village  
There is the rustling of hooves,  
Below the village is the maiden's village  
There is the crackling of wreaths,  
There is the ringing of young men  
The jingling of gold coins  
The cracking of the teacher's wreath.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, poor child of a slave!
Where shall I go soon?
Where to lay my head?!
There are no empty rooms,
No corners left unturned!
I will go to the mole in the turf mound,
To the rat in the grass nest.
The mole will bring me a black apron,
The rat will bring me a Swedish dress.
From the mole I became a mole woman,
From the rat a Swedish lady.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
It has long been time,  
A long time, the journey has been long,  
Probably a hundred summers passed:  
While outside here, entertaining,  
Sitting on the beauty bench.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh poor me, oh poor me,  
oh poor me, a despised child,  
My father despised me, my mother despised me,  
my father's parents despised me,  
All the tradesmen, journeymen, all the tradesmen, journeymen,  
all the tradesmen, journeymen, helpers,  
All complained about me, all complained about me,  
all complained about me,  
I was put on the block, I was put on the block,  
I was put on the block to be taken away,  
That block fell, that block fell,  
that block fell upon me.  
I was sent to the city, I was sent to the city,  
I was sent to the city towards the barracks,  
There my head, there my head,  
there my head was shorn,  
And my hair, and my hair,  
and my hair was cut,  
I sent my hair to Hella, I sent my hair to Hella,  
my hair to dear mother,  
I sent letters to my beloved, I sent letters to my beloved,  
I sent letters to my dear companion.  
There my father cried, there my mother cried,  
there my sisters and brothers cried too.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Cry, cry, little sisters,  
Cry, mitten sisters:  
There goes brother to the war,  
Among the white boys.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
How many bogies do I have,  
I had three bogies:  
One was down in the hayfield,  
The second out in the field on the meadow,  
The third under the home barn loft.  
The one down in the hayfield  
Is to spread rain,  
To drive away bad weather.  
The one out in the field on the meadow  
Is to swing the wind,  
To thunder bad weather.  
The one under the home barn loft  
Is to rock the old woman,  
To swing the old man.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Mother, dear little woman  
When you raised me,  
Raised me, hugged me,  
Breastfed me, nurtured me.  
You took me on your lap to the field,  
To the hayloft at Käävarsi,  
Placed me between the calves,  
Made me sit by the plow handle,  
Made me grow barley,  
Made me crawl rye.  
You thought you were to have two men,  
Two men, two children,  
Two little herdboys.  
But you grew only one man,  
One man and stubborn,  
A stubborn hairy one,  
Became alone on a narrow ridge,  
Rolled under the spinning wheel!  
May God repay grandmother’s toil,  
Grandmother’s toil, mother’s toil,  
Grandmother’s tender milk toil,  
Mother’s precious bread toil.  
I did not want to become a farmer,  
I had a field of money, no property,  
No loving teachers,  
No dear caregivers,  
The straw door and the vodka bottle,  
That was my father and my mother!  
City walls, prison towers,  
Those were my dear teacher  
And my precious caregiver.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
I was like a piece in the river  
I grew like hemp in time -  
I became a wild herb.  
Sleep did not come to me.  
The day did not shine for me.  
It did not rain - rain would not come.  
Rain showers rolled by  
I hung on a snow cloud  
If I had known - could have known  
I would have gone to sleep in the fold  
Borne by the cold dew  
I was alive even before life  
I thought of growing two  
Nine - even more.  
I grow alone to be strong  
I rolled under the yoke.  
For I had a sugar back  
To endure the manor’s whip.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
It was beautiful to live in Tapa,  
The blossom was under the haystack,  
The blossom was under the grass,  
The haystack was over the grain straw.  
The village smells of chamomile,  
The village fields of bluebells,  
The village yards of apple trees,  
The village houses of rowan trees,  
The village fences of rowanberries,  
The field edges of wild strawberries.  
When I ride through the village,  
Through new streets,  
Through the copper gate,  
Through the hay street,  
A horse gleamed with harness bells,  
Clattered down the dusty street,  
The carts creaked with rigid wheels.  
A rooster crowed from the barn,  
A gray bird from the threshing floor,  
A humming bumblebee,  
A little head on the haystack.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Be proper, our girls  
Be proper, walk tall  
The proper ones are taken to the village  
The tall ones are picked up  
The proper ones were taken because of their manners  
The tall ones for their tall walk  
Daughters were taken because of work.  
Suitors came because of hands  
Because of fingers to the suitors  
Not because of the father's jests  
Not because of the brother's cattle  
Because of the father-in-law's good manners  
The horses stayed in the father's yard  
The cattle stayed in the brother's yard  
The herd stayed poor in the paddock  
When I was once courted  
I started to go  
From the yard I got bundles of straw  
From the gate I got twigs  
I even managed to take that secretly  
To surprise across the river  
To wander through the time  
Father called me stingy  
Mother called me premature  
The servant, meltaway  
The children, table sweepers...  
I got to live, I got to be  
Wait, wait, a family, an ancient family  
Let me have a room done  
Let new homes be knocked on  
Let new houses be painted  
Then the father will court  
The mother will court early  
Then I will be able to feed the family  
To sweeten the troubles—

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest

To sweeten the troubles—

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
We have several pieces of oat  
They were chased by the old man  
Across more than five grain fields  
Across the ridge of the summer field  
Straight over the plowed field  
Our oat has no life  
Nor is there a feast on a small pot.

label: Kalendrilaulud  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Ten girls in the village  
Nine across the street  
Eight at the edge of the herd  
Seven nearby  
Six at the hill of the bend  
Five at the brother's meadow  
They were not allowed to come to the swing  
They feared the gold would wear out  
They feared the copper would fade,  
The money pots would dry up,  
The clover pots would break  
The silver beads would diminish  
We have two poor children  
We go, we walk  
We go under the swing  
Let our gold wear out  
Let our copper oxidize  
Let the silver beads diminish  
We have goldsmiths at home  
Outside are silversmiths  
Tinsmiths on the road  
In Pinu’s land are blacksmiths  
From the bag rises gold smoke  
From the yard silver smoke  
Copper smoke from the meadow  
Tin smoke from the roadside  
Blacksmith’s smoke from under Pinu.

label: Kalendrilaulud  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
Oh, I am a poor child prisoner,  
Under the cover of the hideout!  
There were three hides inside:  
One was by the door's side,  
The second in the middle of the floor,  
The third in the back corner.  
The fire never ended there,  
The sparks from the bed post,  
The flame from the fire's embers.  
What was by the door's side,  
That was rocked by the poor,  
What was in the middle of the floor,  
That was rocked by my own,  
What was in the back corner,  
That was creaked by the village girls.  
One was to roll the hemp:  
Another to smoke the tobacco,  
The third to hush it at dawn.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
All my kin are in boots,
All my brothers have vests on their backs,
Brothers-in-law – straw hats:
I am poor – with five-legged feet,
Five-legged feet, round shoes.
Wait, wait, kin, no, no, kin!
Let me go begging:
I have a house where I get mat,
From another house another mat,
From the third I got a boxful:
Then I went
And set the mill to roll,
Two stones to turn,
I pay the miller with mat,
The second with a sack-lifter,
The third – with a hill-toppler.
I brought it home from the mill,
Then I made a fence without oil,
I call my kin:
Come, kin, in boots,
All my brothers, in vests on their backs,
Brothers-in-law, with silver hats,
On the consignor's load.
Come for the feast of the spectrum.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
The crow weighs on the maple  
The ass farts into the rowan  
Waiting for my horse to die  
The poll parsnip to wither  
The flax field to fall  
Crow, crow, little brother  
My horse will not die  
My poll parsnip will not wither  
Let my flax field fall  
I have wise stable boys  
Diligent, careful stable boys  
They made stables in the sky  
Stable doors in the clouds  
Lanterns near the open field.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest
translation:
The crow weighs on the maple

My horse will not die
