label: Kalendrilaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, you crazy swing-makers!  
Look where you have made the swing!  
You made it on the village threshing ground,  
On the guests’ wood-scraping land,  
On the village boys’ stick land.  
The village was left crying on the threshing ground,  
The village children on the wood-scraping land,  
The village boys on the stick land.  
Swing-makers, dear brothers,  
Don’t ask me for eggs.  
The hens stayed silent for eggs,  
And the roosters crowed into the ear.  
Row the swing, reach the swing,  
Row the swing to the neighboring land,  
Where those roosters drink gold,  
Roosters of gold, hens beware,  
Ducks of green silver.  
Ten girls in the village,  
Nine across the street,  
Seven on this side.  
They are not allowed to come to the swing,  
Complaining about the gold wearing out,  
Silver beads in a hateful way,  
And the wallet jars rattling.

label: Kalendrilaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, you crazy swing-makers!  
Look where you have made the swing!  
You made it on the village threshing ground,  
On the guests’ wood-scraping land,  
On the village boys’ stick land.  
The village was left crying on the threshing ground,  
The village children on the wood-scraping land,  
The village boys on the stick land.  
Swing-makers, dear brothers,  
Don’t ask me for eggs.  
The hens stayed silent for eggs,  
And the roosters crowed into the ear.  
Row the swing, reach the swing,  
Row the swing to the neighboring land,  
Where those roosters drink gold,  
Roosters of gold, hens beware,  
Ducks of green silver.  
Ten girls in the village,  
Nine across the street,  
Seven on this side.  
They are not allowed to come to the swing,  
Complaining about the gold wearing out,  
Silver beads in a hateful way,  
And the wallet jars rattling.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go along the paths made  
Along the trails forged  
Where before slaves have walked  
Slaves walked, oxen went  
Early went poor children  
Late came the wolves  
Before dawn the orderly ones  
After day the daytime ones

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Daughters of Viru are sisters  
Maids of Harju are single  
We join three bodies together  
We consult with four bodies  
We go to Riga to spoil  
To kill in Tallinn  
To burn Põltsamaa  
A German shouted from the hall  
A citizen from the threshold of a stable  
A shop boy in front of the shop  
I say again, back  
Daughters of Viru are sisters  
Maids of Harju are single  
Leave Riga unspoiled  
Do not kill in Tallinn  
Do not burn Põltsamaa  
Leave Riga to bring salt  
Tallinn to strike money  
From Põltsamaa bring flaxen clothes for the women  
Bring tools for the boys  
For the girls, empty things

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Brewers and necksmiths,  
Passibo pot washers,  
Who made the beer little by little  
In their proud home  
From the long field's harvest.  
It took the minds from the men's heads  
Half the minds from the boys' heads.  
Men roar without hats,  
Women dance without a ribbon,  
Boys with half boots on.  
Wait, wait, Annekene,  
Endure, Kaiekene,  
Surely you have time to give  
To spin the wheel sock.  
To train the wooden shoe.  
Jaan drinks by the riverbank.  
Between five and six o'clock in the morning  
At the break of dawn.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
It was the right time to go to Paide,  
Joy was to lay out for the fair,  
Oh that barley mash,  
Dear little sack of oats.  
Taught me to make beer,  
To distill vinegar,  
But I started to go wild,  
Moving my feet in the town.  
The Jews reach even the tavern,  
I am lively in the ear,  
In the morning I drank a new six,  
On Tuesday I drank a whole shirt.  
On Thursday I drank among them,  
On Friday I drank clothes off,  
On Saturday I drank a wide belt.  
On Sunday I drank the pants off,  
Then I was finally finished,  
Then I rode to the church with a flea,  
Right onto the donkey over the choir,  
The choir Germans were startled,  
The sexton ran to the kitchen,  
The vicar to the coach,  
Then the benches stayed to be sealed by me,  
The altars to be driven by me,  
The pulpits to be torn by me.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh wonder, oh wonder  
What did I see, oh wonder  
What did I see in Näo village?  
Two women were fighting,  
Two old ladies' daughters,  
Anne had a stick,  
Kaie a broom  
Maie had a rowan tree on her shoulder,  
Leenu had a little soup ladle.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud laulust
translation:
Little musical instrument, little boy  
Dear kannel, little toy,  
Neither instrument nor sustenance without a family,  
The kannel needs a companion to live.  
The instrument walks to taverns,  
Seeks beer houses,  
Trips into the liquor chamber.  
If only my old sounds  
The old sounds in brass,  
Tin sounds in tals (coins).  
I would not hide the instrument’s sounds,  
Nor the German horn sounds,  
Would not let the kannel’s tone be lost.  
I would make the instruments squeak,  
Rooster crows crow,  
The young horn cry.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Sister dear, I have told you,  
Dear sibling, I forbid you:  
Do not run to the drunkard,  
To the wanderer of two taverns,  
A drunkard has no sense,  
A tavern-goer has a hangover,  
A lackey dog has a scab,  
He ties you to the tavern wall,  
He watches you by the tavern ropes.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
your father, your father
has died.
your mother, your mother
has died.
your brother, your brother
has died.
your sister, your sister
has died.
your fiancé
has died.
your father, your father,
has risen.
your mother, your mother
has risen.
your brother, your brother
has risen.
your sister, your sister
has risen.
your fiancé
has risen.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I make a fence, sharpen a stake,  
so that a dream cannot slip over,  
nor a bear over leap,  
nor a snake crawl through.  
Here I leave a knot and  
go to eat tangupuuro (a type of porridge) and drink milk.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Sleep, sleep, little one  
The grandmother will make you a new hood,  
The old man will make a winter coat,  
From flags and patches  
And the old bear’s fur.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go make this path,  
This carved trail,  
Where God has walked before,  
Where Mary has come to earth,  
A holy cross worn on the chest,  
Worn on the chest, worn on the shoulder,  
Risking the chest wearing,  
Enduring the shoulder wearing.  
What could be left on the trail,  
Or fallen on the step from there?  
Gold left on the trail,  
Silver fallen on the step.  
Who foolishly took the gold?  
Who wickedly took the silver?  
I, the foolish Marnikene,  
I, the foolish one, took the gold,  
I, the wicked one, took the silver,  
I took it to my brother's house,  
I took it to my brother's wife,  
I placed it on my brother's table,  
On a nice jug with a lid,  
I tapped a mug for my brother,  
A tin jug with a lid,  
Katik struck the jug's lid,  
Katik, my brother’s wide table,  
Brother dear, little brother dear,  
Take your horse from the yard,  
Don’t complain about the crane's neck.  
Let's take the gold to Viljandi,  
Carry the silver to Tarvastu,  
Viljandi has a skilled blacksmith,  
Tarvastu has a wise smith,  
Let's make wedding rings,  
A pearl necklace for mother’s child.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Moon, moon, little gold
Fern, fern, little leaf
Where was the little gold?
Gold like a haystack
Where was the gold’s little head?
Like a manor measure box
Where were the gold’s little eyes?
Like silver beads
Where were the gold’s little ears?
Like a piece of gold leaf
Where was the gold’s little feather?
A feather from a smoky fur
Where was the gold’s little beak?
Like a bit of wind
Where were the gold’s little feet?
Feet like a hare’s feet
Where were the gold’s little claws?
Like the village ploughshares,
The servant boys’ hardworking ploughshares
Oh, you crazy little gold
You’re looking for the honeycomb
You are looking under the honeycomb’s shadow
We have a chicken on the threshing floor,
The rooster’s golden cellar,
Two chickens in the flock,
Three door keepers,
Four link carriers,
Five key holders,
Six on the locks.
Go to that farm
Where there is a chicken on the threshing floor,
On the dry straw of the rooster.
“Hello, hello man, what are you doing here?”
“I am digging a hole.”
“What will you do with that hole?”
“I will draw in charcoal.”
“What will you do with that charcoal?”
“I will put a pot on it.”
“What will you do with that pot?”
“I will put water in it.”
“What will you do with that water?”
“I will take the feathers of the hen and the rooster.”
“What wrong did the hen and the rooster do?”
“The lord’s trench was plowed,
and the maiden took the peas away.”
“Where were you earlier?”
“Down the hill I built a fence.”
“Where were the women and children?”
“They were carrying wood and reeds.”
“How long was the fence?”
“As high as the sky.
How wide?
As wide as the blade of an axe.
How thick?
Like a sieve for oats.”

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We had one, we had one midrilind,  
he ate away, he ate away his furry berries,  
parsleys, parsleys, beet roots,  
he ran away, he ran away into the rich forest.  
Those with nets, those with nets, let them catch him,  
those with guns, those with guns, let them catch him.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The apple brought a business calf,  
The cow brought a heifer calf,  
The pig went to work, the sickle was on the belt,  
The sheep went to the barn to lay eggs,  
The hen brought two lambs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Moon, moon, little gold,
how do our coins shine?
Like the edge of a sharp sickle...

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Descend, descend, to the grave,
lower, lower the head,
your mother is already dying,
a grave is being dug for mother.
Descend, descend, to the grave,
lower, lower the head,
your father is already dying,
a grave is being dug for father.
Rise, rise, to the grave,
raise, raise the head:
your mother is still alive,
but the mother's grave was filled up.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Don't go to the neighbor's grain field;  
in the neighbor's field comes a sailor,  
another comes, the distant sailor,  
gives a serve along the back,  
pummels you along the chest.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I make a fence in the garden, sharpen a stake,  
so that neither a wolf can get in  
nor a bear can jump over.  
Come, wolf, little one,  
jump, wolf, little bear!  
The woman makes me a new jacket,  
the man makes a furry coat  
from strips and patches,  
from the colorful legs of a goat,  
from old bread crusts' scalp.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Needle, needle, prick,
needle, little needle drop...

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a rich cross-aunt,
She gave me a scruff.
I put the scruff on the dog's neck,
The dog went outside to dig,
To look at the gate.
The wolf ate the dog, the scruff stayed with me,
I put the scruff on the cat's neck.
The irises came to sit,
The rats came to crawl,
They took the cat under the stove.
The stove started to dig
And the floor started to crackle...
The logs started to rumble
Still, the scruff stayed with me.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a rich godmother,  
She gave me a billy goat.  
I put the billy goat around the goat's neck.  
The wolf took the goat, the billy goat stayed with me.  
I put the billy goat around the dog's neck.  
The dog was barking fiercely,  
The billy goat was making a loud noise.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
In Särevere parish at a feast  
I saw a wondrous work:  
Dogs were plowing, heifers were grazing,  
Girls were spinning yarn,  
Old women were weaving carts,  
A sheep went to the barn to lay eggs,  
A hen brought two lambs,  
An apple brought a calf to the calf,  
A cow gave birth to a foal colt.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We have one, we have one piibelind  
our lord, our lord in the garden.  
He ate, he ate the pepper berries,  
he broke, he broke the gooseberries,  
the most beautiful, the most beautiful.  
Father in the forest, father in the forest,  
to the lands of a rich family, a rich family.  
Who has time, who has time, let them chase!  
Who has nets, who has nets, let them catch!  
Who has nets, who has nets, grab hold!

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Six, six, dear,
Kitty, speckled little bird!
I will not give my chicken.
I will feed you with sugar,
and winter buckwheat grains.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The pig went to work, the plow was on the belt,  
the goat went to the herd, a sweater on its back,  
the sheep went to the barn to lay eggs,  
the hen brought two little lambs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I guard grandmother's sheep,  
I protect grandfather's herd.  
Grandmother made me a new sweater,  
a sweater made of white wool.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The red ones were wedding trousers,  
the blue ones were bridge trousers,  
the white ones were cuffed trousers,  
the yellow ones were kos´sa trousers.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were my existence,
if it were my doing,
I would blow away the smoke,
leave the oven cold,
I would make the Germans beg...

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were my existence,
if it were my doing,
I would blow away the smoke,
leave the oven cold,
I would make the Germans beg...

label: other
translation:
I work in the garden, sharpening a stake,  
A golden beet...  
"I'll make a hole here,  
so the bear can't get in,"  
Have you seen my sheep?

label: other
translation:
Let's quickly get dressed,
hurriedly jump up!
To the corner, to the corner, young man!
Let him run away,
let the girl walk far.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
I am grandmother’s ewe,  
I guard the flock.  
Grandmother makes me a new coat,  
to dye the leg warmers blue,  
to dye the ribbons red.  
.......  
The wolf came from the marsh,  
from the pine forest with wide paws,  
took away grandmother’s ewe,  
took away the old man’s stallion.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, the wonder I saw!
A sheep went to the table to lay eggs,
a hen brought two little lambs,
an apple brought a calf for the merchant,
a cow brought a little cock with a scallop shell.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
When we had sieve boats sailing,  
sieve boats, needle boats,  
boys' wooden boats,  
women's wide bird boats.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Owl, what are you looking for  
with your long beak?  
One, two, three,  
all is finished!

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let us go along this made path,  
this carved trail,  
where God has walked before,  
where Mary has come to the land.  
What remains on the trail,  
or what has fallen on that path?  
Gold remains on the trail,  
silver has fallen on the path.  
Kessi took gold to the house,  
Did Kessi’s sister take silver?  
I, the fool, Mannikene,  
I, the fool, took gold,  
I, the sister, took silver;  
gold in the maiden’s palm,  
silver in the maiden’s hand;  
showing the way to the brother at home,  
showing the way to the brother’s wife.  
I placed silver on the floor,  
on the lid of the tin jug,  
the lid struck the jug, the lid,  
the lid struck the wide floor of silver.  
Brother, dear little brother,  
Put  
all in the sister’s hands,  
in the twisted rings on her ears;  
take gold to Viljandi,  
silver maiden to Tartu.  
Viljandi has a skilled blacksmith,  
Tartu has a wise smith;  
let’s make a ring on the finger,  
life bonds for a mother.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
When will I get home,
when will I reach home?
Home is where the heart is,
home is where the heart is
through the forest shining,
over the forest swirling,
flying under the barn roof.
There is a long pine forest,
behind it a wide spruce forest,
in the middle a twisted alder forest.
From home rises golden smoke,
from under the barn the smoke rises.
From the sauna the smoke curls.
Our home has a goldsmith,
the barn is made by the carpenter,
the sauna is built by the cooper.

label: other
translation:
Kuuts, kuuts, little gold,
coo, coo, little nab!
Where was the little gold?
Gold like a feast pile.
Where was gold’s feather?
Feather like a smoked fur.
Where was gold’s little head?
Like the manor’s measuring chest.
Where were gold’s little eyes?
Like little pearls.
Where were gold’s little ears?
Like little gold leaves.
Where was gold’s little nose?
Like a small cake.
Where was gold’s little tail?
Like a little wind vane.
Where were gold’s little feet?
Feet like a rabbit’s feet.
Where were gold’s little claws?
Like the field ploughman’s,
the laborer boys’ ploughing.
What is the gold looking for among us?
Gold is looking for the village hen,
wants the farm’s gift,
the manor’s speckled turkey.
Oh you, silly little gold,
why did you come to our farm,
looking for our home,
looking under our shelter!
We have a hen chamberlain,
a rooster gold cellar,
five key holders,
where the locks are.
Two of us have chicken herds,
three have door guards,
four have tether holders.
Go to that farm,
where there is a hanging hen,
a rooster on a dry straw.

label: other
translation:
What am I a sheep, what am I a sheep?  
I am a guardian, what am I guarding?  
I guard grandmother’s sheep,  
I protect grandfather’s flock.  
Grandmother makes me a new jacket,  
Grandfather makes me a warm coat.  
Come to the herd, little lamb,  
Take the daughter-in-law’s sheep to the forest,  
Leave again the father-in-law’s ram,  
The father-in-law’s ram, the mother-in-law’s sheep,  
The father-in-law’s ram with curly horns,  
The mother-in-law’s sheep from the hollow edge!  
Make a fence, sharpen the fence,  
Around the grass, a ring fence,  
Build from alder wood,  
From slender ash wood,  
From stiff spruce wood,  
From solid pine wood,  
Where wolves cannot eat  
Nor bears snatch  
Nor thieves steal.  
Come to the herd, little lamb,  
Take the daughter-in-law’s sheep to the forest!  
Go for clear bread,  
Bring me a barley cake;  
Go get a glass of vodka,  
Bring me a glass of water;  
Go get porridge without eggs,  
Bring me porridge with bits in it.  
Now I’m going to look for the sheep,  
To ask for it back.  
From here, the sheep has gone diligent,  
From here, the calm  
Woolly sheep has gone to him;  
Here a bee stopped,  
Here cranes complained,  
Here ducks have set their feet.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We had, we had a woodpecker,  
our miss, our miss in the meadow fence.  
There she did, there she did bad mischief.  
She ate away, she ate away the hairy berries,  
parsley, parsley, beet roots,  
chervil, chervil, poppy stems.  
From there she flew, from there she flew to Määküla forest,  
from there she ran, from there she ran to the Likassaare meadow.  
Who has guns, who has guns, may catch her,  
who has nets, who has nets, may catch her!

label: other
translation:
I tend to grandmother’s sheep,  
I protect grandfather’s herd;  
grandmother makes me a new cap,  
a felt cap of white wool.  
I work in the garden, sharpen the staff,  
pour copper into the hayloft,  
so that wolves don’t get in,  
and bears don’t attack the sheep.  
-  
Come, wolf, to the herd,  
take our daughter-in-law’s sheep,  
pull the old woman’s fur coat off her.

label: other
translation:
We had a thrush,  
our darling,  
it caused trouble in the garden:  
it ate the apple blossoms,  
pecked the gooseberries to pieces,  
it flew over our garden.  
Those who have guns, let them catch it;  
those with nets, let them trap it.  
We have guns, we will catch it;  
we have nets, we will trap it.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Surely die, surely die,  
you miserable death!  
Your father, your father  
has long been dead.  
Surely die, surely die,  
you miserable death!  
Your mother, your mother  
has long been dead.  
Surely die, surely die,  
you miserable death!  
Your brothers, your brothers  
have long been dead.  
Surely die, surely die,  
you miserable death!  
Your sisters, your sisters  
have long been dead for a long time.  
Rise up, rise up,  
you miserable death!  
Your father, your father  
has risen up.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Here comes Siim,
here is Siim’s servant,
the honorable lord among all others.
What is Siim looking for,
what is Siim’s servant looking for,
the honorable lord among all others?
Siim is looking for a bride,
Siim’s servant is looking for a bride,
the honorable lord among all others.
Here comes Siim,
here is Siim’s servant,
the honorable lord among all others.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The girl milked the awakened ox from the stall,  
the cow plowed in the field angrily.  
The hen plowed, the stableman forced,  
the sheep went to the barn to lay eggs,  
the hen brought two little lambs,  
the cow brought a calf to the meadow,  
the apple brought a calf from the stall,  
the rooster rummaged in the cabbage field,  
the pig sang on the barn.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I was a slave, I tended the herd,  
yet I had never seen this wonder:  
the spinning wheels boiled the thread,  
the women cut the wheel spokes.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
If it were my being,  
and secondly my doing.  
I would make a room in the wind,  
a sled in the corner of the plain,  
life without a shore.  
I had only one son alone,  
he was wise beyond the world.  
He made a room in the wind,  
a sled in the corner of the plain,  
life without a shore,  
silk ships sailed in,  
trade ships passed through as well.  
If it were my being,  
and secondly my doing,  
I would make a tiny baby,  
a little bread-eating bird.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A petite girl,  
why are you afraid when coming to me,  
coming to me, being with me,  
I will make the room bird cherry,  
the doorposts rowan,  
the partition walls maple,  
the threshold chicken egg.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Uiut tuiut tuura vaara  
Friday and bars, iron and gold  
Kubja Jüri, stout Mari  
Those are my wife's relatives  
But they came to me as guests  
What did I give them to eat?  
Lamb meat, rooster meat  
I killed fresh meat for them  
Rooster dried meat  
Chicken for roasting and frying  
Pepper on the pan

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Dilly-dally, what does it matter,  
I'm going to Narva city to court.  
From there I'll bring my own new Ella,  
as pretty as a wall clock.  
When I came out of the barn,  
oh you fool, I saw a sight!  
The whole world was spinning around,  
a pig wore German shoes,  
a sheep laid eggs on the table,  
a hen brought twin lambs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A strange guest came to us,  
What did we give the guest to eat?  
Lamb's fat, cuckoo droppings,  
brine and swan meat.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let the brewers be healthy,  
Who have combined the three.  
First, the watery liquid,  
Second, the sweet malt,  
Finally, the hop brooms.  
This runs along my vein.  
My throat had dried up,  
Corroded the pipe-cleaner.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
If I were, if I were, I would work in the garden,  
But only the copper old woman would weed,  
Golden handle, copper hoe,  
Silver garden branches.  
So that the wolf cannot get in  
The bear at the sheep,  
I always watch over the old woman’s sheep,  
I always protect the old man’s herd,  
The woman makes me a new jacket,  
The old man makes a hairy coat.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
No, I have not seen that wonder,  
That I saw in Näsuvere.  
Dogs were plowing, oxen barking,  
A sheep went to the table to lay eggs,  
A hen brought two lambs,  
A horse brought the farmer's calf,  
A cow brought a dessert-sized hen.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Quick, quick, barkeeper woman,  
Keep Anne from your beauty,  
Don’t let boys run wild  
Or lead them to the cellar.  
On the table go the lapdogs,  
Drunk strangers without invitation.  
A goat brought you a gray ram,  
Make a hoof lock from its horn.  
When you go out, bolt the door,  
When you come home, open the door wide.  
Give the key to Aadu,  
Or also to little Liisa,  
Old Jakob went to town,  
The boys’ advice was to go there  
To get a sweet kiss from Anne,  
To play her the violin.  
Then the clay hut will echo with howls,  
Straw rustling on the floorboards.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Tall, tall, tall, tall tavern woman  
Keep your beauty, Anne.  
Don't let the board be walked on.  
On the board walk lapdogs  
In the tavern places many people.  
Blacksmith lads, strong boys  
Especially butchers.  
Tavern woman, little woman  
Let me sleep on the straw.  
If you let me sleep on the straw  
Then give your daughter to cuddle  
If you give your daughter to cuddle  
Then give the cradle to rock  
If you give the cradle to rock  
Then give the bow to whistle.  
Don't be angry  
Don't get mad  
There are many words in the song  
To straighten the crooked  
The crooked become riders' ranks  
The straight become horse reins.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Little Sister Birdie  
Do you still remember from last year  
Recall what you told me last year  
You told me last year  
If I had a thousand horses  
Or two hundred oxen  
I would not marry. -  
Now you have married.  
Not coming a thousand horses  
Nor two hundred oxen.  
You were taken on five fingers  
And looked after on ten nails  
What did you drink from the master  
What did you drink for the drunkard  
You went to the pub visit.  
Where is the drunkard’s horse?  
To steal a calf  
And a tavern to hide it in.  
The harness was cold, the reins in the throat  
There is the drunkard’s horse  
To steal a calf.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Be healthy, brewers,
those who have put together six
and mixed in seven.
Estonian water so liquid,
the second came so strong,
the third so sweet with malt,
the fourth dear junipers,
the fifth bundles of hops,
the sixth those hot stones,
the seventh yeast mixed in.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had the thought to set out on a journey,  
To set out, to travel to the countryside.  
I set out, it began to drizzle,  
I reached the countryside, it began to pour,  
The ditch started to fill with water,  
The beams floated on the swamp.  
The wind came, raising a storm,  
Raising my gray oars upright.  
Where then did I go towards the night,  
Towards the night, towards the moon,  
Towards the pale dawn,  
Towards the pitch-dark darkness?

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Mother was Eltse, daughter Kaie,  
Father was Langu, son Vader,  
Bulls barked, dogs plowed,  
The pig was working, the sword was in the belt,  
The sheep went to the barn to lay eggs,  
The hen brought two little lambs,  
Milk was drawn from the cow's horn,  
The horse brought the bull-calf,  
The cow brought a little stallion to the meadow.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The lord had a mouse-gray one,  
The king had a golden ear of grain.  
Ride, ride, little ear,  
Clear the path, little gray one!  
Below I hammered gray irons,  
Lost the boots for the ear.  
The young woman remained unmarried,  
The pewter breastplate definitely stayed,  
The brooch remained on a friend's table,  
The captain ended up with the lord of Paide,  
Clogs were left in the alley!

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We had one, we had a midrilind,  
our master, our master in the garden.  
He did there, he did there much harm,  
much harm, some damage,  
ate up, ate up the currants,  
trampled down, trampled down the barley grains,  
down all those rose flowers.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We had a beautiful girl,  
The girl had two beautiful daughters.  
Which one do you promise to give me?  
I will give you the elder daughter.  
What is wrong with the younger one then?  
The younger one is foolish.  
What do you promise the elder daughter?  
One thousand for the elder daughter.  
What do you promise the younger daughter?  
Two thousand for the younger daughter.  
I will take the younger daughter.  
A year has passed, now give the money,  
I will not give you a single penny.  
I do not want your daughter.  
I will go to another village to ask for marriage.  
I will bring from there a beautiful wife.  
I will bring from there a rich wife.  
Now there is a beautiful wife.  
Now there is a rich wife.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
I rode along the Finnish bridge,  
The Finnish bridge was creaking,  
The foundation log was cracking.  
Do you have a home, girls?  
We don’t have a home, girls.  
The girl went to fetch water from the well.  
Is it allowed to ride on the well road?  
Yes, it is allowed to ride on the well road.  
Did you get your horse?  
And is the horse’s tail tied?  
Yes, I got my horse  
And the horse’s tail is tied.  
Hello, hello, young girl,  
Do you promise to come to me,  
Or shall I go to my brother?  
I have a home with three brothers:  
One tends the horses,  
Another ties the tails,  
The third is a granary clerk.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Come to me, come to me, strange stranger,  
what will I give you to eat?  
Sheep’s bone, bull’s horn,  
Old ram’s foreskin.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
/:Being one of me:/  
/:Making another me:/  
/:I make the granary:/ from pork,/  
/:I make the door:/ from canal meat,/  
/:then I go:/ to courtship road./  
/:Hello father-in-law:/ hello mother-in-law,/  
/:Is the maiden:/ at home,/  
/:the maiden is not:/ at home,/  
/:the maiden went to the well:/ to fetch water,/  
/:the bucket calls:/ bring me water,/  
/:the little girl cries:/ scratched eyes  
/:spoons, bowls:/ unwashed

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Row the swing, reach the swing,  
reach the swing to that land  
where the wind had made a room,  
water had rolled the logs,  
rain had mossed the walls,  
fog had placed doors in front,  
snow had locked the bolts.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Jussi from Kärevere killed the cat  
Made a fur coat for Kaie  
Underwear for Anne  
A sleeping mat for Maie.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Juhan-Jussi killed a cat  
Made a coat for Kaie,  
Made a slip for Anne -

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had to let out a squeak from my butt  
A chubby one came after  
It wasn't my fault  
It was the fault of the tension adjuster  
The adjuster cut the edge of the bench  
My hole stayed slightly open.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Goose, white goose!  
Where are you going?  
To the beach of Riga!  
What are you going to do there?  
To wash the goslings!  
Did you wash mine too?  
No, I did not wash yours either! –  
The very last child will be caught!

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a rich godfather
and an even richer godmother
gave me a little goat
with a bell around its neck.
The wolf ate the little goat
left me the bell
I put the bell in the chest cupboard:
Kolla-kolla-kolla.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Goose, white goose,  
where are you going?  
To the shore of the Riga sea.  
What to do there?  
To wash the gosling.  
Will you wash mine too?  
I won’t wash yours either.  
The very last child will be caught.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a rich godmother  
a rich godfather  
they gave me a little goat  
a little bell around the goat's neck  
then came the wolf in a mad mood  
took the goat, the bell stayed with me.  
I put the bell on the dog's neck  
but the dog barked, the bell clanged.  
Then came the wolf in a mad mood  
took the dog, the bell stayed with me.  
I put the bell on the hen's neck  
but the hen cackled, the bell clanged.  
Then came the hawk in a mad mood  
took the hen, the bell stayed with me.  
I put the bell on the wife's neck  
but the wife groaned, the bell clanged.  
Then came Death in a mad mood  
took the wife, the bell stayed with me.  
Then I put the bell on top of the cupboard  
to mourn the old woman  
to look for the new woman.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
You drank the barrel of beer, right.  
What is there to drink from that barrel of beer:  
foam is on top, yeast is below,  
only the seeds are clear of beer.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Our home far away,  
far away, high away,  
five was to exchange the plain,  
six was for the river to dry,  
seven the swamp's toughness,  
eight the fish trap,  
nine the good river,  
ten from the cold spring.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I am a man, son of a sailor  
Dear son of a fisherman  
I push my boat into the sea  
I push the tar barrels after it  
From there to the western sea island  
There I will build my own little sauna  
A refuge for poor children  
Where command ships do come and go  
Every Friday ships from Riga  
Every day ships from Pärnu  
Every month ships of the king  
Those Germans do speculate  
Ladies marvel  
Whose building could this be  
But I heard a sound in reply  
This is my little sauna  
A refuge for poor children  
The floor was made of elk kneecaps  
The walls of elk shin bones  
The ceiling was of bird feathers  
The benches of pork meat  
The roof made from hen eggs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh you little hop fool  
Little speck on the tabletop  
You make the saw go for a ride  
The saw women to dance naked  
Men roar without hats  
Women dance without shoes.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go, men,
Light the pipes,
A hundred saunas to smoke,
A thousand rooms to storm.
Our home far away,
Five to change the oxen,
Six to dry the rivers,
Seven sturdy hay barns,
Eight fish ponds,
Nine good rivers,
Ten cold springs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I was once a young man,
I was like a beautiful bean,
Grew like a fish in the sea,
Indeed, indeed, young girls
Looked at me across the streets.
I had a little black horse,
A nice neck harness.
Then the girls looked at me like this:
If only this lad were mine,
Riding along the street,
With a saddle under the horse,
A hat like a church bell,
A belt like a rainbow.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
If it were up to me,  
if it were up to my doing,  
I would put you on the edge of the wind,  
on a large island,  
on the other side of the world.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The coal was not for the farter,  
the coal was for the bench turner.  
The bench turned the coal,  
the farter remained a fool.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Töölaulud
translation:
I do not have to go,  
do not, dear, disappear,  
do not leave the people’s year,  
from a better place.  
I knew the life where I lived,  
the harvest season where I grew up,  
I did not know a home,  
how I should go home,  
to the miserable howlers.  
The home was angrily quarrelsome,  
the home was cruelly stern,  
the home was confusingly chaotic,  
the home was envyingly jealous.  
Leenu is laying down by the shore,  
My uncle gives harsh words,  
Jüri ticks off the angry ones,  
Who sings to me lovingly?  
Liisu sings to me lovingly.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Töölaulud
translation:
I do not have to go,  
do not, dear, disappear,  
do not leave the people’s year,  
from a better place.  
I knew the life where I lived,  
the harvest season where I grew up,  
I did not know a home,  
how I should go home,  
to the miserable howlers.  
The home was angrily quarrelsome,  
the home was cruelly stern,  
the home was confusingly chaotic,  
the home was envyingly jealous.  
Leenu is laying down by the shore,  
My uncle gives harsh words,  
Jüri ticks off the angry ones,  
Who sings to me lovingly?  
Liisu sings to me lovingly.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, saddle, devil's skin,  
Kilter, patchwork goat's skin,  
Does not let children be at noon,  
The right time until evening.  
Children's home far away:  
Five was to exchange the land,  
Six was for the river to dry up;  
Seven for strong veins,  
Eight for fish traps,  
Nine for good rivers,  
Ten for cold springs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Strong winds are blowing.
Ärman rode with a roar,
the coachman with six blacks,
a broken wagon, a bare bog,
a dull gelding, a heavy whip,
a crying child and a drunk woman —
(these are a curse to all).

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Go to the corner, young men,  
under the table, pipe men,  
my brother is coming to spar,  
the boot leg is coming to tap.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Here comes Siim, here comes Siim's servant;  
What is Siim looking for,  
What is Siim's servant?  
A two-headed heel,  
A tuft of hair,  
A linen-winged little bird,  
A dutiful father among all the others.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Did Kissi cut her finger yesterday?  
(Leena) The girl cut her finger.  
Did Kissi jump into the diaper?  
(Ants) She jumped into the diaper,  
Took a neckerchief from her neck,  
Tied that finger with it,  
Wrapped up the wet mess.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The mother-in-law was an old woman and  
The father-in-law was an old man and  
Please set the pot  
I break off the little corner  
I had broth pushed away and  
The pot was thrown outside

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The master praised the work generation  
The mistress the spirit generation  
When they came for an hour's work  
For two hours of trial  
Whether they put to sleep on a pillow  
Wrapped in sheets  
The work would make them labor  
Eat a piece of food  
No sleeping on a pillow  
No wrapping in sheets  
They would think of a piece of work  
A yoke thrown around the neck  
Loaded with a stick in front  
Oh, despite myself I plow a piece  
I left the furrow for the donkey  
I left the second for the ox  
Then I got a piece ahead of the other  
On the working day at the other's ear  
I wait for food to be brought  
To bring water in a white basin  
I did see food being brought  
But water in a black basin  
Immediately my mood fell sad  
My heart began to ache  
No matter the thing I was given  
No matter the food brought to me  
That was not grown on the stump  
Picked bitter sorrel leaves  
And toasted bread crusts  
I took it up and got angry  
Drove the oxen to the grass  
Chased the oxen to the corner of the yard  
Put the whip in the garden  
And struck the shafts against the sauna wall  
Then I went to the Aru tavern  
There I took some measures of vodka  
Two weak jugs in the handle  
Brought strong beer  
Then I felt my foot stepping  
A little foot tapping  
Surely feet lead me home  
Legs weave to the bed  
Knees tuck under the blanket

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The master praised the work generation  
The mistress the spirit generation  
When they came for an hour's work  
For two hours of trial  
Whether they put to sleep on a pillow  
Wrapped in sheets  
The work would make them labor  
Eat a piece of food  
No sleeping on a pillow  
No wrapping in sheets  
They would think of a piece of work  
A yoke thrown around the neck  
Loaded with a stick in front  
Oh, despite myself I plow a piece  
I left the furrow for the donkey  
I left the second for the ox  
Then I got a piece ahead of the other  
On the working day at the other's ear  
I wait for food to be brought  
To bring water in a white basin  
I did see food being brought  
But water in a black basin  
Immediately my mood fell sad  
My heart began to ache  
No matter the thing I was given  
No matter the food brought to me  
That was not grown on the stump  
Picked bitter sorrel leaves  
And toasted bread crusts  
I took it up and got angry  
Drove the oxen to the grass  
Chased the oxen to the corner of the yard  
Put the whip in the garden  
And struck the shafts against the sauna wall  
Then I went to the Aru tavern  
There I took some measures of vodka  
Two weak jugs in the handle  
Brought strong beer  
Then I felt my foot stepping  
A little foot tapping  
Surely feet lead me home  
Legs weave to the bed  
Knees tuck under the blanket

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Here comes Siim,  
Here Siim’s servant...  
Honored master -  
Among all the others...

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Virgins, very young,  
Tiny and slender.  
Let’s come together in three,  
Hold a council as four,  
Adorn ourselves in silk,  
Dress ourselves in thread,  
In red ribbons,  
In green checkers;  
Let’s leave this land,  
Go to Finland for the summer,  
To Poland for half the summer,  
To Tartu’s road for the winter;  
There we will build a stone church,  
Carve an iron chapel.  
Let the lack of virgins come here,  
Lack of virgins, maiden hunger,  
An empty fate awaits the girls—  
Then a horse will be sought for us,  
The shanks and legs,  
The hooves of foals will be groomed  
In search of that girl,  
To find that bird,  
That maiden—  
Then we will go as a banner into the town,  
As a crown upon the rooms,  
As a gate at the shop door—  
Then the boys will beg us on their knees,  
Bow without hats:  
“Fall down, little heel,  
Fall onto my hat,  
Roll upon five fingers,  
Fall under a wide hat!”  
I hear and answer back:  
If I fall, who will pay the kiss?  
The cheek pays for a pair of underwear,  
The other cheek for another pair;  
The ears raise a staff,  
The eyes a silk town,  
The nose young as a blossom.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Virgins, very young,  
Tiny and slender.  
Let’s come together in three,  
Hold a council as four,  
Adorn ourselves in silk,  
Dress ourselves in thread,  
In red ribbons,  
In green checkers;  
Let’s leave this land,  
Go to Finland for the summer,  
To Poland for half the summer,  
To Tartu’s road for the winter;  
There we will build a stone church,  
Carve an iron chapel.  
Let the lack of virgins come here,  
Lack of virgins, maiden hunger,  
An empty fate awaits the girls—  
Then a horse will be sought for us,  
The shanks and legs,  
The hooves of foals will be groomed  
In search of that girl,  
To find that bird,  
That maiden—  
Then we will go as a banner into the town,  
As a crown upon the rooms,  
As a gate at the shop door—  
Then the boys will beg us on their knees,  
Bow without hats:  
“Fall down, little heel,  
Fall onto my hat,  
Roll upon five fingers,  
Fall under a wide hat!”  
I hear and answer back:  
If I fall, who will pay the kiss?  
The cheek pays for a pair of underwear,  
The other cheek for another pair;  
The ears raise a staff,  
The eyes a silk town,  
The nose young as a blossom.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Dog, dog, dog, dog, little king,
King, little golden boy.
Why didn’t you visit us last year
The year before last we didn’t have
When you came to take orders
Fives, fives, six callers
Hundred seven messengers. -
Now you come in spring
Spring in a poor time
Now you tear off clothes
Break the curls from the neck
Bead them around the neck. -
Leena, little child
Throw off your beads
Save your pearl necklace
Redeem the silver necklace

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
When will we get there,
where those roosters eat gold.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk;  
Let's go along this road,  
Where slaves have walked before,  
Slaves have walked, oxen have gone,  
Early on, poor children had walked,  
Late those  
Late sang the roosters of Hiiumaa,  
During the day sang the roosters of Pärnu.  
Boys  
Liquids [or fluids] point to time.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Once I traveled to Turkey,  
A bit to Narva land,  
Half a mile to Poland,  
I didn’t see the wonder,  
That I saw in my village,  
In my Otsaje household:  
Bulls were barking, dogs were plowing,  
The sheep went to the barn to lay eggs,  
The hen brought two lambs,  
The cow brought a calf of the meadow.  
The horse was a bull-calf,  
The girls were making weddings,  
The women were chopping wheel wood,  
The men were walking the tavern road.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The leenik, the blade of grass,  
Whose leenik would it be? –  
Who tore the loom reed,  
Who brought water to the little tub,  
Carried with two buckets,  
Dripped tin onto the fish,  
Poured copper from the dish,  
Who is her lover,  
She brought water to the little tub  
Carried with two buckets.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Drip, drip, little needle,  
Drip, little needle's eye.  
The needle would disappear in the heath,  
The copper needle to the oven,  
The golden needle into the chest,  
The tin needle into the thicket,  
The green eye into the willow grove.  
The slave sought donkeys,  
The aunt's children sought uncles.  
The slave did not look for good things,  
The aunt's child did not know me.  
My slave searched for pine wood,  
The aunt's children for oak wood.  
I went slowly,  
Along the road but very small steps.  
I found the needle at the needle's mast,  
In a cloud of linen weaving,  
Peeking at the groom's shirt,  
The father-in-law's shirt, the mother-in-law's shirt,  
The broad father-in-law's shirt,  
The delicate mother-in-law's shirt,  
With narrow cuffs,  
And knitted little mittens.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Töölaulud
translation:
Let's go this stubborn path,  
This carved trail  
Where God has walked before  
Where Mary came to earth,  
Carried the holy cross on her chest,  
Carried on her chest, came to the shoulder;  
Carried on the chest more quickly  
Came to the shoulder more heavily.  
What was left on top again?  
Gold was left on top again.  
What fell on the step there?  
Silver fell on the step.  
Who foolishly took the gold?  
I foolishly took the gold.  
Who wickedly took the silver?  
I wickedly took the silver,  
I stuffed the gold in my pocket,  
I turned the silver to my wrist,  
Turned it behind the wristbands.  
I took it home to brother,  
Put it on the brother’s table,  
On a nice jug’s lid.  
On brother’s table clinking  
Tinkling of a pewter jug  
The jug was damaged, the lid was damaged  
Brother’s wide table was damaged.  
"Brother, dear little brother,  
Take the gold to Viljandi,  
The cup silver to Tarvastu,  
Viljandi has a skilled smith,  
Tarvastu has a clever smith,  
Make a ring for the road servant,  
Make a buckle for the road servant."

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Töölaulud
translation:
Let's go this stubborn path,  
This carved trail  
Where God has walked before  
Where Mary came to earth,  
Carried the holy cross on her chest,  
Carried on her chest, came to the shoulder;  
Carried on the chest more quickly  
Came to the shoulder more heavily.  
What was left on top again?  
Gold was left on top again.  
What fell on the step there?  
Silver fell on the step.  
Who foolishly took the gold?  
I foolishly took the gold.  
Who wickedly took the silver?  
I wickedly took the silver,  
I stuffed the gold in my pocket,  
I turned the silver to my wrist,  
Turned it behind the wristbands.  
I took it home to brother,  
Put it on the brother’s table,  
On a nice jug’s lid.  
On brother’s table clinking  
Tinkling of a pewter jug  
The jug was damaged, the lid was damaged  
Brother’s wide table was damaged.  
"Brother, dear little brother,  
Take the gold to Viljandi,  
The cup silver to Tarvastu,  
Viljandi has a skilled smith,  
Tarvastu has a clever smith,  
Make a ring for the road servant,  
Make a buckle for the road servant."

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Moon, moon, little gold,
Naats, naats, little nabrakene,
What was the gold?
Gold was a pile of hay.
What was the gold's head?
Like a manor measure box.
What was the gold's nose?
Like a seagull's beak.
What were the gold's eyes?
Like silver beads.
What were the gold's ears?
Like gold leaf.
What were the gold's feathers?
Feathers of a smoky chick.
What was the gold's tail?
Like a wind vane.
What were the gold's feet?
Feet like a rabbit's feet.
What were the gold's claws?
Like the village plow iron,
The toil iron of the boys of the parish.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Our Leena, the tallest,  
The tallest, the finest,  
The neatest, the politest.  
She went to the tavern to dance.  
A message was sent after her:  
"Come home, little Leena,  
The farmhand wants to leave with us,  
The farmhand's gloves are pinned shut  
Or someone wants to play  
The churn wants to be tapped.  
Come home, little Leena.  
At home we have five shots of vodka,  
Five shots of vodka, six weddings,  
A hundred and seven messages.  
I cannot come, I cannot be,  
I still want to dance."

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Liisukene, little bow,
A wink of an eye, a little snap,
A little tap in the ear,
An old rascal in the shadows.
Liisa heard the sound of a violin,
Then she flashed outside,
Popped onto the floor,
Tapped on the boy's ear.
Liisu heard the sound of a spinning wheel,
The clinking sound of the strike of a code,
Then she slipped into the barn,
Escaping into the straw.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
It would be my being,  
Secondly my doing.  
I would make the room upon the wind,  
The house upon the raspberry stem,  
The sauna upon the big island,  
The hut upon the coltsfoot leaf.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh dear, take me,
Take me to the party,
Carry me inside the crowd,
Bring me a dancer from Tartu,
A roller from Viljandi,
Who will skillfully dance me,
Roll me to the tune,
Spin me in a circle.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I drink beer, crack my knuckles,  
I throw the whips into the whip case,  
The bough boards into the bog.  
Sõnna had grown large islands  
In every branch there was a squirrel,  
At every tip there was a titmouse,  
In every leaf a songbird.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Jüri Jürk, Jaani Kärki,
Jüri, servant of God,
The little table of Hell's Bottom,
The Devil's grindstone,
Jüri drinks, Jüri is drunk,
Jüri doesn't give to anyone.
He had two jugs in his hands,
And five fingers on the whip handle.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
At Umalas, the mountain owl calls,  
The pine cone rattles down the shore:  
"Come home, young man,  
Take me in a sack to your home."  
Place me on the edge of the board,  
From there I roll into the cluster,  
I crawl into the half-glass of beer,  
I slip between the branches."  
From there, beer reaches the cheek,  
Joy of the strong four-armed.  
I take senses from the heads of men,  
Half the senses from the heads of boys,  
Thanks to the wise women's heads.  
Men make noise without hats,  
Women dance without skirts,  
Girls run without belts,  
Boys with half boots.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I would dance the tatsa-ratsa:  
afraid the whistles would whine,  
the willow bark would crack.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A lazy person created by the family daughter  
Created to be lazy and idle  
Sleeps like a snake on the ground  
Leans against the wall like a pig  
Didn’t bother to bring water  
Sent the cat to bring water  
The cat went to the well road itself  
The tail dozed off to the sauna road  
The body went to the church  
The feathers went to the sanctuary.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The crowd has visited the proud old woman  
A row of two rich women  
Have not made a path to beauty  
A path to beauty, a way to charm  
To the high depths of the forest  
To the wide field of songs.  
I alone am the old woman’s daughter  
The enemy’s daughter, the elder’s daughter  
The only daughter of Anne  
I make the path to beauty  
The path to beauty, the way to charm  
To the high depths of the forest  
To the wide field of songs  
So that no path will take me  
The wide field will not leave me  
The high forest will not speak.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Go to the corner, go to the corner young men,  
Under the bench, under the bench pipe smokers.  
My brother is coming, my brother is coming to fence,  
The cobbler is coming, the cobbler is coming to shear,  
The shoemaker is coming, the shoemaker is coming to clean.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
My worldly brother  
Built a stable under the sky  
Near the corner of the dawn.  
What did he put inside it?  
He put in three stallions  
One was gray, another dark bay  
The third blood-red.  
It went on a trip to Finland  
To see the Finnish stallion.  
Knocking on the Finnish bridge  
Pounding the foundation beams  
The Finnish stallions ran out  
To buy his horse  
To want his stallion.  
They offered a lot of money:  
A hundred measures of rye  
A thousand bushels of wheat  
Countless ruble pieces.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
My worldly brother  
Built a stable under the sky  
Near the corner of the dawn.  
What did he put inside it?  
He put in three stallions  
One was gray, another dark bay  
The third blood-red.  
It went on a trip to Finland  
To see the Finnish stallion.  
Knocking on the Finnish bridge  
Pounding the foundation beams  
The Finnish stallions ran out  
To buy his horse  
To want his stallion.  
They offered a lot of money:  
A hundred measures of rye  
A thousand bushels of wheat  
Countless ruble pieces.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Beetle, beetle, animal flock
Whip, hay tube
Who went to the hayfield yesterday
It was Anne, the maiden
The neighbor girl, the young woman
Who cut this finger
It was the bridegroom boy
Who jumped into the swaddling clothes
It was the bridegroom boy
Where did he take this scarf
He took a neckerchief from his neck
His own silk eye kerchief
Where did he take this ribbon
He took a belt ribbon from his belt
His own silk leg ribbon
Tied this finger
Wrapped it up wet with ointment.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We want to go through  
Our gate is broken  
We want to repair  
Miska, you repair  
Yesterday Väli went to town  
Brought silk velvet  
We want to repair

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I keep my father’s custody  
I protect my gentle brother’s desert  
In the meadows of lords  
In the piles of kings  
Among the German envoys  
This is not a garden of weeds  
Nor a garden of hard spruce branches  
This is a garden of maidens  
Of mother’s children in the barn  
Poured into the arm field.  
Young men came along.  
Sell off my father’s custody  
Trade my gentle brother’s desert  
Then you’ll get a thousand thalers.  
I won’t sell my father’s custody  
I won’t trade my gentle brother’s desert.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We drink in the tavern until dawn  
We lick until wide white (morning)  
If we lack money  
Surely we have work at home  
We have a home and money too  
In the vest pocket there is still money  
In the barn, money for a gray stallion  
In the cowhouse, money for a speckled cow  
In the cowhouse drawer, money for a horn.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Today we drink to full goblets  
Tomorrow the goblets bring a hangover  
Wine cups lead to dizziness.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The dogs plowed to a squat  
The sheep went to sleep on the table  
The hen brought two lambs  
The horse neighed in Hiiumaa.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The road was crooked, crooked  
The plowshare held crooked  
The willow bush looked crooked  
Madis marched crookedly on the highway  
Harness creaking and pants cracking  
Skin of a goose and a pipe stem.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Snore, snore, tavern woman  
Protect your beautiful daughter  
Guard your dear daughter  
Don’t let the rabid go free  
Rabid ones are guarded by watch dogs  
Crazy strangers howl beneath.  
From there he falls onto the rooster’s back  
Startles the sow’s back  
Jumps onto the dog’s back  
Rides the bull’s back.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
There was plenty of beer and the return was wise  
Kali was cunning beyond measure  
It took the minds from the men's heads  
Half the minds from the boys' heads  
The temples fell on the women’s heads  
Men made noise without hats  
Women danced without a towel  
Boys with half their boots on  
You drink the beer, tip it back  
Take the copper against your chest  
The legs will carry you home  
The shins will carry you to the bed  
Dear sister Marikene  
Listen, sweet cousin  
When will we, the dear ones, come together  
The dear ones together, the loved ones united  
The dear ones together in the hayfield  
The loved ones together in the meadow  
You have a brother, I have a companion  
You put the threshing floor to work  
I put the ploughing field to work  
Then we, the dear ones, will be together.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
When will I get home  
How far is my home  
The path seems far  
From the path a couple appear  
The roof ridge of the hut was green  
Now the daughter comes from work  
The child comes poor and weary  
The well-digger  
The water carrier.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Kuts-kuts little darling  
Nats-nats little crumb  
Why is the darling looking for us  
The darling wants farmyard animals  
Manor's speckled turkeys  
The darling was a little darling  
Darling as a little haystack  
Darling is the darling's little eye  
Darling is the darling's little head  
Head like the manor's measure box  
Darling is the darling's little nose  
Nose like the village's well pit  
Darling is the darling's little claw  
Claws like the village's plowshares  
Hello, hello little darling  
What are you doing here, little darling  
Digging a hole, little master  
What are you doing with the hole, little darling  
Pulling the feed in, little master  
What are you doing with the feed, little darling  
Putting it on the pot, little master  
What are you doing with the pot, little darling  
Putting water inside, little master  
What are you doing with the water, little darling  
Taking feathers from the hen and rooster, little master  
What have the hens and roosters done wrong, little darling  
They ate the landowner's beans and master's peas  
and the maiden's lentils, little master.  
Where were you yourself, little darling  
I was making a garden below the hill, little master  
Where were the women and children, little darling  
They brought beams and stakes, little master  
How wide is this garden, little darling  
As wide as a full grain ear, little master  
How long is this garden, little darling  
As long as from the earth to the sky, little master  
Don’t deceive, didn’t I see  
That you slept at the sauna doorstep  
A loaf of bread on your head  
And a clump of cream by your foot.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, you little hop fool,  
Tiny little Taputulg,  
Who helped you onto the stove,  
Placed you on the edge of the stove?  
From there you rolled down the stick,  
Slipped with half a beer,  
The donkey rolled down the stick,  
The beer straw rose up.  
Who tasted there, lost their senses,  
Lost their senses from men's heads,  
Half the senses from boys' heads,  
Temples from women's foreheads,  
Men clanged without hats,  
Women danced without scarves,  
Boys with half a boot on.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Why do you boast for nothing
You waste your own time
There are many of you, many of us
There are actually more of us
We have crowds of hooligans outside
Groups of men at the gates
Come inside, defiant ones
Those who pick the bones together
Those big noses thick
Rare teeth, thick lips
Those too, like a fertilizing predator.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
If it were for me to exist  
If it were for me to act  
I would surely know what I would do  
I would do it on the room’s wind  
On the world’s leftovers  
On the house’s berry branch  
On the hut’s clubleaf  
There big swords would walk  
Lords would roam in groups  
Noblemen would walk on the porch  
Village schoolboys orderly  
All those swords would puzzle  
Lords would wonder  
Is this the ruin Rakvere  
Or is it the dark Petersburg  
Is this the Finnish salt ship  
Or is it the Pärnu sailing ship  
I would answer to respond  
No, this is not the ruin Rakvere  
Nor the dark Petersburg  
Nor the Finnish salt ship  
Nor the Pärnu sailing ship  
This is a poor little cripple  
The shadow of a poor child.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Where do we go tonight  
Tonight, to the moon  
To the bright crescent  
To the dark crescent  
Let's go to listen to the moon  
To look at the stars  
Has the moon made butter  
Has the star rolled the cream  
The moon has not made butter  
The star has not rolled the cream  
Dear little sisters,  
Maidens, young girls.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Bishop's pipe, reed mace  
Viru whip, hay pipe  
Who cut a finger yesterday  
So that Mari, the maiden  
Who jumped to touch  
So that Juhan, the boy,  
Where did he get that scarf,  
Took the neckerchief from his neck  
Tied that finger  
Where did he get the ribbon  
Took the garter from the leg  
Tied that finger  
Bishop's pipe, root bark.  
Viru whip, hay pipe  
Who plows the hill here  
Cultivates down the wilderness  
Juhan plows the hill there  
Cultivates down the wilderness  
Who went to bring food  
Mari went to bring food  
Mari went to bring food  
Mari had cheese pies in her lap  
Under the nose were rye breads  
Bishop’s pipe, grass berry  
Viru whip, hay pipe.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
By our Tiiu road  
There was a misty bush  
Big Germans passed by there  
Every Friday the Germans from Riga  
Every Saturday the Germans from Laupa  
Every month the princes  
By our Tiiu road.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The ancient road was crooked  
The woman with a straw walked crooked  
But poured crooked shots of vodka  
For the whiskey barrels.  
Tilting on a slope  
But I am staring behind the barrel  
Staring behind the stick of the barrel  
How pennies are collected  
Given without reason  
Nights and days are worked as a slave  
A penny over three days  
Even that with great effort.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Row the swing, reach the swing  
Row the swing to the land  
Where my father would live  
Where my mother would live.

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I am awaited at home  
I am watched at the gate  
To bring water home immediately  
Father is drying from the stove  
Grandfather goes to cut oak  
The oak had grown to the sky  
Branches pierced the clouds  
From there you can get ship boards  
Ship boards, sail wood  
Wood for rafts on the water  
Bridges over the swamps  
What was left  
From there you can get a small brother's sauna  
Where my brother goes to beat with a birch whisk  
Apoorles go weaving  
Through pass the Mündi lads  
Through go the Kaalepi lads  
Straight ahead go the Arina lads  
Thinking of Lihula town  
Dreaming of Toompea tower  
This is my little brother’s sauna  
Where my brother goes to beat with a birch whisk  
Apoorles go weaving  
One who cares for flowers  
Two who fears the bulls  
A little said out loud  
It is not my own fault  
Nor my partner's fault growing  
My own into the valleys  
My partner far away  
Keeps a watch over the lake  
Came to the market red-faced  
The mud drinks from the blueberry  
The pike drinks the gray one  
The stone drinks from the kimmel  
He comes home or not  
Brings bread, or does not bring  
The empty one cares even about bread  
The other about city bread  
If he would even come home  
He would bring the horse home  
I left a little bird in the town  
A lover in the borough  
A companion at the fish shore  
City women who have eaten meat  
Fat ones who ate the shore crabs  
Blood-drunk Viru crows.

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:

I am watched at the gate  
To bring water home immediately

Grandfather goes to cut oak

Apoorles go weaving

Two who fears the bulls

My own into the valleys

The mud drinks from the blueberry  
The pike drinks the gray one  
The stone drinks from the kimmel
