Brings bread, or does not bring  
The empty one cares even about bread  
The other about city bread  
If he would even come home  
He would bring the horse home  
I left a little bird in the town  
A lover in the borough  
A companion at the fish shore  
City women who have eaten meat  
Fat ones who ate the shore crabs  
Blood-drunk Viru crows.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Go to the corner, go to the corner young men  
Under the bench, under the bench pipe smokers  
Behind the counter, too lazy  
My brother will come rolling  
A nice shoulder will come turning  
A twisted sock will come rolling  
A nice coat will come turning.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The wolf had gone to the proud grandmother  
The raven had gone to the strong grandmother  
The border had gone to the plump grandmother  
They did not make flowers on the road  
They did not make berries in the field  
The forest's tall tree had a music box  
In the wide meadow a song chain  
I myself will go, a little one  
Along the road, a tiny one  
Across the land, a low one  
But I will make flowers on the road  
But I will make berries in the field  
The forest's tall tree had a music box  
In the wide meadow a song chain  
The trees were reed sheds  
Without youth, without men  
Without sharpness, without strength  
The trees were, the trees were gentle brothers  
The trees were hosts to lice and insects  
Branches full of squirrels  
Crowns full of songbirds.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Go to the corner, young men  
Lazy boys under the table  
My brother will come rolling  
The spurred foot will come turning  
The booted calf will come squeezing.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Now things have gone crazy
The ploughman ploughed, the foreman forced
The pig kneaded bread and rolls
The sheep went to the table for eggs
The hen brought two lambs
The cow gave a male calf of hay
The horse brought a business calf.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Drink to your throat, carry your neck  
Take the twig to the copper chest  
Surely your ears will lead you home  
The shins set the bed  
The knees go to sleep  
The toes move the carpet.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh wonder, oh wonder  
What a wonder I saw  
What I saw in Näu village  
In Illevere meadow  
Between the village of Küllevere  
A pig was kneading bread  
A woman was rummaging in the laughing grounds  
Girls were making garlands  
Old women were driving carts  
Men were weaving fabric  
Boys were pulling here and there  
They go nika-naka  
Floorboards clatter and rattle.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The hops started to go wild  
Moving feet in the city  
It took the senses of the men's heads  
Temples facing the women's backs  
Men made noise without hats  
Women danced without shoes  
Boys with half a boot inside.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, you cat, you lightning eye  
Lightning eye, needle tail  
In the peel of the pot, in the angry woman's pot  
Licked in the lazy woman's pot  
Five but on the he-goat  
I went to bother the cat  
Did it flee to the well?  
Its big tail stayed in the water  
I went to skin the cat  
Rope for the visit, husband for the house  
Two ropes from the thief  
The steward's fine bulls  
The old ones of the steward both  
When I got to the cat's shore  
The mice laughed from under the garden:  
Well, well, little cat,  
Where are my six little boys,  
Five little brothers-in-law,  
All ended in the cat's bottom,  
On the tip of the lightning eye's tongue.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Rikenboogen, rikenboogen  
Rupe-rupe-reroo  
Eistvere's instrument  
Tallasvere's dance,  
Step out of the company  
We have too much company  
Poor, poor fellow  
Rupe-rupe-reroo  
Step into our company  
We have enough company.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Where does he come from, where does he come from,  
Ani ani white  
Where does he come from, where does he come from  
Idle idle mischief  
From the sea ice shore  
Ani ani white  
What is he doing there, what is he doing there  
Idle idle mischief  
Ani washing the calves, ani washing the calves  
Ani ani white  
Did you wash my calves  
Idle idle mischief  
You were even there  
Ani ani white  
We want to pass through  
Idle idle mischief  
Our gate is broken,  
Ani ani white  
Of golden copper shiny gates  
Idle idle mischief  
Who will repair the gate  
Idle idle mischief  
A poor gate will repair it  
Golden copper shiny gate  
With whom will he repair  
Idle idle mischief  
With silk and velvet  
With bright chalk  
Golden copper shiny gates.

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The crow was a diligent little bird  
But laid an egg beneath my hill  
In a hollow under a new era  
Laid six little eggs  
In the hollow, five sons were born  
From one came a good grandmother's child  
From another came wise grandfather's children  
From the third came a dog boy  
From the fourth came a guardian of the threshing floor  
From the fifth came a playmate  
He began making a kannel (traditional Estonian zither)  
Where he broke the kannel's boards  
Smashed and broke the kannel's boards  
Where he twisted the kannel strings?  
The girl with long hair, though.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Hello Reinu from Tedre  
Did you see Reinu from Kadaka?  
To ask Reinu Reinult  
Were your wives healthy?  
The empty knows the wives' health  
Their mouths are tilted towards vodka  
Tilted towards the vodka glass  
Their noses are turned towards beer  
Turned towards the beer jug  
Their legs are crooked towards straws  
Crooked towards the straw bench.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Be healthy, brewers  
Brewers, mashers  
Who made the beer miki-maki  
Miki-maki from the land of meadows  
From proud hops  
From stingy spring water  
Often from healing herbs  
Diligently from hops  
Then they made the beer miki-maki  
Then we drink beer, we make another  
The third ferments in the chamber  
The fourth moves to the city  
The fifth takes it to the field  
The sixth is sowed  
The seventh stands in the seed bag  
The eighth is growing  
The ninth is upstairs  
And the tenth is being aerated  
The hops started to drive crazy  
To move feet in the city  
Took the senses from men's heads  
Half the senses from boys' heads  
The temples from women's heads.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I have been around, I have seen  
Yet I have not seen this  
What I saw at Näsa farm  
Bulls were barking, dogs were plowing  
Girls were making regesi  
Women were cutting wheel spokes  
Father was handling the bull's yoke  
Grandfather was needed for firewood  
Men walked on the straw road  
Men marched across the land  
They shouted on the straw-covered hill  
Sang by the straw table.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Little instrument, little boy  
Dear kannel, little player  
No instrument can live without a family  
The kannel must accompany life  
The instrument walks along the path  
Looking for the beer building  
Peeking into the vodka chamber

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Here comes, here comes Siim and Siim  
Here Siim's, here Siim's servant, servant  
Noble, noble lord and lord  
Among all others, among all others  
Who is looking, who is looking Siim and Siim  
Who Siim's, who Siim's servant, servant  
Noble, noble lord and lord  
Before all others, before all others  
Looking for a bride, looking for a bride Siim and Siim  
Bride of Siim, bride of Siim's servant, servant  
Not from here, not from here Siim and Siim  
Not Siim's, not Siim's servant, servant.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, king little king  
Rich Riga gentleman  
A true German from Pärnu  
Why didn’t you come to me last year  
The year before last when you knew  
When you came to collect orders  
When rivers ran without oil  
Now you have come to bad times  
To the withered autumn  
To the light spring.  
What do you ask of the fences now  
Now you strip and undress  
You break the neck and bones.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Droplet, droplet little needle  
Droplet little needle eye  
Why does this needle seek us,  
The needle seeks sewing  
I went myself, little handle  
Along the path, tiny one  
Along the grass, little rose  
Along the ground, little low one  
I found the needle for sewing  
Peeking into the groom's shirt  
Sewing the mist linen  
Where did this needle disappear  
The needle disappeared into the heath  
Into the green-eyed willow grove  
To the tin-eyed road  
Sewing the mist linen  
Peeking into the groom's shirt.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Jews, Jews, sly brothers  
Jews drink even the straw  
I lick even the ear  
On Monday I drink a new six  
On Tuesday I drink a whole shirt  
Wednesday a nice coat.  
Thursday I drink among them  
Friday I drink all the clothes off  
Saturday I drink a wide belt.  
Sunday off with the pants  
Then I was completely naked  
Where the throat had put the six  
Where the coat was on the throat  
To the tavern room’s beam end  
Under the ceiling on the bench  
Under the strength of five vodkas  
In front of six beer jugs  
Others go to action on Monday  
I after on Tuesday  
The stable attendant resolutely drives  
In the warm place a piece of bacon  
In the swamp to the ram a wet piece  
I stubbornly plough  
I won’t break the stallion’s chest.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We have one, we have one finch  
Our master, our master in the garden  
Ate all those, ate all those blackberries  
Dropped all, dropped all the peppercorns  
Wasted all, wasted all those rose hips  
Kept all those, kept all those parsley roots  
Take the bench from the attic  
The iron poker from the window  
The grass trough from the row  
Let's go hunting the bird

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The horse neighs in the haystack  
The foal lies curled up  
The horse steps on the bottom of the sieve  
The foal lies on the bottom of the cradle.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, you little vodka brother  
Oh, you little hop crazy one  
You go straight to the stomach with a rumble  
And down the belly in a rush  
You go to walk in the stomach  
Marching along the belly.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A little lamb, a little sibling  
Did you see on my behalf  
Roaming on the street  
Coming back from the market?  
What kind of horse do you have?  
In the morning, squirrel-colored,  
In the south, tan-colored,  
It went through our yard.  
The rope was golden behind it,  
Shackles scared its feet,  
It tasted our seawater,  
It tried our well water,  
It pulled our butter from the churn.  
Then it went to our cattle pen,  
Trampled down the stable doors,  
Broke the cattle pen gates.  
Jump, jump, forest spirit,  
Shout, shout, vendor beetle,  
Strike a blow on the leaf board.  
It does not neigh like hay,  
Nor does it shout like barley,  
It neighs like those who sit on the back,  
It shouts like those who jump on the back,  
Those who put headbands on heads.  
Jump, jump, forest spirit,  
Shout, shout, vendor beetle.  
Try our enclosure yard,  
Was the yard made by men,  
Or was the yard made by women?  
The yard was made by maidens,  
Daughters of mothers’ herds,  
Woven from red willows,  
Young men of the Ruksimäe parish,  
White boys of the Halliku parish,  
Come on my behalf to the market,  
Bring wholesale money.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's start our journey  
Our home is far away  
Five was to exchange the outside  
Six was to dry the river  
Seven was the soda’s toughness  
Eight the fish trap  
Nine good rivers  
Ten cold without springs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Drip, drip, little needle  
Drip, little needle eye  
The needle didn’t disappear while standing  
The needle disappeared while working  
In the evening I sew my sister’s shirt  
At twilight my brother’s shirt  
At dawn the groom’s shirt  
At dusk the father-in-law’s shirt  
Jump, round needle  
Leap, square needle  
Roll, pentagonal  
Needle demanders are coming  
Tall men, fine boys  
White boys and sorrowful.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh wonder, or wonder  
What I saw in the Näu village  
Dogs plowed, oxen yodeled  
Men walked to the taverns  
Women chopped firewood  
A horse brought a bull calf  
A cow brought a little grey foal  
A sheep went to the table to lay eggs  
A hen brought two speckled ones for her  
A pig sang on a penny  
A rooster crowed in the barn.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Beer is mad, deposit is wise  
Vodka is without a fool  
It takes the senses from men's heads  
Half the senses from boys' heads  
Temples from women's foreheads.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The hop skated on the hill  
The cone stuck in the fireplace  
Young men came here  
Take me to Kotila’s home  
To Matila’s own house  
From there I roll towards the north  
I slip into the beer hall  
I start fermenting the beers  
The beer barrels rise  
I take the senses from the men's heads  
Half the senses from the boys’ heads  
Temples in front of the women’s faces

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk  
I walked the dotted path  
The land of liverwort  
The green land of roses  
When will we get to that land  
Where we have long been awaited  
Long ago watched for  
There is no sound of a rooster's crow  
Rooster's crow, chicken's call  
The weak child's crying voice  
The mourning of widows  
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk  
Let's walk the dotted path  
The green land, the land of roses  
When will we get to that land  
Where the wind has made a room for us  
Hundreds of walls have mossed over  
Fog has made new doors.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk  
I walked the dotted path  
The land of liverwort  
The green land of roses  
When will we get to that land  
Where we have long been awaited  
Long ago watched for  
There is no sound of a rooster's crow  
Rooster's crow, chicken's call  
The weak child's crying voice  
The mourning of widows  
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk  
Let's walk the dotted path  
The green land, the land of roses  
When will we get to that land  
Where the wind has made a room for us  
Hundreds of walls have mossed over  
Fog has made new doors.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud laulust
translation:
There was a little beer  
There was a little warmth from the stomach  
It walks along my belly  
Marches along my back  
Rides along the spine  
The hop danced in the willow grove  
The cone cracked in the oven’s corner  
Come, come, young man  
Take me out to gather twigs  
Carry the pitcher to the edge  
Lift the tub to the bottom  
Then I’ll hum the twigs  
Let the beer pitchers ferment  
Men shouted with hats off  
Women danced barefoot  
Boys fell over halfway  
When I start composing verses  
Composing, singing  
Then the village comes to listen  
The parish comes to watch  
The manors come to judge thoughts  
Do not take offense  
Do not be angry  
It is not my fault  
Maybe I just sing generously  
Father was a singer, mother was a singer  
The singer was a little brother  
Father moved the jaw  
Rocked the cuckoo bird  
I took stories from the jaw’s mouth  
From the cuckoo bird’s tongue  
Songs from the pike’s snout.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let it be my existence  
Secondly, my doing  
We would make a room for the wind  
A house upon the berry stalk  
Life upon the edge  
Home upon the dock leaf  
From wolf bone, I would make little doors  
From elk bone, I make little beds  
From pig’s horn, I make branches  
Along walked the Mündi Saxons  
Along came the Kapu Saxons  
From afar, the lords of the sacred grove  
We ask, we ponder  
Is this the town of Lihula  
Or is it the shore of Rahala  
Or is it the church of Kõiseri  
I hear, I answer  
This is a poor little sauna  
The shadow of a poor child.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Go to the corner, young men  
Pipe boys under the bench  
Lazy boys under the table  
From my brother’s mischievous path  
My brother is coming to duel  
The boot leg is coming to push away  
The twisted sock is coming to roll away  
A nice shoe is coming to twist away.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Who is your beloved, turn around  
Who is your dear, jump on the neck  
Silk yarn, take into your arms.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, King little king  
Rich gentleman of Riga  
Why didn’t you come to us last year  
When the rivers ran without oil  
The cellars brewed kvass  
Now you have come to a sad time  
Now you have come to a bad time  
In autumn a rough time  
In spring a light time  
Now you skim from the chest  
You also fear the plague on the neck.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk  
This path of the elite  
With ground covered in liverwort  
Where slaves have walked before  
Slaves have walked, oxen have gone  
Early walked poor children  
Late wandered the late ones.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I’m going to the tavern for several days,  
But I’ll stay for several weeks.  
Still, I drink in the tavern,  
Still, I drink in the tavern!  
A message was brought back:  
Miska’s wife is beating him on the thigh,  
He has a stickless rod in his hand,  
A pitchfork without a handle.  
Still, I drink in the tavern,  
Still, I drink in the tavern!  
A message was brought back:  
Your apple is going to die!  
If it is dead, so be it,  
Then I will take new boots.  
A message was brought back:  
Your wife is going to die.  
If she is dead, so be it,  
Then I will take another wife.  
A message was brought back:  
Your house is burning.  
Let it burn,  
I will show my bare ass  
If it burns.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The worm went to town in a jumbled way,  
The louse went at full speed,  
The frog went with three leaps.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Bravo Jüri, the drinking dog,  
You have a sparrow dog,  
A wife the dog of warm beer,  
Children the dogs of porridge and milk.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I shout about other beer,  
I yell about other kvass.  
I have nothing in the house,  
I have no one at home,  
The family eats chestnut root,  
Slaves in the spruce needle shed  
Children in the birch hovel.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Why else do we drink vodka,  
Why else do we pour kvass,  
The hunter does not wear a fur coat,  
The grandparent wears a new hat;  
If there were no foam on the beer,  
Virdel’s anger would lift his hat,  
Oh, you little vodka spirit  
Men make noise without hats,  
Women dance without shoes.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
What dance, what dance?  
The fox’s dance.  
What to eat, what to eat?  
German bread.  
What to drink, what to drink?  
Beer and vodka.  
Where to sleep, where to sleep?  
At home in the shed, at home in the shed.  
A woolen chest, a woolen chest.  
- Does the fox have a home?  
- Yes, a home.  
- What is it doing?  
- Sharpening its tooth.  
- Against whom?  
- Against you.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sex, do not overestimate  
Brushwood, do not take away  
So that I have a small horse  
A modest riding sledge  
I buy a silver horse  
But have a wide sledge made  
Then I go down to father-in-law's Mulgi  
Father-in-law's Mulgi, mother-in-law's Mulgi  
Passing under brother's gate  
Then I arrive to feed the relatives  
To set the table in Langudelle  
To take a settle of the in-law's  
A silver horse gleams ahead  
The reins snap  
The little cart clinks  
The man is topped like this day  
Hat like the Moon's servant  
Belt like a rain bow  
Then I go down to father-in-law’s yard  
Then I go down to mother-in-law’s yard  
Hello father-in-law, hello mother-in-law  
Hello mother-in-law, the neck of the pot  
Hello father-in-law, the boot organizer  
Hello vents, hello ropes  
Hello worn spoons  
Hello used ladle handles  
Hello room, let me in  
Will you take the in-law’s settle  
May I come to the room  
To drive the horse to the yard.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sex, do not overestimate  
Brushwood, do not take away  
So that I have a small horse  
A modest riding sledge  
I buy a silver horse  
But have a wide sledge made  
Then I go down to father-in-law's Mulgi  
Father-in-law's Mulgi, mother-in-law's Mulgi  
Passing under brother's gate  
Then I arrive to feed the relatives  
To set the table in Langudelle  
To take a settle of the in-law's  
A silver horse gleams ahead  
The reins snap  
The little cart clinks  
The man is topped like this day  
Hat like the Moon's servant  
Belt like a rain bow  
Then I go down to father-in-law’s yard  
Then I go down to mother-in-law’s yard  
Hello father-in-law, hello mother-in-law  
Hello mother-in-law, the neck of the pot  
Hello father-in-law, the boot organizer  
Hello vents, hello ropes  
Hello worn spoons  
Hello used ladle handles  
Hello room, let me in  
Will you take the in-law’s settle  
May I come to the room  
To drive the horse to the yard.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We have one, we have one midrilind  
Our master had it in the garden and in the garden  
He had three threads around his neck  
He ate all those, ate all those currants  
He ate away the pepper berries in the hole  
He wasted all the rosebuds  
He himself went to the forest  
Pikapere pikapere forest  
The mouse jumped and the cat leapt  
Out through the back window.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
There is indeed a straight sea reed  
There is indeed a white reed pipe  
I am still whiter, dear brothers  
Go to the corner, young men  
To the dark pipe holes  
My brother will come to duel  
Kenakand will come to twirl  
Umisking will come to wash.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
There is indeed a straight sea reed  
There is indeed a white reed pipe  
I am still whiter, dear brothers  
Go to the corner, young men  
To the dark pipe holes  
My brother will come to duel  
Kenakand will come to twirl  
Umisking will come to wash.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Drip, drip, little needle
Drip, needle eye
The needle did not disappear in the foam
The needle disappeared while working
Into the green eye's desert
Onto the tin eye's path
Into the gold eye's meadow
In the evening, I sewed my sister's shirt
At dusk, my fiancé's shirt
At twilight, my brother's shirt
At dawn, the bride's shirt
At night, the father-in-law's shirt
The needle did not disappear in the foam
The needle disappeared while working
Who went through our yard
A horse went through our yard
Tasted our well water
Tasted our sea water
Pulled without tar, without oil.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh king, little king,  
Rich gentleman of Riga,  
Why didn’t you come to us last year,  
The year before when you raged,  
When rivers ran out of oil,  
When lakes rippled and swayed.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Elpere Pulk and Lassi Kai  
Frogs crawled to the kettle  
I took them under the manor's stairs  
The crayfish was crooked in five places  
The burbot's eyes were spiral-shaped  
The pike had a sharp fin  
The dace had pointed sides  
The minnow had shoes on its feet  
Oh, wonder, or a miracle  
What I saw was a miracle  
Frogs crawled to the kettle

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Still we must go  
Go at night, go by day  
Go at night with the night’s pain  
Go by day with the day’s pain  
Where do we go against the night  
Against the night, against the dawn.  
Against the dark gloom  
Against the golden dawn  
We go chest to chest, side by side  
We go chest to chest towards Riga  
Quietly towards Tallinn  
We go to Riga to plunder  
To Tallinn to kill  
To Russia to shed blood  
To Põltsamaa to burn  
We leave, we leave dear brothers  
We leave Riga unplundered  
Tallinn un-killed  
Põltsamaa unburned  
Russia un-bloodied  
We go home for gold  
Outside we go for silver  
To the copper gate  
To the tin road path.

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Mõraküla Mihkel, a gentle man  
Rides in front of Seidla tavern  
Seven pigs in front of the cart  
A row of buckwheat on his chest.  
A girl’s group behind his sleigh  
One says: take me  
Another says: take me  
A third says: take me away entirely.  
Mihkel, Mihkel with a goatee  
Old Mihkel with a waxed mustache  
Tiny Mihkel with a milk mustache  
Big Mihkel with a smoky mustache.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Iiut, tiiut, others have money  
I had money for home  
Helped grey gelding money  
Kerstu the spotted goat money  
Iiut-tiiut, the road was crooked  
The tavern woman walked crookedly  
But poured crooked shots of vodka  
And crooked jugs of beer.  
The song ended, the child went to the forest  
I soon left the tavern  
Now my debt is paid  
Written on the tavern bill.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, wonder or wonder  
What did I see to wonder at  
A girl milks an ox by the shoulder  
A cow plows the field with its horn  
A pig sings on a penny  
The rooster and hen scratch the ground.  
Roosters and hens crow  
Tails wagging behind  
Brides and grooms come  
Both lifting their legs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
What dance, what dance?  
The fox's dance  
What to eat, what to eat?  
German bread  
What to drink, what to drink?  
Beer-vodka  
Where to sleep, where to sleep?  
At home in the barn, in the wool bin.  
- Hello, hello, is the fox at home?  
- Yes, at home.  
- Where is he?  
- Near the stove.  
- What is he doing?  
- Scratching his tooth.  
- On whom?  
- On you.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Young men of Seidla parish  
White boys of Aravete  
Big men of the large village  
Who would take younger wives  
Feared two missing  
Humalaid, Linnaksid  
Hops calls from the willow  
Cones crackle from the pine branches  
Come here, young men  
Take me home to Kotila  
Matil take me to your house  
Then I will roll to Virde  
Hide in the half-pot of beer  
Jump into the barn loft  
Young men of Seidla parish  
White boys of Aravete  
Under them are grey iron meadows  
Ears of grain weighed down  
Seeking good women  
Yet did not find a good woman  
Time passed, days rolled by  
Brought dark tobacco  
Put the pot on the fire  
The lime lies under the stove  
On top of the hearth pot  
He does not foolishly knead bread  
Nor blindly beat the fat on the thigh  
Begins to weave fabric  
Ten weeks by the cubit  
Seven years by the wall.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I have seen a wonder
I have not seen such a wonder
As I saw in Näu village
Dogs plowed, bulls harrowed
A cow brought leaves to the stallion
A horse brought a heifer to the calf
A sheep went to sleep on the barn floor
A hen brought two little lambs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Aiut-taiut deadly roots  
Hops had crazy roots  
But hops started to go wild  
In the city, however, to move the feet  
It takes the senses from men's heads  
Half of the senses from boys' heads  
Temples in front of men's heads.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We had Merja's men  
To handle Rannan’s money  
To hide it in Peipus' depths  
The money ran out at the border  
The money was left on the paths  
Coins on the tavern table  
In Juuru Juuli's barrel  
In Maidla Maide's pocket.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Snort-snort-snort-snort, tavern woman  
Protect your beautiful daughter,  
Guard your dear daughter  
Don’t let her go to the barn  
Don’t let her be taken under the widow’s roof  
Barn dogs are prowling around  
Strangers passing without asking  
A gray boar, a foolish goat  
You make a lock on Anne’s horn  
If you go out, lock the door  
When you come home, open the latch  
Give the key to Aadu  
Or perhaps to little Vestu  
The boys’ advice was to come here  
For Anne to get a sweet kiss  
To play the fiddle for her  
The singing lads followed Anne in the barn  
Rustling stalks in the threshing floor.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Do not cry, young maidens  
Sigh, girls of Harju  
I am going to the town and will stay there  
I will throw away my black coat  
I will put on a blue coat  
I will throw away this hat  
I will put on the chief’s cap  
Then I will go to take a wife  
To catch a skipper  
I rode onto the bridge over the lake  
Shook the bridge over the lake  
Knocked on the foundation beams  
The bridge planks rattled  
Annes from Harju, Kaid from Järva  
Are sitting on the Sõrve bridge  
Anne from Aruküla understands, responds  
If only that man were mine  
Who passed by, head bent  
Lifted his nice cap.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Do not cry, young maidens  
Sigh, girls of Harju  
I am going to the town and will stay there  
I will throw away my black coat  
I will put on a blue coat  
I will throw away this hat  
I will put on the chief’s cap  
Then I will go to take a wife  
To catch a skipper  
I rode onto the bridge over the lake  
Shook the bridge over the lake  
Knocked on the foundation beams  
The bridge planks rattled  
Annes from Harju, Kaid from Järva  
Are sitting on the Sõrve bridge  
Anne from Aruküla understands, responds  
If only that man were mine  
Who passed by, head bent  
Lifted his nice cap.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Did you see my mare? -  
What color is your horse?  
Flaxen mane, leaf-colored ears  
She has a blue blanket on her back  
Dearly woven by a weaver  
Her tail is tied with silk  
Her mane is in a wide braid  
She went through our yard  
Drank water from our river  
Tasted our well water  
Tasted Mardi's grayish one  
Dear little siblings, brothers  
Come to buy a mare  
To ransom a heifer  
Some are rich with money  
Some were famous penny-rich  
Ready with old thalers.  
Look at the mare, what is ours  
Cannot get out of the garden  
Nor can get out of the stables.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Go to the corner, go to the corner, young men  
Under the bench, under the bench, pipe lips  
My brother is coming, my brother is coming to fence  
The shoemaker is coming, the shoemaker is coming to sew.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Teu boys, dog boys  
Mischievous spirit boys  
There are many people at the tavern  
Niibli boys, neighbor boys  
Drinking dogs, drunks  
Tavern wife, little woman  
Keep your beautiful daughter  
Protect your dear daughter  
Do not let her go to the drunkard  
There are many people at the tavern  
Drunkards go to the tavern.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The winding road was crooked  
The pub woman walked crooked  
She poured crooked vodka jugs  
From the Uurakille beer jugs  
From the Kaarakille braga jugs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Droplet, droplet, little needle  
Droplet in the needle's eye  
But the needle disappeared in the crease  
In the seams of a foggy linen  
Stitching the suitor's shirt  
Sewing the baby's shirt.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
If I were to exist  
Secondly, if I were to do  
I would surely know what I would do  
I would make the room face the wind  
Life upon a haystack  
A house upon a berry stalk  
A hut upon a dock leaf  
A boy comes to ask me  
Is there a town or a flag  
Or is there Keidela church  
Or is there a Finnish salt ship  
Or is there a Pärnu wreath ship  
But I understand, I answer:  
There is no town, nor is there a flag  
There is no Keidela church  
There is no Finnish salt ship  
Nor is there a Pärnu wreath ship  
This is my father's sauna  
Water has rolled the logs  
Fog has covered the doors  
Rain has mossed the walls  
A snowstorm had stuffed the room.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
What’s wrong with me drinking vodka  
Bringing a bag from Kouna mill  
Father was a barn helper in the valley.  
Brother was the master of vodka  
Why are you crying, mistress  
Are the porridge sticks missing?

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Our home is far away  
Far away, high away  
There were five outside  
Six were dry rivers  
Seven were tough swamps  
Eight were fish shoals  
Nine were good rivers  
Ten were cold and gray.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Swing smiths, dear brothers  
Let's go swing swinging  
Let's sing on the swingboard  
Will this swing carry us  
If it doesn't carry, then let it be lost  
Swing smiths, dear brothers  
You made the swing for the pasture  
For the grazing path, for the berry field  
Row the swing, pull the swing  
Row the swing to that land  
Where the roosters eat gold  
Roosters gold, hens beware  
Ducks of green silver  
Swing smiths, dear brothers  
Up, up to that land  
Up along the apple trees  
Down along the berry trees.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The horse went wild, the road was dusty  
The mare was young, the belly was big  
There were drops behind the entire road  
Already one is letting loose  
Took hail to hit the bend  
Stripes of hail to shake off  
I got on the road, it started raining  
The weather was good to go on the road  
I got on the road, it started raining  
Took hail to hit the bend.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
What dance, what dance?  
The fox dance.  
What to eat, what to eat?  
German bread.  
Where to sleep, where to sleep?  
At home in the barn, at home in the barn  
in the wool box.  
- Does the fox have a home?  
- Yes, a home.  
- What is he doing?  
- Sharpening his tooth.  
- At whom?  
- At you.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
In another ear  
Oh how nice  
Standing in a large circle  
A big or maybe small crowd  
I walk through, others too  
They catch each other  
And open the tall gate.  
Open the gates  
I am coming with my turn  
Now through here.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Little child, your father will soon die,  
The grave is already being dug.  
Nine fathoms deep,  
Ten fathoms to the bottom of the grave.  
Little child, your mother will soon die,  
The grave is already being dug.  
Nine fathoms deep,  
Ten fathoms to the bottom of the grave.  
Little child, your brother will soon die,  
The grave is already being dug.  
Nine fathoms deep,  
Ten fathoms to the bottom of the grave.  
Little child, your sister will soon die,  
The grave is already being dug.  
Nine fathoms deep,  
Ten fathoms to the bottom of the grave.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Butter, oh butter  
What a wonder I saw  
Two women were fighting.  
One was beating the other  
Annel slapped, Kaie punched  
Maie grabbed Margapuu by the shoulder  
Leenu had a little ladle of broth  
Anu had a small oven door  
A sheep went to sleep on the table  
A cow gave a calf to the dairymaid.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Be healthy, be healthy, brewers  
Rollers of angry whining  
Endurers of great smoke  
Who have combined the three  
First came the strong  
Second, the harsher junipers  
Third, the rough-leaved junipers  
I sip along as I please  
I raise the mug according to my strength  
I drink beers, clear the mugs  
I throw the switches into the switch basket  
The stumps into the birch grove  
There grew large islands for me  
Large islands, wide glades  
At every end a squirrel  
At every top a coot  
At every treetop a songbird  
Grandfather, grandmother  
From Aali’s barn to the bright one  
Clean the gun  
Miska, I will shoot birds  
Grandfather, grandmother  
From there I bring you large birds  
Then grandfather will eat well  
Then papa will get a pat  
Then mama will get a smack.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go singing, let's go singing through the forest  
Whose voice is that, whose voice is that clearly heard  
That groom, that groom is buying a hat  
A concertina, a concertina's straw and berry  
Over the twig, over the twig of hay and berry  
Who yesterday, who yesterday cut a finger  
Virgin Mari, virgin Mari cut a finger  
Who jumped, who jumped into diapers  
My brother is coming, my brother is coming to wave  
Boot leg is coming, boot leg is coming to grease.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The pig died for us, a junker was born  
Dogs howled  
Gentlemen dug in the snowdrift  
Calves peered out the window  
The lady went to plow the field  
The sow’s backside was sweaty  
The old pig’s eyes were wet  
Golden nuggets embarrassed  
In the humpbacked plowing of Kürevere  
In the field taking of Adavere  
The stone giants teased them  
For they had proud horses  
Runas into ruble pieces  
Kimlis into shield armors  
Boats into copper rivets.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Be healthy, be healthy, brewers of beer  
Bearers of anger and whining  
Endurers of great smoke  
Who have put three together  
First came the strong one  
Second -  
Third, the tougher junipers  
I sip according to my mind  
I raise the mug according to my strength  
But I drink beers, clean the mugs  
I throw the rods into the rods’ pile  
The cap cuts into the grove  
There grew large islands for me  
Large islands, wide plains  
At every end a squirrel  
At every top a titmouse  
At every treetop a songbird  
Mother-in-law, old woman  
To Aali’s barn of bright ones  
I clean the gun until it’s clean  
I let loose the birds there  
From there I bring you big birds  
Oh my dear mother-in-law  
Oh my dear old woman  
Then mother-in-law will eat well  
Then the old woman will eat well  
Then the dad will be patted,  
Then the mom will be kissed.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Flexible, flexible, little needle  
Flexible, little eye of the needle  
The little needle disappeared  
It didn’t disappear while sleeping  
It disappeared while working  
Sewing a fine sheet  
Making a blouse for a girl.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I am crazy, as I was  
You were crazy the year before last  
Oxen plowed, dogs plowed  
The sheep went to sleep on the table  
The hen brought two little lambs  
The horse brought a bull calf.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh you horned German,  
Blow the pipe, little boy.  
I am coming to dance the oak,  
To sway in the field violet.  
I dance, the barn rumbles,  
The rainbow strikes the hangings,  
The clouds blew the pipe.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sisters, dear ones  
We come together as three  
We consult as four  
Let's leave this land  
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For two days to Pärnu County  
Are we looking for a flock  
To catch Piia's herd  
The hooves strike the shoulders  
Sparks fly from the joints  
The flickering hooves of a foal  
When searching for the maiden's flock  
When catching Piia's herd.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sisters, dear ones  
We come together as three  
We consult as four  
Let's leave this land  
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For two days to Pärnu County  
Are we looking for a flock  
To catch Piia's herd  
The hooves strike the shoulders  
Sparks fly from the joints  
The flickering hooves of a foal  
When searching for the maiden's flock  
When catching Piia's herd.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Preespill, preespill, livestock  
Vilevitsa, vilevitsa hayflower  
Who cut the hayflower on the finger  
That Mari, that Mari maiden  
Who went to see her  
That groom boy  
Where did he take that scarf from  
Took it from around the neck, a neck scarf  
Where did he take that ribbon from  
Took it from the belt, a belt ribbon  
Tied it on the finger  
Wrapped it wet-wet  
What a waste of that scarf  
The village groom will buy a new one  
The German boy brings a velvet one  
The Russian boy brings a striped one  
The boy from Moa brings another patterned one.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Virgin girls, youthful  
Delicate little maidens  
Let's gather the three of us  
Let's hold counsel with the four of us  
Let's leave this land  
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For half the summer to the Land of the Berries  
For winter to the Tartu road  
Let's adorn ourselves with jewelry  
In green rye fields  
In blue silk clothes  
There we will build a stone church  
Carve an iron chapel  
We ourselves will go as a flag to the town  
As a doll to the shop corner  
As a crown to the fence  
Let Piia Puudu come here  
Piia Puudu, the hungry maiden  
The result will be empty from the girls  
Then we will be bought by the blacksmith  
Ridden on a swollen shin  
Tiring on a foal's hoof  
Searching for that maiden  
Looking for that girl  
Searching for that bird  
The boys beg on their knees  
Bow without hats  
Fall down, maiden  
Fall on my hat  
Roll onto five fingers  
Fall on a wide cap  
Be quiet, calf's foot  
If I fall, who will pay?  
My side pays a pair of oxen  
The other side another pair  
Eyes like a silk bird  
Legs like a tall foal  
The head of a young horse.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Virgin girls, youthful  
Delicate little maidens  
Let's gather the three of us  
Let's hold counsel with the four of us  
Let's leave this land  
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For half the summer to the Land of the Berries  
For winter to the Tartu road  
Let's adorn ourselves with jewelry

We ourselves will go as a flag to the town  
As a doll to the shop corner  
As a crown to the fence  
Let Piia Puudu come here  
Piia Puudu, the hungry maiden  
The result will be empty from the girls  
Then we will be bought by the blacksmith  
Ridden on a swollen shin  
Tiring on a foal's hoof  
Searching for that maiden  
Looking for that girl  
Searching for that bird  
The boys beg on their knees  
Bow without hats  
Fall down, maiden  
Fall on my hat  
Roll onto five fingers  
Fall on a wide cap  
Be quiet, calf's foot  
If I fall, who will pay?  
My side pays a pair of oxen  
The other side another pair  
Eyes like a silk bird  
Legs like a tall foal  
The head of a young horse.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We have one, we have one bird in the garden
Our gentleman's garden
Golden thread around its neck
Ate all those currants
Wasted all those rosebuds
From there it went to the forest
A long-legged fellow from the family.
Who has time, let them chase after it
Who has dogs, let them catch it
Let them take a gun from their pocket
From the window of the forge
From the row of hay horns.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I’m not making a cake, I’m going to the herd  
But I watch the old woman’s ewe  
I protect the old man’s spotted herd  
In front of God’s house  
Behind God’s house.  
But a wolf came from the big forest  
A bear from the wilderness spruces  
Took five forest sheep  
The wolf tore the old woman’s cloak for him.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For the winter behind the rivers  
We'll build a nice church there  
We'll carry stones to the chapel  
We'll make towers from tiles  
Wide towers from shingles  
Tall towers from boards  
When maiden hunger comes to the land  
pag, 2371 Maiden hunger, drought  
Then they search in the hills  
Send down the ravines  
Test on the foal's hoof  
Searching for the maiden's clearing  
Even when catching the swarm of Piia  
The stones cut the shoulders of the hills  
Lightning cuts the foal's hooves  
Sparks struck in the ravines  
Searching for the maiden's clearing  
Even when catching the swarm of Piia.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For the winter behind the rivers  
We'll build a nice church there  
We'll carry stones to the chapel  
We'll make towers from tiles  
Wide towers from shingles  
Tall towers from boards  
When maiden hunger comes to the land  
pag, 2371 Maiden hunger, drought  
Then they search in the hills  
Send down the ravines  
Test on the foal's hoof  
Searching for the maiden's clearing  
Even when catching the swarm of Piia  
The stones cut the shoulders of the hills  
Lightning cuts the foal's hooves  
Sparks struck in the ravines  
Searching for the maiden's clearing  
Even when catching the swarm of Piia.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
My father is a miracle worker  
My mother is a miracle worker  
They made me a wonderful coat  
From flags and patches  
From old goat legs  
She cooked me a bowl of porridge  
Brewed beer from hops in her hut  
Kneaded bread from pig dung  
Cooked horse saliva into a broth.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A sheep in the pen like that bale  
A tail like a horned club  
The wool was on the dry hay  
A tail behind like a wind broom  
I mowed the wool, I got two  
I found a keys behind.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk  
Milla, how do we get there  
Where a log forest shines there  
And it heats a spruce forest  
Where roosters drink gold  
Roosters drink gold, hens beware  
But ducks wear green silver.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Hops maddens the hill  
The cone crackled in the stove ash  
Come, young ones, to pick  
Old ones, come to take back  
Take me to the home of the kettle  
To the miller’s own house  
Put the barrel on the floor  
Lift it onto the edge of the trough  
From there it rolls onto the vine shoot  
But slips into the half-finished beer  
The hop started to madden  
To move feet in the town  
It took the minds from men’s heads  
The lambs from the faces of women  
The shoots from the daughters of the tang.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Clack clack tavern dad  
Take care of your beautiful Ann  
Don’t let the lath go  
Dogs run up the lath  
Uninvited strangers sail  
If you go to town, lock up  
If you come home, open the cupboard  
Give the key to Ott  
Or maybe to little Vest.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
But I saw, but I saw, little brother  
I saw hunger coming  
I had a wide belt woven  
City strings stacked  
Shop ribbons braided  
The stomach is angry to be filled  
The belly is weak to be carried  
I saw, I saw, little brother  
I saw hunger beating  
Mist at Toomas's door  
Money under Mardi's thigh  
Flour on Kersti's lid.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I would dance tatsa ratsa  
Afraid the tunes would whine  
Willow barks crack  
Men still have shoes in the forest  
Boys have pastlad on the willow backs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh king, little king  
Rich gentleman of Riga  
Why didn’t you come to us last year?  
The year before last, it was announced  
When you went to collect the orders  
Five books were transported  
Six letters were written  
When the rivers ran without oil  
Mead from Finland toward the manor  
The spruces dripped gold  
The birches brightened with frost  
You came at an empty time  
In the light spring  
In the autumn’s plentiful harvest.  
Now you harvest the rye field  
You try the necklaces  
You take off the wreath, pull the cheese from atop.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I drink beer by clinking the mugs  
I lift the edge of the tankard  
I try the strength of the mug  
I toss the whips into the whip bag  
I carry the tables into the grove  
I am not a proper beer drinker  
Nor a true wedding guest  
I am the tattered flag bearer  
Accidentally invited as a stranger.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I drank beer, cleaned the jugs  
I threw the twigs into the peach tree  
The boards on the pot stand  
There I have big piles growing  
Big piles, wide dam  
At each end there was a squirrel  
In each treetop a songbird  
On each branch a hole bird  
At the top my squirrel’s den  
In the barn the songbird’s flock  
In the middle a maiden’s loft  
From a twig came a thick druid’s board.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Here dances Siim,  
Here Siim's servant.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Bulls barked, dogs plowed  
Bulls barked on the hook  
Dogs plowed on the field  
The sheep went to the table to lay eggs  
The hen brought two lambs  
The horse brought a bullheaded calf  
The cow brought a tufted calf.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk  
Let's go along this road  
Where the road was pointed at the top  
The land was covered in liverwort  
Where bulls had walked before  
Bulls had walked and slaves had gone  
The poor children had left early  
The late ones are the housekeepers.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Where we go towards the night  
Towards the night, towards the north  
Towards building a dwelling.  
Towards the complicated dawn  
Our home far away  
There were five versts to change  
Six rivers to dry up  
Seven swamps to harden  
Eight fish racks  
Nine good rivers  
Ten cold gray ones.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a rich mother-in-law  
I had a rich old woman  
She gave me a little goat  
A little bell on the goat's neck.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Jews also drink in the corner
But I run in the earhole
On Monday I drank a new hook
On Tuesday I drank a second shirt
Midweek a nice coat
On Thursday I drank among them
On Friday I drank the clothes off
On Saturday I drank a broad belt
On Sunday I drink the pants off
Mart was otherwise a fashionable man
But he had bad manners
I do not chase girls
I do not sleep at Mari’s
I do not love Anne
I do not put a collar on Kaie
I don’t even pull Liisud close.
Virgin, young girl
Thin to the point
Come to me, I to you
I’ll put the servant to work
I’ll make the socks to sew
Winter’s barley to sift
I’ll put barley to the valleys
Oats to the riverbanks
Flax I’ll make into linen at Liutsalus
Horse’s barley to the valleys
Bear’s oats to the banks.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Jews also drink in the corner
But I run in the earhole
On Monday I drank a new hook
On Tuesday I drank a second shirt
Midweek a nice coat
On Thursday I drank among them
On Friday I drank the clothes off
On Saturday I drank a broad belt
On Sunday I drink the pants off
Mart was otherwise a fashionable man
But he had bad manners
I do not chase girls
I do not sleep at Mari’s
I do not love Anne
I do not put a collar on Kaie
I don’t even pull Liisud close.
Virgin, young girl
Thin to the point
Come to me, I to you
I’ll put the servant to work
I’ll make the socks to sew
Winter’s barley to sift
I’ll put barley to the valleys
Oats to the riverbanks
Flax I’ll make into linen at Liutsalus
Horse’s barley to the valleys
Bear’s oats to the banks.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Lastelaulud
translation:
Taverns and manners, bad manners  
You don’t get vodka without money  
You don’t get beer without buying  
Fishing without a license  
Vodka tells me: take your time  
Beer says: stay here  
What am I, a little beer  
Or what am I, little vodka  
Others went to the fair on Monday  
I will go after on Tuesday  
The bulls are ready in the front  
The plows are on the shoulders in the yoke  
I went beyond the big hill  
Started mending my shoe  
But came the manor’s black cat  
Took away the proper patch  
I chased after the cat  
The cat ran to the master’s room  
I myself to the mistress’s room  
The mistress was making berry jam  
I was tasting the jam  
The mistress gave me a slap  
The boy hit me with a whip handle  
The girl chased after me  
Then I got a sworn enemy to babble  
The man in breeches to tangle  
Along the field, along the other field  
Sometimes along the gardens  
Then I got a split in between  
Then I kissed my foot  
Hands, be strong and feet, be strong  
The captors are coming  
Big mouths, thick noses  
Beards shaved away

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
What's wrong with us drinking at the tavern
My ox mows the home hay.
My cow takes a rest at home.
The pig makes slender bread.
But the rooster pecks in the enclosure.
The sheep is in the barn on a penny.
Oh, wonder, see the wonder
What I saw in Näu village
The oxen barked, the dogs plowed
The oxen barked at the ram
The dogs plowed at the he-goat
The horse got a bull calf
The cow gave birth to a colt
The rooster went to the barn to lay eggs
The hen went to crow more
The sheep gave birth to a speckled goat kid
The goat gave birth to a colt lamb.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh you anger little fire  
And bitter strong liquor  
It went to walk into the stomach  
March down into the belly  
Flows along the veins in the back  
Steps down into the anchor.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The Innkeeper's Wife and the Innkeeper  
Keep your beautiful daughter  
Protect your dear daughter  
Do not let the barn go  
Barn dogs guard the barn  
Under the howl are indeed mad dogs  
They cause trouble in the farmstead  
Hunger drives children to thievery

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Here comes Siim, here Siim’s servant,  
What is Siim looking for, what Siim’s servant  
Noble lord, among all the others  
What is Siim looking for among all the others  
Siim is looking for a bride, Siim’s servant for a bride.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Dear little brother,  
Did you see, on my behalf,  
Wandering to the street,  
Raising your feet at the market?  
Dear little brother,  
What color is your horse?  
Morning squirrel color,  
Noon pig color,  
At sunrise, fox color.  
It went through our yard,  
The rope was golden behind it,  
The shackle a frightening foot,  
It drank water from our river,  
It pulled our tar water.  
Jump on the gray one, leap to the ear,  
Strike a blow to the leaf meadow,  
Roll the blood-red one,  
Try to our garden,  
Is the garden made by men,  
Or is the garden made by women,  
Or is it cursed by maidens?

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I make the fence, I make the fence, I sharpen the stake  
I plant the post firmly in the ground  
So that no wolf can get in  
Nor thieves steal away  
The herd children carry away  
Golden stake, copper prong  
Beautiful juniper switch.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Smoking a pipe, smoking a pipe and grass berry  
The whistle was a hay berry  
Who cut their finger on the hay  
That young maiden  
Who jumped into the swaddling clothes  
It must have been the bridegroom boy  
Where did he get that, where did he get that scarf  
He took a neckerchief from around his neck  
Where did he get that ribbon  
He took a ribbon from his belt  
Tied that finger up  
Wrapped up the wound-berry.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go, darlings, let's go, dears  
Let's go, darlings, along the forest  
Let's go, dears, along the fir trees  
Secretly along the grove  
This path is lined with points  
The land is covered with moss  
Where once the slaves used to walk  
The slaves walked, the bulls went  
Many have left from here, proud ladies  
Riit has left from here, a rich lady  
Pailu has gone to her family man  
They did not adorn the road  
Did not adorn the road, bitter on the land  
Echoing on the tall trees in the forest  
But we, two poor ones, let it be  
Let's go along this road  
We will adorn the road  
Adorn the road, bitter on the land  
Echoing on the tall trees in the forest

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
To Riga’s threshold the edges of the pileflower  
To Riga’s threshold seven sons’ naala  
To Riga’s threshold Siimanni’s islands  
To the bride’s apron the edges of the pileflower  
To the bride’s apron seven house naalus  
To the bride’s apron Siimanni’s islands.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, you crazy barley water  
Oh, you angry spirituous water  
That you break me down  
Still, I swallow you  
You take the minds from men's heads  
Half the minds from boys' heads  
Men make noise without hats  
Boys get half a boot each

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go quietly to Finland  
For the coming year  
Until then, there will be a lack of girls here  
A lack of girls, a shortage of mates  
Then horses will be sought  
Tested on foal hooves  
What will we do there after all  
There we will make a stone church  
Carry a sturdy chapel  
Paint an egg floor

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go quietly to Finland  
For the coming year  
Until then, there will be a lack of girls here  
A lack of girls, a shortage of mates  
Then horses will be sought  
Tested on foal hooves  
What will we do there after all  
There we will make a stone church  
Carry a sturdy chapel  
Paint an egg floor

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Murelaulud  ; Laulud laulust
translation:
Now we shall go away  
To our home far away  
Far away, high away  
There were five ways out  
Six rivers to dry  
Seven tough swamps  
Eight fish shoals  
Nine good rivers  
Ten cold grays  
If we get there  
Where the fire had made a room  
Water rolled the logs  
Mist placed the doors  
Snow drove the locks shut  
Dear ones, little brothers  
Do you still want to sing?  
If you do, then you may  
I left home with bags full  
With baskets full on poles  
When I begin to compose  
To compose, to sing  
Then the village will come to listen  
The parish will come to watch  
Where the child got those songs  
From crazy, wild tales   
I understand, I hear again  
Dear ones, little brothers  
I went to study in Harju  
Did not take the virus  
continued learning in Järva  
I studied at home  
At home, in the home room  
Weaving misty fabric  
Twisting tin thread  
Silver thread into silk  
Broad nets made by midwives  
The thread made big grooms  
With loom legs made along  
Benches built for farmers  
The village will come to visit us  
Suitable for a young couple  
When we start Estonian songs  
Rolling tunes one after another  
Turning verses one after another  
Dear one, little brother  
I will not sing for nothing  
Nor value a ringing voice  
Give money to the singer  
To the boiled tongue beater

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh wonderful, what a wonder  
What I saw in Näu village  
In my own family  
Dogs plowed, bulls yoked  
Calves pulled the furrow  
The pig went to the road, the sickle was on the belt  
The sheep went to sleep on the table  
The hen brought two little lambs  
The cow brought a flower to the pitcher  
The duck brought a piglet to the pigsty  
The wolf brought a bunch of puppies  
The wolf drove the fold to the scythe  
I didn’t have time to help

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's take vodka on credit  
Put pants on a belt  
Let's go to the package in Paide  
I’ll buy a new whip from the store  
New harness from the store-crow  
Then I’ll go through Rüüa village  
Nipri Liisu, the virgin girl  
The praise girl across the village  
The spirit girl across the county  
She was in great sorrow  
In front of the stove and in the pit  
In front of the room and in the granary  
But you come, Kerna boy  
I won’t come to you either  
You are dark and hairy  
Like a shepherd dog  
Your neck is broken, ears worn  
Oh, my partner was far away  
A beautiful partner was across the water  
A true partner was to Pärnu County  
So many troubles for her  
When there was wood in the shed  
Smooth ones, straight ones  
Crooked ones, hunchbacks  
The wind may carry it all off intact  
Clouds long to remember  
May God watch over her in peace.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The horse nods into the stall  
Shakes the need to the front  
Eyes wander, mouth watches  
Its mane plays on the edge of the jaw  
Ears repeat the threads  
The tail waves like a tassel  
Oh those fires of Ärr's village  
And those dear men of Kaiu  
They exchanged the white mare  
When they chased her  
At the blacksmith's forge on a beam  
One got a piece, another a tuft  
The mane went to Laulu-Liisu  
The tail went to Saare-Viilu  
The ears to Kõnnu-Hindrek  
The mane to the village priest  
The penis to Viru-Tiilu.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Who was rich in chest outside  
Who was fiery inside the room  
Or who was free in the chamber  
Ait was rich in chest outside  
The cat was fiery inside the room  
The rat was free in the chamber  
But the cat sat by the roadside  
Who knew the cat's thoughts  
How precious was the cat's soul  
What became of that cat for us  
The girl got shoes on the nose  
The servant got shoes on the nape  
The tail became saddle straps  
The penis became a whistle  
The penis sheath became a wedding trumpet  
From an old pike, an alba jollity.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Who was rich in chest outside  
Who was fiery inside the room  
Or who was free in the chamber  
Ait was rich in chest outside  
The cat was fiery inside the room

The penis became a whistle  
The penis sheath became a wedding trumpet  
From an old pike, an alba jollity.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a wealthy godfather  
He gave me a little goat  
A small bell around the goat’s neck  
The wolf ate the goat, the bell stayed with me  
I put the bell on my wife’s neck  
The wife was a strong sleeper  
The bell was a strong banger  
But the flame was crackling in the stove  
Uiu-tuiu the wolf’s defeat  
Ghosts behind the door  
A rowdy crowd in the yard  
Children on the sauna bench  
Noise at the chamber door.

label: Murelaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
When will we get there  
To that big island  
To the endless land  
Where the fire had made a room  
Water rolled the logs  
Rain mossed the walls  
Fog slammed the doors shut  
Dew fell on the doorstep  
There, the hay is mown sparingly  
The gateposts take the groove  
There, children grew fluent  
Daughters grew serious  
Mother’s children kinder

There, children grew fluent  
Daughters grew serious  
Mother’s children kinder

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The song ended, went went into the forest  
I quickly left the tavern  
I saw some wonders  
The boogeyman chased the groom  
The girl milked the cow from the side  
The groom was at my beloved’s place  
The boogey boy was at the girl’s place  
The crow was at the old spirit’s place

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I was supposed to leave soon  
Long ago to set out on the road  
I set out, it started to rain  
The pilot’s blades cutting through the sails  
Sparks flew from the pole’s legs  
The colt’s hooves cutting the turf  
The fire flashed behind the ears  
Oh my dear, I push forward  
I lift your roof  
Maybe I could get away  
I won’t wander aimlessly  
I won’t go along the green water  
The duck goes along the flock by the shore  
The swan goes along the black land  
Raise the oars, put on the boards  
Take me to the weather  
I won’t beat the broom  
The duck will wash its knees  
The mosquito will endlessly bite the legs

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
At the tavern, I drank a gray gelding for the ear  
To the mill road, grayish water  
To the market, bright red  
To the town road, flaxen  
To the small borough, pale gray

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's start going  
Let's light the pipes  
Our home far away  
Five was between the paths  
Six was for drying the rivers  
Seven bad gray ones  
Eight fish ladders  
Nine dark rivers  
Ten into the cold gray  
Seven swamps with tough clay.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sister dear, don’t cry  
I won’t leave you behind  
I will still take you  
If only we had a fenced garden  
If only we had a yard  
Gates strengthened with branches  
Sister dear, tender one  
Maybe you have resisted  
Given yourself to the boys’ hands  
When you had suitors  
Maybe those few boys were  
Little pipe players  
Kantele players, comrades  
Sister dear, tender one  
Delicate in handwriting  
Lady in block letters  
Sister dear, tender one  
Let’s join together, the three of us  
Let’s consult, the four of us  
Even say out loud, the five of us  
Let’s leave this land  
Let’s go to Finland  
For the winter to Russia  
For three months to Courland  
For two days to Pärnu County  
Let’s build a stone church there  
Let’s build a lilac-colored town  
Half the town in cranberry  
Benches made of rowan wood  
Towers atop made of juniper  
From where no young man escapes  
Just the young men  
Highest hats of winter  
Let the land come to the maiden’s hunger  
Maiden’s hunger, pipe drought  
Then we will be sought urgently  
Ridden upon shinbones  
Tested on young horses’ hooves  
Fires cutting through the upper backs  
Sparks thrown from shinbones  
Lightning striking horse hooves  
Arrows striking the sides of the saddle  
When searching for maiden flock  
Trying to catch a pipe flock  
Oh wonderful, oh miraculous  
What I saw, wondrous  
Even in the yard, while walking  
I saw Saaremaa burning  
Fire blazing, fire coming  
Bogs ignited, lakes burning  
Stones whirled in whirlpools  
Stars cutting through the sky  
Sword tips flashing through the air  
Empty Saaremaa weeps  
Another Saaremaa is without men  
I weep for the maidens of Saaremaa  
Belt weavers, headdress makers  
Wheel pattern writers  
Readers of the wide book.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Virgins, young girls  
What do you think of yourselves  
That you scorn boys  
Choosing white heads  
Do you know where you can find  
Some fall into a drinking gutter  
Some end up in a reindeer’s path  
Walking the tavern roads  
Loitering by tavern doors  
Sleeping in tavern sheds  
Keeping the tavern benches  
Coming home even from the tavern  
Throwing stockings onto the manure  
Shoes in the middle of the yard  
Take Kai, put her out to dry  
Maie, scattered in the barn  
Ann, put her by the oven’s edge  
If not Kai, then Maie to dry  
The village then pays wages  
Then there was spinning of the whip  
Clapping hands alongside  
Fists were clenching  
Virgin, young girl  
Why do you resist when coming to me  
To me, a young man  
Pea-headed, proud ones  
Hat-wearing, tall ones  
I would have long ago made a granary  
Made a granary from anise straw  
Covered the door with eggshells  
Door with wolf’s rib boards  
Floors from reindeer meat  
Made silk beds inside  
Silk beds, thread games  
For a young girl to sleep in

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's get going, men  
Let's light the pipes  
A hundred saunas smoking  
A thousand rooms dusty  
Let's set hell on fire  
To burn one village  
Ten saunas smoking.

label: Pulmalaulud  ; Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Our lord gives the work party  
In great heat  
In more beautiful weather  
On a hotter day  
Full loads are brought  
We were given a wide wagon  
Everyone drank out of desire  
The instrument here calls with a dance tune  
Lift your foot, white socks  
Wait here in dance fashion  
Dust will not do us harm  
If you are willing to be lively  
And you want to act wisely  
Before the dawn breaks painfully  
Sweep the room, wash the tables  
Business under the windows  
Wash the tongs properly  
Bring loads in rows  
Buckets to the kitchen doorstep  
Windows to the garden fence  
Tomorrow the bailiff will come to the village  
The old German arrives to the auction  
He thinks of the moon shining  
Cherishing the evening star  
The morning star glowing  
Our tongs properly  
Bring loads to the room’s eaves  
Buckets to the kitchen door  
Windows to the garden fence  
I went to a wedding somewhere  
Rode to Seitse (a place)  
I cannot give or take  
What I got from that bride  
I got a belt with five stripes  
Gloves with scratches on the palm  
Should I put them under the bench  
Or should I put them under the table  
Or take them home in a bag  
That the girl left at home  
Father-in-law’s shirts, mother-in-law’s shirts  
All gloves knitted  
Fine, slender plaits  
Those the girl left at home.  
Come on, little brother  
Drive home to the barn yard  
Try the key in the door  
Right at the door spots  
Hold on, lad  
Give time, green meadow  
Surely the horse will run  
The lad’s whip hits the head  
Father-in-law’s stall is hammered  
Go, go, men  
Already near the house  
The star is above the barn ridge  
Poles against the fences  
Go, go, men  
There are uncles and a lot more  
Aunts filling the barn  
Flying among the gates  
Now people go wandering  
The young group gets dispersed  
The large group disappears  
Let’s go sleep in Finland  
For a year let’s go to Harjumaa  
For winter, no Russians wanted.

label: Pulmalaulud  ; Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Our lord gives the work party  
In great heat  
In more beautiful weather  
On a hotter day  
Full loads are brought  
We were given a wide wagon  
Everyone drank out of desire  
The instrument here calls with a dance tune  
Lift your foot, white socks  
Wait here in dance fashion  
Dust will not do us harm  
If you are willing to be lively  
And you want to act wisely  
Before the dawn breaks painfully  
Sweep the room, wash the tables  
Business under the windows  
Wash the tongs properly  
Bring loads in rows  
Buckets to the kitchen doorstep

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The lords of Viru drank vodka  
The forest wardens ate bread  
The forest maidens drank honey  
Understand, understand young men  
Who is the hawthorn midwife  
The hazel hawthorn midwife  
What is to be guessed here  
Before the night is half over  
Come here, young men  
Guess, eager boys  
Who is the heath maiden?  
The hare is the heath maiden.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The lords of Viru drank vodka  
The forest wardens ate bread  
The forest maidens drank honey  
Understand, understand young men  
Who is the hawthorn midwife  
The hazel hawthorn midwife  
What is to be guessed here  
Before the night is half over  
Come here, young men  
Guess, eager boys  
Who is the heath maiden?  
The hare is the heath maiden.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I had to go to Piipeje  
I poured into the village of Kodi  
I reached the lord of the parish there  
Oh wonder, look, wonder  
What I saw in the village  
How those girls were trained  
Irons in their mouths and collars on their necks  
Old carts behind them  
Old women as coachmen  
Whoever saw it, surely laughed  
Everyone was frightened by them  
Siim laughed, eyes wet  
He would put a halter on the female bear  
Gold around the village’s gold’s neck  
Pearls around the bridle of the bull  
This is an incomplete story  
Loved before its time.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I was supposed to leave soon  
Long ago to set out on the road  
I set out, it began to rain  
The snow took to fluffing like a scarf  
Dew points to drizzling sleet  
Men should not set out on the road  
Those who do not know how to demand the wind  
Should immediately welcome the dawn  
The moon rises here, dawn arrives here  
The day shines from behind the hill

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Bend down, lower your head  
Cry, little maiden  
Cry, cry, little doll  
Your mother is already dying.  
The grave is already being dug  
A deep grave to the bottom of the earth  
Nine fathoms deep  
Eight fathoms wide  
Ten cubits broad on top.  
Rise up, lift your head  
Get up, raise your head  
Your mother is already coming to life  
Life is bringing you to the room  
Life is spreading on the floor  
Your soul is already awakening, little maiden  
Laugh, laugh, little maiden  
Joy, joy, little maiden  
Your soul is already awakening, little maiden

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh wonder, oh wonder  
What a wonder I saw  
Dogs were plowing, oxen were hoeing  
A dog brought two piglets  
A sheep went to sleep on the table  
To speak into the rooster’s ear  
A goat brought ten spotted piglets  
A pig brought seven spotted lambs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Cross dance was, cross dance was a little dance  
Cross play was, cross play was a little game  
Go, go, shoes and shoes  
Pour, pour, ribbons and ribbons  
Tomorrow new ones, tomorrow new ones will be bought  
Today others, today others will be used  
Take hide, take hide, ox hide  
Uncle’s son, uncle’s son’s stallion hide  
Uncle’s son, uncle’s son’s horse hide  
Pat, pat, ox hide

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Trikenpoogen  
Rope rope reeroo  
Aivere's instrument  
Tallervere dance  
Step out from behind  
I am cold from company.  
Poor fellow, poor fellow  
Rope rope roo  
Auvere's instrument  
Tallervere dance  
Step to keep me company  
I need company.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Jump into the gray, leap into the ears  
Try the gray garden beds  
Are the gardens made by men  
Or are the gardens made by women  
Or are they cursed by maidens  
Or are they woven by boys  
Young men, dear brothers  
Who was rich in money  
Who is famous for a penny  
Buy beer to drink at the celebration

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Cross-dance was, cross-dance was a little dance  
Cross-game was, cross-game was a little game  
Wear out your shoes and wear out the ribbons  
Tomorrow new ones, tomorrow new ones will be given  
Know others, know others will be made  
From cousin’s, cousin’s horsehide  
From aunt’s son’s, aunt’s son’s deerhide  
Shoe, shoe from bullhide  
From Lelle’s son, Lelle’s son’s cowhide.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
If I were to exist  
If I were to act  
I would make the room face the wind  
The sauna face the big island  
Towards the cluster of rowan berries  
Under the broad oak's canopy  
So that the sun would not shine on us  
Nor the moon shine on us, either  
So I could call the sad girls  
And bring along the good fellows  
They would often make spinning wheels  
Bring spinning wheels inside  
Build bobbins too  
When our babies are tiny  
We will soon make spindles  
To hold the head celebrations  
To see the old women  
They are kind in the eyes of men  
Dear in the world of old bachelors.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
When I went out the door  
I saw seven jokes  
The earth and sky were turning around  
The garden was sloping  
The berry garden was sinking  
The rooster was crowing on the melting ice  
The pig was singing on a penny  
The horse brought a bull calf  
The cow brought a duckling  
The sheep went to the table to lay eggs  
The hen went to the barn to bring hay  
Indeed, I saw ten jokes  
When I went out the door  
The pig was crying here, wearing a shirt  
The mistress was bare-bottomed on the bench  
The woman was naked on the stove.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Many proud old women have gone from here  
Four quarrelsome rich women  
One hundred and two cat keepers  
They did not make the road beautiful  
To make the road beautiful, to make the land fertile  
To make noise on the tall tree in the forest.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Many proud women have passed from here  
Four quarreling rich women  
One hundred and two catlike women  
They did not make the road beautiful  
Did not make the road beautiful, the land is bitter  
The tall tree of the forest makes a sound  
Our two poor children  
We go, we walk  
We make the road beautiful  
The tall tree of the forest makes a sound  
It does not make a road for us  
The field is wide, not made for children  
The forest does not speak of tall trees.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh you hop, little mad one  
A tiny slug, very small  
You took the minds of men's heads  
But fell over women's feet  
Men clattered without hats  
Women danced without skirts  
Boys with half boots on.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Maiden, young girl  
Let’s get together as three  
Over the strait as five  
We’ll go to Finland for the summer  
For the winter not across the seas  
Surely we will be sought by horses  
Pike rides on a pig  
On the foal’s hoof is tested  
Vaadu struck fire  
Sparks flew from the pike’s legs  
Riska, Raksa, the gelding’s shoes  
While searching among the maidens  
Catching a maiden from the flock.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Maiden, young girl  
Let’s get together as three

Riska, Raksa, the gelding’s shoes  
While searching among the maidens  
Catching a maiden from the flock.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Whose cattle, whose ox cost the cow
Whose cart, whose cart a pair of oxen
Whose buckle, whose buckle for the harness?
Anne's cattle, Anne's cattle cost the cow
Anne's cart, Anne's cart a pair of oxen
Anne's buckle, Anne's buckle for the harness.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Come inside, come inside, old man  
Sit down, sit down, old man  
Pants on, pants on, old man  
Shirt on, shirt on, old man  
Hat on, hat on, old man  
Pipe in mouth, pipe in mouth, old man.

label: Kalendrilaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go swing on the swing  
Let's sing on the swingboard  
Will this swing carry us  
If it doesn't carry, let it vanish  
Hey, where have they made the swing  
Made the swing in the shelter  
At the gathering place for guests  
At the playground for village boys  
Village women, young women  
Young men, lads  
Bring chickens, bring eggs  
Bring baskets for weaving  
Bring twin chickens  
Forest roosters to sing  
On our dear swing  
Our dear young men  
Made us a dear swing  
Where we young men sing  
For our dear maidens  
Over forests, over lands  
Over distant seas  
Over wide meadows  
Over rocky cliffs  
Over deserts, over mountains  
Over forests, meadows  
My brother is making a new swing  
A new swing, a splendid swing  
He's making a German swing from hazel wood  
He's making a dear swing from birch  
A dull swing from pine  
A long swing from rowan  
A loose swing from alder  
A rowing swing, a reaching swing  
A rowing swing to that land  
Where roosters eat gold  
Roosters eat gold, hens lay eggs  
Baskets green with silver.
