label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a good mother-in-law  
I had a good little mother-in-law  
She gave me a little goat  
A little bell on the goat’s neck  
The goat went outside barking  
The goat barked, the bell jingled.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Here comes Siim, here comes Siim’s servant  
Who wants Siim, who is Siim’s servant  
Looking for a young maiden  
A curly-haired beauty  
With shining hair  
With flaxen locks like a little bird  
If he cannot  
Siim cannot, nor Siim’s servant  
How sadly, how sadly  
Siim dances here as Siim’s servant.  
If he can.  
Here Siim succeeds, here Siim’s servant  
So joyfully, so joyfully  
Siim dances as Siim’s servant.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh wonder, or wonder  
What I saw in Näu village  
Dogs were plowing, oxen were threshing  
Girls were cutting wheelwood  
Girls were making sleds  
A sheep went to sleep on the table  
A hen brought two lambs  
A horse brought a spotted bullcalf  
A cow brought a garlic bullcalf  
Then the devil hopped into the spruce forest  
The fiend banged on the boiler  
But made a cart for the wheel barn  
With which the German hell was carried away.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Go to the corner, go to the corner, young men  
Under the bench, under the bench, pipe men.  
Under the table, under the table, broad jaws.  
My brother is coming, my brother is coming to fence.  
The heel is coming, the heel is coming to turn.  
The boot leg is coming, the boot leg is coming to pierce.  
The twisted sock is coming, the twisted sock is coming to roll.  
My brother is coming, my brother is coming to see the bride,  
Where here stands the brother’s bride  
Among this circle, among this circle the brother’s bride  
Among many, among many the most splendid  
From the middle too, from the middle too the most beautiful  
From in between too, from in between too the whitest  
From above too, from above too the plumpest.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, oh, oh, oh, so much sleep  
so many ghosts behind you,  
so many children on the sauna table,  
so much hardship for my poor self.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Piglet, pig, red piglet  
You must graze, you must feed  
Come to Christmas at home  
For a dear time in the chamber.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh dear little king  
Rich lord of Riga  
Why didn’t you come to us last year  
The year before when you rushed  
When you ran rivers without oil  
Drove graylings across  
Rolled lakes of vodka  
Now you have come to bad times  
To this autumn’s frosty wind  
To the light spring breeze  
Now I am stripping off your clothes  
Trying to get the bones from your neck  
I will save your head from the linen

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let us now go through the villages  
Through the trodden street  
Through the proud alleys  
A man hurriedly, a horse hurriedly  
The street thunders  
The golden claws twitch in the bend  
The cuckoo calls in the hollow crown  
One at the threshing barn  
Another at the wagon’s head  
The third at the sled’s top  
The fourth with the maiden in the carriage  
A swallow on the head  
The man on the shoulder  
The maidens looked even from afar  
Kneeling, bending  
Sometimes four-wheeled  
Through the passage of time  
If only that man were mine  
Who rides there along the road  
Along the road, along the land  
Silver horse ahead  
Golden man behind  
Iron sleigh behind  
A shimmering sled  
I do understand, I hear again  
Dear sister, beloved  
Why didn't you come to me last year  
When rivers ran without oil  
The gray ones drove vodka  
You came at the wrong time  
Winter snowfall  
Autumn twilight

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Not all chickens can get on the perch  
One of them is left behind  
Here prowls a cunning fox  
Who has long been a hunter  
Tii-tii-tii-tii come here  
There is plenty of room here.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I work in the garden, sharpen the staff  
Golden garden and iron staff  
Silver little whip  
So that a wolf does not get inside the head  
A wolf does not go roaming  
A bear does not go roaming  
A bear does not attack the sheep.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Spindle, spindle, young maiden  
Who has the spindles, young maiden  
For father, or for mother  
For father-in-law, or for mother-in-law  
For the village or the outskirts  
For the village, little humpback  
Or for the nail, funny one  
Not for father, not for mother  
Not for father-in-law, not for mother-in-law  
Not for the village, not for the outskirts  
Not for the village, little humpback  
Not for the nail, funny one  
The spindle to oneself  
To one’s own fine lad  
To one’s dear companion too  
Let us exchange a young man  
Who was quick to roll  
A weak silver needle  
Drip, drip, little needle  
Drip, drip, little eyelash needle  
Jump, round needle  
Leap, square needle  
Roll, five-sided

translation:

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
Let's start going home,  
To our distant home.  
But home is waiting for us,  
Watching from the gate.  
Home awaits the golden grandmother,  
At the gate is the bronze grandfather.  
A beautiful maiden went to the well,  
Tender herbs,  
Golden cakes on her neck:  
Give my oats to drink,  
Give the whole herd to drink,  
Give the underworld to calm down,  
The lovage tongue juice.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Butter or curds, what curds,
What curds did I see,
There was a jest in Näu village,
Girls were spinning yarn,
Old women were riding wagons,
A pig was rooting in a bread container,
A woman was rummaging in a laughing field,
Men were walking the tavern road,
Moonshiners in the shed,
Vodka jugs in the basket.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Young men, dear brothers  
Come to buy a steed  
Here stands a good horse  
It neither neighs in the hay  
Nor barks in the corral  
It neighs at those who sit on its back  
It barks at those who jump on its back,  
Young men, dear brothers  
Come to buy a steed  
Here stands a good horse

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had one and only brother  
He was foolish himself  
He made the room open to the wind  
A sauna  
Silk ships sailed inside  
Steamboats went down below  
Wind ships went above

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Drip, drip, little needle  
Drip, little needle eye  
If the needle were to disappear  
The needle disappeared into the heath  
Into the wound of the needle eye

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Sister, dear one  
Maiden, young one  
Let’s leave this land  
Away from the gentleman here  
Let’s go quietly to Finland  
For two days to Pärnu County  
There we’ll build a stone church  
Carry a carved chapel  
Doors made of applewood  
Benches from rowanwood  
We’ll make an iron ladder there  
A roof from swan feathers  
Eaves from eagle wings  
A roof covered with hen’s eggs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I wanted to go to heaven  
I had to crawl into the cloud,  
My pig saw shit holes,  
And the gray little clocks.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Where are we going in the deep night  
In the deep night, to the barn  
To build the foolish ones  
To the golden moon  
We go to listen to the moon  
To watch the days  
Whether the moon has become oily  
The day has thought about fading  
The star has rolled a ball of worry  
The moon has not become oily  
The day does not think about fading  
The star does not roll a ball of worry.  
The moon has played with curls  
The day has played with pearls  
And the evening with silver beads.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh wonder, oh wonder  
What did I see, oh wonder  
In the Ellavere hayfield  
On the Varisvere meadow  
Between Kukimäe village  
Dogs plowed, brushes scratched  
A sheep went to the barn to lay eggs  
A hen brought two little lambs  
A pig brought a gray piglet  
A horse brought a bull calf  
A cow brought a gray calf

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Droplet, droplet, little needle  
Droplet, little needle eye  
Where did you come from, needle?  
The needle came from the heath  
From the needle eye's hollow

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Row the boat, row the boat  
Row the boat to that land  
Where the roosters eat gold  
Roosters gold, hens beware  
The ducks graze silver  
Delicate birds finely.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go along this path  
Where slaves have walked before  
Slaves have walked, oxen have gone  
Late wandered sisters  
Early walked poor children  
Here have gone straight tracks  
Straight tracks, awake tracks  
Here has paused a pole  
A squirrel has slept through the night  
A duck has stayed still during the day

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Ride, ride, little horn  
Slowly along the road  
Ride to the tavern ahead  
There the others danced before  
There you have hay, there you have oats  
October rye is harvested  
In the barn, plenty of rye  
Tavern girl, little maiden  
Find a blanket, take a carpet  
Wipe my horse's sweat  
Wipe the sorrow from its back  
Shake the hail from its tail.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had to go to Piipeje  
I went to Kadi village  
I got there to Salla’s mourning  
From there I saw a wonder  
How those girls were working  
Irons in their mouths and collars on their necks  
Old carriages behind  
Old women as coachmen  
Whoever saw it, surely laughed  
Everyone there was smirking  
Siim was laughing, his eyes wet.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's start walking  
Let's light the pipes  
Our home is far away  
Five was to get out of the way  
Six was to dry the river  
Seven was the tough soda  
Eight was the fishing net  
Nine was the good rivers  
Ten was the cold grayness.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
What I am afraid of when going to the tavern  
Going to the tavern, drinking vodka  
When my father-in-law was very rich  
And my mother-in-law was very rich  
They gave me a little goat  
A bell for the goat’s neck  
The wolf ate the goat, the bell remained with me  
I went to the Jaanipää church  
I put the bell on the woman’s neck  
The woman was angry, stomping  
The bell was loud, clanging  
Those who saw me, laughed at me  
Those who knew me, scolded me  
Oh, you crazy and foolish one  
Bring the bell back to the woman’s neck.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The pig died for us, the junker was born  
Calves were watching out the window  
Dogs were clattering, boots on feet  
The gentleman was honored at the edge of the snowdrift  
The lady went to the field to plow  
The young men were going to the swamp  
The children were going to pick twigs  
The sister village’s role is that of a rascal  
I am such a rogue  
The rogue has a fine horse  
The mare has a slippery stallion  
The prostitute has a proud stallion  
The rogue went with the beauty to church  
The fool went to bed with the woman  
The prostitute went to the town to bring bread

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I smoked a long pipe  
I let the smoke fly straight  
Blue smoke rose from my mouth  
A four-twist from my nose  
But some thought it was manor smoke  
It was pipe smoke from a poppy

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
When I left the tavern  
I saw seven jokes  
A pig sang on a penny  
A rooster rummaged in the garden there  
Ani ate hay from the heap  
A sheep took lovers from Vahva  
A cow plowed the field angrily  
A girl milked an ox from the back.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Rowing, rocking, rowing, rocking  
To the heights, to the distance  
Over the marsh, over the bogs  
Over the long Lake Peipus.  
Rowing, rocking to that land,  
Where the spruces turn yellow  
The young birches turn green  
The straight alders gleam blue  
Where the roosters eat gold  
Roosters gold, hens beware  
The ducks green silver  
The crows old copper.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's get going, men  
Let's light the pipes  
Let the smoking noses smoke.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Droplet, droplet little needle,  
Droplet little needle eye  
The needle didn’t get lost while playing  
While playing, while messing about  
It got lost while working  
Sewing a dowry sheet  
Mending children's clothes  
Trimming the young man's shirt  
Jump needle, jump needle  
Spark spark needle  
Buzzing little girl’s needle  
Jump into Udri's barley  
Into the shepherd boy's turnip garden.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Kuos-kuos little goldfinch  
Kuos goldfinock  
What is the goldfinch looking for us?  
The goldfinch is looking for the ram's barn  
Through the straw pigsty  
Through the chicken coop door  
Through the market sheep's barn.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Sifting-sifting, little sieve  
Bottom of the sieve  
Lend me the sieve, lambkin  
Cannot lend, lambkin  
You have stern servants  
Stubborn flatterers  
They take the sieve to the dung heap  
The pig eats the bottom of the sieve  
The male goat eats the skeleton’s bottom  
The little foal eats the fodder bottom  
Lend it, lambkin

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
If only I knew what I would do,
I would put the room to the wind,
The sauna on a big island,
The city on a grain of sand.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The hazel jumps out of the forest,  
The cone cracks out of the pocket;  
"Come here today, young men,  
Come pick me today,  
Take me to Kotila's home,  
To Matila's father's house!"  
Put the ducks out to dry,  
From there I roll into the rye field,  
I put it into the half barrel of beer,  
Between the stave hoops.  
But I take the senses from the men's heads,  
The wits from the wise women's heads;  
Then the men made noise without hats,  
The women danced without wits,  
Boys with half a boot on,  
Girls with wreaths on their heads.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The flanks of the mares strike fire,
The shanks of the alder strike sparks,
The hooves of the colt strike lightning
On the rough village ground,
At the threshing floor of Esna parish.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I make tales for a drunkard,
I make calls at the tavern counter:
Tavern manners are filthy manners,
No vodka given without money;
No beer without buying,
No small beer without trading,
No mead without thinking.
Tavern manners are filthy manners.

label: Lastelaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Sleep, sleep, little spark  
Sleep, sleep, little mamma.  
Sleep, little mamma-bear,  
Row the swing, reach the swing,  
Row the swing to the sunny land,  
Where the roosters drink gold,  
Roosters gold, hens silver,  
Ducks green silver,  
Drakes drink rainwater  
Geese drink forest water  
Men drink spring water.

label: Lastelaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Sleep, sleep, little spark  
Sleep, sleep, little mamma.  
Sleep, little mamma-bear,  
Row the swing, reach the swing,  
Row the swing to the sunny land,  
Where the roosters drink gold,  
Roosters gold, hens silver,  
Ducks green silver,  
Drakes drink rainwater  
Geese drink forest water  
Men drink spring water.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Snort-snort, pub landlady,  
Take care of your beauty, Anna  
Don’t let your reputation go  
The tavern dogs roam around  
Uninvited mischief boys  
They cause stomach troubles  
Make you sick below the navel.  
If you lock up the town, lock it tight  
Give the key to Ottu  
Or maybe also to little Vestu.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
My father is a miracle worker,  
My mother is a miracle worker,  
They made me a wonderful coat,  
From patches and patches  
Of pigskin spikes,  
Of catskin legs,  
At the top was squirrel fur,  
In the middle patchy dog skin,  
At the back wore cat skin.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Now the meals have been eaten,  
Now the drinks have been drunk.  
Only the songs remain unsung,  
Tunes remain unplayed;  
The cook's honor unacknowledged,  
The cook has diligently prepared the broth:  
Just the right amount of salt added.  
He brought herbs from Sweden,  
Salads brought from Germany,  
Onions from across the sea. -  
What I ate at the meal,  
I ate up all the food,  
And what I drank at the drink,  
I drank up the barrel of beer  
What was left of the food from the meal  
The bone is down, the skin is above,  
A piece of tender meat. -  
What remained after the food:  
The tail and the horns remained,  
The tail remained to sweep the sheathe.  
The horns remained to be pondered,  
The head remained to be carried home. -  
What remained from the barrel of drink  
The yeast settled below, the foam was above,  
The seed without clear beer.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
On its own proud bush branch,  
Hangs on a high rod.  
It bends the row of nails on the house,  
Asks at the family door's threshold:  
Help me onto the oven,  
Put me on the stove board,  
Lift me on the bottom of the stove’s smoke plate.  
From there I will roll to the vat,  
Slip into the halfway beer barrel.  
Oh, beers, bubble up,  
Beer crates rise up,  
Vats sputter and bubble.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I walk through the rustling forest.  
I dress in the eerie street,  
I think the path is ticking,  
The trails are cracking.  
But the little path does not tick.  
Neither do the little trails crack.  
My tin path ticks to you.  
The quiet rods crack.  
A pearly horse lives.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The horse has a gray brother,  
The horse has a gray ornamented loincloth,  
Both legs are white.  
The harnesses are inscribed with gold,  
The tail is tied with silky thread,  
The nose is trimmed with a thread,  
His head is placed in a halter,  
Ears in golden rings,  
He went to the forest for the first time.  
He brought six spruces, each six fathoms,  
From juniper, eight fathoms,  
One hundred fathoms of oak,  
Fifty strong pine stakes,  
A thousand fathoms of sturdy oak.  
He began building the road,  
Built the path to Riga,  
On the cabin’s cabbage leaf,  
On the house’s berry stalk,  
Made a tuft on the wind,  
In the new dawn sky,  
Sat himself on the corner,  
Took a stick on his knee.  
From there the German knights traveled,  
Past by the German coins,  
Many lords wandered,  
Many ladies wandered.  
Some began to guess:  
Is this the town of Lihula,  
Or is this the church of Köiseva,  
Or is this the peaceful beach?  
I hear, I respond:  
Oh you crazy guessers,  
Oh you crazy and foolish!  
If it were the town of Lihula,  
It would be joined with flesh.  
If it were the peaceful beach,  
It would be built with money,  
Or if it were the church of Köiseva,  
It would be woven with ropes.  
This is the thigh of my brother,  
The sauna of brother Sandi,  
The storehouse of brother Halva,  
The room of brother Tumma.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud abielust  ; Töölaulud
translation:
I went to drive the sleigh,  
To let the wide trace run,  
I myself ride along the giant,  
The dogs ran along the bog,  
The shafts along the road tracks.  
I heard the shaft clatter,  
I heard the duck splash.  
I took the duck out of the shaft,  
The hound out of the shaft's mouth.  
I took the duck to Paide,  
The hound I gave away there.  
From Paide I got some money.  
I bought a new little instrument.  
I began to play the instrument.  
Father heard the instrument playing,  
Father began to work without oil.  
Mother heard the instrument playing,  
Mother began to knock the cream,  
To churn butter.  
Brothers heard the instrument playing,  
Brothers went to draw water.  
Sister heard the instrument playing,  
Sister went to fetch salt.  
The woman heard the instrument playing,  
The woman smiled at the young men:  
"Here comes the instrument home,  
Takes my instrument to sleep,  
Takes the kannel to the chamber,  
Calls up the morning.  
Takes the instrument to start playing,  
At the doorway of the kannel chamber.  
Get up, woman, milk the cow,  
Get up, girl, drive the herd.  
Other herds go far,  
Our herd goes to the fold,  
Other heifers in the meadow,  
Our heifers in the mud,  
Other bulls to the hayfield,  
Our bulls to the stall,  
Other mares to the rye field,  
Our mares to the sledge field."  
The mother hears it, again it sounds:  
"Oh, my dear daughter-in-law,  
My son's wife with an apron!  
An instrument does not feed a household,  
The kannel alone cannot provide a living,  
The instrument walks on the straw path,  
Sings on the edge of the straw board;  
The kannel in the straw chamber.  
There it keeps harlots,  
Carries immoral women,  
Takes the red to the gate,  
Gives too much to the harlot."

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, oh crazy one, where does the joke come from,  
From barley you can always make kvass  
Oh that barley mold,  
Dear old woman of oats,  
Taught to make beer,  
Drives anger into vodka.  
At home, a spindle is whirling  
A cone is bobbing in the bush  
It makes the men roar  
The girls dance without shoes  
The boys into half boots.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Aidu-vaidu old pants  
I had three pairs of trousers at home  
Mother made the trousers, father made the trousers  
I had trousers before myself,  
Then there were three pairs of trousers in total  
One pair were clean wedding trousers  
The others were white leg trousers  
The third were worn courting trousers.

label: Laulud laulust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I sing, why don’t I sing
I sing a song about a horse,
A saddleless steed
With a walnut for a head
Eyes made of stars in the sky
Eyelids made of dragon trees
Ears made of hemp leaves
Hooves made of birch sponges
A tail made of oats
A body made of hay shavings.
Then I go on a journey to Finland
I snap the Finnish bridge
I knock on the foundation beams
This horse doesn’t run because of the reins
Because of the thin whip
The horse runs because of the barley
It leaps because of the oats’ sheaf.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let us begin to go  
All the people will start to move  
All those young ones will falter  
We cause trouble within the family  
We waste money  
We eat up the bull’s feed  
We drink up the bull’s drink  
I hear and respond  
I will not leave here before  
I separate the tufts  
I take the wall beams on my back  
The shutters for the doorframe  
Reed ropes on the shoulder  
I devour the stove’s warmth  
Then I will start to go  
Then I will mark the mound  
The scar into the teiup  
The cross into the Riga rope  
Whoever wants to chase behind  
May take the mark from the mound  
The scar from the teiup  
The cross from the Riga rope  
Below I go down without  
Above I go under the red day  
Between I go the path of the five rainbows  
At six, at the break of dawn.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk  
Let's walk to the hilltop  
The land covered in liverwort  
Green, pink  
When will we arrive there  
Where we have long been awaited  
Long awaited  
There is no sound of a rooster's crow  
No rooster's crow, no hen's clucking  
No weak child's crying  
No mourning of widows. -  
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk  
Let's walk to the hilltop  
The land covered in liverwort  
Green, pink  
When will we arrive there  
Where the wind has made a room  
Water has rolled logs  
Rain has mossed the walls  
Fog has made new doors.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go chest to chest, let's go ear to ear  
Let's go chest to Riga  
Quietly towards Tallinn  
Let's go to Riga to spoil  
To Tallinn to kill  
The guard calls from the rampart  
The houseboy from the doorstep  
The little boy at the shop door  
Do not spoil little Riga  
Kill little Tallinn  
Let them bring salt to Riga  
Let them hammer money for Tallinn  
Boys to bring a headdress  
Women to wear patterned shawls  
Girls to carry the blue veil.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's start our journey  
Our home is far away  
Five is to change a fence  
Six is for the river to dry up  
Seven is bitter soda  
Eight is a clever vow  
Nine is the Emajõgi River  
Ten is cold springs

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Where do we go against the night  
Against the night, against the water  
Against the gaze of darkness  
Against the shining of dawn  
Against the golden moon  
Perhaps we drown in the brook  
Perhaps we disappear on the shore  
Who searches for us in the brook  
Or who tries on the shore  
God searches in the brook  
Mary tries on the shore.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a rich godfather  
He gave me a goat  
A little strap around the goat’s neck  
The wolf ate the goat, the strap stayed with me  
I put the strap around Kaie’s neck  
Kaie was eager to dance  
The strap broke, the pants tore  
The pants tore, the money ran out  
Then I took them to the smith in Viira  
To the smith in Viira, Ella’s brother  
“Take my straps and fix them  
Fix the torn pants  
Patch the money holes”

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A little bottle of vodka, little brother  
A little red-necked punch  
When I took you in  
You shoved me down  
A little beer, little ale  
A little fish, little neighbor  
The gentlemen of Viru drank vodka  
The women of Salla ate bread  
The forest maidens drank honey  
The wives of the men from Kal´la  
That's why I drank, little man  
Little boy

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
If it were up to me  
If it were up to my doing  
I would know what I would do  
I would open the room to the wind  
Saunas by the edge of the legs  
I would heat the room with kindling  
Sauna with the beginnings of an island  
Yet I did not get that warmth  
That I got from Anne’s love  
From Kaie, dear, from her delight  
From Liisu, Ella, from the members  
I had to go to Pille  
I fell asleep at Malle’s  
Malle was sweeter to me  
Than those Pille’s cream cakes

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:

From Liisu, Ella, from the members

I fell asleep at Malle’s  
Malle was sweeter to me  
Than those Pille’s cream cakes

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I found a seed in the field  
With the seed, I bought a horse  
With the horse, I harvested barley  
With the barley, I took a wife  
With the wife, I lived life  
With the pipe, I maintained the family  
With tobacco, I fed the children.

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I found a seed in the field  
With the seed, I bought a horse  
With the horse, I harvested barley  
With the barley, I took a wife  
With the wife, I lived life  
With the pipe, I maintained the family  
With tobacco, I fed the children.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
What else do we drink but beer  
If you don’t decorate the path to beer  
Use the pitcher  
Throw the whips into the wild  
Throw the boards into the grove

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Row the swing, pull the swing  
Pull the swing to the field of bulls  
Where roosters eat gold  
And hens reach the yard  
Ducks have silver wings.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go, young men,  
The boys from Võnnu with nets,  
Let's go catch perch,  
Let's take out some crayfish.  
Let's go to the lake for fish,  
To the big spring in the meadow.  
There I caught a hundred eels,  
A thousand bream scales.  
Then I came home from fishing.  
I put the fish to dry;  
Who came to steal fish?  
A cat came to steal fish.  
I started chasing the cat,  
The cat went to the well to splash around.  
I went to the village to get ropes.  
From Migu I brought fir ropes,  
From Tõnu I brought tar ropes,  
From Kangur I brought hemp ropes.  
I started to catch the cat.  
A hundred men were after the cat’s tail,  
A thousand men were on the cat’s back.  
Then I caught the cat in the traps;  
The hemp ropes broke,  
The fir ropes tore,  
But the tar rope pulled tight.  
Then I caught the cat in the traps.  
Mice sang in the corner,  
Very well, very well, little kitty,  
Where are my six golden sons,  
Nine fathers of the poor,  
Ten village maidens.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go, young men,  
The boys from Võnnu with nets,  
Let's go catch perch,  
Let's take out some crayfish.  
Let's go to the lake for fish,  
To the big spring in the meadow.  
There I caught a hundred eels,  
A thousand bream scales.  
Then I came home from fishing.  
I put the fish to dry;  
Who came to steal fish?  
A cat came to steal fish.  
I started chasing the cat,

Where are my six golden sons,  
Nine fathers of the poor,  
Ten village maidens.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A little vodka, a little winch  
A little beer, a little smoke  
A little darling, my dear;  
The beer warms me  
I make the beer hot,  
I heat a coat for the jug  
And a new coat for myself.  
But I started to feel unwell myself,  
Killed the little lark  
Why do you give me ugly drinks,  
Why bring joyless tobacco?  
Why not make the beer pretty,  
Heat a coat for the jug— – –

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I was called by the spring well,  
Kai shouted to me from the echo path:  
Run, boy, the horse needs watering,  
Servant boy, put on the saddle.  
I am going to Finland for a visit.  
Crossing the Finnish bridge,  
Knocking on the plank foundation,  
I am going to my father-in-law’s door,  
I am going to my mother-in-law’s door;  
Hello father-in-law, hello mother-in-law:  
May I come inside the room,  
To take care of the horse,  
Not to tie the foal to the fence.  
Father-in-law heard, mother-in-law heard,  
Soon they answered:  
Ride into my house,  
Here is my little daughter,  
The household girl, the maiden,  
Take a blanket, carry a carpet,  
Wipe my apples from the branches,  
Wipe the dust from my back,  
Spread the grips by the boards.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
If I were myself,  
And secondly my doing,  
I would make a curtain for the wind,  
A shelter for life,  
A roof for the berry vine,  
A thatch for the cabin leaves.  
Wait, wait cold, no no cold,  
Then open the door inside,  
Shout on the chamber threshold,  
Seek shelter in the room,  
Warm the room.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
If I were myself,  
And secondly my doing,  
I would make a curtain for the wind,  
A shelter for life,  
A roof for the berry vine,  
A thatch for the cabin leaves.  
Wait, wait cold, no no cold,  
Then open the door inside,  
Shout on the chamber threshold,  
Seek shelter in the room,  
Warm the room.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
What is wrong with the boys from Vinnu,  
Or is it the fault of the boys from Äntu;  
The wheels run without money,  
The spokes are broken off,  
The hubs are in pieces.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Chill, chill, cold, cold,  
Cold, piercing cold,  
Chill piercing fiercely;  
If only our manor folk would understand,  
They would surely come running after,  
Would bring old coats,  
To warm my fingers,  
To shield my toes.  
So let me say again,  
Dear fellows, brothers,  
Where has the frost settled in the throat,  
Where has the coat covered the neck?

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Chill, chill, cold, cold,  
Cold, piercing cold,  
Chill piercing fiercely;  
If only our manor folk would understand,  
They would surely come running after,  
Would bring old coats,  
To warm my fingers,  
To shield my toes.  
So let me say again,  
Dear fellows, brothers,  
Where has the frost settled in the throat,  
Where has the coat covered the neck?

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Little instrument, little joy,
Dear kannel, little playmate,
An instrument cannot feed alone,
A kannel cannot live without a companion.
The instrument walks the tavern road
Looking for a beer girl,
Trying the vodka chamber,
Let's go singing through the forest
Whose voice then carries far,
That one the groom buys a hat for.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
When I went out of the tavern,  
I saw a lot of fun;  
There were four virgins there,  
In front were lively girls,  
At the back were cheerful girls,  
In the middle were nice girls,  
Then in between were poor children.  
I stare and watch.  
I don't want a lively girl from the front,  
Nor a cheerful girl from the back,  
Nor a nice girl from the middle;  
I want a poor child from between,  
From the end I want to follow a slave;  
This girl has done work,  
The poor child has experienced hardship;  
She will be born in my lap,  
She fits into my house,  
She stretches out to my bedclothes.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
When I went out of the tavern,  
I saw a lot of fun;  
There were four virgins there,  
In front were lively girls,  
At the back were cheerful girls,  
In the middle were nice girls,  
Then in between were poor children.  
I stare and watch.  
I don't want a lively girl from the front,  
Nor a cheerful girl from the back,  
Nor a nice girl from the middle;  
I want a poor child from between,  
From the end I want to follow a slave;  
This girl has done work,  
The poor child has experienced hardship;  
She will be born in my lap,  
She fits into my house,  
She stretches out to my bedclothes.

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Others went early to Tiole  
I go to Iljapeal to fetch  
I hope for the stallion's strength  
For the stallion’s full step  
For the mare’s gait by Kõrvi.  
The stallion did no plowing for me  
The mare didn’t plow the wet earth  
The stallion didn’t wet the rye stalks.  
Surely that lout forced me  
To plow the start of the land  
Wet land by the hill's edge  
Swamp mud by the bog’s edge  
Still, I plowed a piece in defiance  
I left one furrow for the field  
Plowed one, left another  
Then I got ahead of the others  
By the time of the Tiopäev hearing  
Waiting for food to be brought  
For the white bucket to be carried  
But I saw food being brought  
For the black bucket to be carried  
Immediately my heart fell sad  
My heart started to ache  
Look at the thing I was given  
The food that was brought to me  
The middle grew cold  
Velvetfully listed  
Burnt bread crusts:  
I pick them up and get angry  
Scatter the chaff on the grass  
Scatter the chaff in the yard corner  
I stuck the whip into the corner  
I struck the walls of the sauna  
I myself went to Aru tavern  
Took a few shots of vodka  
Two handfuls from the jug  
A strong mug of beer  
I felt my legs turning  
My legs weakening  
Surely the legs will lead home  
The calves will go to the bed  
The knees will put themselves to sleep  
The soles of the feet into the bed.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Juhan Juss killed a cat,  
Made a coat for Kaie,  
A foundation cloth for Anne,  
A quilt for Mari,  
Slippery shoes for Liisu;  
Then he went through our yard,  
Ate our communal food,  
Licked our child's butt.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh little king, little king  
Why didn’t you come to me last year  
When the commands were carried out  
Five letters were taken  
Six letters were written  
Seven messages were sent  
Now you have come in the time of harvest  
In the light springtime  
In the cool autumn time  
Now you are sewing clothes for the mother  
Mending the neckbands.  
Oh little king, little king  
Give me the poor gold  
The poor gold from my sister’s hands  
My poor neck protection  
These are not for the mother-in-law  
These are not for myself  
I got this for boiling soup  
For beating the priest’s thigh.  
Whose soul is blossoming  
Whose wreath is being placed?  
Liisu’s soul is blossoming  
Liisu’s wreath is being placed  
Cast away your souls, little Liisu  
Cast away your souls, lay down your wreaths  
Throw away those silver curls.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The doll was called to the village  
Across the field of oats  
The doll had no time to go  
The doll was in the fabric  
Between fine reeds.  
Lower, lower, doll  
Lower alone two paces  
Already father will die  
Father’s grave is being dug  
A grave deep with an iron bottom  
Nine fathoms deep  
Eight cubits wide  
A cubit plus ten more -  
Lower, lower, doll  
Lower alone two paces  
Already mother will die  
Mother’s grave is being dug  
A grave deep, iron-bottomed  
Nine fathoms deep  
Eight cubits wide  
A cubit plus ten more -  
Lower, lower, doll  
Lower alone two paces  
Already sister will die  
Sister’s grave is being dug  
A grave deep with an iron bottom  
Nine fathoms deep  
Eight cubits wide  
A cubit plus ten more. -  
Lower, lower, doll  
Lower alone two paces  
Already brother will die  
Brother’s grave is being dug  
A grave deep with an iron bottom  
Nine fathoms deep  
Eight cubits wide  
A cubit plus ten more. - - -  
Rise, rise, doll  
Rise alone two paces  
Already father is alive  
Sits on a silk bed  
Golden doll in hand  
Silver flowers around the doll. - - -  
Rise, rise, doll  
Rise alone two paces  
Already mother is alive  
Sits on a silk bed  
Golden doll in hand  
Silver flowers around the doll. - -  
Rise, rise, doll  
Rise alone two paces  
Already sister is alive  
Sits on a silk bed  
Golden doll in hand  
Silver flowers around the doll. -  
Rise, rise, doll  
Rise alone two paces  
Already brother is alive  
Sits on a silk bed  
Golden doll in hand  
Silver flowers around the doll. -

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We must take by force the troops  
What I give to the troops  
I have not taken the ire sword  
The cat's fur not pulled out  
The rat's intestines not cleaned out  
The pot not brought to the fire  
The scales cold in the cellar  
This I give to the troops  
To these troops.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I still drink from a straw,  
Sing with the straw stick,  
Come home from the straw.  
Monday and Tuesday.  
In the middle a fine shepherd.  
Thursday I mow the hay,  
Friday I rake the dry hay,  
Saturday I make a wide pile,  
Sunday I go fishing.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Drink, you, bear the neck on the throat,  
Take against the copper breast.  
Surely the legs will carry the tissue,  
The knees will go to bed.  
The shins will set the bed.  
Let the feet trot.  
Move the shins well,  
Burn the knees.  
The thighs travel quickly.  
So that the tissue is ready before dawn,  
The thighs lie down before the day.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I went to the edge of the great sea,  
Happened to fall asleep there,  
To see a dream,  
I found a little horn,  
And also a little whistle,  
I started to play the horn,  
Kept blowing the whistle,  
Then came together the Kolga Germans,  
The beautiful girls of Hiio,  
The maiden girls with little brooches,  
The dear ones with twisted necks.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a scruffy dog,  
It brought six puppies:  
One was a deer dog, another a boar dog,  
The third was a hare dog.  
They brought a flock of ducks,  
I took the ducks to Paide,  
The ducks paid a flock's worth of money.  
I bought a new little pipe.  
I started playing the pipe,  
Blowing the shepherd's pipe,  
Cutting Swedish grass.  
Father heard the sound of the pipe,  
Then started making beer.  
Brother heard the sound of the pipe,  
Brother started carrying water.  
Mother heard the sound of the pipe,  
Started churning cream,  
Sister heard the sound of the pipe,  
Sister was salting the butter.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a scruffy dog,  
It brought six puppies:  
One was a deer dog, another a boar dog,  
The third was a hare dog.  
They brought a flock of ducks,  
I took the ducks to Paide,  
The ducks paid a flock's worth of money.  
I bought a new little pipe.  
I started playing the pipe,  
Blowing the shepherd's pipe,  
Cutting Swedish grass.  
Father heard the sound of the pipe,  
Then started making beer.  
Brother heard the sound of the pipe,  
Brother started carrying water.  
Mother heard the sound of the pipe,  
Started churning cream,  
Sister heard the sound of the pipe,  
Sister was salting the butter.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Hüia hüia our instrument,  
Laala dear our kannel.  
Feet want to dance.  
The room wants to be swept new,  
The floor wants to be stomped.  
The edges of the walls want to be pressed.  
Little instrument, little joy.  
Dear kannel, little plaything:  
Shall I say it again?  
I sing it back anew:  
No instrument without a master,  
No kannel without companionship.  
The instrument walks to the barn road.  
Searching for beer houses.  
Pulling a load of vodka stench.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I still drink even with a straw.  
I drank bulls and horses.  
I drank bulls from the hike,  
Do not ruin the rake.  
I drank the tavern to its ear,  
To the small town to its hall,  
To the market bright red.  
To the linen town road.  
To the water-gray mill road.  
On Monday I drank from under the bow,  
On Tuesday I drank from under the shirt,  
Wednesday was a nice hat,  
On Thursday I drank among them,  
On Friday I drank from the chest of the sled,  
On Saturday I drank a wide belt,  
On Sunday I drank the pants off.  
On Monday I slept my sleep,  
On Tuesday I went to work.  
I set the plows on the edge,  
I plucked the ridge on the furrow,  
Pour the chaff, pour the grain,  
To the row an old red one.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Straw Woman Krõõdukene,  
City Woman Liisukene.  
Village Woman Annekene,  
Fashionable Woman Maretukene.  
Are your rooms swept?  
Are your chambers cleaned?  
Are your windows polished?  
Are your chairs in the room?  
Are your benches covered with rushes?  
Silks are coming in,  
To Kalev’s chambers.  
Let me say it again:  
I will sing it once more,  
Soon back to the old tune.  
Straw Woman’s dogs took away,  
They won’t give wine without reason,  
Beer without buying,  
Mead without minding,  
Kvass without pouring,  
Water without whining.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I rode a bit through Turkish field,  
Half a verst through Blackberry field,  
A little along Narva road.  
I went through Latvian field.  
At last, I reached the Russian path.  
I did not covet it;  
I saw in the village of Nao,  
In the hayfields of Hillevere,  
In the coppice of Kullevere,  
In the meadows of Varivere.  
Dogs plowed, Bulls barked,  
Girls made sleds,  
Women stretched wheel shafts,  
The pig kneaded dough,  
A woman rummaged through the meadows,  
Cats wove cloth,  
Mice snapped threads.  
The sheep went to the barn to lay eggs,  
The hen brought two little lambs.  
The horse brought a bull-calf,  
The cow brought a cicada calf.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh king, little king,  
Rich Saxon of Riga.  
Why didn’t you come to us last year?  
When the rivers ran without oil,  
When the old men brewed vodka.  
We brewed kvass in the cellars,  
Beat the ashes for firewood,  
Cleared the streets of mud,  
The village smelled of cumin,  
The village pastures of sulfur,  
Now you come to the white time,  
Now you come to the sad month,  
To the light spring,  
To the noisy autumn,  
To the winter snowfall.  
Now you strip off clothes,  
Break neck chains,  
Large brooches from the chest,  
Golden necklaces from the neck,  
Save the wreath from the head.  
Oh king, little king,  
Rich Saxon of Riga,  
Give me back poor gold,  
Poor gold, poor copper,  
Neither were these your shares,  
Nor were these your precious gifts,  
Nor was a fine feast brought,  
These I earned myself.  
I got the brooch from kneading bread,  
The necklace from boiling broth,  
The belt from beating the brachet,  
I bought myself a dwelling.  
Oh king, little king,  
Rich Saxon of Riga,  
What shall I offer you?  
I have one ox for plowing,  
One horse for raking,  
One cow for milking,  
One sheep in the barn,  
One pig in the sty,  
One hen on the roof,  
What shall I offer you?  
The ox I have for plowing the field,  
The horse for harrowing,  
The cow for milking the family,  
The sheep for shading the family,  
The pig for feeding the family,  
The rooster for crowing in the morning,  
The dog was outside barking.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A pies of a pipe, a cloak of grass,
A whip of hay branches,
Who cut the finger?
That lad.
Yes, that lad.
Who leapt into the cradle,
That lad, the boy.
Where did he get the scarf?
He took a neckerchief from around the neck,
Tore apart the chin cloth,
Shattered the headscarf to pieces.
The scarf is gone,
The village lad will buy a new one,
The boy from Viru brings a striped one,
The boy from Sweden brings a rosy one,
The German boy brings a velvet one,
The country boy brings a muddy one.
Where did he get the ribbon?
He took a clogs strap from the calf.
The strap is gone,
The village girl will make a new one,
Make a new wavy one,
Put latticework inside,
Insert ribbon patterns.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Go to the corner, young men,  
Under the bench, pipe drinkers,  
Under the table, lazy boys.  
Brother Mino is coming to spar,  
Brother Mino’s swords are crooked.  
The sea foam is white,  
Whiter still is my dear brother.  
The seaweed is straight,  
Straighter still is my dear brother.  
The long reed is long,  
Make it longer still, my dear brother.  
What is my dear brother looking for?  
My dear brother is looking for a bride.  
Where does the brother’s bride stand?  
She stands here among the crowd.  
Who, dear, will take his neck?  
Who, darling, will hold the bridle,  
Who will take the silk on their lap?

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
In front dances the giant,  
Behind dances the powerless.  
Then the pig snorts,  
Then the bull bellows,  
Then the lightning cracks.  
Sometimes a poor man dances.  
The bull dances in the yoke,  
In the yoke and in the bank,  
In Pepermanis rummaging,  
The calf wants to dance.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We had a hawk bird  
In our lord's garden.  
It caused quite a fuss,  
For it had some faults.  
It ate all the pepperberries,  
Devoured the best of them,  
Then went itself into the forest,  
To the rich family's woods.  
Some had time to chase after it,  
Some to catch it with nets,  
Some demanded notes,  
Some took a shot with a gun.  
The stewards' boys, the Möldre boys,  
Blacksmiths' sons, stable boys,  
Chamber boys with slipper feet,  
German boys with boot heels.  
They had time to chase after it,  
They caught it with nets,  
They demanded notes,  
They took a shot with a gun.  
One man was there in the forest,  
With an oak leaf on his shoulder.  
He was supposed to shoot the bird,  
Come, hunter, and shoot it down.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Swing of the ship’s shine of the ship,  
Move the ship towards the town,  
Swing towards the corner of the swamp.  
Go from the town to bring a ribbon,  
From the shop to bring a gate ribbon.  
From the German box, to the card edge,  
Move the ship below the town,  
From under the town, to Riga,  
From under Riga to Vyborg,  
From Vyborg to the Russian road.  
The watchman calls from the rampart.  
From the shop boy by the door,  
The furnace man at the furnace threshold.  
Long ago Pärnu blessed a cow,  
Sang a speckled rooster in Riga,  
Roared a black bull of the sea.  
Young men, dear brothers,  
Come to buy goods,  
Here is an expensive merchant ship,  
Here men have hats,  
Women have linen handkerchiefs,  
Boys have pipe gate ribbons,  
Girls have blue stones.  
“Five old ones, and six strong ones,  
Seven clear silver coins.”

label: Laulud sõjast ja nekrutist  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
War is stirring in our yard,  
Plague is treading on our street!  
They prepare for battles:  
Clean weapons,  
Sharpening swords,  
Strengthening the healthy.  
On the minds of our men,  
On the sides of the boys,  
On the fields of the maidens.  
The brave go marching to the front,  
The brave go towards Riga,  
The march towards Tallinn.  
They go to Riga to plunder,  
To Tallinn to kill,  
To Põltsamaa to burn.

label: Laulud sõjast ja nekrutist  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks

To Tallinn to kill,  
To Põltsamaa to burn.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Liisukene lipsukene,  
A little darling with cute lips,  
A little snap upon seeing eyes,  
A little crack upon hearing the ear.  
That was famous in the miller's mind,  
Pleasant in the minds of the manor owners,  
Smooth in the minds of the shepherd boys,  
Very beautiful in the gentleman's mind.  
Liisu went to the tavern to walk,  
Fell down on her back,  
Straight onto the pig's back,  
Eyes shut on the pig's back.  
The poor pig was troubled,  
Beautiful Liisu was showing off.  
The poor pig got a painful blow,  
Beautiful Liisu got a blow to her soul.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk,  
Let's make tea from the treetop,  
From the ground with liver hair,  
Who came to answer us?  
The white Saxons came to answer,  
The white young gentlemen answered.  
I asked to strike the dog with a whip.  
Could not strike the dog with a whip.  
Sulevipoeg came fiercely,  
Kalevipoeg came stubbornly,  
Ole had a good horse,  
Kalev had an expensive stallion.  
Under the horse was a plow,  
A big body smoked,  
Men thought of forest smoke,  
Boys of tree smoke,  
Girls of brake smoke,  
Women of Narva city smoke.  
The horse of the great man smokes,  
The tall hat scorches,  
Under the plow, a gray stallion.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Ask, ask, little frost,  
Strike the winter weather.  
I am freezing at the hand,  
My toes are aching.  
Where has the frost bitten the throat?  
The throat bartered for a coat!  
Standing on the wall of straws,  
Watching at the tavern’s beam,  
Under five distilleries,  
Under two beer mugs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Buy a cat with a curled eye,  
A curled-eyed needle tail,  
In the peel of a bad woman,  
Licked milk from a lazy woman,  
Ate butter from a greedy woman.  
I will beat the cat!  
The cat went to the well in clogs,  
Its long tail was dragging.  
I ran to the village.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Here departed many proud mothers,  
The regiment left with rich mothers,  
The flag went to the city soldiers,  
A proper flock left as maidens.  
From here rode great Germans,  
Lords who came late.  
They did not make the roads,  
Nor did they make the land bitter.  
The tall tree of the forest thunders,  
The wide Väila sings songs.  
We are two like little chickens!  
A pair like ducklings.  
We make the road beautiful,  
The beautiful road, the bitter land,  
The tall tree of the forest thunders.  
We alone become poets,  
Poets and singers,  
We write joyful stories,  
We sing the beauty of the wide road.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Farts long, long farts,  
Our manor’s men are tall.  
Who is pulling the long farts,  
Who is breaking the broad bellows?  
The broad shirts are sizzling,  
It is the little mischievous boy.  
Who burns the rooster?  
Sings until the wide white dawn,  
Spins until mid-morning,  
So that the young maiden.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Mihkel, Mihkel, take me to the forest,  
Carry me on your back to the spruce grove.  
We have many Mihkels:  
Old Mihkel, the resin beard,  
Big Mihkel, the smoky beard,  
Little Mihkel, the milky beard,  
Mihkel-Kihkel, the goat beard.  
If this Mihkel were my brother,  
I would surely know what to do;  
Tie him by the pig’s tail,  
Put a tick on his butt,  
A black bull in the mouth of the pit,  
A gray bull in the mouth of the hole,  
Between the speckled bells.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Our men like rascals,  
Our boys like scoundrels;  
We shoot and cheer,  
Scatter the manor's bread,  
Have fun, play tricks,  
Fight each other,  
Carouse and revel,  
Fire shots at Kaie,  
Pellets at sick Anne,  
Down fallen Mai,  
Finally, booze for Rein.

label: Murelaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I was poor to feed the parish!
To tend to the peasants,
To bully the masters,
To tease the housewives,
To quarrel with the girls,
To mock the boys.
The village told me to come to the village to eat,
The village told me to come to the village to drink,
To set the place right.
I heard and responded:
"He eats a lot, what is fed,
Drinks a lot, what is given to drink:
The greedy one eats more.
When I became older,
I grew stronger:
Then I made the room open to the wind,
Life without limits.
The room was strongly open to the wind,
Life in the world was beautiful indeed."

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Here comes Siim, here Siim’s servant,  
a noble father among all others.  
Here comes the young maiden,  
a beauty with curly hair,  
a spark in her hair,  
a little bird with a linen cap.  
Here sits Siim, here Siim’s servant,  
a noble father among all others.  
Here sits the young maiden,  
a beauty with curly hair,  
a spark in her hair,  
a little bird with a linen cap.  
Here walks Siim, here Siim’s servant,  
a noble father among all others.  
Here walks the young maiden,  
a beauty with curly hair,  
a spark in her hair,  
a little bird with a linen cap.  
Here greets Siim, here Siim’s servant,  
a noble father among all others.  
Silent stands the young maiden,  
a beauty with curly hair,  
a spark in her hair,  
a little bird with a linen cap.  
Siim offers his hand, Siim’s servant’s hand,  
a noble father among all others,  
the young maiden rejects the hand,  
a beauty with curly hair,  
a spark in her hair,  
a little bird with a linen cap.  
Here leaves Siim, here Siim’s servant,  
a noble father among all others.  
Here leaves the young maiden,  
a beauty with curly hair,  
a spark in her hair,  
a little bird with a linen cap.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Row the swing, pull the swing,  
Row the swing to the distant land  
Where the spruces turn yellow,  
The aspen leaves turn green  
Alders stand straight and blue,  
White birches flow, –  
Where the roosters eat gold,  
Roosters gold, hens beware  
Geese are greenish silver.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud laulust  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I come to the barn fox  
To the barley barn squirrel  
To the oat barn chicken  
To the rye barn talker  
To the wheat barn maiden  
No one beats the barn with me  
No one hits the calve without cause  
Liquor is drunk without me  
Bread is eaten secretly  
The barn master was a boy  
He piles up the loads thickly  
Demands rough coughing  
Slapping on top  
I take the rake even from the hand  
The rake from the barn wall  
I am not allowed to sing truthfully  
Not to announce the plough,  
The stable master hears when I sing  
The ditch wakes from the bed  
The lady from her pillows  
The young ladies from the linen  
Drop the bacon from the tables  
Break the carts from the sides  
Smash the glasses from the table  
Who is to blame for spanking  
Who is poured for harm  
I am to blame for spanking  
Poured for the chicken's harm.  
Oh you edge of the ditch,  
Lady of the golden crown  
Young foxes of the apple blossom  
Rise up onto the chair  
Step onto the windowsill  
How you are tormented by the lord  
How the little ones are tormented  
The lesser are twisted  
The stronger are pulled  
If I had my being  
If it were me to do  
I would know what I would do  
I would make the foxes alert  
The stable masters behind like cranes  
The carts on top like gleaming  
The young masters inside like stories  
When we came from our road  
And we were wet  
Where do we dry ourselves  
By the big barn  
Where we put things in the barn oven  
The stable master's bones, the cart's cords  
The barn master's chest bones  
We help both sheds  
Our stable master is a dear gold  
The cart is a spotted little bird  
Assistant is a little low  
Let the children go to the noon meal  
Sisters to the evening meal  
If not, I will curse  
Spill the liquor on your pigs  
Eagles onto the chickens  
Foxes onto the eggs  
Plague on the women and children  
Others eat the second helping  
Home folk the third helping  
Viisu folk the fifth helping  
Koordi folk the sixth helping  
Seidla seven times  
Kaalepi eight times  
Öötla nine times  
Kõisi folk ten times  
We are still without food  
Larks without dinner  
Swallows without supper  
Feathers without size  
We'll have to come to hunger  
The servants, foxes before the stable  
Empty sacks in the wagon  
I saw hunger being beaten  
In front of the barn on the rake  
Running after the open field's end  
Behind the pile to the rowan  
Behind the room to the block  
Dear sister of the household,  
Young maiden, young woman  
Sister of the household I told first  
Cowardly bird I forbade first  
Don't go to the drunkard  
Don't go to the table card player  
Walk to the straw walker.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had to go on a journey,  
Long ago set out on the road.  
I set off, it started to rain,  
Snow fell in heavy drifts,  
Water soaked the yoke straps;  
The sled creaked in the Riga style,  
The runners in the Tartu style,  
The shaft poles in the Pärnu style,  
The harness ropes in the Finnish style,  
The lower ones on top,  
The lower shafts,  
The upper sled poles.  
Don’t go astray from the bright path,  
Home to dry bread,  
To tear off the salt herring,  
To lick the leftover lard,  
To babble like a water frog.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
O king, little king,  
Rich Riga merchant!  
Why did you not come to us last year,  
When the rivers ran without oil,  
The springs flowed with vodka,  
Our cellars brewed beer,  
The city bridges were made of onions,  
The city ramparts of maples?  
Now you come in the time of hardship,  
Now you come in the sad month,  
Now you tear off your clothes,  
Remove the wreath from your head.  
O king, little king,  
Rich Riga merchant!  
Give up the half gold,  
The poor gold, the poor copper,  
That is not yours to have,  
Nor your wife's to have.  
That I myself have obtained:  
I got a buckle from bread sucking,  
A necklace from lime boiling,  
A belt from head shearing,  
In summer from heavy work,  
In winter from stamping flax,  
In spring from spinning.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Little raspberry, little raspberry,  
Did you see my orchard?  
Little maiden, little maiden,  
What color is your horse?  
A squirrel's fur,  
The southern lark's feather,  
Bear's fur, covered with dew.  
It went through our yard,  
On its back a blue blanket,  
Wide straps on the saddle,  
A golden rope trailing after,  
Stirrups with curtains on its feet,  
Golden sparks,  
Copper bands on its head.  
Young men of the village,  
Come to buy in the orchard,  
Come to see the little colt;  
Here the horse has eaten barley,  
Eaten barley, grown on oats,  
Played on the flour mat.  
Upwards, Ali, jump to your ears,  
Jump to your ears on the roof,  
Try, Ali, in the garden,  
Was the garden made by men,  
Or was the garden made by women?  
If the garden was made by men,  
It was made from poles,  
Driven together from the beginnings.  
If the garden was made by women,  
It was made from daughters,  
Laid by mother and children,  
Woven from red twigs.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Pulmalaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Go to the corner, young men,  
To the dark pipe men,  
Lazy men under the table.  
From my brother’s sword trail,  
My brother is coming rolling,  
The blue stocking is coming sewing.  
There is a long reed,  
Longer than my own brother.  
There is white sea foam;  
Whiter than my own brother,  
He threw a pile into the meadow,  
Into the meadow of the flute,  
Who cut his finger yesterday?  
It was that young lad.  
Who jumped to wrap?  
It was that young maiden.  
Where did she get the scarf?  
She took a neck scarf from around the neck,  
Tore apart the chin scarf,  
Shattered the head scarf.  
Where then did she get the ribbon?  
She took a leg ribbon from the calf;  
That ribbon disappeared uselessly,  
The village lad will serve a new one!  
That scarf disappeared uselessly,  
The village lad will buy a new one:  
The Russian boy brings striped,  
The German boy brings velvety,  
The country boy brings mud-patterned,  
The Swedish boy brings checkered,  
Finnish patterns make an appearance,  
Magazine patterns appear,  
Logical patterns appear.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Where does it come from, where does it come from  
Ani ani white ani ani white  
Where does it come from, where does it come from  
Idle, idle lynx  
From the seashore, from the seashore  
Ani ani white  
What are they doing there then  
Idle, idle lynx  
Washing Anipoeg  
Ani ani white  
Is it washing my sons  
Idle, idle lynx  
You weren’t even there  
Ani ani white  
Do you want to come through  
Idle, idle lynx  
And we want to come through  
Ani ani white  
We have golden temples  
Idle, idle lynx  
We have copper gates  
Ani ani white.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a rich godfather  
He gave me a little goat  
A little bell on the goat's neck  
The wolf ate the goat, the bell stayed with me  
I put the bell on the girl’s neck  
I went with the girl to church  
The village girls are smiling  
You are not worthy of smiling like a girl  
Like a young woman worthy of.

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Iiut-tiiut, I bought a plow
With the plow, I bought an axe
With the axe, I took a wife
With the wife, I lived life
With the pipe, I kept the family
With tobacco, I fed the children.

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Iiut-tiiut, I bought a plow
With the plow, I bought an axe
With the axe, I took a wife
With the wife, I lived life
With the pipe, I kept the family
With tobacco, I fed the children.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
But I went on the wedding road  
On Black Matsi's wedding road  
An ox was slaughtered for Mats  
But I was called the cook  
I put the meat to cook  
The duck roast to stew  
Along came the cat with quick eyes  
Ate that meat that was cooking  
That duck roast that was stewing  
I went to catch the cat  
The cat ran into the forest  
I went to find a rope  
From Tõnu I brought a tar rope  
Just as well I brought a flax rope  
From the birch I brought a hemp rope  
Then I went to catch the cat  
The hemp ropes tangled  
The flax rope twisted  
The tar rope pulled it out  
The mice laughed in the corner  
Ast ast ast ast little cat  
Where are my six golden sons?  
They are all in the cat's backside  
In Black Mats's belly.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Liisakene little ribbon  
A little snap upon seeing the eye  
A little hop upon hearing the ear  
An old sneaker in the shadow  
Liisu heard the sound of an instrument  
She went out shining  
The floor beneath was pounding  
Hopping by the boy's ear  
Liisu heard the sound of a rod  
Clinks hitting the ramp  
Immediately then falling into the warehouse  
Clink escaping into the chest.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
In the hollow, a pine cone calls into the stream  
A cone crackles into the bush  
In the grassy rye field  
Come here, young men  
Take me to the sack at home  
Set me on the bench by the stove  
Put me on the board to dry  
From there I roll into the cellar  
But I slip into the half-beer glass  
Drift into the beer mug  
Sink into the beer anchor  
Make me a good beer  
That takes away men's senses  
Half of the senses from the boys' heads  
Thanks to wise women's heads  
Men grumble without hats  
Women dance without cloth  
Girls without belts  
Boys with crooked backs  
One drinks from one hand to the other  
Mothers-in-law, mothers with buckets  
Look, I drink from the pail  
I feed my head through the pipe  
Do I drink from the cupboard, or from the jug  
Or do I drink from the colorful cup  
The cone makes the eyes colorful  
The jug makes the neck stiff.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I bought a horse from Oti  
A hat from the village hunter  
A saddle from Saare’s German  
I rode through the fens, I rode through the islands  
I rode onto the Finnish bridge  
But the Finnish bridge was a cracking road  
The foundation beam was a banging road  
My hat was a lifting road  
Pushed onto the ear.  
A maiden is watching from the shadow  
A crouching flower, a visiting flower  
Through time, a bending flower  
If only this man were mine  
Who is there on the island bridge  
The horse was underneath like the stove  
The man was above like the day  
The hat like a church bell  
The belt like a rain rainbow  
Whose handsome master is this  
Take me, handsome lady.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:

The belt like a rain rainbow
