Take me, handsome lady.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Many have gone to the proud mother  
A few lines to the rich mother  
A couple have gone to their families  
They have not traveled the roads,  
Traveled the roads, wandered  
The tall tree in the forest stands.  
I alone, a poor child  
I serve the road  
Travel the roads, wander  
The tall tree in the forest stands.  
Out in the wide meadow  
So that the road will not take me  
Out across the wide fields;  
The tall tree in the forest speaks.  
Let others travel the road  
Across the wide meadow  
The tall tree in the forest speaks.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I go to the town to the taverns  
And I go to the shore to the huts  
I am not greeted in the town  
Nor given a handshake in the borough.  
Indeed, the townsfolk mock me  
Some are even hostile.  
Is he the king of Kuuramaa  
Or is he truly from Pärnu?  
That he does not greet in the town  
Nor gives a handshake in the borough.  
I certainly hear and respond.  
Oh, you foolish townsfolk  
You do not recognize the farm boy  
Nor respect the humble soul.  
I seek my own way  
Giving my horse reins in my hand  
With the colt tethered behind.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The sprouting grain path was crooked  
The straw woman walked crookedly  
But poured crooked liquor glasses  
Investigation beer tubes  
Slope pots on the slope  
Edge crusts of bread crust.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Droplet droplet little needle  
Droplet needle-eye  
The needle did not disappear in the seat  
My needle disappeared into the needles  
Sewing new humming  
Arranging family things  
In the evening I sew sister’s shirt  
At twilight brother’s shirt  
At dawn sweetheart’s shirt  
In darkness father-in-law’s shirt  
In gloom fiancé’s shirt  
The needle disappeared in the heath  
In the woods  
In the golden-eye spruce forest  
In the field of oats  
Young men brothers  
Some were rich in money  
Some were famous for their purses  
Come to demand the needle  
To listen for the golden-eye  
Raise the needle, jump the needle  
Raise the round needle  
Jump the square needle  
Tie the needle’s legs sharp  
You are my soul needle  
You are the smooth sequin needle  
The needle that sparks and sizzles  
Raise the barley bean  
Jump the bear’s oats  
Bring the mountainous beans  
With a bunch of oats

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We walk through the village  
The horse neighs  
The dusty street rattles  
Even the iron chains creak  
The cuckoo calls from the hollow  
The gray bird from the ridge  
The swallow on the eaves  
The recruit sings on the wagon.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
On Monday, I drank a new coat  
On Tuesday, I drank a work jacket  
On Wednesday, a nice hat  
On Thursday, I drank a maiden’s apron  
On Friday, I drank the gelding’s thighs  
On Saturday, I drank the cow’s bow  
On Sunday, I drank the pants off  
Then I drank only naked  
The cross-bearer was left without clothes.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's walk along the meadows of wildflowers  
Let's walk through the blue groves  
So the golden reeds don't sway  
So the silent stems don't bend  
Not a single milk-cap mushroom  
Who has golden reeds  
Our girls have golden reeds  
Who has silent stems  
Our women have silent stems  
Who has milk-cap mushrooms  
Our men have milk-cap mushrooms.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The bog contaminates the meadow  
From behind it corrupts the power  
Then here a feather  
In between corrupts the poor man  
The awake drives the dormant  
Dear work and wool work  
Three times around  
Estut sestut sem vem  
Whisk whisk madipu mall  
Around the sheath tongue arrow  
Raise the dance bring the maiden  
Quickly stone under the mortar  
Alle sahkadi sah sah  
Piibe painti piale  
Put paint into the glass

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Your maidens are coming  
Your maiden has beaten the lieri  
Your mother in the manor  
What shall I give the maiden?  
There is no gold nor silver  
No old copper coins  
There are no small pennies. -  
There is no gold nor silver  
No old copper coins  
There are no small pennies  
I want a young maiden  
Silk fingers, silk thumbs  
Weavers of strong cloth  
Flappers of canvas  
Papershell crackers  
Who put letters into gloves  
Old eyes into canvas  
Shirt tails into shirts

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Brother vermicelli  
I am going to Narva to bring a bride  
From across Petersburg.  
Don’t go in men’s clothes  
Or with an old threshing hat.  
Under plaster to the cellar  
Step down to the storerooms.  
Take new clothes from the barn  
White shirts from the clothes rack.  
Then go to Narva to bring a bride,  
From across Petersburg,  
Pass through Lihula  
The small tin sound ticks  
The iron cart rumbles,  
The little one sings under the sunset,  
On the other side of the hazel branch,  
The cuckoo behind in the sleigh basket.  
A bunch of girls came before the carriage,  
But the young maiden behind the sleigh.  
Whose handsome boy is that?  
And whose good-looking son  
Who sings under the sunset  
On the other side of the hazel branch.  
The cuckoo behind in the sleigh basket.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Brother vermicelli  
I am going to Narva to bring a bride  
From across Petersburg.  
Don’t go in men’s clothes  
Or with an old threshing hat.  
Under plaster to the cellar  
Step down to the storerooms.  
Take new clothes from the barn  
White shirts from the clothes rack.  
Then go to Narva to bring a bride,  
From across Petersburg,  
Pass through Lihula  
The small tin sound ticks  
The iron cart rumbles,  
The little one sings under the sunset,  
On the other side of the hazel branch,  
The cuckoo behind in the sleigh basket.  
A bunch of girls came before the carriage,  
But the young maiden behind the sleigh.  
Whose handsome boy is that?  
And whose good-looking son  
Who sings under the sunset  
On the other side of the hazel branch.  
The cuckoo behind in the sleigh basket.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The family daughter, a young maiden  
When she hears the sound of an instrument  
She straightens her brow  
And steps outside from the threshing floor.  
Softly, she tiptoes  
Now to dance by the oak tree  
To bend the young spruces  
To sway the apple tree  
To tremble on the rowan tree.  
When I hear the sound of the staff  
Then I walk towards the bed  
Step towards the threshing floor  
There I sleep, my little berry  
My beautiful little flower  
I rest, a little wedding bride.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Why are you girls smiling?  
You frighten me too much:  
That I have a shaky horse,  
A colt from a pea stalk.  
I went along the narrow paths,  
The crooked trails.  
The road began to tick,  
The paths began to crack;  
My tin sleigh was ticking,  
My iron horse was cracking.  
A cuckoo fell on the bay horse,  
A swallow on the head,  
A wren on the crest,  
A gray bird on the shaft.  
Village girls were glancing at me,  
Village maidens were laughing at me!  
Where did that fool buy a horse?  
And that fool took a wife:  
A horse from where I bought mine,  
A wife from where I took mine—  
A horse from Oblika Hill,  
A wife from Nappu mill.  
The horse was from Oblika’s Oblika,  
The wife on the May strawberry.  
The horse brings a bunch of Oblika hay,  
The wife brings a handful of May strawberries.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Go to the corner, young men,
Pipe men under the bench,
Big jaws under the table,
Sow pigs under the bed,
Tat whiskers behind the wall!
My brother is coming to fence,
The sow pig is coming to pry,
The clog is coming to wash,
The twisted sock is coming to roll,
The nice heel is coming to turn!

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh wonder, or no wonder,
What I saw in Näu village:
Dogs were plowing, oxen were barking,
Flails were carrying water,
Carding boards were boiling lye;
Girls were making sledges,
Women were cutting wheel spokes,
Father was making yoke pieces for the ox,
The old wise man was making beams for the storehouse,
Men were walking to the tavern road,
They were whistling over tavern beer.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Be healthy, brewers of beer,  
Brewers of beer and barley beer,  
Makers of angry vodka,  
Who put these three together:  
First, the water is liquid,  
Second, the malt is sweet,  
Third, you are foolish madmen:  
This took the minds from men's heads,  
Half the minds from boys' heads,  
Filled the daughters' bosoms;  
Men made noise without hats,  
Women danced without skirts,  
Boys half drunk.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Reach the boat, row the boat,  
Reach the boat to that shore:  
Where those roosters have eaten gold  
Roosters of gold, hens of silver,  
Ducks of shining copper,  
Crows of old brass,  
Nest birds of pennies,  
Wise birds of thalers,  
Where those cabbages have grown  
Cabbages up to the height of the spruce.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We fell oak forests,  
We cut down pine forests,  
We leave birches growing,  
Birch branches moaning,  
Birch leaves mourning:  
Branches moan for father,  
Leaves mourn for mother,  
Hollows weep for poor children.  
Let us go along this road,  
Along the lowlands,  
Where the path was pointed,  
The land was covered in moss,  
Where slaves had walked before,  
Slaves had walked, oxen had gone,  
Early the poor children had gone,  
Late the household heads.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We fell oak forests,  
We cut down pine forests,  
We leave birches growing,  
Birch branches moaning,  
Birch leaves mourning:  
Branches moan for father,  
Leaves mourn for mother,  
Hollows weep for poor children.  
Let us go along this road,  
Along the lowlands,  
Where the path was pointed,  
The land was covered in moss,  
Where slaves had walked before,  
Slaves had walked, oxen had gone,  
Early the poor children had gone,  
Late the household heads.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A slender little girl  
A young maiden  
Let's go on Saturday for a purple petal  
Onto the top of the hill  
What will we find there on the ground  
Six gilded belts  
A hundred German silk shirts  
Where will we put these gold pieces  
We will sew silver threads  
We'll hump them onto the hat  
We'll paint them onto the gate  
There we'll put the gold  
We will sew silver threads  
Oh oh oh oh brother  
Let it be straight by tomorrow  
By next Friday  
By New Year's Eve evening.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The rye harvesters are coming  
The rye harvester has struck the field  
The old woman's manor's maid  
The old woman's two chambers  
The old woman's scythe blade  
What can I give the harvester?  
There is no gold nor silver  
No copper, old money  
No small coins at all  
No gold nor silver  
No copper, old money  
No small coins at all  
I wish for a young maiden  
Silky fingers, nimble hands  
Hard fabric weavers  
Paper bangers  
Lecture rattlers  
Who put letters in gloves  
Two letters in fabric  
Herring eyes in canvas  
Perch scales in shirts  
Who knows how to walk fire  
How to cross a bitter fleet  
How to lie without sparks

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Where have they put the throat to the floor,  
Where have they put the windpipe coat?  
At every tavern I drank to the floor,  
To Kapu Cross a coat;  
To the big tavern I drank my socks,  
In the cold the shoes remain.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh wonder, oh wonder  
What a wonder I saw  
Between Kuksema village  
In the Varivere pine forest  
In the buildings of Olustvere  
Dogs were plowing, oxen were harrowing  
A sheep went to the table to lay eggs  
A hen brought two lambs  
A horse brought a gentleman calf  
A cow brought a blond chick.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Young men, tender brothers.  
If you are lowly, buy Mustad,  
If you are noble, buy Kõrvid.  
Buy Oller's horses,  
Leeland’s bays and stallions.  
Ten-ruble hats,  
Hundred-ruble saddles.  
Ride to the wedding at Soare manor.  
To that young lady’s wedding,  
Oh, how I turn my eyes,  
With strength in neck and hands.  
"If only I had a horse of my own,  
My own mare, my own saddle,  
I would ride to the wedding at Soare manor,  
To that young lady’s wedding.  
When the lady invites us to the wedding,  
Then I would ride proudly,  
And step out gallantly."

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I pour beers from the jugs  
I throw whips to the little whip  
Cap boards to the little bog  
I rush bottoms to the field  
There grew big islands  
Big islands, wide plains  
A wet pine grove  
On every top a titmouse  
On every end a squirrel  
In every treetop a songbird.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The beer made me warm  
I make the beer hot  
I heat the coats on the heel  
To the little bear a new shoe  
But the beer made me dizzy  
Moving feet in the city  
It took the minds from men's heads  
Half the minds from boys' heads  
At the gaze of the women’s eyes  
At the sight of the daughters’ grace.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, oh, Ots Matsi  
Did you see Kadak Matsi  
Let it come to me as raw meat  
Full of lice flesh  
Bedbug as broth  
Bog as a dry part.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Alled sang, Malled sang  
Girls sang down by the village.  
Dear ones killed me  
On the cobblestones.  
Where then did they bury the young maiden  
Under the berry mound in the bog?  
From the maiden grew a dear birch —  
What will be made from that birch?  
They speak of the violin  
Where one can play the kannel (Estonian zither)  
And blow the beautiful instrument?  
Let’s take the kannel to the chamber  
Where can the kannel get its curved sides?  
From the jawbone of a salmon.  
Where can the kannel get its curved strings?  
From the hair of the young maiden.  
Let's try with fingertips  
The hammering of the iron striker,  
Beat with your own stick,  
To put the young maiden to sleep,  
To lull her into a new life.  
The fingerboards whispered,  
The soundboards rumbled,  
Such a child cannot be made —  
A calm soul is needed.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
We go up to the hill by the meadow  
Among the blue feathers,  
We go shoulder to shoulder - marching ear to ear  
We go shoulder to shoulder towards Riga  
Towards Talli town in the back  
We go to Riga to rob  
To kill in Talli town  
To burn Põltsamaa  
The pig ruins Riga city  
The sheep kills Talli town  
The piglet burns Põltsamaa.  
We will leave salt to bring to Riga  
Money to bang in Talli town  
To bring a comb to Anne  
Ear rings to Krõeda  
Ribbons to Liisu  
Boys to bring tobacco  
Daughters to bring blue stones.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Curved one, sexton’s son  
Virve-colored Westerner  
Where do I lay the curved one  
Curved one, sexton’s son  
Virve-colored Westerner?  
Behind the garden fence  
In the furrow of tall stitches  
Among wide leaves  
There he stands unlaid to rest  
Untouched by a dear hand  
Unfelt by a young finger  
Where shall we put the curved one to sleep?  
Right by the stove. In a woolen basket  
A fine boy at the ear,  
Wearing a multicolored jacket.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Rise up, old man  
Wash your eyes, old man  
Pants on, old man  
Vest on, old man  
Feet together, old man  
Warm head, old man  
Hat on head, old man  
Scarf on neck, old man  
Sweater on, old man  
Overcoat on, old man  
Belt on waist, old man  
Pipe in mouth, old man  
Gloves on hands, old man  
Seek your old man  
Seek your careful one  
Touch your furry one.  
What is born from your spit  
Fits into a berry basket  
What is better from your mind  
Kinder from your heart  
How your companion.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Bloom, piggy pig  
Golden-haired little golden one  
Iron-haired little harnessed one.  
Go to the mountain to root around  
Root the mountains, root the pines.  
Root the spruces on the hills  
Old saunas on the backs  
Bundles on the behinds  
Come, come young men.  
Come to slaughter the pig  
To stick the hooks in the throat  
To hold the kilts tight  
Brothers to take blood  
Sisters to prepare the salt  
Dogs to do the trick  
Bring these pitchforks to the feast  
Old bones only.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Row the boat, reach the boat  
Row the boat over there.  
Where those roosters eat gold,  
Roosters eat gold, hens peck.  
Geese of shiny green color,  
Delicate birds with money,  
Crows with old money.  
Ship’s captain, little boy,  
Come row the boat with me,  
So it doesn’t scrape on the stone,  
Roll into the water streams,  
Don’t pour on the stone ridge,  
Let silk boats run inside,  
Let Kalev’s ships sail.  
My dear old woman,  
I came to ask for a pot,  
I had a lot of rye,  
Two were roaches in the bathhouse,  
Three were herrings in the towel,  
Roaches want to be boiled,  
Goose meat wants to be stewed,  
Duck meat wants to be roasted.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
Sisters, dear ones  
Where are we going toward the night  
Toward the night, toward the day  
Let’s go listen to the moon  
Let’s play all day  
Let’s touch the gentle evening  
Does the moon serve without oil,  
The day contemplates pleasure  
The evening recites a hymn.  
No, the moon does not serve without oil  
The day does not contemplate pleasure  
The evening does not recite a hymn  
The day plays with necklaces  
Plays the sieve with rings  
The evening with glass beads  
The moon with golden rings  
I had three suitors:  
One was the moon, the second the day  
The third was full of stars  
I will not go to the moon,  
Nor go to the day  
The moon has three duties:  
Sometimes it rises early  
Once it rises at dawn's gold  
Once in the middle of dawn  
Let the starry one come  
With horses full of stars  
With clothes full of stars  
The star softly shakes  
Rings with golden sparkle  
Bring my own tub  
Drive me to Salme’s manor.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A young cook came to us  
We have many maidens  
Who is this dear one turning around  
Who is this beloved jumping on the neck  
Dear Liisa turning around  
Beloved Liisa jumping on the neck  
Take silk and velvet in your arms.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
It was beautiful to live in Tapa,  
The flower was under the roof,  
The flower was laid under the hay,  
The roof was over the grain stack.  
The village smells of field flowers,  
The village fields of hay flowers,  
The village yards with apple trees,  
The village houses with rowan trees,  
The village fences with rowan trees,  
Time hedges with motherwort.  
When I ride through the village,  
Through new streets.  
Through the copper gate,  
Through the roofed street,  
A horse with a tinkling bell,  
Clattered down the dusty street,  
The wheels creaked on the wooden shafts.  
A hen fell on the beam,  
A gray bird on the threshold,  
A flicker on the bench,  
A hat on the roof.

label: Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
It was beautiful to live in Tapa,  
The flower was under the roof,  
The flower was laid under the hay,  
The roof was over the grain stack.  
The village smells of field flowers,  
The village fields of hay flowers,  
The village yards with apple trees,  
The village houses with rowan trees,  
The village fences with rowan trees,  
Time hedges with motherwort.  
When I ride through the village,  
Through new streets.  
Through the copper gate,  
Through the roofed street,  
A horse with a tinkling bell,  
Clattered down the dusty street,  
The wheels creaked on the wooden shafts.  
A hen fell on the beam,  
A gray bird on the threshold,  
A flicker on the bench,  
A hat on the roof.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Lace ribbon of wild strawberry  
Bright twig of hayberry  
Who cut the finger yesterday?  
It was Anne the maiden who cut it  
Who jumped to the bouquet?  
It was the suitor boy  
Where did he get the daffodils?  
He brought the daffodils from the town of Narva  
He brought the ribbons from the town of Paide  
Anne tied it tight around her finger  
The girl wrapped it tight around her finger  
Oh, Anne herself  
Oh, carefully herself  
Protect with dear worry.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Flax, dye, the road was crooked,
The straw woman walked crookedly.
Aadu thought things were crooked,
Muru Mikk otherwise walked crookedly.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I rode, I rode, little brother  
I rode over the Finnish bridge  
The Finnish bridge creaking  
The foundation beam cracking  
I looked at the Finnish girls  
Under the fence, above the mound  
If only those men were mine  
A horse in front like a stove  
Men above like the sun  
A belt on the waist like a rainbow  
A hat like a church bell.  
(This goes to men's senses  
To boys' white heads  
To the maiden's tangled hair.)

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I rode, I rode, little brother  
I rode over the Finnish bridge  
The Finnish bridge creaking  
The foundation beam cracking  
I looked at the Finnish girls  
Under the fence, above the mound  
If only those men were mine  
A horse in front like a stove  
Men above like the sun  
A belt on the waist like a rainbow  
A hat like a church bell.  
(This goes to men's senses  
To boys' white heads  
To the maiden's tangled hair.)

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Let's go men  
Let's light the pipes  
All the village ovens are burning  
The sauna ovens are smoking  
When I start to go  
Then sorrow begins to come  
Tears roll from my eyes  
I feel sorrow for Anne  
Sorrow for Anne's children  
How am I supposed to go home  
Answer the wall's watching  
Home number one – all angry  
Home number six – all were angry  
Home number seven – all confused  
Home number eight – all jealous  
Home number nine – all superiors  
Home number ten – all were cold  
These come upon me  
Lower ones like whip lashes  
Higher ones like linden bark  
Grow and fall upon my eyes.  
Let the sky's year arrive a hundred times  
Let the rain's year pour a hundred times  
Still I must go  
I don't care about the proud  
I don't heed the tall ones  
No, I don't boast with pride  
I tear apart the rich  
I am cursed by those under dung  
Slandered by gossipers  
Keeper of tavern benches  
Wanderer of tavern doors  
Mover of tavern chains  
I was not a howler at doors  
Nor a mover of chains  
Nor keeper of tavern benches  
Nor a sleeper of gossips.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud laulust
translation:
Let's start to go  
To our home far away  
Five was the time to exchange  
Six was the drying of the rivers  
Seven was the strong soda  
Eight was the fish basket  
Nine was the good river  
Ten was the cold spring.  
Let's go singing through the forest  
Whose voice calls well here  
That groom will buy a hat.  
Ring, ring little voice  
Tinkle, tinkle little bell  
Sing back little forest  
I have a good little voice.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Our two peasants,  
Let's leave this land!  
Let's go to Finland for the summer,  
For the winter behind Russia.  
We will build a stone church there,  
Carry a corral chapel,  
A long rowan tower,  
A wide tower lantern.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Here, here comes Siim,  
Here comes Siim’s servant,  
A noble master  
Among all the others.  
Let us through!  
You cannot let us through,  
Our bars are broken.  
Fix them!  
With what shall we fix them?  
With silk, with thread,  
With old felt yarn.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
King, little king!  
Why didn’t you come to us last year,  
When the rivers ran without oil,  
And the wells were sore to embrace!  
Now you have come at a bad time,  
In the harsh autumn rain,  
In the light spring rain.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A squirrel scurries up the mountain  
A cone rattles in the pinecone  
Bring the young men here  
Take me home with a sack  
Carry me to the hill meadow  
Put me on the edge of the plank  
From there I will roll to the river  
I slip into the beer half-pint  
From the river, to buzz  
To rise on the beer's edge  
Then I take the minds of men by the head  
The eyes of women before them  
Half the minds from the boys’ heads  
Then men would tip their hats noisily  
Women danced without a dance  
Boys with one boot on  
Girls with wreaths on their heads.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
At the mound  
At the grave  
At the ant hill  
At the willow  
At Kostivere  
On the sand bank  
Down  
Bog  
Then Totti came home

label: Laulud laulust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I sing what is not sung  
I sing of a little barn  
Behind the barn a little stable  
Into the stable a ladder  
Into the ladder a foal  
To the foal a saddle  
To the saddle a German boy  
To the German boy a hat  
On the hat a golden crown  
On the golden crown a knitter  
To the knitter a golden sword  
To the golden sword a maker  
To the maker a partner  
I sing what is not sung  
I sing a song about a horse  
Its head chiseled with a nut  
Eyes from stars in the sky  
Ears from cabbage leaves  
Body from hay  
Legs from pitchfork tines  
Horseshoes from whip branches  
Tail tied behind  
Then I will go to Finland trip  
Knock on the Finland bridge  
Knock on the under-beams

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh king, little king!  
Rich lord of Riga;  
True boy of Pärnu.  
Could the king have come last year,  
If the rivers ran without oil? –  
The springs ran with vodka –  
The village flocks in the courtyard  
The field edges withered,  
Now came the time of peace.  
Now I strip even my chest,  
From the top of my head a wreath,  
Oh king, little king,  
Rich lord of Riga,  
True boy of Pärnu.  
Give me poor gold,  
Daughter’s tin silver.  
That is not what you received,  
That is what I myself obtained.  
That is brought by my fine groom,  
That is brought by my dear wife. –  
The master brought me a pin,  
The mistress brought me a pendant;  
I bought a bead string myself,  
My wife brought a necklace,  
Got a brooch from bread kneading,  
A pin from cleaning the master’s knee,  
Beads from hay mowing too;  
The necklace from bending the bow.  
Oh king, little king,  
Give me poor gold.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
"Oh king, little king,  
Rich lord of Riga,  
A true boy from Pärnu,  
The king might have come last year:  
When rivers ran without oil,  
Springs flowed with vodka,  
Village hills were barren,  
Outskirts were desolate—  
Now you have come at a bad time."  
"Now I strip off my chest girdle,  
From my head I take the crown."  
"Oh king, little king,  
Rich lord of Riga,  
A true boy from Pärnu,  
Give me some poor gold,  
Copper and silver:  
They are not yours to give,  
They were mine by right,  
Brought by my fine bridegroom,  
Brought by my dear wife.  
The master brought me a bracelet,  
The mistress a brooch,  
I bought a bead,  
My spouse brought a necklace.  
I got a clasp from baking bread,  
A bracelet from threshing grain,  
Beads from hay mowing as well,  
A necklace from twisting the hoop.  
Oh king, little king,  
Give me some poor gold."

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The hop drifts to the mountain valley,  
The cone crackles in the oven’s hearth:  
Come here, young men,  
Take me to the barrel’s home,  
Carry me to the cellar house,  
Put me on the edge of the board,  
From there I roll into the cask,  
I slip into the half-pint of beer,  
I make the cask whistle,  
The beers rise on the straw -  
Then I take the senses from the heads of men,  
The drifts go to the ends of women,  
Half of the senses from the heads of boys;  
Then the men shout without hats,  
The women danced without a tanuta*,  
Boys in half-boots,  
Girls with wreaths on their heads.  

*Note: "tanuta" is an Estonian folk dance or festive term that has no direct English equivalent.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
You drink the throat, carry the neck  
Accept the copper breast  
Surely the legs will take you home  
The legs lead to the bed  
The knees carry the straw  
The toe lightly lifting the mat.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The flea went to town with a scrap  
The louse at full pace  
I for a daily drink  
Ann was at the kettle's neck  
Sits on the barn roof  
Leaning against the oat stalk  
Whether he put a crown on his head  
Lost his throat coat  
To Vaide tavern’s hook  
To Kargu tavern’s room  
To Lahu tavern’s table  
Oh little throat, what a glow.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Let's go along the made roads  
Along the carved paths  
Where slaves have walked before  
Slaves have walked, oxen have gone  
Slaves have walked until evening  
Spirit children until dawn  
May evil take the German eyes  
May both hands rot  
Who placed the slaves in the wandering hut  
In the moonlight, the moon hut  
In the moonlight, the dawn light  
In the dawn light, hay was struck  
Sheep were secretly driven.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:

Along the carved paths

Slaves have walked until evening

May both hands rot  
Who placed the slaves in the wandering hut  
In the moonlight, the moon hut  
In the moonlight, the dawn light  
In the dawn light, hay was struck  
Sheep were secretly driven.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The malt moth calls in the garden  
The murder cone on the fence  
Bring young men to the fire today  
Take me home to the hut  
Put me on the edge of the raft  
There I will lie as a swaddled child  
I will make the fences rise  
Drive the stocks over  
I will take the senses from men's heads  
Half of the senses from boys' heads  
Men shout without hats  
Women dance to the drum  
Girls wear wreaths on their heads.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Dear little sisters,  
Where are we going towards the night?  
Towards the night, towards the moon.  
Towards the clearing to build,  
Towards the darkness to look,  
Towards the dawn to warm,  
We go to listen to the moon,  
To watch the stars,  
Has the moon already made oil,  
Has the star rolled the arrow?  
The day is thinking about resting.  
The moon has not yet made oil,  
The star is not rolling the arrow,  
The day is not thinking about resting.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Dear little sisters  
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk  
Let's go along this path  
This path full of tops  
Full of moss and liverwort  
Where once slaves have walked  
Slaves have walked, oxen have walked  
Poor children walked early  
Lords wandered late  
They did not make quarrels with brooms  
Quarrels with brooms are bitter on land  
No knocking in the high forest  
We two poor children  
Sister, dear maiden  
We make quarrels with brooms  
Quarrels with brooms are bitter on the land  
Knocking in the high forest  
Wandering widely in the open field  
The high forests do not speak  
The wide field does not blame us.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I bought a horse from Oti,  
A hat from the village hunter,  
A saddle from the Saare merchant,  
I rode to Finland, I rode to the islands,  
I rode onto the Finnish bridge,  
But the Finnish bridge is a rickety road,  
The main beam is a creaky road,  
My hat is a light road,  
But it cuts over the ear,  
A maiden watches from behind,  
A hop step and a skip step,  
A crooked step through time,  
If only that man were mine,  
Who is on the island bridge,  
There was a horse beneath him like a stove,  
The man was above like the day,  
A hat like a church bell,  
A belt like a rain rainbow,  
Whose is this handsome lord,  
Take me as a handsome lord.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I bought a horse from Oti,  
A hat from the village hunter,  
A saddle from the Saare merchant,  
I rode to Finland, I rode to the islands,  
I rode onto the Finnish bridge,  
But the Finnish bridge is a rickety road,  
The main beam is a creaky road,  
My hat is a light road,  
But it cuts over the ear,  
A maiden watches from behind,  
A hop step and a skip step,  
A crooked step through time,  
If only that man were mine,  
Who is on the island bridge,  
There was a horse beneath him like a stove,  
The man was above like the day,  
A hat like a church bell,  
A belt like a rain rainbow,  
Whose is this handsome lord,  
Take me as a handsome lord.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The horse runs because of the reins  
Beats its head because of the whip  
Jumps because of the oat bran  
Steps because of the bridle.  
Then the horse trots home  
Under the fur blanket.  
When the drunkard comes near  
The straw walker to the ear.  
Stop, stop lifting.  
The family daughter, a maiden  
The farm daughter, a young girl  
You bring the striped blanket  
The woolen, patterned coverlet  
Wipe my horse from mist  
Wipe off the many splashes  
From the wide-brimmed, wooden board...

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let’s go adorn the doll  
Make a poor child.  
The cuckoo fastening gold  
Dress the doll, dress the doll  
Dress the doll, dress the doll  
Dress them with the decorations,  
What the mother had before  
The old mother made.  
Put on duck feathers  
Add bird feathers on top  
Wild man feathers from the forest.  
Wash the eyes with warm water  
Dress it in linen.  
Put a thin cloth on top,  
Put on feathered stockings  
On the feet, complicated shoes  
Put around a large ring  
Like half a reindeer skin  
Put a large belt on the waist  
Like a wide tar whip  
Put a wide apron in front  
Like a father’s barn door  
Put a large brooch on the chest  
Like the moon in the sky  
Grandma’s necklace  
Put a wide wreath on the head  
Decorate the fingers with rings  
Between the fingers with a bridge  
Make the calves run  
Make the soles move  
Make the spokes rattle.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I rode, I rode, little brother  
I rode through a piece of Turkey  
A little bit of Narva land,  
Half the world of Pohlamaa.  
I had never seen the wonder  
That I saw at the Nässu household,  
Bulls were plowing, dogs were plowing,  
Girls were making sleds,  
Women were cutting wheelwood,  
Father was chopping oxwood,  
Old wise man was needful wood,  
They knocked on spoke wood,  
Men plowed the road with straws,  
Vodka jugs in the sack,  
Beer mugs on the bench,  
They were reveling over beer in the tavern.  
They sang on the straw table.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
To the valley in the evening
Water like dew on the field
Come on
When the three of us get together
The three will come together
The four will sing as one.
On this great Christmas holiday.
Then the three of us get together
The three will come together
The four will sing as one.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Go away west, I will stay here  
Under this evil barn  
Under this lowly German  
Where we will meet again  
Up in the high sky city  
In the paradise meadow  
There I pick silk flowers  
To give to my sweetheart

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I had a rich godmother  
She gave me a little goat  
A little bell for the goat's neck,  
A little bell for the goat's neck.  
But a wolf took that goat away  
I put the bell on the dog's neck  
The dog was barking, the bell was ringing,  
The dog was barking, the bell was ringing.  
But the wolf took that dog away  
I put the bell on the wife's neck  
The wife was loud and sleepy  
The bell was loud and banging  
Come now, come now, young maiden  
Come now dance with me  
Come now, come now, the bagpipe is playing  
So does my heart then  
What harm is there in living  
To enjoy the bachelor’s days  
The rooster went to the barn to eat the grain  
The hen went into the sack to rest  
The elk went into the forest to cut hay  
The cow went to fetch feed  
Is this pipe following the foot  
Or is this man following the heart

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
A lot of noise from the knife-bird  
A lot of noise behind the door  
A lot of gold for the evil lord  
A lot of sticks for the rogue’s back  
A lot of lashes for the watchman  
A lot of pain for the poor people  
A lot of work for the young boys  
A lot of laziness in the large hall  
A lot of farting on the household rack  
A lot of money at the end of the chamber.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Beer is drunk indoors,  
The second ferments in the chamber,  
The third sleeps in the grave,  
The fourth swings in the town,  
The fifth is taken to the water,  
The sixth wavers on the stalk,  
The seventh’s seed is in the ground.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Little women, little moths,  
Do not drink vodka,  
Vodka makes you go crooked.  
Do not drink beer,  
Beer makes you cheer.  
Do not drink kvass,  
Kvass plays a cruel joke,  
Drink water and clean water,  
Water brings joy and delight,  
I cheer for other beer,  
There is nothing in the house,  
The family eats cow parsnip roots,  
Slaves eat pine needle hay,  
Children eat pine tops.  
Who is nibbling the roots of the bladder campion,  
Gnawing the roots of hunger grass,  
Long piper, red heather,  
Nettle of needle grass,  
Strong fire grass,  
Mighty aquatic willow herb. -

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Where did the little boy put  
The spinning top made of hornbeam  
My son is going to dance  
On Vikkelsukka’s rolling cottage  
On the stilt-legged cottage

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let us go  
To our distant home  
Distant and high  
Five are the fields in between  
Six are the drying rivers  
Seven hundred are the tangled ones  
Perhaps I, too, into the beautiful shores  
Sink between the tussocks  
The earth is not seeking the sister  
Nor digging out the brother  
Whoever digs me out from the shores  
Is searching between the tussocks  
Kadrina church is crooked  
Madise church is low  
Jaani church is slender  
Koeru church has a golden ridge  
Peetri church has a tall tower  
From there the sister says she is searching  
Digging out the brother  
He who digs me out from the shores  
Is searching between the tussocks

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I was at Uba's place,
I went to Käbi's village,
I did not see the wonder
That I saw in my own village:
Dogs were plowing, oxen were barking,
The woman was scouring the laughing field
And the pig was rooting in the smithy,
The sheep went to the table to lay eggs,
The hen laid two in the barn for her,
The horse brought the bull-calf,
The cow brought a lame calf.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The crooked road was crooked,  
The tavern woman walked crooked,  
Poured crooked wine barrels,  
Beer bottles in disorder,  
Rock jugs on the slope.  
The tavern was taken, the dogs were taken,  
No drinking without money,  
No kopik to anyone,  
No beer without waiting,  
No kvass without carrying.  
Tavern lady, oh grain talk!  
With willow bark reins on her head.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh, poor Tõnu of the herd
I always received harsh words.
The wolf ate many sheep
And the goats from Kiviääre. -
My father-in-law was very rich,
My mother-in-law was very rich.
I was once rich from the cross,
And even richer from the godfather,
He took me to Riga for baptism
And to give me a name in Tallinn.
He gave me a little goat,
A bell for the goat's neck.
The wolf ate the goat—the bell remained with me.
I put the bell around the woman’s neck,
The woman dozed off, the bell jingled.
The woman was a heavy sleeper,
The bell made an even louder noise.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I went to the swamp for the harebell
To the river for the cloudberries,
Found many ducks.
I took the ducks to Paide,
Got a lot of money from Paide.
Bought a small musical instrument from Paide.
Started to come home.
My mother heard the sound of the instrument:
My mother began to knock on the door.
My sister heard the sound of the instrument:
My sister began to draw water.
My brother heard the sound of the instrument:
My brother began to fetch salt.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Little Mikukene,  
Give those horns to me!  
I will take the horns to the blacksmith,  
The blacksmith will make a fire-hat for me. –  
I will get new bast shoes,  
And I will put red ribbons on them,  
Inside, I will put silk laces  
And large glossy tassels made of fat.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
Because my brother was a little weakling  
A little finger's big one  
He made a room open to the wind  
A sauna on the edge of the island  
On a purple flag  
Half a tower made of lingonberry leaves  
How those bulls  
Work well for him  
Both pull the furrow  
He plows and cultivates  
He carries the yoke on his back  
The white head trembles.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
Because my brother was a little weakling  
A little finger's big one  
He made a room open to the wind  
A sauna on the edge of the island  
On a purple flag  
Half a tower made of lingonberry leaves  
How those bulls  
Work well for him  
Both pull the furrow  
He plows and cultivates  
He carries the yoke on his back  
The white head trembles.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Oh wonder oh wonder  
What a wonder I saw  
Dogs plowed oxen's ears  
A sheep went to the board to lay eggs  
A hen brought two lambs  
A horse brought a gentleman calf  
A cow brought a clumsy little chicken

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I am a man, son of a sailor  
Dear man, son of a fisherman  
I push the other man into the sea  
I chase away the tar-worms  
But I go up to the island in the sea  
I build a little sauna there  
A poor child’s shelter  
Where wooden ships do pass  
Every Friday, ships from Riga  
Every month, royal ships  
Every day, ships from Pärnu  
Already those Germans are wondering  
The lords are amazed  
The ladies are whispering  
Whose building could this be?  
I certainly hear, I answer back:  
This is my little sauna  
A poor child’s shelter  
The floor is made of reindeer knee bone  
The walls are reindeer shin bone  
The ceiling is of down feathers  
The roof is eggshell  
The eaves are pork fat.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I am a man, son of a sailor  
Dear man, son of a fisherman  
I push the other man into the sea  
I chase away the tar-worms  
But I go up to the island in the sea  
I build a little sauna there  
A poor child’s shelter  
Where wooden ships do pass  
Every Friday, ships from Riga  
Every month, royal ships  
Every day, ships from Pärnu  
Already those Germans are wondering  
The lords are amazed  
The ladies are whispering  
Whose building could this be?  
I certainly hear, I answer back:  
This is my little sauna  
A poor child’s shelter  
The floor is made of reindeer knee bone  
The walls are reindeer shin bone  
The ceiling is of down feathers  
The roof is eggshell  
The eaves are pork fat.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Kõrsinaene Krõõdukene  
Linnanaene Liisukene  
My wife Maretukene  
Kõrsinaene’s walk was crooked  
She spilled crooked brandy  
And beer jugs on her necklace  
Kõrsi lady and Kõrsi gentleman  
Keep your beautiful daughter  
Protect your lovely daughter  
There are many people in Kõrsi  
Butchers, blacksmiths  
Citizens of both kinds,  
And some other landlords.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
How should I begin
Begin to learn
In a strange forest, in a dreadful forest
In a strange pasture, in a cunning one
In a strange fodder field, in a deep one
In a strange farmland, in a dark one
Remember your own forests
Pastures are with you
Fodder fields are in your lap
Farmlands are in drought
Let's go together towards Riga
Quietly towards Tallinn
Let's go to Rome to get rich
To Tallinn to slaughter
To take Pärnu captive
May Riga remain unharmed
Tallinn unravaged
For daughters to bring blue stone
For women flax bloom.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The master went to town  
To the town's loan agent  
The boys advised to come there  
They took their vodka glasses there  
There they hooked their own desires  
Tiina said: listen Juru  
Our bed is quite broken  
Juru heard Tiina’s voice  
Fell on the edge of the soup kettle  
Tiina on top of the soup kettle  
Juru went shouting towards the tavern  
Talked against the tavern keeper  
Cheered with a beer glass  
Boasted about bringing half a measure  
Oh, how miserable is this story  
That the proud Uustalu family  
Keeps going along Reinu’s barn  
Travels along Reinu’s walls  
Steps inside Reinu’s door  
Hello my father-in-law and hello my mother-in-law  
Hello to my other half as well  
Tiina went to get a chair from the room  
There is no time to sit  
The horse stayed waiting for luck  
The leafy meadow stayed rattling  
I have a beautiful haytime  
Dear the time of the swallow’s nest  
Tiina mows in Oodu forest  
Her white head wide above  
Mihkel of Mihkli is right behind her  
Juru heard Tiina’s voice well  
Fell on the edge of the soup kettle  
Tiina on top of the soup kettle.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
I drove across the Finnish bridge  
The Finnish bridge cracked  
The foundation beam thudded  
I drove up to my father-in-law’s door  
Hello, hello little father-in-law  
Is the home yours, young ladies?  
No, the home is not ours, young ladies  
The girl went to the well to fetch water. -  
Are you a suitor?  
No, I am not a suitor  
I am a poor traveler  
Handling German business  
Confirming the lady’s letter.  
Why is your horse shod,  
Why is the horse's tail tied?  
I have three brothers at home  
One shoes horses  
Another ties tails  
The third writes in the garden.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The crooked-crooked road was crooked  
The innkeeper’s wife walked crookedly  
She poured crookedly wine and brandy  
And beer jugs for the customer.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I am a man, a sailor’s son,  
Dear man, a fisherman’s son!  
I pushed the other man into the sea,  
I drove the tar barrels after him:  
Then I went onto the big sea  
And made a little sauna there,  
A poor child’s shelter.  
The walls are made of reindeer hemlock,  
The roof is made of chicken eggs,  
The rows are made of pork meat,  
Where the warships sail,  
Every Friday the Riga ships,  
Every month the king’s ships,  
Every day the Pärnu ships.  
Then those Germans pondered,  
The lords marveled,  
The ladies whispered,  
Whose building could this be!  
I heard and answered:  
This, however, is my little sauna,  
A poor child’s shelter.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I am a man, a sailor’s son,

I pushed the other man into the sea,  
I drove the tar barrels after him:  
Then I went onto the big sea

The walls are made of reindeer hemlock,

This, however, is my little sauna,  
A poor child’s shelter.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
When I walk through the village,
What shines is the horse shining,
The street thunders with pounding,
Golden nails clink in the horseshoe.
The cuckoo calls from the roof,
Another from another fence,
The third from the barn,
A pigeon from the barn roof,
A songbird from the hayloft roof.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go sail the tavern sail,
Let's go sail the tavern! says Pontsius.
What then, let's drink it away.
What then, let's drink it away? says Pilatus.
Our old sheepskin coats,
Our old sheepskin coats! says Pontsius.
A cold, cold winter is coming.
A cold winter is coming? says Pilatus.
We have warm, warm rooms visiting us,
We have warm rooms visiting us! says Pontsius,
Who will take us there,
Who will take us there? says Pilatus.
We'll go for a spree,
We'll go for a spree! says Pontsius.
What will we eat there,
What will we eat there? says Pilatus.
There are plenty of geese in the village,
There are plenty of geese in the village! says Pontsius.
Who will take us to heaven,
Who will take us to heaven? says Pilatus.
We'll go willingly,
We'll go willingly! says Pontsius.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The reapers come here,  
What I give to the reapers  
Is neither gold nor silver,  
I want a young maiden,  
Who carves inscriptions on stone,  
Who makes marks on the mound,  
Carvings on the garden stakes.

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Tiidu, I found a small mare  
With the mare I bought a horse  
With the horse I took a wife,  
With the wife I lived my life  
With a pipe I kept a family  
With tobacco I fed the children  
There was not a crumb of bread.

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Tiidu, I found a small mare  
With the mare I bought a horse  
With the horse I took a wife,  
With the wife I lived my life  
With a pipe I kept a family  
With tobacco I fed the children  
There was not a crumb of bread.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
My brothers, when they were young lads  
Cousins, when they were youngsters  
Male cousins, full-grown men  
They made a room open to the wind  
By the shore of Saaremaa  
From there they sailed past Muhu  
Every day the gentlemen of Pärnu  
Every month the royal ladies  
Every Friday the Germans of Riga.  
Every Saturday to Laupa  
A young maiden  
From her comes a question from me  
Is there a town or is there a ship  
Is there a town’s flag ship  
Or are there Pärnu’s decorated ships  
Or are there Muhu’s hat ships  
Tallinn’s deck ships.  
But I understand and answer back.  
It is neither a town nor a ship  
It is neither a town’s flag ship  
Nor is it Pärnu’s decorated ship  
Nor is it Muhu’s hat ship  
Nor Tallinn’s deck ship.  
It was my brother’s sauna  
Inside the walls were covered with silk  
On top, the roof was covered with boards.  
The stove was made of perch  
The hearth was made of salmon jaws.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
When will we get our pasture
Where those spruces turn yellow
John's graves flourish green,
Straight alders turn blue
White birches drip
Like these trees' bull horns
Silver ox horns
Linen cow horns
Copper calf horns.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
In the swamp by the winding stream,  
The cone croaked on the shore.  
Come here, young man,  
Take me home with a bag.  
Put me on the edge of the barrel,  
From there I will spark the tinder.  
I creep halfway into the barrel,  
Taking the senses of men's heads,  
Half of the senses of boys' heads.  
I will make the men in the garden shout without hats,  
The women dance without skirts,  
The boys half-naked,  
The girls naked.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I was a young man before
The crotch was in front of my pants
I saw berries growing
Blue legs straightening
I will start eating roach fish
I rode onto the Finnish bridge
To the Finnish bridge's edge
The base beams cracked
Where has it come here
It has come from Finland
Or maybe from the Russian side

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I was a young man before
The crotch was in front of my pants
I saw berries growing
Blue legs straightening
I will start eating roach fish
I rode onto the Finnish bridge
To the Finnish bridge's edge
The base beams cracked
Where has it come here
It has come from Finland
Or maybe from the Russian side

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's start going  
Let's light the pipes  
Let's fire up all the village ovens  
A hundred saunas smoking  
A thousand rooms dusty  
Let's start going  
To our home far away  
Five was for exchanging fields  
Six was to dry the rivers  
Seven for tough sod  
Eight for fishnets  
Nine good rivers

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Daughter-in-law created a lazy one  
The created lazy one was called lazy  
She did not bother to fetch water  
Water for the maiden’s eyes  
Porridge water for the family  
She sent Aas to fetch water for the cat  
The cat went into the water and splashed  
Its tail was flicking  
I will go to the village to bring ropes  
From Nigul, I bring a flax rope  
From Tõnu Jüri, a tarred rope  
From Kandel, a hemp rope  
I will start pulling the cat  
The hemp ropes broke  
The flax ropes snapped  
The tarred rope pulled it out  
The cat was put into the cage  
Then there was regret for the new cage  
More regret for the cage’s water  
But the mouse watches from the corner  
Good, good, good, good little cat  
Where did you put my spruce’s seedlings?  
Seven good comrades  
Eight elder brothers  
Nine good fathers  
Ten young men from the village  
All were in the cat’s backside.  
Into the stomach of the black marten  
What of that cat to us  
From the side got ten  
From the tail got tail straps  
From the nose the girl got shoes  
From the head little patches

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Where we go towards the night  
Towards the night, towards the north  
Towards the barren wilderness  
Towards the difficult dawn  
Our home far away  
The distance was five versts  
Six to the dry rivers  
Seven to the stubborn marshes  
Eight to the fishing grounds  
Nine to the good rivers  
Ten to the cold springs  
When will we get there  
We will get there, we will stay there  
Where the spruces bloom yellow  
The aspen trees turn green  
The straight alders turn blue  
The birches grow apples  
I will cut down the wet pines  
The wet pines, the dry spruces  
I will leave the birches growing  
The birch branches will moan  
The branches moan to the father  
The leaves mourn the mother

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Let's go, let's go, let's walk, let's walk  
Let's go along this road  
Where the road was covered in moss  
The ground was hairy like a liverwort  
Where oxen had walked before  
Oxen had walked, slaves had gone  
The poor children had gone early  
The servants did so later.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
What goes through the village  
Through the market street  
Shines a man, shines a horse  
Shines a shining horse  
The market street claps  
There were golden nails on the log  
The log has a bony surface and is wooden  
The log nails are made of alder wood

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
In the hops calls the marten  
The cone cracks on the shore  
Come, youths, to pick  
To weave the blue aprons  
Take me home in a sack  
Hang me on the rafter to dry  
From there I roll in a bundle  
But slip into the beer mug  
I hide between the twigs  
I take senses from men's heads  
Half the senses from boys' heads  
The rest from wise women's heads  
Women dance without dance  
Girls without belts  
Boys half on their knees

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Here are maidens gathered  
In the flock, daughters of the flock  
Bring another to gather  
Collect the third to gather  
Let's go to Riga for plundering  
To Tallinn for killing  
To Põltsamaa for burning

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Hiiu road crooked  
The reed woman crooked  
But poured crooked spirits  
Uurakille beer makers  
Reed aunt, reed uncle  
Protect your beautiful daughter  
Guard your dear daughter  
Many men come to the reed place  
Don’t let her be taken away  
Many people come to the reed place  
Blacksmith fellows, mill lads  
Especially city men

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I had one and only son  
He hurried to live without a woman  
To manage without a partner.  
He started living, he started being,  
But he couldn't live without a woman  
Nor manage without a partner.  
He went from home to the courting path  
For a short time to the path of maidenhood.  
He came back home from courting,  
Then went to the back room,  
Put his hat on the table,  
Gloves on the edge of the hat,  
But himself started to cry.  
Then the father asked his son,  
"Why are you crying, young son?"  
"Why am I crying, dear father?  
I cannot live without a woman  
Nor manage without a partner.  
So I went briefly on the courting path  
For a short time on the maiden's way.  
The girls did not tolerate me  
They started to bark at me:  
'You don’t have a riffle shirt on,  
A riffle shirt, a silk vest,  
A cardaman at your neck,  
And German boots on your feet.'"  
Then the father understood and answered:  
"Don't cry, young son,  
Let's go to Riga to buy clothes,  
We'll buy you a riffle shirt,  
A riffle shirt, a silk vest,  
A cardaman to the neck,  
And German boots for your feet."  
"Then I went again on the courting path,  
For a short time on the maiden's way.  
The girls then loved me,  
They embraced me warmly,  
And kissed me sweetly.  
Then I came home from courting,  
I brought a wife to the house,  
Brought a daughter-in-law to the doorstep,  
Her chest was shining with jewelry,  
Her apron with proud lace."

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Oh, Mari, low on the grass!  
Again Mari calmly:  
Twisting and twirling  
Dewgrass-haired  
Why didn’t you come to me last year?  
I couldn’t come to you the year before last.  
When you went to get the orders:  
Two or three beer cans  
Seven hundred messages  
I make a dance hall  
On my thigh I make a swing  
A shelter made of rowan berries  
A goat shed made of rose hips  
I set up a silk bed  
A silk bed, a silk game  
For the young heifer to sleep  
To lull a new bride to sleep  
Let it be, let it be, I curse!  
The daughter to go home to ferment  
To stand in front of the stove  
To gather mushrooms by the wall  
To rust a knife in its sheath  
Let the axe sink into the stone  
Let the hens lay eggs in the well  
Let the wolf eat the dog’s dung.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to the setting sun for a story,
To Maria's meadow!
I took a bow, I took two arrows.
What did I find under the bow?
I found an eagle under the bow.
What is under the eagle's wing?
A partridge under the eagle's wing.
What is on the partridge's wing tip?
A splinter was on the wing tip.
What was carved from that splinter?
John's barn was carved.
What is inside John's barn?
Beds inside John's barn.
What is under the beds?
Four young maidens.
One is flashing canvases;
Another is banging papers;
The third is weaving a golden belt;
The fourth is crying for a young man!
Don't cry, maiden!
They are already heard coming,
The tinkling of tin harnesses:
The rattling of iron harnesses,
The jingling of silver harnesses;
The ringing of golden harnesses.
Be quiet, maiden,
They seem to be coming!
The head is visible above the forest;
The legs below the sparse forest,
The body in the middle of the forest!

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:

I took a bow, I took two arrows.

I found an eagle under the bow.

A partridge under the eagle's wing.

Don't cry, maiden!

The rattling of iron harnesses,
The jingling of silver harnesses;
The ringing of golden harnesses.
Be quiet, maiden,
They seem to be coming!
The head is visible above the forest;
The legs below the sparse forest,
The body in the middle of the forest!

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let others not do so  
Let the daughter of another woman do  
What did I, a fool, do  
With my crazy head  
With a fool's thoughts  
With my empty pranks  
I went from the forest to a husband  
From the top of a tree to the boys  
From the stump to the comrades  
The trees were pure wedding guests  
The white birches were bridesmaids  
The fern was the wedding horse  
The thicket was the steed  
The fodder piece was the dining table  
The field garden was the bench  
The wolf was eating, the wolf was drinking  
The old wolf was on the tray  
The mare's head was placed on a pot  
The horse's thighs on the rack  
How proud the wedding guests were  
The wolf was with five fiddles  
The old buck with a horn  
A ghost with a bagpipe  
Hands clapped, the drinks were drunk  
A long wedding was held  
Others do not know.

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Looduslaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I went to take the herd  
What did I find on the pasture path  
On the pasture path, in the berry field  
I found a hen on the pasture path  
On the pasture path, in the berry field  
I took the hen home  
Mother put the hen to grow  
Under the basket to fall asleep  
Mother went to look at the stems  
Under the lid to touch  
A maiden grew alone  
She became Salmekene alone.  
Who came to court Salmekene  
A young man from Kalevite.  
He came to court Salmekene  
He brought the clouds to the fire  
Other clouds went away  
On the cloud were drops of water  
On the tears were long sails  
On the sail was a red duck  
On the duck was a blue tail  
On the tail was a silken bed  
Silken bed, thread play  
For a young lad to sleep  
To lull his new bride  
For a young lad to sleep  
To lull his new bride  
Still, I will not come to you  
To you, the bachelor  
To the horse’s dream  
Maiden, young man  
Why do you refuse when coming to me  
When coming to me, when reaching me  
To me, a young man  
To a tall hat  
To straight boot tops  
We have apple trees outside  
And berry bushes in the house  
Rowan trees by the road  
Saturday evening is coming  
Sunday evening arrives  
Take the ladder along the row  
Go outside to the apple tree  
To the rowan tree near the road  
I will not come to you  
I will rather go there  
Maybe there is a pillar in the yard  
By the pillar a bundle of twigs  
Still I will go there  
Where my blood has led me  
Where my luck has taken me.

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Looduslaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:

On the pasture path, in the berry field  
I found a hen on the pasture path  
On the pasture path, in the berry field  
I took the hen home  
Mother put the hen to grow  
Under the basket to fall asleep  
Mother went to look at the stems

On the sail was a red duck  
On the duck was a blue tail  
On the tail was a silken bed  
Silken bed, thread play  
For a young lad to sleep  
To lull his new bride  
For a young lad to sleep  
To lull his new bride  
Still, I will not come to you  
To you, the bachelor  
To the horse’s dream  
Maiden, young man  
Why do you refuse when coming to me  
When coming to me, when reaching me  
To me, a young man  
To a tall hat  
To straight boot tops  
We have apple trees outside  
And berry bushes in the house  
Rowan trees by the road  
Saturday evening is coming  
Sunday evening arrives  
Take the ladder along the row  
Go outside to the apple tree  
To the rowan tree near the road  
I will not come to you  
I will rather go there  
Maybe there is a pillar in the yard  
By the pillar a bundle of twigs  
Still I will go there  
Where my blood has led me  
Where my luck has taken me.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
It was said and said  
It was thought and thought  
It was said over the district brothel  
To grow as a fisherman’s companion  
A little bird of city boys  
A little sparkle of village boys  
A sweetheart of farm helpers  
A darling of coachmen  
I do not fear trials  
The oppression by manor men  
The trickery of village boys  
The twisting by coachmen  
Young men, lively brothers  
Bring the drink, I would piss  
A wide glass, I would pour  
I will let you smell  
And young men to sniff  
Am I a district brothel  
Or am I a fisherman’s companion  
A little bird of city boys  
A little sparkle of village boys  
A darling of coachmen  
Sleeping by the squire.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Oh, oh, oh, oh, if only I took a wife
When there is misfortune, where then to put it
She would not become a field of scorn
Or land left abandoned.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
If only I had a man!  
Who rides along the road there;  
Rides along the path,  
With a rainbow belt around his waist;  
A hat on his head like a wreath,  
Riding over the Finnish bridge:  
Along the Curonian Spit’s mountain.  
Bend the Finnish bridge!  
Let the Curonian Spit’s mountain thunder,  
Let the horse lie down!  
He struck my brother down,  
My brother fell into the water;  
My brother’s wreath stayed on the water.  
I am going to look for my brother;  
Who will come to meet me?  
The moon came from Curonian land,  
I greet the moon:  
Greetings, moon from Curonian land.  
You walk from above;  
You aim from afar,  
You watch from the light.  
Have you seen my brother?  
Idle on the river,  
Rowing on the island sea;  
In the hollow of a slanting pilot boat,  
In the middle of a leafy sail!  
The moon answered me:  
I have not seen your brother!  
Idle on the river,  
Rowing on the island sea;  
In the hollow of a slanting pilot boat,  
In the middle of a leafy sail.  
I am going to look for my brother!  
Who will come to meet me?  
The sun came from Pärnu land:  
I greet the sun,  
Greetings, sun from Pärnu land!  
You walk from above;  
You aim from afar;  
You watch from the light.  
Have you seen my brother?  
Idle on the river,  
Rowing on the island sea;  
In the hollow of a slanting pilot boat,  
In the middle of a leafy sail!  
The sun answered me:  
I have not seen your brother!  
Idle on the river  
Rowing on the island sea;  
In the hollow of a slanting pilot boat,  
In the middle of a leafy sail!  
I am going to look for my brother!  
Who will come to meet me?  
The star, another man, came;  
I greet the star:  
Greetings, star, another man,  
You aim from afar,  
You watch from the light;  
You walk from above.  
Have you seen my brother?  
Idle on the river,  
Rowing on the island sea;  
In the hollow of a slanting pilot boat,  
In the middle of a leafy sail!  
The star answered me:  
I have not seen your brother!  
Idle on the river,  
Rowing on the island sea;  
In the hollow of a slanting pilot boat,  
In the middle of a leafy sail.  
I will start to weep for my brother,  
Moon, I wept for my brother’s coffin,  
Sun, I wept for my brother’s wreath,  
A year for my brother to rest.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
There is no such man here;  
There is none at all here  
In the parish.  
Who feeds the large herd!  
The cat waters the large herd;  
To the level of the big calf.  
But the big ones sing in the lantern:  
Puts the ducks in the pen,  
Gray ones in the harefield;  
Ears in the earrings,  
Runes in the ruble pieces;  
The stallions into full money.  
Big eyes into rings,  
To the shield of Kimmel!  
Another reply came from the stove:  
Why do you empty praise,  
Waste your own time?  
I myself was with you yesterday;  
The day before yesterday came to you.  
You still had mice in the house!  
Cats guarding the stake.  
Rats creeping in between.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Essu-lessu, the leaves were wide;  
The boys of our parish tall!  
They went to ride on the heath!  
A horse beneath as if it were an oven  
By itself as if it were a day.  
A hat like a king’s crown;  
A belt on the waist like a city ribbon  
Boots shining on the feet,  
Gloves embroidered on the hands!  
Essu-lessu, the leaves were wide;  
The boys of the other parish tall!  
They went to ride on the heath  
The horse was covered with mold beneath;  
By itself rotting,  
The hat on the head moldy  
The belt on the waist rusted  
Boots muddy on the feet  
Gloves worn on the hands

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
If only my old grandmother knew!
If only my old grandfather knew.
If only my other half knew,
My husband would guess,
My young men would understand!
If only my sweetheart knew,
Longing here in this world,
Worrying in another world,
He would put his ears to run,
He would let the stallions run wild;
He would come after me.
He would take my dear home,
He would take my berry to the house!
To the flower maiden,
To the pretty chamber.
He would take it for my father-in-law as a gift,
He would take it for my mother-in-law as a gift,
For my husband as joy of the heart,
For my spouse as a delight!

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Village girls, little old ladies  
Village men, little old men!  
Help me understand,  
Give a bit of advice.  
Will I soon leave, or soon stay,  
Or soon give my engagement ring?  
Rings into the lap of the sister,  
Pins placed on the table.  
I am a girl for today’s day,  
A maiden until tomorrow morning.  
I keep my honor and grow long,  
And soon a good lineage of wreaths,  
A lineage of straight hair;  
A lineage of white sleeves.  
I have ten suitors:  
Nine admirers;  
When I start to reckon,  
One was Ants with a rare beard,  
Another Lauri with a broad jaw.  
The third Mihkel with a thin mustache;  
The fourth a worthy maiden.  
The fifth Virviku’s servant,  
The sixth Kuuskeroika Jaan.  
The seventh the better one,  
Eighth the fisherman’s son;  
The ninth from beyond the seas,  
The tenth the village blacksmith’s guest.  
*  
Let it be, let it be, I curse;  
What do I want from the suitor:  
Should I gift him to the letter carrier?  
Wolves upon his flock,  
Hawks upon his chickens!  
Bears upon all his herd,  
Sheep to the forests’ pastures!

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Virgin girl, young one  
Maiden, slender one  
They were like parts of beauty  
Grew like hemp fibers  
Like flax fibers for weaving  
Rising fibers for spinning  
The boy is made to be a seeker  
A seeker, a deceiver  
In the darkness of night he deceives  
In the white of day he swears  
If you believe the boy's words  
Then you carry a cuckoo in your arms  
An owl under your armpit  
A singing thrush in your scarf.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I'll tell the old woman  
I'll tell the old man  
Get me a cunning wife  
Try to bring me gold along  
If you don't take her, don't ask  
I'll go to the huts to the village  
To the cold village barn  
To the unheated building  
There I'll make little children by the roads  
Clumsy ones for the edges  
Gossiping women for the swamps  
Wrong women for the gate  
On the soul of father and mother  
On the neck of a black cat  
I'll make tiny little children  
I'll make sons who are potters.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Go home, boys of Koigi  
Boys, dogs of Koigi village  
You have ten jobs at home  
Ten places untouched  
You have pigs unfed  
Piglets uncollected  
Puppies not urinated  
Dog feces not cleaned up  
Hens’ eggs untested  
Goslings unhatched  
Your own head unwashed  
Lice on your neck unkilled  
Fleas not caught  
Nose not wiped from mucus.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
We go through the villages  
Through the Padise street;  
Through the new alleys  
Through the brass gate.  
I think the road is ticking,  
The paths are cracking.  
My tin sled is ticking,  
A shining pearl horse gleams.  
Golden nails crack the shaft,  
The cuckoo calls from the log roof,  
A little tail-tied log roof,  
A gray bird on the barn roof.  
I ride up to father-in-law’s door:  
“Hello, father-in-law, my lord,  
Hello, mother-in-law, my lady,  
Hello, slender maiden.  
May I come inside the house?  
To drive the cattle into the yard,  
Tie the pike to the wall,  
Fasten the foal to the mare?  
Are there springs in the room,  
Linen strips on the floor?  
May I step into the springs,  
Slide over the linen?”  
“Our maiden, young girl,  
She is walking the tavern road,  
Looking for beer houses,  
Trying the stone chamber.”  
“Come home, young girl,  
At home, three suitors await.  
A hundred loaves of bread,  
A thousand beers.”  
“I will not come home,  
My coat is not crowned,  
Silver shirt unsewn,  
Copper breastplate unpolished,  
Golden coverlet unembroidered.  
Let my coat be crowned,  
Silver shirt sewn,  
Copper breastplate polished,  
Golden coverlet embroidered.  
Then I will come home,  
Will not tire the gatepost,  
Nor break the well bucket,  
Carrying the spoils,  
Before the heads of the household,  
Leading the suitors’ bridles.”

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
A little hellucan, little brother  
When going to the bride’s house for matchmaking  
Take me with you  
Take me as a belt around your waist  
I know dull girls  
And I see snobbish girls.  
The dull girl’s hair is long  
The lazy girl’s braid is wide,  
The peach tree belt is loose,  
The snobby one has a crooked nose.  
Take from the start the slave children  
From the start the slave followers  
They know how to walk in fire  
To set the fire under the belt  
To fasten with a buckle.  
Do not look for the proud ones  
Nor watch the fair-skinned ones.  
The family daughter is too tall  
Too tall, born lazy,  
Sleeps at the foot of the bed without a belt  
Does not hear the call to rise  
"Watch the woman from the stalk  
Aim at the woman from the bract  
Look even under the hay arch.  
From the stalk you get a hurtful woman  
From the bract you get a smooth woman  
From the hay arch a beautiful one.  
Who breaks down the berry branches  
Drives in the mustard branches  
Under the arch the meadowsweet  
Above the arch the globeflowers  
Mows the hay, traps the forest  
This one bangs the willow branch."

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's leave this village  
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For half the summer to the land of the bog  
And for winter to Alutaguse.  
When a girl comes to the village hungry  
The girl hungry, lacking food;  
When it’s time to look for a horse  
Riding on the shin of a slim person:  
The shoulders of the hurser strike fire  
The shins of the slim one threw sparks  
The hooves of the colt strike lightning  
When a girl goes on the road  
Far to catch the herd.  
Young maidens, young girls  
When you leave  
Who will then do the work?  
"The girls do the work  
Young girls mowing hay  
Heaps appeared in Kuuramaa  
Big flocks in Saaremaa  
Young flails in Alutaguse.  
These are made by our girls  
Heaps gathered by daughters  
Flails started by maidens.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's leave this village  
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For half the summer to the land of the bog  
And for winter to Alutaguse.  
When a girl comes to the village hungry  
The girl hungry, lacking food;
