label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I make, I make gentle bands  
I make capercaillie bands for the road  
Great bird bands for the family  
Maiden bands from the village  
From the road, I brought a strong capercaillie  
From the meadow, I brought a large magpie  
From the village, I brought a young maiden  
A young maiden, from the other side.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Brother dear Vellekene,  
when you go to marry a woman.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Little Ella, little brother,  
Buy a colt from the foal,  
If you want a dog from a puppy,  
Take a wife from a young lady.  
When you come home from the field,  
The horse neighs from the colt,  
The dog barks from the puppy,  
The woman laughs from the young lady.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud abielust
translation:
Mine is not here,
My companion does not grow here.
Mine is in Holland,
My companion far away.
May the wind carry health to her,
May the clouds long for her,
Hail falls like books.
Little sisters, little souls.
Let me say again
When shall I roll to the brothers?
In autumn the swamps are deep,
In spring the waves are wide.
The apple sinks into the ditches,
Between the wet hummocks,
I myself leave for the waves
But the brother stood guard at the door
"From there come great eaters,
Great eaters, those who drink much,
Those who put much in place.
They eat up even the ripe rye
And drink even from the mead barrel."

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud abielust
translation:
Mine is not here,
My companion does not grow here.
Mine is in Holland,
My companion far away.
May the wind carry health to her,
May the clouds long for her,
Hail falls like books.
Little sisters, little souls.
Let me say again
When shall I roll to the brothers?
In autumn the swamps are deep,
In spring the waves are wide.
The apple sinks into the ditches,
Between the wet hummocks,
I myself leave for the waves
But the brother stood guard at the door
"From there come great eaters,
Great eaters, those who drink much,
Those who put much in place.
They eat up even the ripe rye
And drink even from the mead barrel."

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Little boys with budding eyes,  
Budding eyes, ring ears,  
Went to sleep by the wall,  
Put their pants under the pillow,  
Pants straps turned into pillows,  
Pants buttons turned into youngsters.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Some tree in the forest,
Some kind of tree.
Some tree with branches,
Some a cubit tall.
There is no trimming of the head,
No shortening of the hair,
Heads cannot be shaved to the scalp,
Hair cannot be cut to the scalp.
I take my head home to the old woman,
I give it to the gardener’s daughter,
If the old woman does not trim it,
The old man Ella will trim it.
If old man Ella does not trim it,
Probably the brother Ella will trim it;
If brother Ella does not trim it,
Sister-in-law Ella will trim it,
If sister-in-law does not trim it,
Then let it stand right there,
Let it walk on the forearm,
Be on my shoulders.
When I go to church on Sunday,
I walk the road myself,
My hair waves in the alder grove.
Young boys looked at me,
From below, from above,
Between the fence posts,
Between pairs of rods,
If that girl were mine,
If she were my wife too,
Who walks along the road,
Steps along the edge of the road,
She would be tied with silk,
Sewn with flax thread,
Wrapped in paper.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
It was said and often said,
It was thought and often thought,
It was said to go alone,
It was said to take me away,
It was thought to take me to the manor,
To the lad Kiltre in Mulgi’s hand,
To Kubja for hot ruble notes.
If only I were taken away,
To be taken to the manor,
To have Kiltre’s lad in my hand,
Then I would have a son like half a man,
A daughter desired by suitors.
I dance on the barn floor,
I leap through the manor’s pastures,
I stretch out at the cattle market,
Yet I do not have a son like half a man,
No daughter desired by suitors,
No child reaching the height of the table.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Little Ella, little brother,  
Little boy, little lad  
Promised to take the truth,  
To give a hand to the beloved,  
To firmly give the engagement ring.  
Summer comes, autumn passes,  
The time approaches Christmas.  
The truth is revealed,  
The hand is given in grace,  
The ring is exchanged,  
The engagement ring is given as a gift.  
You can sleep at night  
Two nights cunningly  
You turn your back, mock the eye.  
You laugh at the girl in vain,  
Mock the other one in vain,  
Insult the girl:  
She is indeed a prostitute,  
Indeed she is a harlot,  
Indeed she is half a port!  
I hear and answer back  
She is not a prostitute, indeed,  
She is not a harlot, indeed,  
Nor half a port.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Hello, smith, weather sage,  
Wise striker of God,  
Make me an apprentice  
To the iron plow,  
A little whip from reed,  
A little stalk from oat straw,  
I have traveled a stretch of the Turkish road,  
A bit of the Narva road,  
Half a verst of the Polish road.  
It is time for the servant,  
It is time for the five sister-wives,  
It is time for the six knot-necklaces,  
It is time for the seven small ones,  
It is time for me to go too.  
For the brother to mend the vest,  
For the father to repair the scabbard.  
The brother beat the vest on the beam,  
The father beat the scabbard on the stone.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Mari grandma, Mari granny,  
Mari my little mountain goat.  
The cuckoo was in the spruce grove;  
The liverleaf was in the alder grove;  
She called the boys to the spruce grove,  
She herself played in the pine forest.  
She herself slipped into the alder grove.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Mari paid in Malla parish,  
Mari’s tail in Salla parish,  
Mari’s tongue in Keidla parish,  
Mari’s garland in Pärnu parish.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The fire would catch and burn,  
So that Saaremaa would melt,  
Läänemaa would burn through,  
Põltsamaa would burn purely.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Here I play with tussocks,  
I jump with dwarf birches,  
Before those maidens,  
Which were half-held.  
Loved before time.  
Aa-aa-aa-opp-sap-saa  
Let's throw a party all at once.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The evening comes through the yard,  
Twilight comes through the grain.  
Everyone goes with their own:  
The master with the mistress,  
The young man with the young maiden.  
Who has no partner, let them take a cat,  
Who has no cat, let them take a rat,  
Who has no rat, let them take a mole;  
The rat from the reeds,  
The mole from the turf.  
I have no rat, I have no cat,  
I have no rat, I have no mole.  
But I go crying toward the corner,  
Toward the empty bed hollow.  
I rummage through the old coat.  
I fold my sleeves against each other,  
I hold a conversation with the lichens,  
I make merry with the bedbug.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Ellakene, little brother,  
When you go to ask for a wife,  
From the other side to seek,  
Do not take from the church's way,  
Everyone lives by the church's way,  
The customs belong to other peoples.  
Look at the wife in the evening with the sickle,  
Watch the wife at the spinning wheel.  
Is she smooth for the sickle  
Or nimble for the spinning wheel  
Right on the hay stalk.  
Young women were taken because of skill,  
Because of standing tall in the field,  
Rich because of money,  
Proud because of jewelry.  
A daughter was taken because of work,  
Because of her useful hands.  
A poor child was taken because of hardship,  
Because of her great labor.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Suitors are to come,  
Shining through time,  
Flashing over the fields,  
Enemy’s child, walk purely,  
Slave’s child, walk proudly,  
House daughter, walk richly.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
What I fear when singing,  
Singing, composing poems,  
Is that my own is not here,  
Nor my partner growing here.  
My own is in Olland,  
My partner far away.  
There she swings in the cuckoo's nest,  
Bends in the flexible bow,  
Sways on the beam's ropes.  
If there is strength, may God help,  
If there is health, may work be done,  
May the wind bring complete health,  
May the sky grant wise mind,  
May the clouds endure for a long time.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
There are no men at home,  
No boys in the yard.  
Noses nodded, necks bent,  
Blood boils on the forehead.  
Take (it) to Viru, bring (it) to the market,  
Crouch in the pigsty.  
They go to Viru to be flogged,  
Searching for a doctor in the slaughterhouse,  
Lifting navels in Narva.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sex, do not exaggerate  
Bush, do not steal  
That I have a little horse  
A modest riding sleigh  
I will buy a silver horse  
And have a wide sleigh made  
Then I go down to my father-in-law's mulgu  
Father-in-law's mulgu, mother-in-law's mulgu  
I pass under the brother's fence  
Then I arrive to bring the bride  
To set the table with langudelle  
To receive the bride from the mother-in-law  
A silver horse shines ahead  
Reins click softly  
The little wagon creaks  
The man’s head is like the day  
Hat like the servant of the Moon  
Belt like the rainbow of rain  
Then I go under father-in-law’s yard  
Then I go under mother-in-law’s yard  
Hello father-in-law, hello mother-in-law  
Hello mother-in-law, kitchen stove  
Hello father-in-law, shoe horn  
Hello slippers, hello clogs  
Hello worn spoons  
Hello worn ladle handles  
Hello room, feel me  
Will you accept the bridegroom?  
May I come into the house  
To drive the horses into the yard.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sex, do not exaggerate  
Bush, do not steal  
That I have a little horse  
A modest riding sleigh  
I will buy a silver horse  
And have a wide sleigh made  
Then I go down to my father-in-law's mulgu  
Father-in-law's mulgu, mother-in-law's mulgu  
I pass under the brother's fence  
Then I arrive to bring the bride  
To set the table with langudelle  
To receive the bride from the mother-in-law  
A silver horse shines ahead  
Reins click softly  
The little wagon creaks  
The man’s head is like the day  
Hat like the servant of the Moon  
Belt like the rainbow of rain  
Then I go under father-in-law’s yard  
Then I go under mother-in-law’s yard  
Hello father-in-law, hello mother-in-law  
Hello mother-in-law, kitchen stove  
Hello father-in-law, shoe horn  
Hello slippers, hello clogs  
Hello worn spoons  
Hello worn ladle handles  
Hello room, feel me  
Will you accept the bridegroom?  
May I come into the house  
To drive the horses into the yard.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Oh, you little deceiver  
A cunning companion  
You deceived the father-in-law, deceived the mother-in-law  
Deceived the fine young lady of the family  
Promised to bring slippers  
A hundred pairs of boots  
A bunch of clog shoes  
A blue skirt, a chintz apron  
Green, pinkish  
A warm fur coat  
It was going to be, it was going to live  
Then she began asking  
Where are my bunch of clog shoes  
A hundred pairs of boots  
A thousand pairs of slippers  
Blue skirt and chintz apron  
Green, pinkish  
Warm fur coat  
Be silent, poor woman  
Let me go outside to look for them  
From the pine forest, I will bring rowan  
From the back of the room, I bring dogwood  
From under the thigh, I bring Arctic bramble  
From the end of the chamber, I bring maple leaf  
Come here, poor woman  
Let me take some sense  
Along the spine, along the side  
Along the edge of the back brace  
Ten strokes on the side  
Seven strokes on the back  
Eight strokes on the neck  
These are your bunch of clog shoes  
A hundred pairs of boots  
A thousand pairs of slippers  
Blue skirt and chintz apron  
Green, pinkish  
Warm fur coat.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Before I play with the tufts
I leap over birch stumps
I trample the dry reeds
I pull the carriage wheels
Before those girls
Who were half-married
Loved earlier than expected
Played across the forest
Danced across the room
Let's take those maidens
Who wore braided shoelaces
Snow-white hair
White hands, white cheeks,
White heads, red wreaths.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Young men of Kaaruka village  
The white boys of Allikuvalla  
Go to Midsummer at the church  
Under the horse as if it were an oven  
By themselves as if it were daytime  
A belt like a rainbow  
A hat like the moon’s servant  
They buy large quantities without beer  
Pay only with marked money  
A purse shines with small coins  
Pockets crackle with money  
They walk on Salga like Germans  
They walk around in large numbers like lords.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
If the groom dies  
What will grow on his grave?  
Buttercups, knapweed leaves  
Thistles and wild herbs  
These grow on his grave.  
If the old maiden dies  
What will grow on her grave?  
Buttercups, lilies of the valley  
Beautiful and proud roses  
These grew on the maiden's grave.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Uiut, tuiut, vagina Eva  
Beautiful Eva all over the world  
White Eva all over the parish  
She shone over Paide.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Oh, you poor wretched man
Lives a life of misery
Where he sits, the seat is wet
Where he stands, the walls are wet
Where he cries, the corners are wet
How cold are the side tables
The edge tables are pale
I turn over, I find coldness
I turn the sail, I find the wall
He searched for a beautiful woman
He wanted a playful woman
He encountered a wild mess
Who couldn’t bring firewood inside
Without lifting the chair up
Without helping with the stove
Without placing it on the beam’s edge
Oh, I, poor unfortunate man
Always have lack
Many times have sad thoughts
Whose are dull to plant
Whose are cruel to trample
Whose are sad in spirit
Whose are always low
To the stone by the church road
To the rope outside the shed
The stone was proud, didn’t speak
The rope was thick, didn’t whisper.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Oh, you poor wretched man
Lives a life of misery
Where he sits, the seat is wet
Where he stands, the walls are wet
Where he cries, the corners are wet
How cold are the side tables
The edge tables are pale
I turn over, I find coldness
I turn the sail, I find the wall
He searched for a beautiful woman
He wanted a playful woman
He encountered a wild mess
Who couldn’t bring firewood inside
Without lifting the chair up
Without helping with the stove
Without placing it on the beam’s edge
Oh, I, poor unfortunate man
Always have lack
Many times have sad thoughts
Whose are dull to plant
Whose are cruel to trample
Whose are sad in spirit
Whose are always low
To the stone by the church road
To the rope outside the shed
The stone was proud, didn’t speak
The rope was thick, didn’t whisper.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
Here had gone sirgu tracks  
Sirgu tracks, nirgu tracks  
Through the willow, the duck’s tracks  
Straight the squirrel’s tracks  
Here sirku paused  
The wedding of the elderberry rod’s rat  
The mouse’s daughter got married  
To the younger son of nirgi  
They had a proud wedding party  
The crow wore copper boots  
The rooster had golden spurs  
The titmouse had a tin beard.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
Here had gone sirgu tracks  
Sirgu tracks, nirgu tracks  
Through the willow, the duck’s tracks  
Straight the squirrel’s tracks  
Here sirku paused  
The wedding of the elderberry rod’s rat  
The mouse’s daughter got married  
To the younger son of nirgi  
They had a proud wedding party  
The crow wore copper boots  
The rooster had golden spurs  
The titmouse had a tin beard.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I feed the black one, he wears a collar  
So I can put a log in front  
I carry bundles of oats  
From the window I give hay  
In my pocket, I have farm flours  
When I got a strong horse  
Then to the horse’s harness  
To the bright bridle straps  
To the grey ear flakes  
To the ear fur skins  
To the gelding’s ruble pieces  
Then I go courting Koiki.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Tobacco was brought to the woman  
The master of the house smoked a pipe  
The servant’s wife ate bread  
The master of the house drank milk  
The farmhand’s wife ate fish.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The girl from Kilju, the girl from Kalju  
The girl from Kilju was caught  
When hanging from Kalju's neck  
She came closer, stood secretly  
Put her head under the boy's arm  
But her hands around the boy's neck  
Feet under the boy's skirt.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Virgin girl, young maiden  
Delicate in writing  
Why do you complain when you come to me  
When you come to me, when you are with me  
To me, a young man  
Come to me, I hurry  
I will bring you three pairs of shoes  
Smooth, joyful shoes I bring  
Wooden wedding shoes I bring  
Calm dancing shoes I bring  
Stony church shoes I bring  
They do not creak on dry ground  
Nor rot on wet ground  
Nor snap with a bang  
Come to me, I hurry  
I will bring you three aprons  
One I bring is cotton-silk  
The second is Swedish checked  
The third is golden-crowned.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The maiden was beautiful from afar  
The maiden was lovely from a distance  
Empty knows the maiden’s deeds  
The maiden’s ways flutter  
Perhaps the little ones were under the wing  
The scoundrels at the belt  
Before I play with the tufts of grass  
I jump with birch stumps  
Before those maidens  
Who were half-married  
Loved prematurely  
Kissed tip to tip  
Danced through the winter’s end  
Let go through the hands  
Otherwise, my black one could run  
Could carry a chisel in a basket  
Could whirl a boat around a corner  
Could scream in the gray barn  
Before the maiden’s maidens.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Do not cry, young maidens  
Be cheerful, girls of Harju  
I am going to town and will stay there  
I will take off my black coat  
I will put on a blue coat  
I will take off this hat  
I will put on the leader's cap  
Then I will go to take a wife  
To catch a sailboat  
I rode to the lake’s bridge  
To the lake bridge, to the bending one  
To knock on the main beam  
To slap the bridge boards  
Harju's Annes, Järva's Kaies  
Are sitting on Sõrve bridge  
Aruküla’s Anne understands, responds  
If only that man were mine  
Who passed by, head lowered  
Lifted his handsome hat.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Do not cry, young maidens  
Be cheerful, girls of Harju  
I am going to town and will stay there  
I will take off my black coat  
I will put on a blue coat  
I will take off this hat  
I will put on the leader's cap  
Then I will go to take a wife  
To catch a sailboat  
I rode to the lake’s bridge  
To the lake bridge, to the bending one  
To knock on the main beam  
To slap the bridge boards  
Harju's Annes, Järva's Kaies  
Are sitting on Sõrve bridge  
Aruküla’s Anne understands, responds  
If only that man were mine  
Who passed by, head lowered  
Lifted his handsome hat.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Ellakene, little granny  
Put this daughter into a husband  
Who walks around outdoors  
Foolish heads, misty hair  
Neat hands in hands  
If you don't put her, don't ask for her  
Let her stay at home knocking  
By the edge of the ditch, getting moldy  
By the wall, gathering mushrooms  
A birch tree on his neck  
A spruce on his eyebrow  
A juniper between his legs.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Otherwise, my black horse could have run  
My gray horse could have carried firewood  
My brown horse could have pulled a sled  
Before those maidens and girls  
Who were halfway attended to  
Loved before their time  
Dear little mother, dear little sweetheart  
Take care of your beautiful daughter  
Don’t let her runaway  
Runaways are chased by runaway dogs  
Uninvited, they roam like wild sails.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
If I were Alev  
I would wear a kalev  
I would have a long spear  
I fear touching the heels  
I would buy a new horse  
I would take a young bride  
I fear the young one crying  
The young one wants to eat bits and pieces  
The dear one wants to eat fish  
Tiny fine bread  
There is no shit on our land  
Springs beneath the village  
Where could one get fish for the dear one  
Tiny fine bread  
The young one to eat bits and pieces  
They put the churn into the enclosure  
The silver churn outside the yard  
The copper wheel for the smith  
The tin churn on the path  
I have the courage to go to the field  
Starting from the neck of the spindle  
Then I put on silk shirts  
I put a golden hat on my head  
A cockroach belt on my waist  
Then I have the courage to go  
To call my heart out  
Starting from the neck of the spindle.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I am going to Koiki parish
To the three daughters of Koigi's farmer
One was Anne, the second Eeva
The third was Mari Madal
I do not want to marry Anne
Nor do I want to marry Eeva
I will take Mari, the young woman
She was my wife before
My young maiden.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
What do I care about the flower field  
Afraid in the tavern’s shouting  
My mother is not here  
My spouse is not here  
My own is in Holland  
My spouse is far away  
May the wind carry them well  
May time bring blessings  
May the clouds be lengthy  
May the sky hold wise thoughts  
If sick, may God help  
If healthy, may they work.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Murelaulud
translation:
But I play with tussocks  
Joke with laughs  
And also with those girls  
Whom I half-dated  
All summer flirted  
All winter danced  
I would like to build without a building  
Who will cut the logs for me  
But the wind cut the logs down  
The water rolled the logs  
The rains mossed the walls  
The fog put doors in front  
Now knives are on the door  
Wall clocks on the wall  
They were tippy-tappy  
Round all around.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Murelaulud
translation:
But I play with tussocks  
Joke with laughs  
And also with those girls  
Whom I half-dated  
All summer flirted  
All winter danced  
I would like to build without a building  
Who will cut the logs for me  
But the wind cut the logs down  
The water rolled the logs  
The rains mossed the walls  
The fog put doors in front  
Now knives are on the door  
Wall clocks on the wall  
They were tippy-tappy  
Round all around.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The family's young maiden  
She had many servants  
If I start guessing  
One was Hantsu Arva's bearded one  
The second Lauri with the broad jaw  
The third Mihkel with the small mouth  
The fourth Marti with the crooked leg  
The fifth Virviku's servant  
The sixth Kuusk Roika Jaan's  
The seventh the better one  
The eighth the fisherman’s son  
The ninth the commander's son  
The tenth the village cobbler's son.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I had many friends  
One was Intu from Hiiumaa  
Another Antu from Harjumaa  
The third Peeter from Peipus  
The fourth Jüri from Järvamaa.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Passing by the bridegroom's gate  
Through the attached building  
Let's go inside to see  
How many beds they have in the room  
They have seven beds in the room  
Father's bed was wooden  
Mother's bed was beaded  
Younger sibling's bed was paneled  
Bridegroom's bed was pressed  
Maiden's chin was pointed.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I went to the village swing  
The girls didn’t like me.  
They started barking at me  
You don’t have a silk shirt on  
A silk shirt, a silk vest  
A kerchief around your neck  
German boots on your feet  
I went home crying  
Put my hat on the table  
Gloves on the edge of the hat  
Crying myself  
Auntie asked me  
Why are you crying, young lad?  
Why am I crying, dear auntie?  
Why am I crying, uncle?  
I went to the village swing  
The girls didn’t like me  
They started barking at me  
That I don’t have a silk shirt  
A silk shirt, a silk vest  
A kerchief around my neck  
German boots on my feet.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Mother, dear mother  
Marry off this daughter  
Who walks around outside,  
A scatterbrain, with messy hair  
Nice little hands in hand  
Nice little breasts on the chest  
If you don’t marry her off, don’t even ask  
She will go digging at home  
Sit by the edge to wither  
Pick mushrooms by the wall  
A spruce instead of a birch over the eyebrow  
A willow instead of oats on the head  
A juniper between the legs.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The evening comes through the outdoors  
Twilight comes through the exit  
Everyone goes with their own  
The master with the mistress  
The young man with the young girl  
I have no companion, I have no cat  
Whoever has no cat, take a rat,  
Whoever has no rat, take a mole  
Rat from the reeds  
Mole from the sod clods  
But I go crying toward the corner  
Toward the empty bed  
I rummage through the old fur coat  
But I sew sleeves together  
I have to converse with lichens  
I strike joy with a bedbug.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Mine is not here  
My companion does not grow here  
Mine is in Holland  
My companion far away  
Let the wind carry greetings  
Clouds long to travel  
Hail to the books  
Sisters, dear ones  
Let me say again  
When I roll to the brothers  
In autumn, deep swamps  
In spring, wide floods  
The horse drowns in ditches  
Between wet clumps  
I myself leave for the floods.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
What are you staring at, old bachelor?  
Why don’t you get yourself a wife?  
Your beard has already turned gray,  
Your ear edges yellowed,  
Your mouth is running deadly water,  
Your beard is gray grave water,  
Your nose is dripping molten lead,  
Your teeth below are loose.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Oh you deceitful little boy  
A cunning companion  
You deceived your father-in-law, you deceived your mother-in-law  
You deceived the fine young lady of the family  
You lead her to the cold village room  
To an ugly building  
All the buildings were crooked  
The cowsheds were on a slope  
The stable was slanted  
The well was crooked  
The stove was tilted  
You deceived the fine young lady  
All the corners were left crying  
The seats longing  
The steps uneasy  
The cowsheds were crying  
The corners wept for the young woman  
The seats for the lovely girl  
The steps for the honest one  
The cowsheds for the beautiful one  
The pigs had good feeders  
The cows had good milkers  
The bulls had good waterers  
The doors were left wandering  
The doorframes creaking  
The horns squealing.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The girl kissed, the girl shouted  
The girl got caught  
The boy shouted while pulling away.  
She got pulled, but didn’t say a word  
Turned her back towards the boy  
Towards the wall near the anus  
Started barking at the boy  
You untie the penis straps  
Pull the garter straps  
Untie the trouser straps.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Oh, you little deceitful boy  
Don't deceive the little father-in-law  
Don't deceive the little mother-in-law  
You deceived a fine maiden  
To vent your anger with alcohol  
Sneaking around secretly  
Rushed to take the truth  
Probably to bring along some alcohol  
Made strong promises  
Went along for six months  
That land for six months  
They began to understand  
The maiden was no longer naive  
Don't trust a young man  
Young men have short tempers  
Short tempers, slippery tongues  
Then the bride cries day and night  
She first spoke to the old grandmother  
Then she spoke to the old man  
What should I do?  
Should I jump into the water?  
Or should I jump to the bottom of the well?  
The grandmother understood and immediately answered  
Daughter, dear little bird  
Don't you do that.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Slept through the night  
Immediately she laughed in his face  
Derided the other's glance  
Yet she was a whore  
And was also quite a slut.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The lice, the lice, the lice were eating  
The lice were eating, the fleas were gnawing  
The mites bit in defiance  
The bedbugs made a story  
Why didn’t you come to us  
When this pawn was tarred  
The old wheel greased  
From there, one could have gotten mind ointment  
Mind ointment, tongue ointment,  
Mouth ointment, heart ointment.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
You are young men  
And thinking of going courting  
No merit at all  
Nor to put a wreath on your head  
The horse sought from the stable  
The harness viewed from the village  
The hat heard from the village  
The vest is without others' blood.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Our men like forest bulls  
The gray bulls of Ane Hans  
We go mooing to the hill  
Beating towards the edge of the well  
Ike swings his head  
Hail falls like iron inside  
The silver bulls’ horns  
Jüri comes to take the bull  
I, for Jüri’s prayer  
Don’t you take a pair of bulls  
A pair of bulls, a pair of bulls  
Strong bulls to row  
Strong bulls to wrestle  
Just take an old white pair  
The white one plows, the head shakes  
The black plows, the back bends  
The brown plows, the earth splashes.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Men have already left us  
The rifle carriers have disappeared  
The bent hoe had become dented  
The iron was heavy, the lead was dull  
The copper was harsh to the greenery  
Swedish iron had rusted  
We are our men  
Brothers in arms, just the two of us  
We eat from one, we drink from one  
We buy one horse  
We take one wife.

label: Laulud laulust  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
My village says kuku, kuku  
Village children laala, laala  
I understand, I respond  
Why should I fall like a sad bird  
Or why should I sing badly to the children  
I have no singing board in my head  
Nor knots of words in my mouth  
In my head there are singing boards  
In my mouth there are knots of words  
If I had a singing board in my head  
And knots of words in my mouth  
Then I would sing a bird’s song  
And I would fall in the tune of the cuckoo  
If this man were mine  
Who rides along the road here  
Rides along the path  
Like a horse under the stove  
The man is as bright as the day  
Hat like a church bell  
Belt like a city’s ribbon  
Arch like a rainbow in the rain  
If this man does not take me  
Let it be, let me complain  
Under the horse let it mold  
The man let him age  
Hat yellowed on the head  
Belt mushroomed around the waist  
If, however, he does not take me.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The mockers of Piia come  
The girls who laugh seem like  
I heard them talking behind the shops  
Men to be led to the sea  
I ran running, walked walking  
Took frequent steps  
I thought of my dear brother  
I thought of my lover  
There was a stranger, there was a fiend  
There was a stranger rogue  
Another stranger scoundrel  
A third stranger villain  
I helped to draw the bow  
To tie the wheel to the axel  
To fasten the rope to the neck.  
Thrown like the devil into the sea  
Went like a swan, moved like a stake  
Went like a heron under water  
Moved like a stake through wood  
Strawberry across the mountain  
Then I was freed from the crazy boy  
Our girl laugher  
Our Piia mocker.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Before I play with tufts  
Before those maidens  
That were half-held  
Overplayed  
Danced beyond use.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Listen, you young men  
Do not boast empty words  
Do not strike excessively  
Long ago I visited you  
Yesterday beneath your yard  
You had mice still there  
Rats scurried between the walls  
Cats struck the rods out  
The flea went into the corner, the bag was on the foot  
Lice walked, straws in the mouth  
Women chopped the wheel spokes  
Girls spun the flax  
Come to our outhouse  
We have bulls still inside  
Runes scurried between the walls  
Ears stroked the rods out.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Little boy, little lad  
May I come into the room  
To drive cattle from the yard  
To tie a colt to a post  
To set up a sprat by the pillar  
Maybe the grey mare is inside  
The linen boards are by the threshold  
Maybe I will step into the grey mare  
I will slip onto the linen boards  
Dear grandfather, dear grandmother  
Dear uncle, dear aunt  
I would not have come to you otherwise  
Driven the cattle for no reason  
Otherwise used the black one  
I fed Alli for Anne's sake  
I fed Kõrv for Kaie's sake  
Otherwise used the black one for Liisu's sake  
I fed the speckled one for Krõõda's sake  
The grey one takes Anne to the hayfield  
The black one takes Maie to other places  
The flaxen one takes Liisu to town  
The speckled one takes Krõõda to church.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Oh, those girls, Brown haired boys  
Blond and fair heads  
If there were a handful of them  
If there were a basketful of them  
You go to church on Midsummer's Day  
Under the horse, like to the stove  
On the man, like to the day  
A belt like a rain rainbow  
A hat like a church bell  
The girls watched from the shadows  
At the Persel family gate  
Squatting in the village street  
Between the birch twigs  
If that man were mine  
If that companion were mine  
What is riding past on the road  
Galloping along the path  
Under the horse, like to the stove  
On the man, like to the day  
A belt like a rain rainbow  
A hat like a church bell.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
We are those men  
Among this large group  
Among half the country's boys  
Did not let our heads be washed in the harbor  
Did not let our hair be washed with foam  
Did not wander in bands with whores  
Brought home the head to wash the old woman  
To wash the old woman, to wash the old man  
If he did not wash the old woman  
Or did not wash the old man  
If I myself do not wash  
Surely the day's boy will wash  
The six-year-old will rinse  
Taking it to the river's water  
At the stump's cold spring.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I warmed my thighs on the deadwood  
By the root of Saana Island  
Still, I did not get the warmth  
That I got between the girls  
Between the five girls’ waists  
Between the two girls’ underarms.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Our men like wild bulls  
The gray bulls of Harju-Jaani  
They were tough to fight  
To scatter the manor's boys.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Who told you to come and propose  
But sent a pack to take a wife  
You are no fool to take a wife  
You spill spit on the wife  
Spit on her forehead  
Coffee on the dear’s apron  
Saliva on her beauty  
Indeed, stable boys wanted me  
And valley boys watched me  
Coaches came to propose  
Also scribes, gentlemen  
The heart desired a soldier  
The soldier feeds sugar-bread  
Gingerbread from the farmyard  
Dried cabbage turnips.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Our men like wild bulls  
Aru Jaan’s gray bulls  
Went to the forest bellowing  
Down the grove mooing  
To dig up the Aru birch  
To hang around the manor’s grove  
Strong bulls to hang around  
Kahu oxen to dig around  
Then our oxen walk on their own  
Opening the water gates wide.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Wait-wait-wait-wait tomorrow evening  
Let the dear Saturday evening come  
Then the dear trading evening will come  
Young men have a fishing evening.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Aiu, a wedding on Raiu beach  
Some man held a wedding in the forest  
I will hold a wedding in the treetops  
Behind the room, at the top of an oak  
Some carcass in the logging area.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Oh, we poor fool boys  
Two together, little cousins  
We take the runes to the rye field  
Wet into the wet meadows  
The stallions to the manor stable  
I myself go to the village to the girl  
I heard the rooster crowing  
The hen clucking to the stallion  
In the morning I went in front of the stable  
Where have you been, fool boys?  
At the girl’s place, fool boys.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Aiu-raiu with wheels  
Before I play with the clumps  
Before those girls  
Whom I had half-asked out  
Driven for over a year  
Overfed excessively  
Trampled over winter  
The girls were loose  
And their thighs were filthy  
They were not afraid of being touched  
Nor the efforts of the manor boys.  
The girls had flaccid penises  
The boys had long slender penises  
Oh, you crazy one, oh, standing tall  
Hairs prickling upright and askew.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Mari, fond of strawberries,
Called the boys to the pine forest,
Behind the room, to the oak grove.
Mai went to swing on the linden branch,
Let the linden branches move.
Then the boys’ eyes reddened,
The weather changed completely.
Pull your back like a billy goat,
Your bed is at the top of the linden tree.

label: Laulud laulust  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I sing a song about a horse  
With a lovely mane tuft  
Its head topped with a walnut  
Eyes like stars in the sky  
Ears like oak leaves  
Tail like linen fibers  
Mane like dried hay  
Body covered in hay flakes  
Tail made of hay products  
Legs under are like forks.  
Horseshoes under are like antlers  
Coat covered in dewy grass  
Then I go to the Sõrve trip  
To look at the Sõrve maiden  
The maiden gazes through the fence gate  
If only she were the honey to me  
What is riding past on the road  
Riding along the path  
The horse is like an oven  
The rider is like the day itself  
Her belt is like a rainbow  
Hat like a king’s crown  
My dear little brother  
If you go to take a wife  
Go catch a little sail  
Go court a little sparrow  
Take me under your belt  
Turn me into a six-sleeved coat  
I know a gloomy maiden  
I guess a bad maiden  
Gloomy maiden, long hair  
Lazy maiden, long braid  
A slender belt dripping  
Angry black eyebrows  
My dear little brother  
Don’t take a tall wife  
Tall ones go to sleep on a bench  
Don’t take a big wife  
Big ones tend to sulk  
Take a very small one  
A tiny little steel one  
A little diligent crazy one.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I went inside to snoop around  
To look for my bride  
The bride was dozing by the fire  
Her head between her knees  
I asked the bride  
If she wants to come to me  
To come to me, to be with me  
To me, a young man  
The bride went to ask her mother  
I am black as a soot tuft  
Bring me that white shirt  
That you made from a piece of sackcloth  
You washed it in a small pot  
Kicked it with your foot  
Took it to dry on the churn stand  
Then the bride asked me  
If you have the means  
To take my patterned kerchief  
And take my white suitcase  
I asked the bride  
If your father has property  
Or if your mother has property  
That my stallion could not carry  
Or pull the horse away.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I will not go to the miller  
The miller’s stone is rumbling  
I will not go to the spinner  
The spinner’s wheel is creaking  
I will not go to the coachman  
The coachman’s hands are clammy  
I will not go to the blacksmith  
The blacksmith’s children are hissing  
The children of the sooty striker  
I will not go to the herdsman  
Nor to the herdsman’s son  
The herdsman tends the herd  
But I will go to the ploughman  
To the turner of black soil  
I leave behind the scoundrels,  
Scoundrels and rascals  
Before I go to the ploughman,  
The turner of black soil.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Girls, little birds
Do not rely on boys
On boys' white hair
On long hair
The boy is fair, his knuckle loud
His fist is checkered
Shaft striped
Berry sweater-haired
Girls, little birds
Twinkles in the hair
Keep yourselves indoors
Protect yourselves in the chamber
Keep a pile in the granary
Do not go to sleep in the barn
The barn is full of young men
They make stomachs upset
They make troubles below the navel.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sisters, dear ones  
The three of us gather  
We consult as four  
Let's leave this land  
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For two days to Pärnumaa  
Are we sought among the crowd  
To catch Piia's flock  
Hooves cut through the fire  
Sparks flew from the horse's shins  
Flickering, flashing, foal's hooves  
When searching among the maidens  
To catch Piia's flock.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sisters, dear ones  
The three of us gather  
We consult as four  
Let's leave this land  
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For two days to Pärnumaa  
Are we sought among the crowd  
To catch Piia's flock  
Hooves cut through the fire  
Sparks flew from the horse's shins  
Flickering, flashing, foal's hooves  
When searching among the maidens  
To catch Piia's flock.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Aidu band with wheels  
Before me, I play with tussocks  
I leap with spruce stumps  
But I strike the maidens with flails  
Before those maidens  
Who were half-kept  
Prematurely loved  
Danced through the winter.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sleeping, sleeping, people  
Everyone with their own  
Each companion with a scarf  
Who has no companion, take a cat  
The mouse was more beautiful  
The dog was more faithful  
The young woman was more handsome  
The other half was more active  
Father went with the old mother  
Sister went with her husband  
Brother went with the young maiden  
I go crying to the corner  
To the empty side of the bed  
I rummage through the old coat  
Oh, my old coat  
It was good to be awake  
Even better to sleep on a patch  
How cold were the side tables  
Worn out edge tables  
I turn to one side, find coldness  
I turn my back, find the wall  
How cold were the side tables  
Worn out edge tables  
The elbow had water, cold water  
The blade had water, laborious water  
A bachelor's sorrow, a wretched sorrow  
Sitting on the edge of the bed  
An old silver spoon in hand  
Where the coal was inside  
Waiting for happiness to come  
If I had a beloved woman  
Surely she would be dear to me  
I have no wandering at night  
No falling off the roof  
No sleeping on the edge of the bed  
No licking of the soup ladle.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
What are you afraid of coming to us  
Coming to us, arriving to us  
The girl has long fields  
The ends run into the stream  
We have a home with three barns  
One winnows the wheat  
Another counts the money  
The third threshes the rye  
The doors are made of eggshell  
The floors of pork fat  
The shelves of cake crumbs  
When you go to walk into the barn  
Take a cake crumb from the shelf  
Pork fat from the floor  
Eggshell from the door.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For the winter behind the rivers  
Let's build a fine church there  
We'll wear a chapel made of tar  
Let's make towers from boards  
Wide towers from shingles  
Tall towers from poles  
When maiden hunger comes to the land  
pag, 2371 Maiden hunger, drought  
Then the shores will be searched  
Willows will be sent  
Trotters will be tested  
When looking for a maiden's heron  
Even when catching a flock of piia  
The shores' backs cut with fire  
The trotters strike with lightning  
The willows' backs spark  
When looking for the maiden's heron  
Even when catching the flock of piia.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to Finland for the summer  
For the winter behind the rivers  
Let's build a fine church there  
We'll wear a chapel made of tar  
Let's make towers from boards  
Wide towers from shingles  
Tall towers from poles  
When maiden hunger comes to the land  
pag, 2371 Maiden hunger, drought  
Then the shores will be searched  
Willows will be sent  
Trotters will be tested  
When looking for a maiden's heron  
Even when catching a flock of piia  
The shores' backs cut with fire  
The trotters strike with lightning  
The willows' backs spark  
When looking for the maiden's heron  
Even when catching the flock of piia.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The girl is shouting, the girl is shouting  
Shouting to get caught  
Shouting while carrying to the barn  
Arrives at the barn, stands secretly  
Starts asking the boy  
What is swinging on your thigh  
What is tugging on your rag  
Can it be taken off  
The boy hears, it echoes again  
It causes trouble under the blanket  
Quickly under the carpet  
It makes one want porridge  
And buttered bread.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Before I play with the tussocks  
I cast joy rings,  
Before with those girls  
Who were half-promised  
Prematurely loved  
Kissed over the ditch  
Danced through the winter.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Girls, do you hear,
What do you think of yourselves,
Why do you scorn boys
And laugh at young men.
Do you know where you can
Give to beggars, gatherers,
Bearers of two sticks,
Foolish girls,
They do not fear God,
They come into the room, pee in the cupboard,
Behind the stove into a hole,
Behind the chamber bin.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Taiut-taiut to the oak grove  
Taiut to the oak grove hill  
Ritse Leenu to the kitchen  
Lalli Anne to the granary  
There was bad girl merchandise  
Five got an old tune in return  
Six got a dried shoe in return  
Seven got a roach scale in return  
Eight got a coat in return  
Ten ploughed with a whip in return.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Under me, the gray iron yoke  
I wore ear rings on my ears  
I broke black horseshoes  
While searching for my maiden  
Could my black mare run otherwise  
Could the gray mare break loose  
Could the boat wander around  
Could the mare pull the cart  
Before turning the maiden into a bride  
Who were half-promised  
Loved prematurely  
Led across the tavern  
Played beyond control.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
But we are sturdy Brown boys  
The head cannot be washed by a forge  
The hair cannot be rinsed with a scythe  
The head I will wash at home, at grandma’s  
At grandma’s, at grandpa’s  
If grandma doesn’t wash  
If grandpa doesn’t wash  
If they don’t wash or ask for it  
Then I will wash myself in the village  
Taking water from the stream  
In the cold, gray frost.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let other men not do
What our men had done
With their crazy heads
With foolish minds
Some bought an old horse
Some took a widow for a wife
The widow was a wife to the death of bread
The horse was old to the death of oats
Buy a horse from the stable
Take a wife from the maidens
The horse neighs from the stable
The wife laughs from the maidens.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The frog bit Kaie's thighs  
The snake broke Maie's thighs.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Before I play  
I wrestle a bear with moss  
Before those girls  
Who were half-promised  
Loved before their time  
Caressed over the hollow.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Hello father-in-law, hello mother-in-law  
May I come into the room  
To sweep the yard  
To hang sprats on the wall  
To nail the pole to the post  
Hello new door posts  
Hello waxwings  
I myself am the fire starter  
I myself am the corner chopper.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I could have otherwise run in my black [coat]  
Could have driven a cool car  
Could have sailed a boat  
Before those girls  
Who are half-married  
Loved too early.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
If this man were mine  
If this partner were a companion  
Who rides along the road here  
Galloping along the trail  
Two are grey in front  
Six have crane feathers  
Seven are big horses  
If this man does not take me  
Let his horse die  
And himself grow old  
Wearing a hat yellowed  
Let his belt vanish from his waist.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Do not hate me for the storm  
Do not hate me for the frost  
Hate the berries of the bird cherry  
The branches of the rowan tree  
Of course men hate  
Otherwise boys despise  
Gray spruces bark  
Bluecoats mutter  
So they don't demand men's consent  
Do not follow the boys' lead  
I demand the old woman's consent  
I demand the old man's consent  
I sit at the old woman's chair  
I step on the old man's step  
I walk on the old woman's path to the well  
I step on the old man's path to the barn  
Because men hate me  
Because boys despise me.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Auntie, granny  
Auntie, uncle  
Heard me coming home  
To be at Väila  
To sing behind the forest  
He immediately jumped into the cabbage garden  
To throw out the pile  
To trample down the malt  
May I come indoors  
To tie up the salted fish by the wall  
To chase around the yard  
To tie foal to mare.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Young men, gentle brothers  
The sashes are for the boys  
We put on black uniforms  
Ears covered in bear skins  
We ride down to the city of Riga  
There were three groups of girls there.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sister Ella, didn’t I say
Not to tease the young, didn’t I scold
Dear wife, didn’t I say
Friend Ella, didn’t I argue
Don’t go to the young man
Without a grown-up partner
A partner does not grow on its own
A man does not want to take one
But a partner inherits faults
And a man inherits troubles
Every noon new plates
Every month a new coat
Every day a white shirt.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to Soole's field  
Down outside to the hay meadow  
I take a bow, leave the other  
I begin to gather hay  
What do I find under the bark?  
I find an eagle under the bark  
What is under the eagle's wing?  
An axe under the eagle's wing.  
What is on the axe's nose?  
A shaving on the axe's nose  
What is cut from that shaving?  
A sauna shed for Jaan is cut.  
What is put in that shed?  
A bed is put in the shed.  
What is inside that bed?  
Straw is inside the bed.  
What is on top of the straw?  
A mattress is on top of the straw.  
What is on top of the mattress?  
A pillow is on top of the mattress.  
What is on top of the pillow?  
Two young maidens in pairs  
One is sewing a linen ribbon  
The other is tying a silk ribbon  
The third is weaving a golden belt  
The fourth is twisting a twisted braid  
The fifth is braiding a sow's tail  
The sixth is braiding a cat's paw.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Oh, our one little sister  
Oh, our two little darlings  
Sparks of one room  
Little birds of one bread  
Together we eat, together we drink  
Together we sleep  
Together heads on the pillow  
Together feet under the carpet.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's take Maie as a bride  
Another takes another woman's bride  
Another takes another old woman's daughter  
You get Kaie, I get Maie  
Kai was clumsy at work  
Mai was plump on her feet.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
My brothers like those stakes  
Cousins like those streams  
Male cousins full-grown men  
Didn’t take debts, didn’t steal  
Did no harm to anyone  
No excess for anyone  
The Germans did not bring them to court  
The parish did not take them to court.  
Munde’s brothers like those frogs  
Took debts and stole  
The Germans did bring them to court  
The parish did take them to court.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Ellakene, little brother  
We had been many before  
Had many, walked in herds  
It was like a band of comrades  
Walked like a village in a valley  
Now the herd has come to an end  
The flock has been driven into place.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Young men, strong brothers  
Let us put the horses in the harness  
Grey ones with silver beads  
Brown eyes with rings  
Then we go through the villages.  
Through the trampled street  
A shining pearl horse gleams  
The street echoes with powder  
Golden claws snap at the curve  
Behind, the iron sled rattles  
The girl looks from the side  
Calls along from the chamber  
If only that man were mine  
Who goes through the villages  
Through the trampled street  
A shining pearl horse gleams  
The street echoes with powder  
Golden claws snap at the curve  
Behind, the iron sled rattles  
I would wash his head  
And comb his neck  
Wash his stockings and shoes  
Dip them in the water.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Jews drink in the tall grass  
But I run in the ears too  
On Monday I drank a new skin  
On Tuesday I drank another shirt  
Wednesday a nice fur coat  
On Thursday I drank among them  
On Friday I drank my clothes off  
On Saturday I drank a wide belt  
On Sunday I drink my pants off  
Mart was otherwise a fashionable man  
But had bad manners  
I don’t chase girls  
I don’t sleep at Mari’s  
I don’t love Anne either  
I don’t put a collar on Kaie  
I don’t even pull Liisud close  
A virgin, a young girl  
Slender and neat in writing  
Come to me, I’ll come to you  
I’ll put the hem to work  
I'll make stockings for sleeping  
Rope for winter’s tether  
I’ll sew barley into the fields  
Oats on the riverbanks  
Flax I’ll grow in Liutsalu  
Horse’s barley in the fields  
Bear’s oats on the banks.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I was supposed to go to Pille  
But I fell asleep at Malle’s  
Malle’s (man)nuts are sweeter  
Than Pille’s cheesecakes.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Last year I fed the little blackbird  
The year before last, the little marten  
I fed the black one for the lads  
I fed the gray one for Anne  
For the flaxen-haired Leena  
I took the black one to Maie and others  
The desert took Krõõda to the church  
The flaxen-haired one took Leena to the hill  
The gray one took Anne to the barn

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
My black one might run otherwise  
My gray one might carry a saddle  
The boat might drift around the raft  
Before it pulls the peachwood  
They ride those curly tails  
That were halfway fought  
Prematurely loved.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The Vitt was a little liquor mug  
The Pugu was a little dandelion  
The Auk was a dear sweetheart  
But sits on the Harju granary  
Above the Viru distillery  
The boys down to Palve'elle  
Pick the golden button off  
Pick it onto my hat  
Put it on my wide-brimmed hat  
Roll it between my belt  
Why do you milk the gray cow so much?  
You chatter, old red one  
You milk the restless fool  
From the blue ox's secret  
From the red bull's belly  
You are suckled by oxen  
You are teased by bulls  
Courted by young bulls  
I already know the day  
And I think the time  
When your honor was chased away  
When you were boxed in the pen  
When you were stabbed in the street.  
At night it was thought over  
I was called the overseer's whore  
Traded for the fisherman's mate  
If only I were the overseer's whore  
Or the fisherman's mate  
The daughter would have been a worker  
The son would have been a farmer.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
If I were Olev  
I would wear Kalev  
I would keep my own long spear  
I fear stepping on thorns  
I would buy myself new shoes  
I fear the shoes rolling off  
I would take a young wife  
I fear the young one’s crying  
Longing for beauties  
I would hide it in the room  
Put the bow under the bed  
Hiding is troublesome in the room  
The bow might poke the eye  
Father-in-law’s eye might get poked  
Mother-in-law’s eye might get poked

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Fall down, golden doll  
Come down, German apple  
I hear, I respond  
I will not, gold, fall down  
Nor come, German apple  
My life unlived  
A proper swelling unburdened

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
All haytimes are beautiful  
And courtships are lovelier  
All the forests are full of maidens  
And maidens hiding in bed  
My sister, a young maiden  
Runs on the alder leaf  
Dances on the oak branch  
The boy went down to the prayer  
Fall down, golden doll  
I will not fall, little boy  
I will not fall, little boy  
If I fall, who will pay for me?  
Fall on my six shoulders  
Let it fall on my dear shoulders  
I will not fall, golden doll  
But I grow in grandmother’s fear  
In grandmother’s fear, in uncle’s love  
Papa’s dear shoulders  
You will surely grow on the shoulders too  
You will grow into silk beds  
Devil, you also grow on shoulders  
My heart swells into pillows

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go wander to Finland  
For a year or two  
Until then, there is a hunger for girls here  
A hunger for girls, a lack of bread  
Then horses will be sought  
Tried on foal hooves  
What will we still do there  
There we will build a stone church  
Carry a chapel from a karraste (a type of small wooden building)  
We will paint an egg floor

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I had a rich godfather
But an even richer godmother
They gave me a black stallion
I rode to Riga behind Liisu
To Alevis behind Anne
The King of Sweden would have heard
If this man were ours, truly ours
This man would surely pay for berries
From your mouth I would eat, from your mouth I would drink
From your mouth I would eat sugarrille
From the shoulder, however, hazelnuts
In a pile, chicken eggs

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Our men like wild bulls  
Aru Hantsu's gray oxen  
We go into the forest roaring  
Sliding down under the woods.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Listen, dear little brother  
If you are going to take a wife  
Go ask for permission  
I will bring a gray cloak from the barn  
I will bring a blanket from the lantern  
A nice hat from the cellar  
A golden saddle from the stable  
Do not look at the old ones  
Turn your head towards the young ones  
Leave the old troubles  
To the sons of the family  
Look for a fair maiden from the district  
Choose a straight maiden from the crowd  
A beautiful, dear maiden  
A plump, red maiden  
A white maiden like sea foam  
Who would feel like going to the fire  
Without a candle to turn it  
Without a chandelier to illuminate  
To milk the cow at night  
To strain the milk by moonlight  
For the ox to bear a blow  
For the beauty of patterned gloves

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let marti day come  
Marti day, market time  
I’ll buy a nice gray one  
Or that black little goat  
I ride along Nurme bridge  
Snapping Nurme bridge  
Knocking on the sill beam  
I jump on my gray one  
Hat on my head, I lift it  
Then I ride through the villages  
Along the trodden roads  
My cuckoo fell on the song  
Another on another gatepost  
A swallow on my head  
A white bird on the fences  
The maiden watches from the yard  
Squatting, peeking  
At Põhju’s yard gate  
On the street behind Töllak  
If only this man were mine  
Who rides along the road there  
He has a Dutch horse  
A small sleigh with a whip handle

label: Laulud laulust  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I would sing, but must not spread
I fear the song will be criticized
The voice will be shamed
The words will be scrutinized
They will be trimmed in the book
Inserted into the Bible
Brought into disorder
Raised to the testament
Thrown forward tomorrow
Filled tonight
Still, we eat at the table
On unwashed hands
On a sleepy bed
Do you hear, young men
Why are you loitering in the village
In the cold barn of the village
In the empty room of the farm
In the wretched hut of Võera verse
In the unheated building
I certainly understand, I hear again:
Father, mother
If you don't give me a wife
I will still loiter in the village
In the cold room of the village
In the empty barn of the farm
In the wretched hut of Võera verse
In the unheated building
If only I had a woman’s embrace
That too would be dear to me
I would not wander at night
Nor doze off in the room’s corner
Nor fall off the bench

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Dear little brother, dear brother,
When you called me,
Called me, tried to call me,
You shouted over the fence.
I will not take you to the hayfield,
Nor to the alley,
The village boys are mowing the hay,
The pole carts are loading,
I know how to mow properly,
The rake bends eightfold,
I will put you to work on the hill,
I will make you shout,
To prepare a place for the year,
To find a place for the day.
I managed to send it to you,
Again I left it to you,
You put the sod into the pit,
To chop the firewood for the day,
To make a fire for the year.
Come to me, young maiden,
Come to me and speed,
I will bring you a blue apron,
A blue, red apron,
I will bring you a cardboard collar,
Up to your waist with fur,
Indeed, I will bring a striped one.
Come to me, young maiden,
Come to me and speed,
I will bring buns with my greetings,
I will bring cakes with my kisses,
Apples when coming outside,
Honey breads when leaving.
I managed to send it to you,
Again I left it to you,
You took me to the delicate hayfield,
You carried me to the golden alley,
You put the sod into the pit,
To chop the firewood for the day,
To make a fire for the year.
The fist was a checkered apron,
A precious cardboard collar,
A fur shawl,
A bundle of twigs striped.
Oh, poor me, oh poor me,
If I had known, could have known,
If only the ashes of the beer barrels,
The vodka jugs had been drawn away,
That I was courted to evil,
A target of anger.
I thought to find a place to be,
To find a great bread place,
To know a barley place.
I got a big beggar's place,
Found a big place of sorrow,
I knew the place of thistles.
Young maiden, young girl,
You were made for village boys,
Needed by farmhands.
There, boys are not cooked,
Farmhands are not wanted,
Thistles are searched for there,
Nettles are picked,
Herbal tea is brewed,
You are given food,
No salt inside,
No milk drops outside.
You eat until your heart cries,
Your eyes shed tears,
From the eyes they run down the chest,
From the chest to the waist,
From the waist to the toes,
From the toes to the furrow,
From the furrow to the field.
From there the geese swim,
The grandmother waters the cloth shed.
Your young crying water,
Your poor laboring water.
You think of the husband's honey cake,
Bringing along the Kalev container,
You got a husband who confused you,
A husband who cut your throat,
You got an apron for your knees,
You got a thresher as a nuisance.
You could have been alone,
Been alone, gone alone,
From a girl made a worker,
Put your head into braids,
Tied your hair with silk.
If only the child came up to the forehead,
To wear the skirt to the ankles.
Oh that golden maidenhood,
Hair smooth and untouched,
Beloved brush not used.
Now you have become an old woman,
Now your tufts face the wind,
The braids are against the rain.
Women praised their aprons and knees,
Respected the thresher underfoot.
You were mad to believe,
You quickly jumped to try,
Take an apron for the household,
Take the thresher underfoot,
Because I thought better of the apron,
To be more honorable under the thresher,
So that no whore stories flow,
Nor touch prostitute tales.
I got to be, I got to live,
I heard a whore in the morning,
A broad whore, white-faced,
I heard prostitutes whispering,
I hear, respond to it.
The whore had a beautiful life,
Whores walked proudly,
Whores too white,
Prostitutes walked properly.

I heard a whore in the morning,

I heard prostitutes whispering,
I hear, respond to it.
The whore had a beautiful life,
Whores walked proudly,
Whores too white,
Prostitutes walked properly.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sister dear, don’t cry  
I will not leave you behind  
It is still me who will take you  
If only we had a garden  
If only we had a yard  
Gates strong from the whip  
Sister dear, sweetie  
Perhaps you have opposed  
Given yourself to the boys  
When suitors came for you  
Perhaps those few boys were  
Pipe players, little boys  
Kantele strummers, comrades  
Sister dear, sweetie  
Slender in handwriting  
A maiden of the framed script  
Sister dear, sweetie  
Let’s gather the three of us  
Let’s consult as four  
Maybe as five or more  
Let’s leave this land  
Let’s go to Finland  
For winter behind the Russians  
For three months to Courland  
For two days to Pärnu County  
We’ll build a stone church there  
We’ll make the town bloom  
Half the town from cranberries  
Benches from rowan trees  
Towers from bird cherry  
From where no young man escapes  
No, not big young men  
The highest hats of winter  
Let the land face a maiden’s hunger  
Maiden hunger, poverty of bearings  
Then they’ll surely search for us  
They’ll ride along the boned legs  
Test on the foal’s hooves  
Cutting fire with the shoulder blades  
Sparks flew from the boned shins  
Lightning cutting foal’s hooves  
Whip lashes struck the reins’ sides  
Searching for the maiden flock  
Trying to catch the bearing flock  
Oh wonder, or no wonder  
What I saw, no wonder  
In the yard, while walking  
I saw Saaremaa burning  
Fire coming through the hatch  
Swamps ignited, lakes burned  
Stones spun in whirlpools  
Stars cut like dice  
Sword tips like stabbing  
The empty Saaremaa mourns  
The other Saaremaa men  
The maidens of Saare weep  
Belt weavers, coif makers  
Writers of wheel script  
Readers of the wide book.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Virgins, young girls  
What do you think of yourselves  
That you scorn boys  
Choosing white heads  
Do you know where you might end up  
Some end up as drinking companions  
Some become Sami girls  
Walking the tavern roads  
Loitering by tavern doors  
Sleeping by tavern bars  
Holding tavern benches  
When coming home even from the tavern  
Throwing stockings into the manure  
Shoes into the middle of the floor  
Take Kai, put her to dry  
Maie, spread out in the barn  
Ann, put by the edge of the stove  
If not Kai, then put Maie to dry  
Then the village buzzes, paying wages  
Then there was spinning the whirl  
Clapping hands together  
Fists were striking  
Virgin, young girl  
Why do you resist when coming to me  
To me, a young man  
To pea-heads, to the proud ones  
To the hat-wearers, to the high ones  
I would have long ago built a granary  
The granary built from anise straw  
The roof made from chicken eggs  
The door framed with wolf's ribs  
The floors made from elk meat  
Inside made silk beds  
Silk beds, thread games  
For the young young girl to sleep

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Fuck off, song thrush  
Of course, I’ll get a husband  
From a second family’s nonsense

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Dear little brother,  
Do not take a tall woman.  
The tall one goes to sleep in the parlor,  
But the sparks hit the wall,  
Flat feet on the stable floor.  
Always take a short woman,  
The short one milks the cows,  
Drives the herd far away,  
Calves all to the barn,  
Goats to the Kriima fold,  
Horses to Aru hill.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Maybe my black could otherwise run
Maybe Kimmel could pull the sled
Maybe the gray one could rush to the barn
The boat could sail around the shore
Before the peaches are ripe
Before those maidens
Who were half-promised
Loved prematurely
Overplayed
I got the chance, I hear again
A bit of those rowans
Certain of those aspens
Barely of the grove
Where there are no spruces
No juniper around the hill
Near the visible alder
Thus those maidens
Who were untouched by the boy
But the hand, dear, untouched

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Then the hayfield was beautiful  
And the arches were lovely too  
When the hay was not yet cut  
The scythe mows, I whistle  
The rake rakes, I sing  
And there was still warm weather  
Mosquitoes despite efforts to chase away  
Pushing in for company  
Horseflies to live in pairs  
The bulls went to the forest  
Energetic bulls, good oxen  
Lazy bulls remain for me  
Lazy bulls, the barn oxen

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I thunder as I pass by  
I rush through, I strike with my hand  
I do not hide my head in my lap  
I do not cover my eyes with a cloth  
I do not want to be a stable boy  
The stable boy’s nose is runny  
I do not want to be a miller  
The miller’s stone hums  
I do not want to be a miller  
The miller’s stone hums  
I do not want to be a weaver  
The weaver’s shuttle rattles  
I do not want to be a stable boy  
I do not want to be a coachman  
The coachman’s hands are wet

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Last year, the south wind blew elsewhere  
In Hiiumaa, the south wind came late  
In Harjumaa, the south wind came long ago  
In Järvamaa, the south wind came again  
Our south wind remains uncut  
Fish remain uncaught  
Seaweed porridge unpounded  
Salt untwisted inside  
The south wind clock has already struck  
Everyone else has long eaten  
I have no one forcing me to eat  
Nor setting a wide table  
I have no wife who bakes bread  
Nor child who braids hair  
I walk alone and walk alone  
I sleep alone and worry alone  
Saddened beneath the spruce  
Who will come to see the poor  
Bring the distressed to healers  
To comfort the sorrowful

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Last year, the south wind blew elsewhere  
In Hiiumaa, the south wind came late  
In Harjumaa, the south wind came long ago  
In Järvamaa, the south wind came again  
Our south wind remains uncut  
Fish remain uncaught  
Seaweed porridge unpounded  
Salt untwisted inside  
The south wind clock has already struck  
Everyone else has long eaten  
I have no one forcing me to eat  
Nor setting a wide table  
I have no wife who bakes bread  
Nor child who braids hair  
I walk alone and walk alone  
I sleep alone and worry alone  
Saddened beneath the spruce  
Who will come to see the poor  
Bring the distressed to healers  
To comfort the sorrowful

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The little huts of Koigi village  
The patched shoes of Tamsi taku  
The ones hanging sprats eye to eye  
The ones knocking down the spinning wheel  
The fish drying rack legs  
The rope village’s containers  
Who considers this village insignificant  
The village of Kahala insignificant  
From there came the smell of pigs  
The smell of pigs, the smell of manure  
The smell of the earth, the smell of soil  
Even rats were in their barn  
Even in the corner of the stove  
Even on the edge of the tray  
Those were the barn doors made of straw  
The sagging garden gates  
Who considers this village insignificant  
The village of Keris insignificant  
The village smells of caraway  
The village’s grain smells of sulfur  
The stove’s pot full of bow-headed lice  
The trays filled with bread and pastries  
The sheaves of wheat piled even higher  
The barn doors silver in color  
The stove lids golden in color  
The garden gates made of copper  
The young men of Keri village  
Went to Viru to bring sprats  
At that time there was the clattering of fives  
The clattering of old hats  
The rattling of bicycle hubs  
The ringing of spokes  
The horses gave great beats  
When they rode to the shore of Viru  
From there they got sprat fish  
They paid the shore taxes  
They brought a load of expensive grain  
A hundred puds of sprat fish  
The shore man gave them a home  
The hostess ran to the barn  
What are you waiting for, wash the horse  
I will give you a sprat bath  
The hunchbacked women of Köisi village  
Came quickly with sprat trays  
I gave them as a gift without forbidding  
I gave an explanation without stopping  
Go home, give thanks  
Take good care of the family  
Stop eating containers  
Now take good care  
Eat sprats, sip and eat  
Do your work, then shake it off  
The girls are quick to spin  
The boys are cutting wood  
Balls of wives were brought inside  
The heap of wives was assembled

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Evening came unexpectedly  
The day ended without hope  
The village boy came uninvited  
He put his hand on without asking  
Uiu uiu, sleep came over  
Sleep came over, Jüri came closer  
Jüri told me  
That there is a moose in the field  
A bear was in the oat field  
The bear bit Kaie’s thighs  
Anne was bitten by a wolf  
The fox ate Tiiu’s thighs  
There was a high lump on the belly  
Below the navel, there was a little bit

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust

Sleep came over, Jüri came closer  
Jüri told me  
That there is a moose in the field
