Anne was bitten by a wolf  
The fox ate Tiiu’s thighs  
There was a high lump on the belly  
Below the navel, there was a little bit

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Mother, little old lady  
Marry off this daughter  
If you don't marry her off, don't beg either  
She will come knocking at the door  
Mushroom picking along the wall  
Boys farting on top of her  
Birch, birch on her neck  
Willow on the sheaves  
Willow bush on her knee  
Juniper between her legs

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Why do you men entice me  
You bad boys love me  
I can’t become a bride for you  
Nor bear a husband for you  
Mine is not here  
Nor a husband growing here  
Mine is in Holland  
Even in the town of Pärnu  
May the wind carry greetings to him  
May the clouds stretch long  
May the hail carry messages  
If he is sick, may God help him  
If he is well, may he work.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Oh me, poor white lamb  
I crouch in the village street  
All the rams take my part  
All the male sheep follow me  
May the lambs be used up.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I would wait for a beautiful horse  
I fear going to Germany  
I should have a long mind  
I fear touching the heel  
I would take a young bride  
I fear the young one’s crying  
Hiding things indoors  
The hiding was a nuisance in the room  
A bow to the eye pricker  
The father-in-law pricks the eye  
The mother-in-law pricks the eye  
Those are scoldings that grate

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Uuksi manor was a playroom  
Koigi manor was a somersault  
Kareda village was a hill  
Sargvere stood as a tiny pin  
Palu lay belly down  
At Esna manor teeth were bared  
At Kodasema the nape was bent  
Müüsleri manor brought out the Moors  
It gave the bailiffs a beating  
Köisi manor was a crouch  
Silmsi manor was a pin  
At Mäo manor there were wet eyes  
Aruküla showed no mercy  
It pulled village girls’ hair  
At Ärva there was good work  
People sang day and night  
Norramõis had sleepy eyes  
Spirit boys, all single  
Udeva had good deeds  
Filled the stomach of the stable jockey  
Our men like wild bulls  
Aru Jaan’s gray bulls  
Raging and bellowing up the hill  
Running to the ditch furrow  
That the manor’s thoughts pass by  
Keri manor is a poor manor  
It reports the aftermath  
I do a piece of work after another  
There is no nape behind the stable  
There is no assistant behind the barn

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Keri and the rough boys  
The boys of Silmsi and Sigapusma  
The gentle brothers of Veikse Köis  
They all gather on the meadow  
I put one ribbon on  
Then I get a good bunch from there  
I take a big bundle inside  
Girls, take them to meet  
Take care of the dear ones

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
Do you want to come to me  
No, I’ll come to you  
I had two little dogs  
One black, the other brindle  
The black one barked in the yard  
The brindle one went with me to herd

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
Do you want to come to me  
No, I’ll come to you  
I had two little dogs  
One black, the other brindle  
The black one barked in the yard  
The brindle one went with me to herd

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I had a horse with blinders  
Dear little shopkeeper  
I endured thistles in my pocket  
I carried water in my sleeve  
I managed to feed the stallion lice  
Fed the lice, watered the lice  
I started to drive with a grey horse  
To rush with a roan  
I rode along home  
Let the horse run under the gate  
There was hay, there were oats  
There was hay made by grandmother  
Oats grown by grandfather

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
At the front, in the evening  
Gather the bundles together  
Home to eat, to the threshing floor  
Put the flail down, to sleep  
Straw alone, two stalks  
People in couples  
Each with their own  
Each partner with a necktie  
The old one went with his old (partner)  
The sister went with her man  
I go crying towards the corner  
Towards the empty bed  
I fumble for the old coat  
But strike the frame against the wall  
All were cold side tables  
Cool footstools  
Weathered edge tables.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
What is under our yard there  
The sea is under our yard there  
What is in the middle of the sea there  
A well is in the middle of the sea there  
What is in the middle of the well there  
A pillar is in the middle of the well there  
What is even on the top of the pillar  
A sieve is on the top of the pillar  
What is on the bottom of the sieve there  
A brooch is on the bottom of the sieve there  
What is on the eye of the brooch there  
A ring is on the eye of the brooch there  
Where is this ring brought from  
It is brought from there, it is obtained from there.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
What is under our yard there  
The sea is under our yard there  
What is in the middle of the sea there  
A well is in the middle of the sea there  
What is in the middle of the well there  
A pillar is in the middle of the well there  
What is even on the top of the pillar  
A sieve is on the top of the pillar  
What is on the bottom of the sieve there  
A brooch is on the bottom of the sieve there  
What is on the eye of the brooch there  
A ring is on the eye of the brooch there  
Where is this ring brought from  
It is brought from there, it is obtained from there.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Evening comes, arrives late  
Evening comes late along  
Twilight brings the fruit along  
When will my partner come  
My partner from far away  
A lover on the Harju path  
The other half to Pohlamaa  
May the wind take health to him  
Hail to the rye in the fields  
Clouds long for spring  
So many greetings to him  
When the birch was at the fire  
The apple tree's true mother  
The currant bush's upright mother  
The maple tree's white mother  
The apple tree on the hill is true  
The maple tree on the hill is white  
I am truly the bride's mother

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
A girl with wheels from Aidu-raidu  
Before me, I play with tussocks  
I jump with birch stumps  
Before those girls  
Who were halfway courted  
Lovemaking prematurely done  
From above, a virgin was visible  
Below, long a woman  
Below, bitten by a snake  
Above, squeezed by a boy  
The girl twitched from nerves.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Little lad, little boy  
Why do you brag for no reason  
Boast excessively  
About your hat in the village  
Too early about your pipe stem  
Pride about six ears of grain  
Greatness about your boot sole  
Little lad, little boy  
Where is the midwife’s cradle cloth  
Where are the father-in-law’s tobacco rolls  
Where are the bride’s red shoes  
You don’t even have a barn  
You have no pocket either  
That is your bragging  
Excessive boasting.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Maiden, young girl  
Let’s gather the three of us together  
Across the isthmus, the five of us  
We will go to Finland for the summer  
Not to the seas for the winter  
Surely we are sought on the horse  
Salmon are ridden on the pig  
The foal is touched by the hoof  
Vaadu struck fire  
Sparks flew from the salmon’s shins  
The harness of the ricksha, the reins, the horse’s shoes  
While searching among the maidens  
Trying to catch the maiden’s flock.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Maiden, young girl  
Let’s gather the three of us together  
Across the isthmus, the five of us  
We will go to Finland for the summer  
Not to the seas for the winter  
Surely we are sought on the horse  
Salmon are ridden on the pig  
The foal is touched by the hoof  
Vaadu struck fire  
Sparks flew from the salmon’s shins  
The harness of the ricksha, the reins, the horse’s shoes  
While searching among the maidens  
Trying to catch the maiden’s flock.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go swing on the swing
Let's sing a swinging song
Will this swing carry us
If it doesn't carry, let it go away
My brother is making a new swing
A new swing, a grand swing
He makes a German swing from hazel wood
A golden swing from dry spruce
He makes a proper swing from apple wood
A fine swing from rowan
A smooth swing from alder
Here are the men from this land
My man will come from Germany
A beauty from Hiiumaa
A maiden from Muhumaa
Antsukene comes from Aaveri
Peetrikene from Pikevere
Jaanikene from Jalalõpe
To Jaani's sled I will go
I will go to Peetri's place
Under Ants's grey coat.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
What are you staring at, old boy  
You should sometimes get a wife  
Your beard has turned gray  
Your asshole has become fungal  
The edges of your ears have yellowed.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The wolf bit large thighs  
The snake bit Maie's thighs  
The bear bit Kaie's thighs  
The snake had bitten Anne  
The bear had bitten Kaie’s heel.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
After all, my black one could have run otherwise  
The boat could have rocked on the shore  
The coffin could have been quietly removed  
Before those maidens  
Who were half-married  
Loved prematurely  
Overacted  
Hopped over the fence  
The pigs had nested inside  
The groves had washed the calves’ legs.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I am always bored
I am always miserable
Many times my mind is sad
Because my dear one is far away
My good man is across the sea
I cannot even talk to him
Nor listen to his voice
I cannot look for him by day
Nor patch clothes by the month
Nor make plans for the year
Across the marsh I gave my effort
Across the bend I gave my hands
Across the river I told words
The wind may carry them whole
Hail may carry them to the books
The clouds may carry them far
The sky keeps a wise mind
So many blessings to him
As there is wood in the stove
Wood of alder by the white mother
Wood of hazel by the straight mother
Wood of apple by the true mother.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
We had four young girls  
One of them flaps canvases  
Another claps hands  
The third weaves a golden belt  
The fourth cries for a young man  
Why do you cry for a young man  
You have a thick forest above your head  
You have a deep forest beneath your feet  
Your body is from a twisted forest.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Feed Halja, give water to Halja  
Find a place for Halja.  
At Kimmel  
In the shade, I drew water  
In my pocket, I carried flour  
In a bundle, I carried oats  
So that I could get strength again for Hall  
Then I go to Koiki  
Koiki, the miller’s daughter.  
Koiki the miller had three daughters  
One was Anni, the second Kaie  
The third a slippery Leenuke.  
One was Annes - the naked one  
The second Krõõta - burnt chin  
The third Leenu - with clubfoot.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Little brother, little man  
Don’t take a wife from the manor  
At the manor there are certain women  
The beautiful tries to seduce  
The white one glares over the shadow  
Little brother, little man  
Take a wife from the village instead  
She will always work  
She must support your family.  
Don’t you take a wife from the manor  
The manor woman plows with a tail  
She will drag the land away.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Who is allowed to be evil,  
To be loud to me second:  
I am the sweaty lord’s slave,  
The lady’s ancient servant.  
Let the lord come to the field,  
I will complain to the dear lady,  
I will talk with the gardener,  
Immediately I will speak with the bailiff.  
The lord brings beautiful pearls,  
The lady brings a red wreath,  
The young lady brings a pink scarf;  
The gardener brings a red ribbon,  
The steward brings a spotted scarf,  
The bailiff brings a moonlight glow.  
Then the region goes through the villages;  
All village dogs bark,  
Old women watch.  
But I, I let the dogs bark:  
Noise-noise-quietly, little drummer,  
Blacksmith’s little neat one,  
Pärtel’s little gray wreath!  
From you the wolf will get a share,  
The wild one will have his fun;  
The black one will get the boys’ bride,  
The manor men’s plaything,  
The foster boys’ calf,  
The tea boys’ little infant,  
The gardeners’ darling,  
The stewards’ little kantele.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's start our journey  
So we can be home by dawn  
To Ämarik's father-in-law's yard  
To Ämarik's mother-in-law's yard  
So we can join the procession  
Those who have no partner, take a cat  
The cat is a beautiful companion  
A four-legged little maiden.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Come to me, young maiden,  
Come to me and yield,  
I will set work to your lap,  
For a month I will have you patching coats,  
For a day I will have you searching for heads,  
For a night I will have you giving strength,  
For a year I will have you arranging places.  
I will not take you into the rain,  
Nor send you into storms,  
I will not send you into cold dew,  
Be silent, you lie,  
Hold your mouth, you deceive.  
If I get to make love to you,  
Then I will remain yours again,  
Then you will take me into the rain,  
Then you will send me into storms,  
Then you will make barley grow into valleys,  
You will grow oats far away,  
You will make linen halfway through the town,  
Then you will take me into the rain,  
Then you will send me into storms.  
Come, young maiden,  
Come and yield,  
Come to my village to keep watch,  
To look after my property,  
To the village, to the hamlet,  
To guard my belongings.  
Be silent, you lie,  
Hold your mouth, you deceive.  
If you were in the village,  
You surely would have a caretaker,  
The young maid would sit on the village porch,  
The father-in-law on the edge of the bench,  
The cattle on the hay cover.  
If you were in the village,  
You surely would have a caretaker,  
You would have granaries in your barns,  
Your food would be in the cellar.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The cuckoo calls from the log roof beam  
Another from another beam end  
Last year I fed the little blackbird  
The year before, the marten  
Long ago I fed the gray one  
When the stallion had eaten the oats  
Then I go to shoe the horse  
I put the shoes in front from the iron shop  
Behind I put the shoes from the blacksmith  
Then I go to fetch the maiden  
Then I go under the father-in-law’s door  
Then I go under the mother-in-law’s door  
The father-in-law comes outside to see  
The mother-in-law comes outside to see  
Whose horse is this beautiful one?  
It is my son-in-law’s dear one  
My daughter’s humble one  
That splits the market gates  
Splits the rowan bushes  
Breaks the fragile gates  
This one brings horseshoes from the iron shop  
A ring from the edge of the sieve  
This one is fit for a small house  
To be the husband of the family's daughter  
To own a silver ring  
To be a neighbor with golden corn tassels

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Before I play with tufts of grass  
I jump with birch stumps  
I make jokes with laughs  
Before with those young women  
Who were half courtshiped  
Loves made too early  
Kissed over the valley  
From below bitten by a snake  
From above hugged by a boy

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's get going, men  
Let's light up the pipes  
Smoking noses to smoke  
So we can be home by dawn  
Like a spirit in the father-in-law's yard  
Like a spirit in the mother-in-law's yard  
So we can take the wife to the cottage  
The clock is not for the wife, let the cat take it  
The cat was a dear companion  
A four-legged maiden.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Before I go to the hungry ones  
Before the village herdsmen  
The herdsman drives the herd  
Drives me to wander  
In the foggy morning  
At the early white dawn  
The fog spoils the new month  
The rain spoils the sleigh cover  
The dew spoils the white coat  
Before I go to the thief  
Before the village herdsmen  
Herding, starving  
They killed our black bull  
Our village’s farm bull  
The manor’s red bull  
The servants’ intestines to the zombie pit  
Feet to Jaani’s hayfield  
Ears to the top of the tall spruce  
Herding, starving.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Little Ella, little brother  
When you go to ask for a woman  
Take me along as well  
Even as a bottle in your belly  
As a bread bag in your hand  
Immediately I feel an angry girl  
Soon I will feel a loud girl  
An angry girl from her eyebrows  
From the peach’s belt above  
An angry girl with high eyebrows  
From the peach’s belt below.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Little Hellak, little brother  
We feed it from the barn  
Those who had wide planks  
We take those girls  
Those who had twisted shoelaces  
Pitch-black boots  
Snow-white legs  
White hands, white legs  
White heads, red wreaths  
But shirts underneath are rough  
Patchy and patched  
An envelope with five twigs  
Sulky but brought a twig home  
Underneath it barks the wide fence  
On the back is a silk shirt.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's start walking  
Let's light the pipes  
So that by dawn we get home  
To the father-in-law's yard in the dusk  
To the bridegroom's yard in the dark  
There we have hay, there we also have oats  
Hay is laid out in front  
Oats are carried to the hands  
Spinning wheel is lifted  
To take oneself inside  
To put the grain to sleep  
The tool is left hanging  
Through the spreading barn  
The daughter also shows the old woman  
Is this the paw of a mad wolf  
Or is it the paw of a forest bear  
Or the dried leg of a hare  
But the old woman replies to the daughter  
This is not the paw of a mad wolf  
Nor is it the paw of a forest bear  
This is a woman's joke stick  
It goes under the shirt  
There it twists a twist in the stomach  
There it was warm to wiggle.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's now go through the villages  
Through the trodden street  
Through the proud alleys  
A man rushes, a horse rushes  
The spring street thunders  
The hook snaps with golden nails  
The cuckoo calls to us in the barn loft  
One in the other's barn  
The third on the sleigh's back  
The fourth with the maiden in the cart  
A swallow on the head  
A plow on the man's shoulder  
The maidens watched stealthily  
Crouching, crouching  
Sometimes with four wheels  
Through the thread of time  
If only that man were mine  
Who rides along the road there  
Along the road, along the earth  
A horse silver in front  
Golden itself on top  
An iron sled behind  
A little sleigh with a bubbly tail  
I surely understand, I hear again  
Dear sister, beloved  
Why didn't you come to me last year  
When the rivers ran without oil  
The gray ones brewed vodka  
You came at a bad time  
In the winter snowfall  
In the autumn frost

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Kalendrilaulud
translation:
Come to swing, village maidens,  
Come to Jaan's fire  
Come to keep the fire  
Bring chickens, bring eggs  
Bring birds with webbed feet  
Bring capercaillies with wings  
Come to keep the fire  
The fire is roaming through buildings  
The spark is scratching the walls  
Then the maidens came for fun  
The girls for cheerful songs  
The girls climbed onto the swing  
The girls swung, wreaths gleamed  
But the men watched down the forest  
The boys down by the bridge  
If they had a rifle, they would catch a maiden  
If they had a net, they would demand a maiden  
If they had a trap, they would catch one  
Then I understood, I responded back  
I am not to be caught by a rifle  
Nor demanded by a net  
Nor caught by a trap  
The wolf is caught by a rifle  
Fish are caught by a net  
Caught by a trap.

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Kalendrilaulud
translation:
Come to swing, village maidens,  
Come to Jaan's fire  
Come to keep the fire  
Bring chickens, bring eggs  
Bring birds with webbed feet  
Bring capercaillies with wings  
Come to keep the fire  
The fire is roaming through buildings  
The spark is scratching the walls  
Then the maidens came for fun  
The girls for cheerful songs  
The girls climbed onto the swing  
The girls swung, wreaths gleamed  
But the men watched down the forest  
The boys down by the bridge  
If they had a rifle, they would catch a maiden  
If they had a net, they would demand a maiden  
If they had a trap, they would catch one  
Then I understood, I responded back  
I am not to be caught by a rifle  
Nor demanded by a net  
Nor caught by a trap  
The wolf is caught by a rifle  
Fish are caught by a net  
Caught by a trap.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Father brings me a tender cloth  
Mother a cloth with a warm embrace  
Brother brings me a small cloth  
Sister a very delicate cloth  
My beloved brings a fine and fitting one  
I ride, wheels rattling  
Father takes me to the hayfield  
Mother lays me out on the field  
Brother makes me fence  
Sister gently embraces love  
Beloved’s bike is slender  
It gives me shelter  
Brings me a proud tower farm  
There was a ribbon on the barn roof  
Bracelets were put on my wrists  
Money jingling on my neck  
Garden  
Beads were also on the neck  
Silver pendants  
I had plenty of silver chains  
These hung shimmering on my chest.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
One father gave birth to me  
One mother breastfed us  
Together they rocked us in their arms  
Together we walk hand in hand  
Together we eat meals  
Together we drink drinks  
Together we hug and cuddle  
Together we give kisses  
Together we sleep dreams.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The girl screams, the girl shouts  
Shouts while being caught  
Shouts while being taken to the barn  
She reached the barn, stood still  
Then she stood secretly  
When morning came out  
Then she got loose again  
Let work begin  
Or rather go outside  
Neither has time  
No sound of talking can be heard

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Before I play with the pine cones  
I jump over birch stumps  
But I strike with merry brooms  
Before those maidens  
Who were half-danced  
Lovingly embraced too soon  
Allowed to slip through hands  
To the pain of night, the pain of the moon  
The pain of the sun-crowned bull’s head.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Pulmalaulud
translation:
My father is in the grave, my mother is in the grave,  
My younger brother was in the card barn.  
The sisters sang by the graveside,  
They came into the room sleepwalking,  
Swaying over the yard,  
Watching the gate,  
Neatly sweeping the yard.  
Then the suitor came into the room,  
A suitor, Piia Parve.  
They began to confer,  
Give me some advice,  
Then I should get a long knife,  
A long knife, a fine rifle.  
The knife was as wide as a small table,  
The rifle was just a suitable log,  
It rested on the shoulders,  
Traveling on men's hands,  
Rolling on five fingers,  
Resting on the shoulders.  
I rode past the miller,  
Passed by the smith.  
The miller's stones grind,  
The smith's bellows roar.  
I thought the moon was shining,  
I thought the day was blushing,  
I thought the evening was setting,  
The dawn's gold lighting up.  
I am suitable to be a housekeeper,  
To be the husband of the family daughter,  
To be the owner of a silver ring,  
To be a neighbor with a golden grain.  
I buy a hat for every village,  
I buy a vest for every brother,  
I buy a cleat for every nail,  
Then I am invited in  
And painted for the militia.  
The silky ones come in,  
The tough ones leave out,  
I am the miller's son,  
The wife of Jaak the grinder.  
Below then the alliance of the earth,  
Above the red heaven.  
Then I am suitable to be a housekeeper,  
To be the husband of the family daughter.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Pulmalaulud
translation:
My father is in the grave, my mother is in the grave,  
My younger brother was in the card barn.  
The sisters sang by the graveside,  
They came into the room sleepwalking,  
Swaying over the yard,  
Watching the gate,  
Neatly sweeping the yard.  
Then the suitor came into the room,  
A suitor, Piia Parve.  
They began to confer,  
Give me some advice,  
Then I should get a long knife,  
A long knife, a fine rifle.  
The knife was as wide as a small table,  
The rifle was just a suitable log,  
It rested on the shoulders,  
Traveling on men's hands,  
Rolling on five fingers,  
Resting on the shoulders.  
I rode past the miller,  
Passed by the smith.  
The miller's stones grind,  
The smith's bellows roar.  
I thought the moon was shining,  
I thought the day was blushing,  
I thought the evening was setting,  
The dawn's gold lighting up.  
I am suitable to be a housekeeper,  
To be the husband of the family daughter,  
To be the owner of a silver ring,  
To be a neighbor with a golden grain.  
I buy a hat for every village,  
I buy a vest for every brother,  
I buy a cleat for every nail,  
Then I am invited in  
And painted for the militia.  
The silky ones come in,  
The tough ones leave out,  
I am the miller's son,  
The wife of Jaak the grinder.  
Below then the alliance of the earth,  
Above the red heaven.  
Then I am suitable to be a housekeeper,  
To be the husband of the family daughter.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Mother, little mother  
Give that daughter to me  
Who walks around outside  
Foghead, fog-haired  
Pretty strutters  
If you don’t give, don’t ask  
Let her go home to dig  
Let her stand at the edge to cool  
Let her pick mushrooms by the wall  
Birch by the birch's neck  
Willow tree by the wheat ears  
Spruce by the eyebrow  
Juniper among the hairs

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Marry this daughter off  
To love the oven hearth  
To poke the ash with a stick

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Drive, drive, little horned one  
The plain road is a little plain  
My black horse is able to run  
Able to carry a saddle on the back  
Able to roll a bundle of firewood  
Before bringing those dull ones  
He twisted those wet tails.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Before I play with the tussocks  
Albin with the garden poles  
Before them with the girls  
Whom were half-conquered  
Excessively played out

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Be silent, you lie,
Shut your mouth, you deceive
The barns while helping me
Stacking rye stalks
If you have barns while helping
If you have stalks while stacking
Surely you have a caretaker for the village
And watchers after the property

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Everyone else goes to sleep  
Each one goes their own way  
I go crying toward the corner  
Toward the empty bed  
I fumble with the old fur coat  
Shaking the old garment  
Oh, you old fur coat  
You are good for walking even in harsh weather  
Nice to walk in the swamp  
Beautiful to walk as a shepherd  
It was not appropriate to go to church  
To stand upright in the holy chapel.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
We, both noblemen  
Together comrades  
Both of us have swords at our waists  
Two stirrups on our feet  
Sword tips dragging on the ground  
Stirrups clinking from the motion  
We eat together  
We drink together  
We sleep together  
We do not take each other's wife  
One takes another's wife  
Another takes another's sister  
Then the brawlers gathered  
The brawler drinks, the brawler screams  
Fathers-in-law and mothers-in-law argue  
Men and spirits watch.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I dragged the black mulgu under  
The door of Tuhkur's room  
I myself looked through the door crack  
To see what was going on in the room  
Peeru was clearly burned  
The girl was dozing on the chair  
A runny nose in front  
Rags and tatters around her neck

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Come to my home, little maiden  
To me, a son of a man  
To a sower, to a plowman  
To a turner of black soil  
I will set the scythe to work  
For a month, I will set you to patching clothes  
For a day, to washing pots  
For a year, to making bedding  
I will make barley for the stove  
I will make flax for the linen worker  
Oats straight to the oven  
I understand, I respond  
Dear life, little brother  
You will make barley for a long way  
Flax along half the town's road  
Buckwheat behind the fence

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Before I play with clods  
I strike with joyful rods  
Albin with garden rods  
Before those girls  
Who were half-promised  
Prematurely loved  
Excessively played with  
Before I put gold on the golden neck  
Silver on the bison’s neck  
Rubles on the stallion’s neck  
Pearls on the heifer’s neck  
Before the neck of that girl  
Who was half-promised  
Prematurely loved

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
One that I care for, two that I fear  
One that I care for when greeting  
Two that I fear when shouting  
Three that I listen to in the bushes,  
Mine is not here  
No spouse grows here  
Mine is in Holland  
My groom is in Peipus  
He drinks deeply from the lake  
On the mud, but from the blueberries  
By the garden, but from the greys  
In the chimney, to sprout  
In the room, but from the sable  
The wind takes it all away  
In the sky, with a wise mind  
The clouds long for it  
As many healths to him  
As there are grains of wood  
Of hazel straighter  
Of maple whiter  
Of rowan fresher  
Of sorbus plumper  
The story is over, the child went to the forest  
I quickly left through the gate.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Who got a horse at the gate  
Sent the sled to labor  
The gentleman does not tolerate me without a wife  
The master does not keep a good horse  
What is riding along the road there  
A horse with a pearl in front  
Like a sled behind  
Like the master himself  
A belt like a rainbow in the rain  
When it passes, it nods its head  
Lifts a nice hat  
If that were a man for me  
I would follow after sleep  
I would walk even at the heel of a fly  
I would stand on the leg of the leg.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud laulust
translation:
What I fear when singing  
When singing, when in poetry  
My own is not here  
My companion does not grow here  
My own is in Holland  
My companion far away  
Drinks by the lake with a sigh  
From the mud, a blueberry  
From the stone, a glitter  
From the edge of the fence, a little gray  
I would sing, but must not sing  
I fear the singing will cause harm  
I have listeners at home  
Speakers in the bushes  
Words are taken seriously  
Scratched into a book  
Inserted into paper  
Thrown forward tomorrow  
Carried forward in meaning  
Still, at the dining table  
On unwashed hands  
On an unfed heart.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
In the town of Paide, a pack of Germans  
All were young masters  
None wore a linen shirt on their back  
Nor a leather coat on their back  
In our village, ten boys  
All wore golden hooks on their backs  
They were always at the manor  
On Sundays, pants with suspenders

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Mari was fond of strawberries  
In Ahto's boys' hawthorn bush  
Behind the room in the oak grove  
A linden tree, very beautiful  
Mari already knew that  
She went on the swing at the end  
She let the linden branches move  
Then the boys' eyes turned red  
They made completely different weather  
Fall down, little Mari  
Cheer me up, little Anne  
If you don't fall, we won't call  
If you don't let go, we won't try.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Others had brothers five or six  
Kalevites two or three  
I still have no brother  
From Kalev not even two  
My brothers sleep in the soil  
Six below in Ambelas  
Bluebirds in the sandbox  
But I bring a sand sieve from the town  
From Alevi I bring Alva’s sieve  
I sieve the sand towards the town  
Black soil towards Narva.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Young girl, young lad  
Fine to write letters to  
Come to me and hurry  
I will put you to easy work  
To thresh rye ears  
To stretch the bundles  
To stack the heaps in the barn  
Dear one, brother  
This is not easy work  
Put me to sleep on the rug  
To wander on the stove  
To turn on the bed  
This is easy work

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
What did we have in the middle of the village?  
We had a swing in the middle of the village.  
What else was in the middle of our village?  
There was a forest in the middle of our village.  
What was in the middle of the forest?  
The sea was in the middle of the forest.  
What was in the middle of the sea?  
A lake was in the middle of the sea.  
What was in the middle of the lake?  
A well was in the middle of the lake.  
What was in the middle of the well?  
A pole was in the middle of the well.  
What was on top of the pole?  
A sieve was on top of the pole.  
What was at the bottom of the sieve?  
A ring was at the bottom of the sieve.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The shepherd tended the edge of the flock  
But I had to go look for them  
On that foggy morning  
The fog ruined the new hay  
The dew kissed the grasses  
I put the hay to dry  
On the rowan tree to fade  
On the apple tree to wither  
But a crow came and took the first batch  
The second crow took the second batch  
The third wicked one took the whole lot away  
I went home crying  
The woman came to meet me at the gate  
The old man came to meet me in the street  
Why do you cry, young daughter?  
The shepherd tended the edge of the flock,  
But I had to go look for them  
On that foggy morning  
The fog ruined the new hay  
The dew kissed the grasses  
I put it to dry  
On the rowan tree to fade  
On the apple tree to wither  
But a crow came and took the first batch  
The second crow took the second batch  
The third wicked one took the whole lot away  
Don't cry, young daughter  
I will make you a new bundle  
Of birch bark to wrap  
From a pea pod to clip  
If it wraps, it rustles  
If it clips, it squeaks.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Help reached the oak grove  
Reached the oak grove path  
There was bad girl’s goods  
Five got an old song as payment  
Six got dry shoes as payment  
Seven got a row of boots as payment  
A hundred old rows as payment  
A thousand winds for a breeze as payment  
They were taken to the herd’s pen  
To be separated into a shed  
From them a bridge was made to the swamp  
To make a fence to the field  
Still, the old hunchbacks didn’t end  
Nor did the old village boys

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Who is allowed to boast,  
Here is a man who wants to fight,  
Or to pull by the hair,  
Or to pull a tug rope.  
Whether it is an ox or a horse,  
A man, some kind of one,  
I still resist with all my strength,  
I will not give up power.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I had a good horse,  
Regi the iron one behind,  
A little sled with a shining tail;  
Silver runners on the sled. -  
The cuckoo sings on a curved branch.  
The capercaillie's second perch,  
The third on the sled's head;  
When I walked down the street,  
The graves barked, the cracks snapped,  
Chunks of mud squished. -  
When I walked down the street  
The girls looked from the shadows.  
Spinning tops, floating tops,  
Humped tops, pawed tops,  
Crouching tops in the village street,  
At the Persel family's gate. -  
If only this were a man for me,  
If only he were a companion too,  
Who walks through the street;  
The graves barked, the cracks snapped,  
Chunks of mud squished.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Young maiden, little one,  
Why didn’t you come to me last year,  
The year before that you did not know me.  
When did you go to fetch orders?  
Fifties but spirits, sixties courtship,  
Hundreds of endless messages.  
I would have long ago built a shed,  
Built a shed to shelter you,  
A chamber to keep the hen.  
Made silk beds inside,  
Silk beds, thread plays,  
To sleep on the young young one;  
To lull the new sleeper,  
To take the beloved husband in your arms.  
The maiden hears, responds back:  
“I will go there where it was created,  
Maybe the garden is more loving,  
To milk the cow by the flank,  
Gates twisted from branches.  
To bark like a dog by the side,  
I will go there, where it was created.”

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
If I had a beloved woman,
That would indeed be dear to me,
Then there wouldn’t be wandering at night,
Napping in the corner behind the barn,
Not falling off the bench,
Not slipping off the sauna bench,
I turn over and find cold,
I turn my back and find a wall,
The sideboards were cold
And the edge boards were chilly.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Sister Ellakene  
You were married off from the forest  
From the tops of the trees to the boy  
The trees were your wedding guests  
The white birches were the maids  
The alders were the taller sisters  
The wolf was the wedding elder  
The bear was the dear blanket  
The pig was the great carriage horse  
The fox was the coachman  
The hare was the lively bridegroom  
A piece of fodder was the dining table  
The garden bed was the bench  
In front was the sow's roast  
At the back was the lamb's roast  
In the middle was the colorful bird's roast.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Why should I care while lazing around
Fearful despite shouting
I have no fiancé in that land
My fiancé is in Peipus
Stayed by the lake in Paitsi
May the wind bring greetings
The clouds long for it
The sky for a wise spirit
Garden rows for a beloved
So many health wishes for him
If he is in the woods at the store
Smooth and straight
False and fair-skinned
Crooked and righteous.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Listen, you brother, little brother,
If you go to ask for a wife -
Do not turn your head from the girl,
Do not squeeze her chest;
Then put your apple
Green leaf under your shoulder
Call me to be a friend,
I know a dull maiden:
The dull one has tousled hair.
I know a lazy maiden:
The lazy one’s face is wider.
I know a bitter maiden:
The bitter one has a sharp nose.
I also know a weak one:
The weak one’s waist is low.
But I recognize a lively maiden,
The lively one’s strides are quick,
Round all around.
Finally, I know a brisk maiden:
The brisk one has lighter strides.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Your fiancé, a lively young lad,
Goes to church on Midsummer's Day,
Under an apple tree if it's shady,
By himself if it's windy,
A small rein of leather,
A piece of rope on his hat.
Our fiancé, slender,
Goes to church on Midsummer's Day,
Under an apple tree like an oven;
By himself like the daylight,
Hat like a church bell,
Rein like a rain rainbow.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Old man, old and weak,  
With a gray beard, damn it,  
Would take a young wife,  
Fears a young woman’s crying,  
A beautiful longing,  
Would buy her a ripe apple,  
Fears breaking the apple,  
Would need a long stick,  
Fears touching the stick,  
Would wear the boots of Kalev,  
Fears the fur rolling away.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Oh, this alba men's matter,  
You go towards the tattered cloth,  
Withered towards Narva,  
In rags towards the distant dawn,  
Oh, this dear girl merchandise.  
Or dear indirect goods.  
They went to the city's tavern,  
To the city's revelry,  
They did not greet in the market,  
In the town did not shake hands;  
She herself goes towards the golden spruces,  
She herself goes towards the dear birches,  
Sister to the apple tree branch,  
Boys down to the prayer;  
Without a hat, without gloves.  
Without a belt, without footwear.  
Come down, little heartbeat!  
Roll down, little sparrow,  
Fly down, little bird,  
Do not fall on a stone,  
Do not fall on a stump.  
Fall on my hat,  
Let it fall on the wide brim,  
Roll between my arms,  
Go to my drought,  
Come close to me and warm me.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
The girls played on the hill,  
Elms stood in the resting area,  
The maidens adorned with rowan berries. -  
If those were my men  
Who walk this way and that,  
Ride along the path,  
I would stand without eating,  
Without tasting winter's hardship,  
A year without a meal.  
I would raise them on eggs,  
Butter or a slice of butter,  
I would roll them in flower fields,  
Cover them with a dear cloth,  
Put them to sleep on a pillow,  
Set them in a silk bed.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to the Creator for a song,  
To Marija for bread,  
Now the Creator sings a song,  
Marija is going to the meadow.  
I take one branch, I take two,  
I start to take the third,  
What do I find under the branch?  
I find an eagle under the branch,  
What is under the eagle's wing?  
A nest under the eagle's wing.  
What is on the corner of the nest?  
A bone was on the corner of the nest.  
What is made from that bone?  
A room is made from it for Toomas.  
A barn is chopped for Ants,  
A house is built for Märt,  
Beams are laid for Peeter,  
A bone is found for Juhan.  
What is inside those barns?  
Beds are inside those barns.  
What is under the beds?  
Straw is under the beds.  
What is on the straw?  
Sheets are on the straw.  
What is on the sheets?  
Pillows are on the sheets.  
What is on the pillows?  
Four young maidens:  
One is sewing a silk headdress,  
Another is weaving a golden belt,  
The third is weaving a silver belt,  
The fourth is crying for a young man.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to the Creator for a song,  
To Marija for bread,  
Now the Creator sings a song,  
Marija is going to the meadow.  
I take one branch, I take two,  
I start to take the third,  
What do I find under the branch?  
I find an eagle under the branch,  
What is under the eagle's wing?  
A nest under the eagle's wing.  
What is on the corner of the nest?  
A bone was on the corner of the nest.  
What is made from that bone?  
A room is made from it for Toomas.  
A barn is chopped for Ants,  
A house is built for Märt,  
Beams are laid for Peeter,  
A bone is found for Juhan.  
What is inside those barns?  
Beds are inside those barns.  
What is under the beds?  
Straw is under the beds.  
What is on the straw?  
Sheets are on the straw.  
What is on the sheets?  
Pillows are on the sheets.  
What is on the pillows?  
Four young maidens:  
One is sewing a silk headdress,  
Another is weaving a golden belt,  
The third is weaving a silver belt,  
The fourth is crying for a young man.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
It is no good having beautiful hay  
If the hay is not mown,  
It is no good having beautiful pasture,  
If the meadow is not taken,  
It is no good having fine flax,  
If it is not arranged properly,  
It is no good having slender sheep,  
If they are not sheltered in a barn,  
It is no good having a pile of hay,  
If it is not taken home,  
It is no good having red berries,  
If the berries are not picked,  
It is no good having a virgin girl,  
If she is untouched by a boy,  
Untouched by the gold of a hand,  
Not gripped by a weak finger,  
It is no good having a groom boy,  
If he does not take a wife,  
Nor gather his bride’s gold.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let other men not do  
What I too have done,  
With my foolish head,  
With a foolish mind,  
I took an old widow woman,  
Bought fancy horses,  
Oh dear brother,  
Take a wife from a young maiden,  
Buy a horse from a foal,  
Take a puppy from a dog. -  
When you come to visit home,  
The wife laughs at the maiden,  
The dog digs from the puppy,  
The foal neighs at the horse.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
A young, virginal maiden,  
A slender one for writing,  
Why didn’t you come to me last year?  
The year before last you did not appear,  
When you went to collect orders  
Five bottles of vodka, six pecans,  
Thousands of beer mugs,  
Hundreds of bread cakes.  
You would have built a new yard shed,  
Set up a silk bed inside,  
A silk bed, a thread play;  
For a young lad to sleep.  
To lull a new bear to sleep.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
What do I care about songs.  
Songs, poems.  
Or what I fear in the howling,  
I resist even when raising my voice.  
I am not listening eagerly.  
A suitor is watching from above,  
My home is in a distant place.  
A young man from Peibe.  
A man across the sea,  
No stories from you,  
No words to listen to.  
Nor would he fall asleep near you.  
May God sleep with him.  
May Mary also keep him safe.  
May the wind carry health to him,  
May hundreds of blessings be spoken.  
May clouds long for him. -  
As many greetings to him  
As many drops of rain between;  
As many trees as there are drops:  
Apple trees, true mothers,  
Hazel trees, slender mothers,  
Rowan trees, young mothers,  
Bird cherry trees, fine mothers,  
Strong oak trees.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
There were two in the village of Kaieda,  
One was broad, the other was lazy. -  
Whom do I take, whom do I leave -  
I take the broad one, leave the lazy one.  
The lazy one is kind even to children.  
The broad one is fluent in work. -

label: Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I went to Kolka to court,
To the women by the shore. -
Kaie bargained with me.
Pille offered me a deal.
Malle even wished for me.
I went to listen to Kaie;
To peek at Pille from afar,
To briefly visit Malle.
Kaie was like a herd girl,
Kaie was even quite mean. -
Pille had rowan berries in her mouth.
She made a cake in the oven,
Blew from the campfire among the embers. -
Malle picked berries,
Gave me the riper ones -
Malle's berries were better
Than Pille's cream cakes. -
From Malle I got a pleasant wife.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to the meadow, to the hayfield of Marija.  
Golden rakes in hand,  
Silver pitchfork handles.  
I took one, I took two.  
I will take the third,  
I found an eagle under one,  
Between the haystacks.  
What is under the eagle's wing?  
A young one under the eagle's wing.  
Where did that young one come from,  
Came here, wanted to be here.  
To chop firewood for the barn,  
But the young one went to the forest.  
What is inside the barns?  
Beds inside the barns.  
What is under the beds?  
Pillows under the beds!  
What is on the pillows?  
Four young maidens,  
One is crying for a young man,  
Another is weaving a silver belt,  
The third is making a golden wreath;  
The fourth is crying for her father and mother.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to the meadow, to the hayfield of Marija.

I will take the third,  
I found an eagle under one,  
Between the haystacks.

Came here, wanted to be here.
