The mud, however, enriched the crown,  
The nest washed the clock.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The rich man killed a great bull,  
A great bull, a black bull.  
Could neither eat it, nor sell it,  
Could not take the meat to town.  
I, the poor man, killed a raven,  
An old and gray raven;  
From there I got to eat, from there I got to sell,  
From there I got meat to take to town.  
From the hornbeam, I got a hornpipe,  
From the tuft, I got a snow hat,  
From the tail, a fur coat;  
From the shin bone, I got bedposts,  
From the kneecap bone, I got main boards,  
From the collarbone, I got side boards.

label: Laulud laulust  ; Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I sing, why not sing,
I sing a song about a horse.
Its legs are made of horsehooves,
Its tail from hay,
Its body from hay stalks,
Its ears from cobbler’s leaves,
Its mane from fireweed,
Its eyes from stars in the sky,
Its hair from dewdrops,
Its harness from antlers.
Then I will go to Sõrve to ride,
To see the maidens of Sõrve.
The maiden gazes through the boarding,
Through the boarded gate.
If only I had that man
Who rides along the road,
Along the road, along the land,
Riding along the trails.
His horse’s legs are like a stove,
He himself like the day,
His belt like a rainbow in the rain,
His hat like a king’s crown.
Then the maiden begins to sing:
Go past me, man,
Go past the sly boy,
Take notice of this road,
Mark the trails in the book,
Seal the road letters in gloves,
Come back in autumn!
Take me from the rain showers,
Redeem me from the snowfalls,
Stop the winter weather,
From the strike of the Viru scythe,
From the ard blades of Harju farmers.

label: Laulud laulust  ; Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I sing, why not sing,
I sing a song about a horse.
Its legs are made of horsehooves,
Its tail from hay,
Its body from hay stalks,
Its ears from cobbler’s leaves,
Its mane from fireweed,
Its eyes from stars in the sky,
Its hair from dewdrops,
Its harness from antlers.
Then I will go to Sõrve to ride,
To see the maidens of Sõrve.
The maiden gazes through the boarding,
Through the boarded gate.
If only I had that man
Who rides along the road,
Along the road, along the land,
Riding along the trails.
His horse’s legs are like a stove,
He himself like the day,
His belt like a rainbow in the rain,
His hat like a king’s crown.
Then the maiden begins to sing:
Go past me, man,
Go past the sly boy,
Take notice of this road,
Mark the trails in the book,
Seal the road letters in gloves,
Come back in autumn!
Take me from the rain showers,
Redeem me from the snowfalls,
Stop the winter weather,
From the strike of the Viru scythe,
From the ard blades of Harju farmers.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Study, study, what are you studying,  
Study on the new thigh,  
Border on the long border,  
Let it on the wide board.  
There is tar water from a broom,  
Five vats of virutis water.  
Six vats of the devil's piss.  
There the investigators swim,  
Borders wash their heads.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Master, little master!  
Mistress, little mistress!  
Said to eat a lot,  
Eat a lot, drink a lot.  
Put a lot in place.  
What will I eat at the village!  
What will I drink at the village!  
What will I put in place at the village!  
The ox eats even while eating,  
Drinks from the vat even while drinking.  
What can I eat from the ox!  
What can I drink from the ox!  
The skin is outside and the bone is inside.  
What can I drink from the vat!  
The yeast is below and the foam is above,  
A drop of red beer,  
I won't go, I won't rush,  
I leave this place.  
I pour to the other room.  
I give the place to others,  
Let the better ones come to the place,  
The more honest to the place,  
Let the most famous be at the door's side.  
I will come to see tomorrow,  
Then I will come back again.  
The order was where I left,  
The chair was where I raised,  
The bench was where I stayed.  
Sweep the room to the corner,  
Split kindling for the stove,  
The broom cried like a whisk.  
The clogs asked for cleaning,  
Spoons burned in the fire,  
The ladle handles got hot.

label: Laulud laulust  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Now the beautiful Viru without water  
Is lovely to sing to the shores.  
Viru rolls the words.  
Dear is the speech below.  
The dew carries the vocal cords.  
The eel takes the voice to Harjumaa,  
The dew carries the voice to far away.  
Harju women bark,  
Viru women roll,  
Järva daughters divide.  
Harju woman [is] toothy,  
Viru woman [has] a vengeful back,  
Western woman [has] slippery feet.  
Do not eat my heart,  
Eat the good vocal cords.  
I am great to set the pot on the stove,  
To lay it wide on the table,  
Easy to cook in the kettle,  
White to put on the plate,  
Want to put on the platter.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The devil dug in the spruce forest,  
The pagan hammered in the willow.  
He made a cart's wheelhouse,  
An old gentleman's wagon,  
Rims rusted from iron,  
Spokes like a wolf pack.  
Rungs like ladders.  
Toe boards placed on the head beam,  
Cast brass hitches,  
Axles broken from jaws.  
End rods without teeth.  
Side parts of ear wood.  
Feet considered shafts.  
Backbeam load of harness wood.  
A rod made of dill-like wood.  
The horse's yoke in front,  
The gentleman between the shafts,  
A bunch of ears, a jaunty jingle.  
The gentleman's ear on the shaft.  
The lady with a whip on the bench,  
The maids on the cart to tar,  
Nailing the carriage hide.  
Then the Saxons were sent to hell.  
The peasants to heaven.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Lõõri-lõõri swallows,
daybird sparrow.
Sang the bird under the eaves:
A rich man killed a great ox.
I am the enemy, the carrion.
Got meat to eat, got meat to sell,
The hide went to Narva’s tanner.
The head went to the Pärnu chief.
From the beak, I got a snuff horn,
From the tuft, I got a woolen cap.
From the tail, I got a hundred-cap.
Then I went to roam the town.
To Paide parish to wander.
The manor Germans pondered,
The town lords wondered:
Where did this man come from?
He has come from afar.
Is he a rich lord of Riga,
Or is he a true lord of Pärnu?
Or is he the king of Courland?
I heard, I replied.
I am not a rich Riga lord,
Nor the true lord of Pärnu,
Nor the king of Courland.
I am a poor stable boy,
A servant boy of the Alliku parish.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, I herded the flock.
I was a Dutch servant,
A sheep boy from England.
What was my reward?
An old coat, an old sheepskin.
Do you accept this,
And are you satisfied with us?
I do not accept this,
Nor am I satisfied with you.
If I serve, I serve still
A rich man and a decent one.
I was a slave, I herded the flock.
I was a Dutch servant.
A sheep boy from England.
What was my reward?
An old crooked back,
An old worn-out foot.
Do you accept this,
And are you satisfied with us?
I do not accept this,
Nor am I satisfied with you.
If I serve, I serve still
A rich man and a decent one.
I was a slave, I herded the flock,
I was a Dutch servant,
A sheep boy from England.
What was my reward?
A sheep with a lamb,
A pig with a hundred piglets.
Do you accept this,
And are you satisfied with us?
I do not accept this,
Nor am I satisfied with you.
If I serve, I serve still
A rich man and a decent one.
I was a slave, I herded the flock.
I was a Dutch servant,
A sheep boy from England.
What was my reward?
A horse with the finest harness,
A hundred-horned calf in the barn.
Do you accept this,
And are you satisfied with us?
I do not accept this,
Nor am I satisfied with you.
If I serve, I serve still
A rich man and a decent one.
I was a slave, I herded the flock.
I was a Dutch servant,
A sheep boy from England.
What was my reward?
From Tallinn to Tartu roads,
From Finland to the big cities.
Do you accept this,
And are you satisfied with us?
I do not accept this,
Nor am I satisfied with you.
I was a slave, I herded the flock.
I was a Dutch servant,
A sheep boy from England.
What was my reward?
A virgin with a golden braid,
All the house utensils gold-plated.
Do you accept this,
And are you satisfied with us?
Yes, I accept this,
And I am satisfied with you.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I used to be a large mountain,
Among the bluebells,
Above the globeflowers
Between the meadowsweet.
The bluebells fade,
The globeflowers yellow,
The meadowsweet turns gray.
The grass grows weak and young.
I weep for the young grass:
I was once the master’s boy,
The stick-bearer, a hen’s keeper.
Now I am a forced servant.
The master had big hooves,
Wide eyelids, thick boats,
They wanted to furrow in summer.
To iron the ridge.
To comb the golden comb.
If you couldn’t furrow in summer,
Comb the golden comb—
Then they would beat me with a whip,
Feed me as dog’s food,
Drive me as ox’s fodder.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I am not born among the great,  
to fit into a rich man's house,  
the rich take against the rich,  
the wealthy against another wealthy,  
I am born in the corner of the sauna,  
among the poor and middle-aged.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, we poor manor spirits  
Other poor laborers  
Ordinary third ones  
Helper spirits fourth  
Drinkers fifth  
Were driven to howl at night  
To march across the land  
To walk across the desert.  
Night is painful, moon is painful  
God’s fire is painful  
Night is painful, I cut the hay  
Moon is painful, I cut the stack  
God’s fire is painful,  
Golden rakes in the shed  
Silver sickle handles  
When will I escape the manor's year?  
The manor’s year is a hellish year.  
I escape as if from under your mouth  
Between the jaws of a lion.  
Between the teeth of a monkey  
Now I look back carefully  
On my dog’s load I turn my head.  
Before the home gate  
Before father’s barn door,  
Before brother’s chamber threshold  
Then I look over the ridge  
Towards the worse side,  
See how the hell looks  
The underworlds blaze  
Hell’s windows glow red  
Ghost towers stand idle  
Life seems a joke.  
There are large German fields  
Bottomless lady’s fields.  
There our feathers melt  
Silver ornaments straighten out.  
Wallets grow empty.  
There on the big field  
On the bottomless field.  
On the angular meadow.  
Now I say again in reverse.  
To sing again in Danish  
I turn the words upside down.  
There fall on the back of the German  
Break the mistress’s neck  
Lady’s hip bones fall apart  
Virgin’s limbs twist  
Our backs become whole  
Our scythes sharp  
Our necks stiff.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our manor’s prisoner manor  
The spiritual destruction of people  
A poor refuge for children  
The bulls keep eating  
Runes, weak ones, in harness  
Men in chest clothes  
Women walk the manor road  
Gloves on hands  
A fat wheel in a kerchief,  
Oh, you are a gentleman.  
A maiden with silver lace.  
Young gentlemen with golden buttons,  
Let us rise onto the chair,  
Step onto the window table,  
Step onto the window table,  
Let us look upon the poor parish.  
How this parish is tormented  
The little ones are tortured.  
Women are cursed,  
The swamp comes from the black manor side  
Gray comes from between the gardens  
Blue from behind the grove  
Oh, I am a poor farmhand:  
I plow all day  
Towards the blessed evening  
May the devil take this steward.  
Scratch both hands  
Bring me a big piece  
A big piece from the bottom of the pond.  
Wet earth from under the hill  
I plow but cannot reach.  
I keep staring towards the manor.  
Our steward, a dear one  
Manor man, Madalukene  
Roller of the silver rod  
Spinner of the golden staff,  
Let the children go to the noon meal  
Sisters to the evening meal.  
Already the day rolls in Viru  
Already noon long ago in Harju.  
Our noon not exhaled  
Fish not cut up,  
Cauldron not filled with gold  
Silver handle not tempered.  
Dear little brother  
Come to us to carve pike perch  
To cut fish into pieces  
To split the roach.  
The pike roaring into the sea  
Fish singing on the shore  
But throwing a splash into the wave.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The Hellish Mistress  
Boiled me some shoe-leather  
Put some apnemaie in the oven  
Kept the keriksele boiling  
And leesimaie under the flame.  
The shields were washing their behinds  
The pigs rinsed their piggy snouts,  
The servant ate a large amount  
During the long daytime,  
I, poor, all year round.  
The mistress twisted her neck  
Twisted the neck of the string  
Tied the string to the dog's neck  
And the other end to the servant's neck  
She made me stand outside  
To watch the gate,  
The hole for the three-legged dog.  
The milkmaid rubbed her horn,  
The milker's ear was sore,  
A little milk in the vessel  
A bit of soot inside  
A little butter upfront,  
Oh, that first living!  
Couldn't get bread with butter  
Couldn't touch a chicken egg  
Nor tear any meat,  
The housemaid was a pepper sack  
A pepper sack of buckwheat  
She walked the way to the barn  
Switching between two chambers  
Ripping the blacksmith's thighs  
And breaking the neck of a cake.  
Pinched the end of the sausage.  
The housemaid, a young maiden  
Certainly could eat in the kitchen  
Secretly could eat in the sauna  
Even more in the chimney corner  
Twice as much in the chamber  
Her knife even stretched from hanging  
The other one broke at the phallus  
The third broke from a dear nail,  
Always to the butter dish,  
Took a bite of cake.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The spruce was large in Courland,  
The birch was on Harju's pasture road,  
The alder was on the town street,  
The willow on Vinni's heath.  
Their roots grew together,  
Their branches intertwined.  
Their leaves fluttered as one,  
Their crowns swung together.  
The river flowed beneath the roots,  
The spring beneath the clearings,  
Golden fish inside.  
Make the net, young men,  
Cast the net, boys;  
Let's go fishing,  
Catch the sacred fish,  
For father, the coveted fish,  
For mother, the coveted fish;  
For brother, fresh herring.  
I brought the fish home,  
Started to cook the fish,  
To cut the fish,  
The fish began to speak,  
To speak of sorrow;  
I was not brought in alive,  
Nor carried in fresh,  
I was brought as a ploughman,  
A ploughman, a sower,  
Turning the black soil.  
I turn under the hay side,  
Above the black soil side,  
Into these are the tender shoots.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The spruce was large in Courland,  
The birch was on Harju's pasture road,  
The alder was on the town street,  
The willow on Vinni's heath.  
Their roots grew together,  
Their branches intertwined.  
Their leaves fluttered as one,  
Their crowns swung together.  
The river flowed beneath the roots,  
The spring beneath the clearings,  
Golden fish inside.  
Make the net, young men,  
Cast the net, boys;  
Let's go fishing,  
Catch the sacred fish,  
For father, the coveted fish,  
For mother, the coveted fish;  
For brother, fresh herring.  
I brought the fish home,  
Started to cook the fish,  
To cut the fish,  
The fish began to speak,  
To speak of sorrow;  
I was not brought in alive,  
Nor carried in fresh,  
I was brought as a ploughman,  
A ploughman, a sower,  
Turning the black soil.  
I turn under the hay side,  
Above the black soil side,  
Into these are the tender shoots.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I do not belong to the great ranks  
Nor fit in a rich man’s house  
I have no shame of honest parents  
Nor of rich garments  
I sit especially  
Among other poor people  
Amidst mediocre children.  
Let me pour words back  
I do not care for the proud ones  
I fear breaking the rich ones  
I break long planks to splinters  
Thick pieces to chips  
Wide boards to shavings.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The devil's den  
Look, there the hellpit appears  
The smaller ones are tortured there,  
The bigger ones are killed there  
If I escape from the manor,  
Then I escape from hell.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If I were in charge,  
And it were up to me to act,  
I would tear the Germans to shreds,  
And turn the gentlemen into oxen,  
The Junkers into chatterboxes,  
The ladies into sticks,  
And the peasants into cranes.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When we come from work  
And if we then also get wet,  
Where can we dry ourselves?  
Let's go by the manor's threshing barn.  
What will we find there by the oven?  
The cooper's legs, the miller's boots,  
We will help both of the lads.  
The cooper came from the sack side  
And the miller from the chaff side.  
A stick bundle was on the shoulder strap  
A switch bundle was tucked into the belt,  
Then I begin to wonder,  
What kind of forest is closest,  
The birch forest was far away,  
And the willow forest grassy,  
The aspen forest was the closest.  
Then I run to the aspen grove,  
Jump down into the birch grove.  
I knocked the cooper over.  
And the miller face down.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, I am a poor servant girl,  
A little wretch, a servant girl!  
I have water instead of long-lasting milk,  
Fire hay instead of enough tobacco,  
Dugout cake instead of enough kvass,  
Oh, I am a poor servant mother,  
A little wretch, a sauna mother!  
I spin around the mistress,  
I walk around the German woman,  
I break the corner of the flax roll,  
I ask for my own little pot,  
I have broth to push the flaxes,  
I have broth for the tand (a kind of footwear),  
I have pea broth for punishment,  
I stir coal into the ladle,  
They beat me themselves,  
They thrash this little wretch.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me,  
And my doing besides,  
I would surely know what I would do.  
I would turn the gentlemen into oxen,  
The old ladies into iron slags,  
The German children into clods,  
The peasants into backwoodsmen,  
And myself into their overseer.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was in the barn, I went to the herd,  
I was in Holland tying up,  
Linder in the sheepfold by the lamb.  
What will you pay as a wage  
And also as a price for the gift?  
The church with the parish,  
The manor with the entire estate.  
Do you agree with this,  
And are you satisfied with us?  
No, I do not agree with this,  
Nor am I satisfied with you,  
I want two thousand money  
And to buy expensive clothes too,  
Then I will agree with this  
And be satisfied with you.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The rich man killed a big bull,  
A big bull, a draft bull,  
I, a poor old wretch,  
Because I got to eat, because I got to sell,  
Because I could still take meat to the town,  
Because I made a lot of things,  
I made a pouch from the skin,  
I made a pouch from the snout,  
I made a walking stick from the thigh bone,  
I made a bedpost from the shin bone,  
I made a bedspread from the wings,  
I made a big pillow from the feathers.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, I herded cattle,  
I was a servant of the Ollender,  
In the service of Tillermann.  
Reinu had big horses,  
Tall ears, thick boats,  
They wanted to mate in summer,  
In winter to work with an iron plow.  
I, a lazy boy,  
Did not bother to mate in summer,  
Or to work with the iron plow in winter.  
They had to beat me with a whip,  
Strike me with an iron rod.  
I became an enemy, a cattle driver,  
And a knickerbocker...

label: Murelaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If my being  
were changed by my doing:  
I would set the roofs on the wind,  
the barn near the island.  
..................  
I would turn the oxen into oxen,  
the horses into ploughmen,  
and the dogs into harrows behind.

label: Murelaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If my being  
were changed by my doing:  
I would set the roofs on the wind,  
the barn near the island.  
..................  
I would turn the oxen into oxen,  
the horses into ploughmen,  
and the dogs into harrows behind.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
But I was drowsy, roaming in the herd,  
I was a servant of Ol´lan´di,  
Enchanted by Leeverlan´di.  
Ol´lan´t made me a new jacket,  
Leeverlan´t a wide coat,  
From Al´li cat skins  
And colorful goat legs,  
Hedgehog hides of pig skin  
And old hat made of piña cloth.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If I were to exist,  
Secondly, my doing:  
I would make otters into otter-like beings,  
Horned cranes into crane-like beings,  
Sparrows into sparrow-like beings.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The village calls me to the village to eat,  
to eat in the village, to drink in the village.  
Oh, how I understand, I will answer soon,  
I will reply to you shortly:  
How many villages would I eat at  
or to which village would I run.  
I eat the ox while eating,  
drink a keg of beer while drinking.  
What good is the ox to me to eat?  
What good is the keg of beer to drink?  
The ox has its hide on,  
the hide on, the bone underneath,  
a little tender meat.  
Or to drink from the keg of beer?  
The yeast is beneath, the foam on top,  
a little drop of dark beer.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me,
if it depended on my actions,
I would send you away with the wind,
left to the cold of the oven,
I would make the Germans beg...

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me,
if it depended on my actions,
I would send you away with the wind,
left to the cold of the oven,
I would make the Germans beg...

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, I tended the herd,  
I was Ollard's servant.  
Ollart made a new coat,  
Leemert a wide jacket. -  
I watch over the old woman's sheep,  
I protect the large flock of the village.  
The old woman makes me a new coat,  
a warm coat of white wool.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Come, slave, take what is yours,  
come, poor one, take the hardship!  
...The long feast of Kuinarpuu,  
the loom's sling on the shoulder...  
I don’t want to go to heaven,  
when you are living on earth.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If only I knew what I would do:  
I would turn the mares into mares with foals  
and the young ladies into sticks!

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I am a traveler,
who has widely traveled the world,
my scissors, curling iron, needle case,
all together in my pouch.
My work is delicate and precise.
Where wedding guests gather,
I am always brought there
and honored
and carried in hands.
He asked in great anger:
What is your name, old crone?
I am tailor Jüri Ants,
who has widely traveled the world.
Then he let me shear,
and pile a thousand backs.
My back then became straighter
and my legs became lighter.
Then I cursed the Germans:
let the Germans perish,
let the cane-bearers disappear,
let the masters suffer famine,
today alone, tomorrow two,
the day after tomorrow five or six.
May God grant us many Antsus,
may there be many Siimuses,
may the Jüris vanish to the roots,
may the Mardis fall to the ground.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Here where the hellish pit shines,  
where the devil's chapel rings;  
there my cheeks are tormented,  
the knuckles are afflicted,  
young fingers are scolded.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me,
if it were my doing,
then I would know what I would do:
I would turn lazy people into diligent ones,
dogs into sled dogs,
louts into talkers.
I would call on everyone:
Plow for Kiitu, sow for Lauk,
go to battle, old Red!
I would have ten pairs of ploughs,
the ninth saved one.
Seven sickles cut in Väila.
The eighth mowed with a scythe.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
But I was a slave, I tended the herd,
I was Olland’s servant,
Liiberland’s shepherd boy.
Ollan´t made a new coat,
Liiberlant a wide cloak
from scraps, from patches,
from the colorful legs of a patchwork dog.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
But I was a slave, I tended the herd,
I was a servant of Olland,
a pauper of Leeverland.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
I was a slave, I tended the herd,  
A knee-high little boy.  
At night I beat the threshing floor,  
By day I plowed the manor land.  
Where I stood, the walls were wet,  
Where I cried, the corners were wet.  
With the door, a little pond dripped,  
A fishpond grew.  
From there all the guests could drink,  
The townsfolk gained enlightenment.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
I was a slave, I tended the herd,  
A knee-high little boy.  
At night I beat the threshing floor,  
By day I plowed the manor land.  
Where I stood, the walls were wet,  
Where I cried, the corners were wet.  
With the door, a little pond dripped,  
A fishpond grew.  
From there all the guests could drink,  
The townsfolk gained enlightenment.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, we poor night slaves  
Night slaves, crowned with wreaths  
Springtime slaves left at home.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, I tended the herd
I was a servant at Holstre manor.
There were tall rye stalks,
Tall rye stalks, thick boats
I had to comb them with a rake,
Brush them with an iron comb.
I did not want to comb them,
Brush them with an iron comb,
Then I had to be whipped with a whip,
Swept through the rye.
Then I had to cast a spell of enmity,
Weave breeches.
I ran along the village meadow,
Along the plowed fields of ten villages,
The breeches were tucked under my armpits.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh my dear little gentleman  
Little iron nail of gold  
Little reindeer of Sicily  
Sweet as honey, little glutton  
Look at this poor parish  
How this parish is tormented  
The greater ones are killed  
The weak ones on the tip of a needle  
The clever ones on the tip of a rod  
The tiny ones on the tip of a thorn.  
Now it’s no longer a joke  
When it is the painful fate of a slave.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
How many slaves are on the land?  
Six slaves are on the land.  
One is the slave workman  
The second is the slave workhorse  
The third is the regular slave  
The fourth is the young slave maiden  
The fifth is the Russian slave  
The sixth is the manor’s coachman.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If only I were alive  
If only I were real  
Then I would know what I would do  
I would make the nobles beg  
Make the peasants dance,  
Make the lords sing  
Make the ladies mow the grass  
Make the servants plow the land  
Make the serfs whip the wagon  
Make the old gentlemen guard the gate.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If I were to exist  
if my doing were otherwise  
I would make gentlemen into oxen  
and ladies into little sticks.  
Kilts beneath wedges  
young men in front as stories.  
I myself went after as a driver.  
Turn the stove, pour into the hollow  
A furrow of a fault, old red.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When we come from the sea
and we are wet, –
where do we dry ourselves?
We go to the manor houses,
to the threshing floors of the manor,
we lay the drying rack,
we stretch it over the back,
on clean barley straw,
down to the floor of the barn.
Who among us arrives first,
ahead of the others, before the others,
asks the barn keeper,
the dusty barn keeper,
to take us under the eaves,
under the warm barn floor of the room...

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The devil dug in the pine grove  
The old devil in the willows

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Behold where the hellpit glows,  
The devil's house shines,  
The whore’s tower is blooming green,  
Where the poor are tormented,  
The little ones are tortured,  
The finger gets cursed,  
The whip cracks until dark,  
At dusk the bull’s whip,  
At dawn the bailiff’s little stick.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me  
if it were my doing  
I would turn gentlemen into forks  
coppers into ear plugs  
ladies into skirts  
and chamber boys into poles.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh my dear gentleman doctor  
Lady of the golden crown  
Let us rise onto the chair  
Let us step onto the window frame.  
Let us look at the poor parish  
How this parish is tormented  
The smaller ones are tortured  
The bigger ones are killed  
The horse was golden in front  
Its hooves were in the stable

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The devil was hammering in the spruce forest,  
The devil was banging in the grove,  
He made a wagon wheel barn,  
With which to take the Saxon to hell,  
The lords also into the fire.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Why the hell are you studying me,  
a snake sprawling wide upon me,  
a long-ass fence enclosing me.  
Study on the new thigh,  
enclose on the long stick;  
There is a tar barrel filled with pitch,  
six barrels of devil's piss,  
there those examiners swim,  
the enclosers wash their heads.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, we poor night slaves,  
Night slaves, crowned with wreaths,  
Fastened servants,  
We must serve others.  
Serve the German for a hundred years.  
The German's four silk coats  
The poor child has an old quarrel.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The others went to work on Monday,  
I went on Tuesday to follow.  
I put the plowshare on the plow,  
Suddenly it turned aside.  
The smith came from the manor,  
I to the smith’s plea.  
Dear smith, dear smith,  
measure me a tiny piece,  
a tiny and narrow piece,  
pleasant land from beneath the hill.  
The smith took the dog’s mind,  
measured me a large piece,  
a large piece, a long piece  
from the hilltop mound,  
from the swamp’s hollow.  
I plowed that day,  
that blessed little evening,  
then I let the oxen rest,  
I myself went walking in the wilderness.  
From the wilderness came a spotted dog,  
it tore Kriimu, broke Must,  
from the forest came a wide paw,  
both ate at once crazily.  
I went to the lord to plead,  
then I got a thousand on the back,  
seven hundred on the shoulders.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If I were in charge  
Secondly, if it were up to me  
I would make the gentlemen oxen  
The ladies wear bonnets  
Put the boys into stories  
And give the girls whips.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me,  
If it were my doing,  
I would surely know what I would do:  
I would make men into guards,  
Ladies into gossiping cranes,  
Misses into full jays,  
Young men into winter guards.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, we poor theorists,  
Bound servants,  
Imprisoned wage slaves.  
We must enslave others,  
Torment ourselves,  
Wet by day, slaves by night.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh farrier, devil’s skin!
Black boots’ kisser.
Took steak from my ass,
From there got lean meat,
From the groin got lard.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
I am still a youngster,
A knee-high little boy.
At night I swept the manor's yard,
By day I plowed the meadow land.
Where I stood, the walls were wet;
Where I cried, the corners were wet.
From that dripped a little pond,
It grew into a fish lake.
From there the village drank for their herds,
Grandmother's fabrics were dipped.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
I am still a youngster,
A knee-high little boy.
At night I swept the manor's yard,
By day I plowed the meadow land.
Where I stood, the walls were wet;
Where I cried, the corners were wet.
From that dripped a little pond,
It grew into a fish lake.
From there the village drank for their herds,
Grandmother's fabrics were dipped.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me,  
if it were my doing,  
I would make the nobles beg  
and the peasants dance.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh I am a bright little thorn,
Lady with a golden crown,
Let the children go to lunch!

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Rain, rain, little rain,  
don’t rain on us,  
rain, rain on the Germans.  
We have nowhere to dry,  
the Germans have home dryers,  
fine shirt washers,  
white shirt ironers.  
Let's go down to the threshing barn.  
What do we have there, a stove basket?  
The bailiff’s thighs, the farmhand’s legs,  
we help both stacks.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Rain, rain, little rain,  
don’t rain on us,  
rain, rain on the Germans.  
We have nowhere to dry,  
the Germans have home dryers,  
fine shirt washers,  
white shirt ironers.  
Let's go down to the threshing barn.  
What do we have there, a stove basket?  
The bailiff’s thighs, the farmhand’s legs,  
we help both stacks.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh we poor night slaves,
Night slaves, wreath-crowned,
Clenched servants,
Wage workers in chains!
We must serve others,
Wear ourselves out early.
Serve the German for a hundred years -
The German on his silken coat,
The poor child in old clothes,
The little slave in their own clothes.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our chief little ram,  
A quirky, colorful little bird,  
Lets the children go to their noon meal,  
At the right time to their evening meal.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Shit falls from Jüri’s mouth,  
to the bottom sucking filth,  
from the top the tongue chews wool;  
Gold falls from Liisu’s mouth,  
to the bottom sucking nectar,  
from the top the tongue boils milk.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Where did our goat go?
The old nanny goat went,
her belly ran along the ground,
her back ran along the roof.
There were barrels of tar water,
five barrels of virsa water,
six barrels were the devil's urine.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The niece created laziness!  
She didn't bother to bring water,  
peed on the stove,  
eyelashes stuck to her chest,  
tears on her head.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If tomorrow were Saturday,
the coming Friday,
then I would know what to do.
I would put the boots on shaggy,
the gentlemen in front as grooms,
the sheepskin coats behind as cranes,
the kilts inside as wedges,
the ladles behind as handles.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our bright little ram,  
A brightly colored little bird,  
Lets the children to lunch,  
At the right time to dinner.  
The children are industrious at work,  
Early before the day is light,  
Their heads washed before daylight,  
Their backs washed before dawn.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me,  
if it were my doing,  
I would know what I would do.  
I would make Germans into sacks,  
wolfhounds into oxen,  
hearts into cranes,  
buckles into wedges.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, I tended the herd,  
I was Olland's servant,  
A shepherd boy from Lööveland,  
Olland made a new hoe,  
Lööveland a wide coat.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
A hundred, a hundred little rains  
A hundred rains in a year  
Fire and tar in a year of sky  
Still, I must go  
Still, I must be a servant  
Still, I must walk as ordered  
Let it rain a hundredfold and water the grass  
Let the rain go towards Vigula  
Let it bring momentum to Harju County  
Let it send rain to Germany  
Let it rain on the German's back  
The German has a wide umbrella  
But the fog would spoil the new moon  
Rain would spoil the sowing moon

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
A hundred, a hundred little rains  
A hundred rains in a year  
Fire and tar in a year of sky  
Still, I must go  
Still, I must be a servant  
Still, I must walk as ordered  
Let it rain a hundredfold and water the grass  
Let the rain go towards Vigula  
Let it bring momentum to Harju County  
Let it send rain to Germany  
Let it rain on the German's back  
The German has a wide umbrella  
But the fog would spoil the new moon  
Rain would spoil the sowing moon

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The Master praised the generation of seasons  
The Mistress praised the generation of spirit  
When they came to the master for an hour  
To try for half a day  
Whether he put him to sleep on a cushion  
Wrapped in linens  
The master would put him to work  
To eat a piece of bread  
He was not put to sleep on a cushion  
Not wrapped in linens  
The master thought over a piece  
And threw the yoke on the neck  
Loaded with a whip in front  
Oh, I plow a piece against my will  
The furrow I left for the donkey  
The fool I left for the ox  
Then I got a piece from the ox in front  
On the day of work, beside the ox's ear  
I wait for food to be brought  
For water to be brought in a white bowl  
I saw food being brought  
But water was brought in a black bowl  
Immediately my mood fell sad  
My heart started to ache  
Not the things that were given to me  
Not the food that was brought to me  
That didn’t grow up from scraps  
That slipped from the sparse leaves  
And burned bread crumbs  
I took it up and got angry  
I chased the bulls to the grass  
I drove the bulls to the corner of the fence  
Put the whip under the bedstead  
And I struck the plow against the sauna wall  
Then I went to Aru tavern  
There I took some measures of vodka  
Two pitchers of weak  
Bring strong beer  
Then I felt my legs turn  
My legs start to turn  
Surely my legs will take me home  
My calves go to the bed  
My knees fall to the bed

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The Master praised the generation of seasons  
The Mistress praised the generation of spirit  
When they came to the master for an hour  
To try for half a day  
Whether he put him to sleep on a cushion  
Wrapped in linens  
The master would put him to work  
To eat a piece of bread  
He was not put to sleep on a cushion  
Not wrapped in linens  
The master thought over a piece  
And threw the yoke on the neck  
Loaded with a whip in front  
Oh, I plow a piece against my will  
The furrow I left for the donkey  
The fool I left for the ox  
Then I got a piece from the ox in front  
On the day of work, beside the ox's ear  
I wait for food to be brought  
For water to be brought in a white bowl  
I saw food being brought  
But water was brought in a black bowl  
Immediately my mood fell sad  
My heart started to ache  
Not the things that were given to me  
Not the food that was brought to me  
That didn’t grow up from scraps  
That slipped from the sparse leaves  
And burned bread crumbs  
I took it up and got angry  
I chased the bulls to the grass  
I drove the bulls to the corner of the fence  
Put the whip under the bedstead  
And I struck the plow against the sauna wall  
Then I went to Aru tavern  
There I took some measures of vodka  
Two pitchers of weak  
Bring strong beer  
Then I felt my legs turn  
My legs start to turn  
Surely my legs will take me home  
My calves go to the bed  
My knees fall to the bed

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
(But) the rich man kills the big bull,  
The big bull, the straight bull;  
I am a poor old crow.  
The meat was eaten, the hide was sold,  
The meat went to the birds of the city,  
The hide went to the Narva conscript,  
Some stays in their own houses,  
The dung becomes food for the pigs;  
From the scrap, I get a tuque hat,  
From the beak, I get a nose horn,  
From the shinbone, I get a bed post.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud abielust
translation:
Oh you beautiful farms,  
Oh you slender fences,  
Young gentlemen of Silver Nuku,  
Ladies of Gold Lace,—  
Rise up onto the chair,  
Get up into the hall,  
Step onto the windowsill,  
Look upon the poor parish,  
See how this parish is tormented,  
The little ones are tormented,  
The size of a finger is scolded,  
The size of a knuckle is pointed at.  
If only our shoulders were as strong as alder trees,  
Our forearms like maples,  
Our fingers as nimble as hazel rods,—  
Then they would not fear torment,  
Nor the strain on their forearms.  
Oh God Almighty,  
Be a helper to the poor,  
Protect the poor child.  
Why did you leave me asleep,  
A little mischievous one,  
If that portion was meant to be shared,  
Seed for men sown,  
The gift of boy’s fortune given,—  
Others got the fun,  
I was left with the black and the ugly,  
The tar stump and the ailing one.  
Where must I go with the black,  
Face the sick one,  
Rush with the ugly?  
Where there is water, I beat,  
Where there is a puddle, I splash,  
Where there is a roof, I pour again—  
So that I may sleep a little,  
A tiny angel.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud abielust
translation:
Oh you beautiful farms,  
Oh you slender fences,  
Young gentlemen of Silver Nuku,  
Ladies of Gold Lace,—  
Rise up onto the chair,  
Get up into the hall,  
Step onto the windowsill,  
Look upon the poor parish,  
See how this parish is tormented,  
The little ones are tormented,  
The size of a finger is scolded,  
The size of a knuckle is pointed at.  
If only our shoulders were as strong as alder trees,  
Our forearms like maples,  
Our fingers as nimble as hazel rods,—  
Then they would not fear torment,  
Nor the strain on their forearms.  
Oh God Almighty,  
Be a helper to the poor,  
Protect the poor child.  
Why did you leave me asleep,  
A little mischievous one,  
If that portion was meant to be shared,  
Seed for men sown,  
The gift of boy’s fortune given,—  
Others got the fun,  
I was left with the black and the ugly,  
The tar stump and the ailing one.  
Where must I go with the black,  
Face the sick one,  
Rush with the ugly?  
Where there is water, I beat,  
Where there is a puddle, I splash,  
Where there is a roof, I pour again—  
So that I may sleep a little,  
A tiny angel.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Now it is time to go home,  
It is time to count the household;  
There was time for the father, there was time for the mother-in-law,  
There was time for the family daughter.  
When the time comes to my home,  
And the cat awaits me at home—  
The dog awaits me at home;  
The dog, however, awaits the puppies,  
The cat awaits the crumbs of bread.  
The home is spoken of in ill terms,  
Harsh words are given,  
Bad words are offered.  
They speak against someone,  
Harsh words are given to someone  
Someone else, not me.  
When I go home,  
No one asks for a splinter of firewood,  
Nor gathers kindling in a row,  
The straw stalks do not speak;  
The mother-in-law is angry with me,  
The father-in-law is angry with me,  
Even the loud family elders—  
They do not even offer me food,  
Nor offer me a little bit;  
It is not allowed to enter the room,  
Nor to light a fire under the hearth.  
But I take a handful of twigs from the pile,  
Pull tinder from the room’s ceiling,  
Snatch splinters from the firewood—  
Then I make a fire under the hearth,  
There I warm my soup.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were my being,
if it were my being

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Mother, dear,
Wash my fine shirts,
Whiter than whale's cuffs,
Then I will go to the beautiful hayfield,
To the slender German field.
But mother understood, replied:
Fine shirts will deceive you!
Before you are deceived, fine sand,
The golden plague of the church.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Little piece of sand, sauna woman,  
I would ask for a pot,  
A corner of a nook for broth,  
I have broth for the soup,  
I have broth for the stew,  
To put the pot outside,  
To fill the ladle with ashes,  
To beat myself upon.  
I, a poor poor woman,  
Would ask for a row of fields,  
Where I could sow garlic.  
Father would watch around  
How those gold pieces would grow.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The village calls me to eat at the village,  
To eat at the village, to drink at the village.  
How many villages would I eat at,  
Or at which village would I drink?  
I eat the bull in the eating hall,  
Drink barrel beer in the drinking hall.  
Oh, when I understand, I will answer soon,  
I will respond to you shortly:  
What is there to eat from that bull,  
The bone was inside, the skin was on top,  
A strip of lean meat;  
Or to drink from that barrel of beer,  
The foam was on top, the yeast was below,  
A drop of dark beer.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
A flock of girls came against me,  
Like a herd of goats in the village.  
A herd as big as a calf herd,  
The sergeant’s herd of pigs,  
A Dutch herd of geese,  
A herd of sheep from Leeverland.  
I was diligent, I tended the herd,  
I was a Dutch servant,  
A sheepboy from Leeverland.  
Holland made me a new jacket,  
Leeverland a wide coat,  
Made of patches and scraps,  
And dogskin from the legs.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When we are wet,  
And we come from the tavern,  
Where do we dry ourselves? -  
By the manor's malt barn,  
There we dry ourselves,  
What do we put in the oven there?  
The boot's thighs, the cobbler's legs  
Those we put in the oven there.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Poorly poor peasants,  
They eat porridge, sell reeds,  
Lentils move towards the town,  
But beans follow, nodding.  
Peas boil in the pot,  
The dog's paw was at a loss.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Rõa parish, parish of joy,  
The parish was made of copper in front,  
The staff was golden behind.  
It does not strike a man, it does not strike a woman,  
It does not strike a poor girl,  
It does not touch the boys.  
It strikes logs into the waves  
And sends them beneath the water.  
Väätsa parish, parish of toil;  
The parish was burdensome in front,  
The staff was like the devil behind.  
It strikes men, it strikes women,  
It strikes poor girls,  
But it touches the boys.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Now it is time for the court to go home,  
The right to go to the evening.  
Already the moon is in front of the home,  
The sieve was at the edge of the south.  
The star was on the ridge of the barn,  
The white cart is watching.  
The home people are all sleeping,  
Without belts, without work,  
Without stockings in the beds,  
Without shoes in the room,  
Barefoot on the pillows.  
They do not see my beauty,  
My rattling of rods,  
The cracking of the high whip.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The others went to work early,  
I went to the peat late at night,  
Right away though, out to the manor fields  
I began smoothing the furrows,  
Twisting the old rope,  
Beating the bundle of sticks,  
Cutting the wooden planks with iron,  
Beating the plow through the winter.  
Kubjas came from Kuura,  
Kiiter from Kiima tavern,  
Pundar with a stick on his shoulder,  
A bunch of switches in his belt.  
Vidas followed with the whirl.  
Kubjas steps toward me,  
I step toward the open field.  
In the open field, I have help,  
On the support road, I have support,  
So I can challenge Kubjas,  
Swing my chest at him.  
I struck Kubjas with a spruce branch like a devil,  
Kiltre quickly rushed after.  
Karmen jumped in both directions,  
The tassel struck behind the little whips.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, Mari with the low grass,  
She dances on Tõnismäe.  
The cross of Riga on the neck,  
The strike of Tallinn in the fist.  
She searches the town for that man,  
In the shop for that boy  
Who will save me from doom  
Redeem me from the snowstorm:  
From the Viru sickle blade,  
From the Harju plow cranes.  
After the Järva harrows.  
Viru women, the last lazy ones,  
Harju women, long lazy.  
They don’t bother to work,  
Nor to gather cream together.  
They fed strangers,  
Cream to the village herdsmen,  
To Peti’s family boys.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Why do you, ummer, examine me,  
long-assed, restrict me,  
wide-tailed, fence me in?  
I am not, ummer, to be examined,  
long-assed, not to be restricted,  
wide-tailed, not to be fenced!  
Examine, examine, what you examine,  
leave behind what you leave!  
Examine the new thigh's side,  
the corner of the young thigh,  
the gate of the old thigh -  
there is tar water in the pig's pen,  
five barrels of fresh water,  
six barrels of devil's stinger,  
where those examiners swim,  
where the trembling restrictors wash!

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Uist-hoot, dog barking,  
old lion growling!  
To the edge of the hole to breed,  
to the spikelet, lamb's pen!  
At the edge are the mighty sows,  
at the spikelet are the big lambs!  
Long ago you came close in vain—  
still your mouth is bloody,  
still your teeth rusted!  
Come to our feast,  
from there you will get blood sausage,  
from the street you will get lamb sausage,  
from Kubja copse dog sausage!

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The landlady of the cider house,  
went outside to look into the yard,  
to watch the gate!  
She kindled either the coal or the butter stove,  
the herdsmen of the Vare village,  
cheated the piglet of milk!

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
It would be my being,
for others my doing!
I would make scythes into ploughshares,
ploughs into harrows,
a wagon into a cart,
a forge into a ploughshare,
a forge into vines,
a shield into stakes,
and a farmhand into a winter man!

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our sturdy little lamb  
The lively colorful little bird  
Let the sisters go to the evening  
The sisters diligent in the morning  
Early before the white ones  
Before dawn, the cuckoo callers  
Before the day, the swallows

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
When I was a teenager  
A knee-high little boy  
At night I beat the manor’s barns  
By day I plowed the manor’s land  
Where I cried, the corners wet  
Where I stood, the walls ripe  
There a little pond sprouted,  
Grew into a fish pond  
From there the parish got boiling water  
And the old bachelor gained enlightenment

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
When I was a teenager  
A knee-high little boy  
At night I beat the manor’s barns  
By day I plowed the manor’s land  
Where I cried, the corners wet  
Where I stood, the walls ripe  
There a little pond sprouted,  
Grew into a fish pond  
From there the parish got boiling water  
And the old bachelor gained enlightenment

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The rich man said to me reeksa  
Monetary wheelseksa  
Vaseline carriage-seksa  
Or let me say it again differently  
On the mountain was a wet pine  
In the grove a treeless juniper  
From there come registrations for the rich  
In the monetary wheel barn  
In the golden arch  
In the copper carriage.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I started to leave  
I began to feel sad  
Tears rolled down my cheek  
I gave my place to another  
The spot to a different fool  
The room to another scoundrel  
I went there to see  
If I could find a better place  
From my seat an honest one  
I found from the place a worse one  
From the seat a more miserable one  
The room swept into corners  
The stove split into logs  
Kibu cried like a little snuffler.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh my little doctor sir
Little lady of the Golden Crown
Stand up on the chair
Step onto the window frame
Look at the poor parish
How the parish is troubled
Where did the German get those boots
Where did the servant boy bring the slippers from
If this parish has not made an effort.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When I had to leave the manor  
Then I had to leave hell  
Then I had to leave the warm mouth  
From the fangs of a spotted dog  
From the teeth of a monkey dog  
I don't look back at the milestone  
I don't turn my head for a dog load  
Before the home gate  
There the grave of hell is visible  
The home of death is grim  
The destroyer of people's souls.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When I had to serve in the manor  
Manor service, hellish service  
Between sharp teeth  
Between the jaws of a wolf dog  
It was truly hellish service  
That was in our manor  
The foreman was like the devil in front  
More crooked than the devil behind  
The workers were miserable in between  
It was an evil foreman indeed  
That was in our manor.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If only I were alive  
If only I were doing it  
Then I would know what I would do!  
I would make the gentlemen into bulls  
The knights into cranes  
And the maidens into sparrows

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When these Germans go to the poorhouse  
The gentlemen themselves start working  
The red trousers plundering the land  
The bluelegs making a mess.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, you gentlemen of Härbsi  
Where are you, Mrs. Roone  
Get up, go for a ride  
Get up, get ready  
Look at the poor parish  
How the parish is troubled  
The parish here is resting on a stake  
Farm boys to the whip.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When I escape from the manor
From the manor, from the marriage
I escaped like something warm from the mouth
From between the lion’s jaws
From the traces of sharp teeth
The wolf was left to rummage in its mouth
The lion was left to snap its jaws
The devil was made a chief
The thief became the leader of the district.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me  
Secondly, my doing  
I would surely know what I would do  
I would turn the Germans into burdock roots  
Gentlemen into oxen  
Kiltreds into sticks

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, that master Ärsi,  
The silver-tongued fellow,  
Rise up onto the chair  
Look upon the poor parish  
How this parish is tormented  
Tortured in every way.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The empty stomach is already gnawing
The weak lower back is bending
Gnawing empty without eating
Bending weak without drinking
There is no appetite for eating
Nor appetite for drinking
Dead are the appetites for eating
Dead are the appetites for drinking
Lost are the fish carriers
Gone are the bread carriers
What did I eat while eating
Or what did I drink while drinking
I did not eat the bull’s food
I did not drink the barrel’s drink
What could be eaten from the bull
The bone was underneath and the skin was on top
What could be drunk from the barrel
The yeast was underneath and the foam was on top
The tail drank and the horns drink
The tail remained as the horn’s fetter
The head remained to carry the liquors
The hooves to pour the liquor.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Who is waiting for me at home  
The dog is waiting for me at home  
Is it just a stranger watching  
The dog [waits] for bread crusts  
The sheep [waits] for bread crumbs  
The cat [waits] for little fish.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When I am dead  
The city birds will eat the flesh  
The coastal crabs will eat the fat  
The Viru crows will drink the blood  
The city birds became meaty  
The coastal crabs became fat  
The Viru crows became mighty  
Even if my flesh is eaten  
Even if my fat is wasted.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Mistress, young woman
Look for new slaves
Try new herdsmen
Find new bread-eaters
My year is over
My weeks are visible.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
What is a nap, that's why  
That I was in a bad hour  
For the lightness of spring  
For the gloominess of autumn  
For the winter snowfall  
The wealthy calls me a king  
A money wheel  
A property cart  
A golden carriage

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The hellish landlady  
Boiled the shoes, tanned the laces  
Gave snakes to the servant to eat  
Sprouts for the aunt to nibble  
Rags and scraps for the children to eat  
She herself ate the best parts.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The master praised the foolish generation  
The mistress the spirited generation  
Come to see for an hour  
And touch for two hours  
What kind of life I have  
Siberian life I have  
The prison life of a poor child.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Look, where the hell town shines
It seems to be the Devil's home
Here his ramparts whiten
Here his ridges turn green
Every day there is beating here
Meat slices are brewed
Blood is spilled on the altar
Food is sent to the German
Drinking is donated to the Junker
Salt is sprinkled from the smith
We help by extracting teeth.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Was the slave's luck gone
Or was it the family son's luck
The slave's luck was not gone
But it was the family son's luck.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh we poor servant boys  
Surely we will get wet here  
Where will we dry ourselves  
Let's go to the manor's threshing floor  
Who will we stoke the oven for there  
The blacksmith's thighs and the soldier's calves  
Let's help both of the lads.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The devil was hiding in the grove  
Rottenness played in the pine forest  
The demon hammered in the willow grove  
Made his cart a wheel shed  
An old lord had wagons  
With whom the German hell was taken  
Mistresses into the fire of life  
Peasants into heaven.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
How I must go home  
The dog is waiting for crusts  
The cat for bread crumbs  
From the bag came a crust for the dog  
For the cat, a bread crumb.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our billy goat lamb
Naughty colorful little bird
Our helper, giver of grace
Bachelor cheese flower
Let the children go to lunch
From the right time to the evening
The day has long since passed
Bigger bones die
Smaller hands grow tired
Long backs weaken

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me,
And my doing as well,
I would surely want
To know what I would do:
I would make the gentlemen cowards,
The barley sacks woodpigeons,
The warders handcuffs,
The junkers chatterboxes.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our young men from the manor -
When we came from the manor,
And we were wet,
Where do we dry ourselves?
We go to the barnyard at the manor,
There we dry ourselves.
What then do we put into the oven?
Boar legs, hindquarters of calves,
We help both stables,
The shinbones and ribs.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The master is angry with me  
The mistress is harsh to me,  
But I am eager for the day,  
For it is better to pair.  
Well, I eagerly sing,  
Quietly I apply the runes,  
I plow low and steady,  
I do not break the God's root,  
Nor the heart of the burdock's stalk.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I do not have to go,  
I do not have to leave my dear,  
I do not have to abandon the peoples,  
The better places.  
I leave this place behind,  
I give the spots to others,  
I rush to another,  
I proclaim the place to another.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
What was hell for ages past,  
That was in our manor too,  
Oxen trample the fields,  
Rough horses rake the grass,  
Ploughs between the shafts.  
Men ask you to pack up,  
Women weep by the doors.  
Hold the mitten in hand,  
Chicken claws bent,  
Bare under the shoulder.  
Oh master, dear master,  
Give me back my work,  
Give back the payment,  
For the German soldiers’ march.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The bailiff did not allow me a wife,  
The lord did not allow a purchase.  
But my sons are landlords,  
Daughters are maidens of Riga,  
Sister is a kitchen girl in Pärnu.  
There they do not give a wife to a servant,  
To the lady a servant girl,  
To the emperor the end of a rope.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, but I tended the herd.
I was a servant in Öland,
A shepherd boy in Livonia;
Öland gave me a new jacket
Livonia a wide coat.
Öland had proud stallions,
The draught horses had large humps,
They wanted to breed in the summer,
To raise golden combs,
To strip the bear’s kin.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I do not cry even at death,  
Nor fear the cold grave,  
I have no proud jewels,  
No pointed earrings.  
I have no wealth of money.  
The rich cry even at death,  
Fear the cold grave,  
The rich cry for jewels  
And pointed earrings.  
Ale but rilla-rallall-la

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our lord has several stables,  
In each stable seven horses,  
All want to feed the Aryan,  
To comb the golden mane,  
To harness the iron plow.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Little church boy Juani,  
Takes me away from the raindrops,  
Redeems me from the snowfall;  
Tall tower Peter,  
Buys me away from slavery,  
Removes the local brightness from my head.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When we come from the road  
And we are wet  
Where do we dry ourselves  
By the new thigh stove  
What do we then put in the stove there  
Kiltri’s thighs, tanner’s hides  
We both help with the yard work  
Then we dry ourselves.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, this ace of a gentleman  
Lady of the golden crown  
Rise up onto the chair  
Step onto the window frame  
Look at this poor parish  
How this parish is troubled  
The district is tormented  
There were five spirits, six were misers  
If it were up to me  
To do things differently  
I would make the gentlemen alert  
Put wedges into the shutters  
Add plows behind the horses  
Put yokes on the oxen  
Place bonnets on the young ladies  
Make myself the caller  
Plow the ridge, pour on the furrows  
Oh, old red furrow  
Lift a little soil  
Turn your head, little harness.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
We are expected home  
At the gate we are watched  
Hurrying home to fetch water  
The bucket waits by the room  
The bucket on the path to the well  
The milking stool by the barn door  
But what do I push to the bucket  
I push my shoulder to the bucket  
I reach my hands to the bucket  
My fingers push to the milking stool  
Oh, shoulder, be strong  
Oh, hands, be quick  
Oh, fingers, be nimble.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our men like those moles  
Carrying bags like those rats  
On the calf of the shin  
Half a peck on the hip  
On a cellar's claw

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Now it is time to go home  
The right to go to the evening hall  
The time was thought to be at home  
Sisters, dear ones  
When will we get home  
Five was taken out in between  
Six was to dry the river  
Seven soda tough woman  
Eight fish creek  
Nine good rivers  
Ten cold springs.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The German boy had drunk himself full  
His silk pants full of shit  
The whole world was full of stench  
The willow bush full of pus.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, we poor purchase slaves  
Purchase slaves, wreath bearers  
Bound servants  
Who buys us from slavery  
Who crowns us under the wreath  
Redeems us from the snowfall  
Takes us away from the rain showers  
From the plow cranes of Harju  
From the blade of Viru's sickle  
From the thunderclaps of Järva  
From the golden church of Kos  
From the low towers of Madise  
From the tall tower of Peeter  
That one buys us from slavery  
And crowns us under the wreath  
Redeems us from the snowfall  
Takes us away from the rain showers  
From the plow cranes of Harju  
From the blade of Viru's sickle  
From the thunderclaps of Järva

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Ingu-tranku-triina-trei  
Pressi-kinder, viira-vei  
Where did the smith's head go  
Because he got the ghost's storm cap  
Where did the smith's hands go  
From them became stiff toes in front of the thigh  
Where did the smith's fingers go  
From them became the barn rods in front of the thigh  
Where did the smith's forearms go  
From them became stiff toes in front of the thigh  
Where did the smith's thighs go  
In front of the thigh stiff toes  
Where did the smith's soles go  
From them became the spirit's frying pans  
Where did the smith's stomach go  
Because he got the ghost's bagpipe  
Where did the smith's intestines go  
From them became the coachmen on the sled  
Where did the smith's ??? go   
From them became river barriers  
Where did the smith's testicles go  
From them became the maiden's trumpet glasses.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Oh, I am a little man  
A knee-high little boy  
At night I beat the manor's threshing floor  
By day I plowed the manor fields  
Where I stood, the walls were wet  
Where I cried, the corners were wet  
From there the rivers kept running  
The grays kept brooding  
Mother, the rivers kept running  
Into my grown tear water.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Oh, I am a little man  
A knee-high little boy  
At night I beat the manor's threshing floor  
By day I plowed the manor fields  
Where I stood, the walls were wet  
Where I cried, the corners were wet  
From there the rivers kept running  
The grays kept brooding  
Mother, the rivers kept running  
Into my grown tear water.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If I were in charge  
And doing the work myself  
I would know exactly what I would do  
I would make the gentlemen into oxen  
The lads into cranes  
The girls into matches  
I would call the saw myself  
To plow the field, to carry the load  
The old red walks the track  
If it does not plow the field  
I will change the old red  
Then it will plow the field  
Then it will pour the load  
The old red walks the track  
Turns its head with a halter  
Lifts the ground with a little pull.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, this gentlemen of the noble pea  
Madam with the golden crown  
Rise up onto the chair  
Step onto the window frame  
Look at the poor parish  
How this parish is tormented  
The little ones are tortured  
Those with fingers are cursed  
Those with a thumb are pointed at.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Now many have fallen asleep  
Tablefuls lie fallen  
The people quiet down to sleep  
Oh, you Jaan, dog of Järva  
Come down from the hayloft  
Take the horse from the yard  
To the new streets of the sled  
A bag of gold on the street  
Ride to the valley, wait there  
To the hill, look there  
Wait for that comrade  
Who sings here at the table  
Calls out at the end of the long bench.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, my beloved little gentleman  
Golden crown’s little lady  
Silver-buttoned young Saxons  
Let us rise from the chair  
Ah, rise from the seat  
Let us get to the great hall  
Let us step onto the window ledge  
Look at this poor parish  
At the miserable parish  
How this parish is tormented  
The little ones are being tortured  
Time must be given  
Hours are being pulled  
Tears were heard into the meadow  
Harshness in Aru village  
Whip lashes in Paide.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, I herded sheep  
I was under Dutch custody  
A shepherd boy from Livonia  
From Holland I got a new coat  
From Livonia I got a fur jacket  
Made of flags and patches  
From the legs of an old dog.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Some expect me home  
They look toward the gate  
Who is waiting for me at home  
The dog is waiting for me at home  
Who is waiting for me at home  
The cat is waiting for me at home  
The dog is waiting for some bones  
The cat for some fish heads  
Oh, we poor bought slaves  
Bought slaves, crowned with wreaths  
We must walk at night  
Walk at night in the pain of night  
We must walk during the day  
During the day under the day's glare  
The moon withers our moonlit work  
The day fades our wreath's inscription.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Master, little master  
Mistress, little mistress  
Look for new slaves  
Try new herders  
Mourn new bread-eaters  
My years are over  
My days are done  
My weeks are visible.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The Devil digs in the pine grove  
The fiend bangs on the cliff  
But makes a cartwheel shed  
An old gentleman's wagon  
With whom the German hell led  
Peasants to heaven.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If I were to be myself  
If I were to do my thing  
I would surely know what I would do  
If the Karner family didn’t interfere.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our little hay boys  
When we came from the field  
And we were wet  
Where do we dry ourselves  
Let's go to the manor's threshing barn  
There we dry ourselves  
What do we then put on the oven  
Carpenter's legs, miller's thighs  
We help both barns  
The barn father's chest.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The master is angry with me  
The mistress is harsh to me  
But I am a good ox for the lord  
And even better for the bailiff  
I plow diligently and early  
I quietly harness the reins  
I plow the land low  
I do not damage the clover root  
Nor the burdock heart beneath the hoof.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
But the brother stood guard at the door  
The one who sets the barrel in place  
He eats even the bull's leftover feed  
And drinks from the vat's leftover drink.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh God, little god
Throw down the woolen ropes
Push down the tar ropes
Drop the long ropes
Stretch out the bonds
Pull me up to the sky
Disturb the wealthy guards
Despise the household owners
The rich called me a rascal
A cog in the money wheel.
I was not a servant of the rich
Nor a cog in the money wheel.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Evening comes, arriving late  
Darkness begins to take over  
Evening brings straw to the barn  
Twilight leads to sleep  
Dawn awakens  
Spreads white everywhere  
Breaks into pieces at the break of day  
One here, another there  
To some manor’s yard  
To a couple of manor fields  
Even this is just right for a couple  
Of those manor fields  
Five were for the spirits to go away  
Six followed the reapers  
Seven were for the flail-beaters  
Eight for the punishers  
Nine were for the callers  
Ten for the thoughtful ones  
A strike along my forehead  
Properly along my shoulder  
Straw along the edge of my back  
So that my envelope jumps  
Wide and broad it flaps  
A patchy crack echoes

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Evening comes, arriving late  
Darkness begins to take over  
Evening brings straw to the barn  
Twilight leads to sleep  
Dawn awakens  
Spreads white everywhere  
Breaks into pieces at the break of day  
One here, another there  
To some manor’s yard  
To a couple of manor fields  
Even this is just right for a couple  
Of those manor fields  
Five were for the spirits to go away  
Six followed the reapers  
Seven were for the flail-beaters  
Eight for the punishers  
Nine were for the callers  
Ten for the thoughtful ones  
A strike along my forehead  
Properly along my shoulder  
Straw along the edge of my back  
So that my envelope jumps  
Wide and broad it flaps  
A patchy crack echoes

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh our dear sir  
Or our ancient lady  
Silver buttons, young gentlemen  
Golden goblets, young ladies,  
Lift them up onto the chair  
Get up onto the hall  
Step onto the windowsill  
How this land is troubled  
The smaller ones are tormented  
The larger ones are killed  
The smaller ones’ hands grow weary  
The long arms weaken  
The larger ones’ bones die  
Here on the great field  
On the endless field.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
A hundred rains, pour German,
Leave me without dew
I have no home dryer
For whitening white shirts,
For washing fine shirts,
Who whitens my white shirts
Who fades the fine ones
If only it were under my control
Control of mind, control of language
Control of mouth, control of heart
Control over white hair
Then I would know what I would do
I would make gentlemen into oxen
Ladies into slaves
I myself would become a serf.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
A hundred rains, pour German,
Leave me without dew
I have no home dryer
For whitening white shirts,
For washing fine shirts,
Who whitens my white shirts
Who fades the fine ones
If only it were under my control
Control of mind, control of language
Control of mouth, control of heart
Control over white hair
Then I would know what I would do
I would make gentlemen into oxen
Ladies into slaves
I myself would become a serf.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh you sudden little antler  
Golden crown little lady  
Wake up by the window  
Rise up onto the chair  
Get up onto the hall  
Look upon the poor parish  
Defy the parish  
How this parish is tormented  
The parish is bullied  
The feeble are wasted  
The little ones are tormented  
Oh you dear village elder  
Honeyberry manor lord  
You should start thinking  
And remind your mind  
Our food without a meal  
Expanses without greatness  
The day turns to mid-morning  
The dawn lingers into the south.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The mistress, the maiden  
When she hears the servant coming  
Sings behind the forest  
Rings in front of the forest  
Then she starts gathering grass  
Looking for sorrel  
Then she starts cooking soup  
Does not add salt  
Does not sprinkle a drop of milk on top  
The servant eats, the heart weeps  
The face lets down a tear  
The eyes roll with water  
The eyebrows with a drop of gold.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, that handsome gentleman  
And that dear officer  
Stand up onto the chair  
Step onto the edge of the sled  
Look at this poor parish  
How this parish is tormented  
The little ones are being tortured.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
How do I have to go home  
Home to the evil message  
To the worse prayer  
Home the evil ones are cast down  
The bad words are forgiven  
The bad words are scattered  
Master, little master  
Mistress, little mistress  
Hears me coming home  
To be sorted out  
Singing behind the forest  
Jump, Eltse, spring, Kaie  
Turn the gates around  
Lock the doors tight  
Listen where the eagle falcon falls  
The fierce titmouse chirps  
Now comes home the big eater  
Big eater, big drinker  
The one who places the barrel  
The barrel eats even his food  
Doesn’t eat even his food  
Drinks from the barrel too  
I hear, I answer  
What use is the ox to me for food  
And what use is the barrel to me for drink  
Shit is inside, the skin is on  
Sometimes there is bone in the middle  
What use is the barrel to me for drink  
The foam is on top, the yeast is below  
A mug of beer in the middle  
Master, little master  
Mistress, little mistress  
You walk to the village in heaven  
With a loaf carrier in hand  
And herring boxes in the armpits  
Come to eat, poor spirits  
Most unfortunate servants  
That was yours, that was mine.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
How do I have to go home  
Home to the evil message  
To the worse prayer  
Home the evil ones are cast down  
The bad words are forgiven  
The bad words are scattered  
Master, little master  
Mistress, little mistress  
Hears me coming home  
To be sorted out  
Singing behind the forest  
Jump, Eltse, spring, Kaie  
Turn the gates around  
Lock the doors tight  
Listen where the eagle falcon falls  
The fierce titmouse chirps  
Now comes home the big eater  
Big eater, big drinker  
The one who places the barrel  
The barrel eats even his food  
Doesn’t eat even his food  
Drinks from the barrel too  
I hear, I answer  
What use is the ox to me for food  
And what use is the barrel to me for drink  
Shit is inside, the skin is on  
Sometimes there is bone in the middle  
What use is the barrel to me for drink  
The foam is on top, the yeast is below  
A mug of beer in the middle  
Master, little master  
Mistress, little mistress  
You walk to the village in heaven  
With a loaf carrier in hand  
And herring boxes in the armpits  
Come to eat, poor spirits  
Most unfortunate servants  
That was yours, that was mine.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I do not care for proud women  
Nor do I fear strong women  
I put proud ones to sleep  
I take down strong ones  
I will not leave, I will not retreat  
I leave this place to you  
I give the place to others  
I push others to the rooms  
Let better ones come to the place  
Let more honest ones take their place  
Let me come back tonight  
Let me come to see tomorrow  
If there are better ones in the place  
If there are more honest ones in the place  
There were no better ones in the place  
There were no more honest ones in their place  
The fences are still where I left them  
The benches still where I kept them  
The chairs still where I put them  
There were no better ones in the place  
There were no more honest ones in their place.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If I were in charge  
If it were up to me  
I would surely know what to do  
I would turn the gentlemen into oxen

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me,  
If it were my doing,  
I would surely know what I would do:  
I would turn the gentlemen into oxen.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
What an evil curse it is  
A cruel old gentleman's curse  
The oxen eat the fodder  
The runes wither in the rake  
The smith has his hat in his hand  
The spirit has a stone on his neck  
The apple tree branch swings  
The rowan branch sways  
The bird cherry branch beats  
The maple branch moans  
This went past the girl  
Caressing her back  
Caressing the row of boards
