Let it be, let it be, I curse it  
Let it sicken and die  
Let the lord fail  
Lonely today, two tomorrow  
Today and tomorrow five or six  
May St. George vanish to the root  
May St. Martin fall to the ground.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
So it has been through ages in hell  
As it is in our manor as well  
The young men row into the house  
The poor lads into the racks  
The men beg for a bundle  
The women wander by the door  
Oh, this young lord  
The lady with the golden crown  
The young lady with silver lace  
The virgins with pins  
Rise up onto the chair  
Look at the poor parish  
How this parish is tormented  
How the congregation is troubled

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Little blossom and little crabapple  
We waste German money  
We keep our own money  
How will we get it,  
If we go to town  
We take grain there  
Then we go to help  
Cold in front and shovel inside  
We pile up the sacks  
If the master happens to come  
To stop by the young master  
To dash to the threshing floor  
Can we then look for it there  
I do not want heaven  
Heaven is high and cold  
Hell is closer and warm  
There we go like those Germans  
With boots on  
Clicking and snapping.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Now it is time for the court to go home  
The right to go to the evening meal  
It is time to return to the house  
Who is waiting for me at home  
The dog is waiting for me at home  
The cat admonishes me to come into the house  
The dog is waiting for scraps  
The cat for crumbs of bread.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Pailu causes trouble in the family  
Pailu eats the family's food  
The ox eats even what he eats  
The barrel drinks even what he drinks.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The windows of hell are burning  
It seems like the devil's house.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, I herded sheep  
I was a servant of Olland  
A sheep boy of Liiderland  
My wage was the foam road  
A sheep to the meadow with a ram.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The devil dug in the spruce forest  
The devil hammered in the bush  
Made a carriage wheel  
To take a boy to hell  
An old girl to go to heaven.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
I was a little child  
And a boy taller than my knee  
Vat luli, vat luuli  
A boy taller than my knee  
At night I beat the manor’s stack  
By day I mowed the meadow pieces  
Where I sat there I cried  
Where I stood the walls were wet  
There formed a little pond  
A little lake grew fish in it.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
I was a little child  
And a boy taller than my knee  
Vat luli, vat luuli  
A boy taller than my knee  
At night I beat the manor’s stack  
By day I mowed the meadow pieces  
Where I sat there I cried  
Where I stood the walls were wet  
There formed a little pond  
A little lake grew fish in it.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, I tended the herd  
A knee-high little boy  
Then I beat the manor barn  
And also plowed the fertile land  
Yo yo yo yo yo yo yo.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If only I were,
If only I did,
I would surely know what I would do:
I would make gentlemen oxen.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I do not know the devil’s place  
I do not know the cursed spot  
Hell’s windows are burning  
Ghost towers are looming  
Where is the path of my punishment  
The painful way of the wrist  
The cursed path of the finger bone  
Young gentlemen to the silver button  
Apprentices to the golden lace  
Rise up onto the chair  
Step onto the window frame  
Look upon the poor parish  
How this parish is tormented  
The parish is being harassed  
Oxen eat the fodder  
Runes lie neglected  
Daughters at work  
Men putting on their clothes.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh my good sir  
Oh my good madam  
Silver bonnets for the young ladies  
Gold buttons for the young gentlemen  
Rise up from the chair  
From the edge of the barn hall  
Step up to the window frame  
Come to see the parish  
How the parish is tormented  
The little ones are tormented  
The parish is troubled  
The unclean are wasted  
The poor spirits are on the stake  
The workers are on the staff.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Bitter was the squire's lineage  
Fierce was the old gentleman's lineage  
The bulls still graze here  
The oxen feebly work in the harness  
The oxen groaned with their horns  
The oxen swung their chests around  
Men suffered on their knees  
Calf muscles were asleep.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When I must leave Tapa  
From the pile of Tapa, from the land of Tapa  
From the place of Tapa’s evil  
From beneath the poor German  
Beneath the loud old gentleman  
Beneath the cruel lord  
I would look back quietly  
Turn my head on the wagons’ load  
Before I go home as a companion  
Under a man's love outside  
Then I would look back a little  
Turn my head a little  
See where the devil’s place is  
See where the damn spot is  
The windows of hell are burning  
Heating the evil spirit  
The ghosts’ towers are standing  
Here, on my head, the path of torment  
In the sleeve, the path of trouble  
On the finger bones, the path of curses  
If only my yards were rowans  
My sleeves were maples  
My fingers were bird cherry boughs  
Then I would know what I would do  
I would make the gentlemen oxen  
The young lads oxen calves  
The Germans plowshares of the fields  
The peasants plowshares of the furrows  
I would strike the hill of the tufted one  
The thigh bark of the one with spots on his chest  
The old gentleman down to the ground.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Whose manor does this seem to be  
Whose buildings are in ruins  
Karjalaada on the slope  
It seems to be Põdrangu manor  
Põdrangu buildings in ruins  
Karjalaada on the slope.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The belly already aches from emptiness  
The weak waist bends  
Because it aches, it is empty  
Because it bends, it is weak  
The ache is caused by emptiness  
The bend is caused by weakness  
Motherly, little old woman  
Seek confirmation from truth  
Endure beneath the cover  
If there is no confirmation from truth  
Do not endure beneath the cover  
Motherly, little old woman  
Have a wide belt woven  
Twisted from shop yarn  
Laid from city yarn  
Rolled from Russian yarn  
To support the body  
To strengthen the weak body.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, I tended the flock  
I was a servant of the Hollender  
A shepherd boy of Livland  
The Hollender made a new coat  
A wide coat for Livland  
From flags and patches  
And from dog skin legs.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I worked, was not thanked  
I struggled, was cursed  
All my fingers were scolded  
The soles of my feet were slandered  
I did a bitter work  
I boast but at a crazy builder  
I sweep a mad room  
Tight but a sparse chamber  
Do not insist on understanding thanks  
Crazy but wanted to be proud.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Vamos, vamos a Vaita  
Vamos al granero de la finca Vaida  
En la finca Vaida hay grandes graneros  
Grandes graneros, anchos establos  
Si no puedo entrar en la casa  
Dejo entrar por el techo  
Me siento al borde de la plataforma  
Plancho con la plancha de hierro en mano  
En la atalaya, en el establo  
Reparto pan a los pobres del condado,  
A los mendigos del distrito.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh God, little god,
Hear the sighs of the slave
The groans of the poor child
The slave sighs in the morning dew
Poor children in the white light
Oh God, little god
Throw down the whip ropes
Push down the tar ropes
So one could become a tenant of heaven
A haymower for the father
A creator's receiver
A plowman for Mary.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Mother, little granny  
Stoke the oven for the kitchen lady  
Honor the whisk for the sulfur  
Whip my reins from my hand  
That which the angry scoundrel struck  
Pressed the throttle firmly

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh my Mr. Ärsi  
Lady of the Golden Crown  
Young gentlemen with silver buttons  
Copper-colored parish elders  
Rise from your beds  
Get up from your soft pillows  
Rise onto your chairs  
Get up into the hall  
Step onto the windowsill  
Look at the poor parish  
How this parish is afflicted  
The small are tormented  
Those with less are twisted  
The great bones die  
The smaller bones tire.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Hello room, you must feel  
Hello lee, you must find  
Hello honest hosts  
Get out, householders  
Already the sieve stands at the edge  
Already the moon in front of the house  
Poles against the white fence  
Hello father-in-law, hello mother-in-law  
Get out, householders  
Hello room, box pattern  
Hello ceiling, grass pattern  
Our village smells of caraway  
Village paths to sulfur  
Streets to the plaster  
We sing through the villages  
Through the trampled street  
Through the new lanes  
Dear granny, little granny  
Dear grandpa, little grandpa  
Heat the stove with caraway  
The sauna to the smoke of the island  
Room for the market  
I come to thresh the shaken  
To weave the flaxfield  
Duck, I come to wash knees  
Praise from the whip strokes on the hip  
From the whip strokes on the arm  
What the angry smith had beaten  
The fierce village head had done  
We rush, serious sisters  
Let’s go with two hands  
So that our faces shine  
Let’s sink through hardship  
We’ll reach the edge of the island  
At the end, we have red beer  
In the wagon, we have jugs of rock  
In the garden, we have milk pies  
Then we will not be beaten with a whip  
Not let go on the pink meadow  
Listen, dear gentleman  
Rise up onto the chair  
Step onto the high table  
Look upon the poor parish  
How the parish is troubled  
The poor spirits are whipped

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Hostesses, tall women  
Farm women, wise women  
These women, stubborn women  
Eat cream, drink milk  
Gave butter to the nobles  
Cream to the village herdsmen  
In the evening the servant comes home  
Then these weeds are picked  
Thistles are searched for  
Mallow tea is brewed  
The servant eats, the heart aches  
But the shoulders let the waves pass  
Eyebrows like drops of gold.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Hostesses, tall women  
Farm women, wise women  
These women, stubborn women  
Eat cream, drink milk  
Gave butter to the nobles  
Cream to the village herdsmen

The servant eats, the heart aches  
But the shoulders let the waves pass  
Eyebrows like drops of gold.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me  
And my doing as well  
I would surely know what I would do  
I would make the gentlemen plow  
And the ladies work the fields.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our little golden knight
A brightly patterned little bird
Let the children go to lunch
Gentle sisters to the evening
The children ask to rest
The gentle sisters to put to bed
But I am waiting for the morning
For the new day to rise
To turn the yellow dawn
The lazy one waits for the evening
The sluggish day to roll on

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Where did that German get those boots  
The village boy brought those slippers  
If this manor had no troubles  
The village boy did no work  
The child of the spirit was troubled  
The village boy was tired of the task.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The family tree was cruel to me  
The family house was loud to me  
I was good to the wake-goers  
I was just right for the oxen pairs  
I grazed nicely  
I harnessed the horses gently  
From low ground I plowed the land  
I do not damage the root of the yoke  
I do not fear the family roots  
The yoke has large roots  
The sturdy branch has wise children  
The family is just right for the branch

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Home is waiting for us  
They look at the gate  
Who is waiting for me at home  
The dog, home is waiting for me  
Who is calling into the house  
The dog is waiting for the puppies  
The cat, however, is in the corner of the box

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Because I was happy  
Always it was sad  
That my bride went to be honored  
Honored and scorned  
Under the window of Aruküla manor  
The porridge pot was on the hip  
The fish net was on the neck  
Digging a deep ditch  
Turning the soil.  
Afterwards, you come at the boy’s request  
A sheaf of oats over the shoulder  
Two pillows under your arm  
A linen cloth fluttering in the hand  
Dear boy and beloved boy  
Buy me out of slavery  
Rescue me from the days  
Redeem me from the snowfall  
From the swing of the Viru sickle  
From the cranes of the Harju plow  
From behind the harrows of Järva.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Master, little master  
Mistress, little mistress  
Pay well the servant’s wages  
Servant’s wages, poor labor  
If you don’t pay to that land  
Then you will pay to that land  
Surely you will pay to heaven  
You will walk in the hand of the hangman  
Take the wages, poor servant  
Cannot take, not permitted

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, this young gentleman  
The lady with the golden crown  
Step onto the window frame  
Climb up onto the chair  
Look upon the poor parish  
How this parish is troubled  
The greater ones are killed  
The smaller ones are tormented

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, this young gentleman,  
The lady with the golden crown,  
Rise up onto the chair,  
Look at the poor parish,  
How this parish is troubled,  
How the little ones are tormented.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our trusty little gold one  
The striped, colorful little bird  
Let the children go to lunch  
At the proper time in the evening  
Your trusty devil’s son  
The striped colorful rascal’s son  
Does not let children go to lunch  
At the proper time in the evening

label: Lastelaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Iidu-tiidu, I’m coming to you  
Silent sir out in the field  
Stockings torn, shoes broken  
I sit down on a stone  
I begin to mend the shoes  
Out came a black cat  
A multicolored cat of the writer  
An old man’s white cat  
He took my just-right patch  
I chased after the cat  
The cat ran to the master’s barn  
I ran to the mistress’s barn  
The mistress cooked berry jam  
Melted sweet honey  
I went to taste the honey  
To taste the berry jam  
The girl struck with a match  
The housewife with a fart  
The granny with a jam ladle  
Then I got the enemy grumbling  
The man-in-long-pants weaving  
Along the field, along the little field  
Sometimes along the garden’s edge  
I got behind the garden corner  
There I look back once  
Lo and behold, where the hell’s pit shines  
The devil’s abode seems to appear  
Hell pits are turning green  
Phantom towers are glaring

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Teupoissi, dog boy  
He chose to steal  
He was angry to carry the sack  
To straighten his back  
His tent was his fingertip  
His cellar was his fingernail  
His whip was on his shin  
His bundle was on his back.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If only I were alive  
And secondly, my doing  
I would surely know what I would do  
I would set the horses to plow  
Help drive the furrow

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Last year, the noon was elsewhere  
In Hiiumaa, the noon came late  
In Harju County, the noon was long ago  
In Järva County, the noon came again  
Our noon is unharvested  
Fish are broken, not cut  
Seaweed porridge is not pounded  
Salt is not kneaded in  
The noon bell has already struck  
Everyone else has long eaten  
I have no one forcing me to eat  
Nor a wide table to set  
I have no wife who bakes bread  
Nor a child who braids hair  
Alone I go and alone I walk  
Alone I sleep and worry  
Saddened beneath the spruce  
Who will come to look after the poor?  
To bring aid for the needy?  
To comfort the troubled?

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Last year, the noon was elsewhere  
In Hiiumaa, the noon came late  
In Harju County, the noon was long ago  
In Järva County, the noon came again  
Our noon is unharvested  
Fish are broken, not cut  
Seaweed porridge is not pounded  
Salt is not kneaded in  
The noon bell has already struck  
Everyone else has long eaten  
I have no one forcing me to eat  
Nor a wide table to set  
I have no wife who bakes bread  
Nor a child who braids hair  
Alone I go and alone I walk  
Alone I sleep and worry  
Saddened beneath the spruce  
Who will come to look after the poor?  
To bring aid for the needy?  
To comfort the troubled?

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Today the order was given  
Tomorrow presented at the manor  
Tomorrow we spirits go to the manor  
The steward will take us to work immediately  
When I am in the master’s service  
We cut wood on the hill near the hay barn  
In the field we work hard  
Big tasks have been assigned  
The steward’s stick is pressed to the nape  
Oh, how frightening it is to wait  
We have to do the wretched manor work  
He gives a blow, gives two  
Sometimes comes a third  
The blow comes afternoon or evening  
So I run home again  
Who waits for me at home?  
The cat waits for me at home  
Who watches me at the gate?  
The dog watches me at the gate  
The dog waits for the puppies  
Runs to the corners of the box  
When I finally came home  
By the fence I stood guard  
The hellish housewife  
Saw me coming home  
By the fence I stood guard  
Wondering where the great eater comes from  
Great eater, great drinker  
The one who puts the barrel in place  
Ends all our drinks  
Our drinks, our food  
Cuts off the barn corners  
Rips off our storage corners  
Empties everything -  
Oh, I poor servant child  
It is miserable for me to have a spirit  
The housewife scolds me  
The manor steward presses on my nape  
When will I be on my own  
When will I see a man  
See a man, recognize a boy  
Live on my own  
Live well on my own  
I bother with my work  
I have no fear, I have grace  
The young man tempts me  
Holds me tight in his arms

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I respect father  
I respect mother  
I respectfully pass to the brothers  
Yet the brothers are fighting  
The sisters-in-law nod  
That's where the big eater comes from  
Big eater, big drinker  
One who puts things in place  
The ox eats even his food  
The vat drinks even his drink  
I understand, I again respond  
What is there to eat from an ox  
The bone is below and the skin is above  
A stick of lean meat  
Or what to drink from that vat  
The yeast is below and the scum is above  
A drop of good beer.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
When I was a young boy  
A knee-high lad  
At night I beat the manor’s barn  
By day I mowed pieces of hay  
Where I cry, corners are wet  
Where I stand, walls are wet  
There appeared a little pond  
A small fish pond grew  
From there the village herds could drink  
To bring forth from the manor  
My life was very hard  
My work was great and high  
The manor lord was very angry,  
Mealtime was very scarce  
To bring to the hut, the sack was heavy  
The mistress was very proud  
She put dried fish into the bread  
The cabbage pot was halfway empty

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
When I was a young boy  
A knee-high lad  
At night I beat the manor’s barn  
By day I mowed pieces of hay

The cabbage pot was halfway empty

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
What the hell are you investigating me for  
Long ass borders me  
Wide lamias me  
I am not to be investigated  
Ass is not to be bordered long  
Belly is not to be lamed wide  
On the new, new thigh  
On the wood on the thigh  
There is pitch, tar water  
Two vats of devilship  
Where those investigators swim  
Field borders are washed.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Loitsud
translation:
This is how people react to us  
They react, they laugh  
Empty talk is spread  
False talk is told  
Like fire from wood  
Water in a boiling kettle  
So let there be talk about me  
Empty talk about a girl  
False talk about a poor child  
Stories ran, but I did not run  
Noise happened, but I did not go  
Let the stories run without drinking  
Noise in the tavern without going  
What do I promise to the reacter  
To the reacter, to the laugher  
To the spreader of empty talk  
To the blamer of oneself.  
Let it be, let me curse  
Let it be as I curse  
Worms coil on the tongue  
Maggots settle on the lips  
Food to the heart  
This I promise to the reacter  
To the reacter, to the laugher  
To the blamer of oneself.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Loitsud
translation:

To the spreader of empty talk

To the blamer of oneself.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Sister, dear little brother  
Where are we going towards the night  
Towards the night, towards the darkness  
Already the hellmouth shines for us  
It seems like the devil’s house  
The ghost house is turning green  
That’s the children’s pasture  
The daughters’ dance place  
The shield-rim’s assembly place  
The priests’ mockers  
Every day there is beating here  
Every temple is opened  
Blood is drawn and measured  
On the carpet, halfway the chest  
The carpet was brought for a visit  
The stove emptied from the farmstead  
But the chest was preserved for the family  
Then blood is measured  
On the carpet, halfway the chest  
Oh, that dear Kapu parish  
Love Aruküla  
There the barns are beaten  
The pipe organ plays the wind path  
But the devil take Väinjärve parish  
Among other parishes.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh German tailless dog  
The master without a tail  
You yourself travel along the road  
Running along the edge of the fence  
Eyes burning towards the field  
The heart yearns for this hunting  
When the eyes of that mad wolf  
Are like the eyes of a spring hare

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Young gentlemen with silver buttons  
Young ladies with golden combs  
Rise up onto the chair  
Get up into the hall  
Go out to the window path  
Look upon the poor parish  
How this parish is troubled  
The smaller ones tormented  
The greater ones killed.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
We turn the plows into harrows,  
The caps into cranes,  
The ladies into sticks,  
The kilts into wedges,  
Plow Küit and pour into Lauk,  
And into the old Vaol, the Red.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our stout little ram,
A colorful, speckled little bird,
Let the children have lunch,
The gentle sisters under the evening,
Others eat for the second time,
The household people for the third time,
Our dear ones without a meal,
The salmon without lunch,
But young master Ärsinakke,
The golden-lace young lady,
Rise up onto the chair,
Step onto the windowsill,
Look upon the poor parish,
The colorful parish,
How the parish is troubled,
The congregation tormented.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The solemn crow, little bird  
Laid eggs beneath our hill  
In the barn under our granary  
Then brought out five chicks  
From one came a handsome father  
From another a calm mother  
From the third came a dog boy  
From the fourth a night guard  
From the fifth a playful lad  
The solemn crow, little bird  
The rich man kills a big bull  
I will kill the old crow  
Because I get to eat, because I get to drink  
Because I get to play and fool around  
From the beak I get a snuff-box  
From the tail I get a hundred cap  
From the side bone I get a seed hook.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When we get to our house  
Where we dry ourselves  
We go to the countryside manors  
To the countryside manor knees  
Where we dry ourselves  
What do we put in the oven there  
Kubja smith’s squire’s thighs  
The thick thighs of the barn keeper  
We both help the threshing floors  
That’s what we put in the oven there  
Where we dry ourselves.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Who is waiting for me at home  
Who is urging me into the house  
The cat is waiting for me at home  
The cat is urging me into the house  
Who is watching me at the gate  
The dog is watching me at the gate  
The dog awaits the choir  
The cat the corners of the basket  
The mistress, the shepherd dog  
Heard me coming home  
At the edge of the path, indeed being  
Behind, in the forest, singing  
The tune from his bread table  
And the knife from the corner of the table  
Carried to the cupboard the cake bowls  
Mother, the gentle old woman  
Brought me the full cake bowl  
You came from work, weary  
You come from the field, tired  
What’s the use of being tired from nothing  
What’s the use of being tired from trifles.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Who is waiting for me at home  
Who is urging me into the house  
The cat is waiting for me at home  
The cat is urging me into the house  
Who is watching me at the gate  
The dog is watching me at the gate  
The dog awaits the choir  
The cat the corners of the basket  
The mistress, the shepherd dog  
Heard me coming home  
At the edge of the path, indeed being  
Behind, in the forest, singing  
The tune from his bread table  
And the knife from the corner of the table  
Carried to the cupboard the cake bowls  
Mother, the gentle old woman  
Brought me the full cake bowl  
You came from work, weary  
You come from the field, tired  
What’s the use of being tired from nothing  
What’s the use of being tired from trifles.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our handsome ram lamb  
A quirky, colorful little bird  
Let the forehead be exposed to air,  
If it doesn’t, don’t ask for it either  
Let Monday morning come  
I will go to the gentleman’s hayfield  
Then to the lady’s field  
I am darker than this mole  
Dumber than this rat  
Then I will disgrace the gentleman  
And do good to the lady.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Kalendrilaulud
translation:
If it were my existence  
If it were my doing  
I would make the gentlemen oxen  
The young ladies headscarves  
I myself would start plowing  
Go to the village to plow  
Furrows in the meadow under the fence  
The oxen pulled strong ahead  
The plows followed behind  
I went under the village swing  
What did I find under the swing?  
I found a flea swinging  
On the old lady’s lap  
I sat on the flea’s stomach  
On the old lady’s knee  
I went again under the swing  
The men watched from the forest  
The boys kneeled behind the stove  
If those men were mine  
I would first put beads on the old woman’s neck  
Before on the neck of that maiden.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Kalendrilaulud
translation:
If it were my existence  
If it were my doing  
I would make the gentlemen oxen  
The young ladies headscarves  
I myself would start plowing  
Go to the village to plow  
Furrows in the meadow under the fence  
The oxen pulled strong ahead  
The plows followed behind  
I went under the village swing  
What did I find under the swing?  
I found a flea swinging  
On the old lady’s lap  
I sat on the flea’s stomach  
On the old lady’s knee  
I went again under the swing  
The men watched from the forest  
The boys kneeled behind the stove  
If those men were mine  
I would first put beads on the old woman’s neck  
Before on the neck of that maiden.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Damn this manor  
Kärvaku Kareda manor  
To be praised, talked about,  
To be boasted among the folks.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Already the moon stands before the hut  
Already the sieve stands at the edge  
Already the dawn to the golden day  
A sign of evening star is seen  
Now it is time to go home  
Time to settle in the house  
Right to go to the evening meal.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me  
Secondly, my doing  
I would know what I would do  
I would make the gentlemen oxen  
Kilts cranes  
Maidens as necklaces  
I would go behind to call  
Plow the field, pour the grain  
Do not furrow the old red  
Turn the head of the brindle  
I went on Tuesday to the fair  
I had a piece of hay against the grain  
A patch of bog at the edge  
A patch of wet at the hill’s edge  
I would plow against the grain  
I would not ruin the ridges of the rye  
I would not spoil the shoots of the grass horns

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Fallen valley, cursed valley  
This valley, Siberian valley  
Pigs in a row, men in prison  
Boys stand at the post begging  
Women walked to the manors  
Egg gloves on their hands  
Butter churn to the cellar  
Under the gray shawl  
Sheep in the rolling cart  
Save the noblemen from the column  
The sons-in-law from the pole  
Prisoners from the wagon wheel  
What evil did I do in the city  
I brought a piece of tobacco from the market  
Half a pound from the shop boy  
Then I got the enemy cursing  
Along the road, along the side road  
I reached behind the garden corner  
There I made my escape  
Swing your legs, move your feet  
Bare soles, let them gallop  
The arresters are coming  
There I beat the mark into the turf  
Be men, catch him  
Take the mark from the turf

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
In the land of Viisu, in the land of Villa  
A poor man got into the land of Vodja  
The oxen are grazing on the meadow  
The runes are grazing in the field  
Men are putting on their shirts  
Strong men became tired  
Mighty oxen got weary  
So did the girls, who are tired  
Who is tired of emptiness  
Growing weary of cancer  
Breaking from eggshells  
Becoming right from silver straw  
Who will buy me from slavery  
Who will save me from hell  
Redeem me from the snowfall  
Peter has a tall tower  
Jaaniste has a low tower  
In Koeru there is a nice chapel  
He will buy me from slavery  
And save me from wreaths  
Redeem me from the snowfall  
From the strike of Viru’s scythe  
From Harju’s sloping hills  
And from behind Järva’s plows  
I am fit for men’s work  
Men’s work, women’s work  
For the clever women’s weaving.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The scarecrow wanted to go to hell  
The swineherd got caught by the back  
The cowherd got caught by the hair  
Where are you going, little scarecrow?  
To hell, to hell, little boy.  
Why are you going to hell, little scarecrow?  
Because I’m going to hell, little boy,  
To torment the village girls  
And torture the farmer’s daughters.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
They were driven to town with grain  
The old gentleman with grain  
The great gentleman with salt  
They drove us to the small borough  
The rye was packed in paper  
We were called to the courthouse  
Why is this rye wet?  
And the grain has gotten wet  
I will start to plead  
Dear court, have patience  
It is not my fault  
Nor is it the fault of the poor parish  
The fault was the landowners’  
Secondly, the fault of the farm boys  
It is the fault of the evil bailiff  
Of the malicious squire  
The grain was exposed to rain  
It was covered with dew  
It was caught in the fog,  
The barn roof was broken  
The barn was still unfinished  
Because the rye had gotten wet  
The bailiff was summoned to court  
And brought before the council  
The upper sheaf was tied on his back  
And fire was set to it  
The squire ran toward the manor  
The farm hand stayed in the field  
One at the other end

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If I were to be myself,  
If I were to become myself,  
Oh, I would know what I would do.  
I would make the gentlemen oxen,  
The ladies would wear bonnets,  
German girls would be servant maids,  
The rowdy ones would be stumblers,  
The proud ones behind cranes,  
I myself would be the caller behind:  
Plow the furrow, pour the story,  
Go straight, old red one!  
My plowman plows, the bend bends,  
My slope plows, the foot slips,  
My bridle plows, the head trembles,  
The dark one did lift the land indeed.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our brave little ram  
Tender little brother Kilter  
You should start counting  
Counting the hours  
For long ago in Harju it was noon  
Our noon undisturbed  
Mid-morning unboiled  
Fish uncut  
The salmon roars into the sea  
The fish sings on the shore  
The streamer beats a splash into the sea  
In our parish there is no man  
Who breaks the salmon from the sea  
Cuts the fish from the shore  
I had, I had the only brother  
The only brother, worth two  
Stood for seven measures of honey  
Carried ten measures on his hat  
He broke the salmon from the sea  
Cut the fish from the shore

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me  
Secondly, my doing  
I would know what I would do  
I would make gentlemen into oxen  
Kilts into cranes  
Ladies into lutes  
I would start forcing it myself.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The stump was split open  
The mausoleum was broken  
The head became a Pärnu tombstone  
The belly became a devil's rawhide shell.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
You there, you other one,
Pig's sieve, bag needle
Sifted with a pig's sieve
Pricked with a bag needle
Pierced through like a fart
You, except for a little belly

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Now it is time to go home,  
Time to gather into the house;  
Already the moon stands before the home,  
Already the sieve stands on the edge,  
The rods against the white fence,  
The windmill turns towards the dawn.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Family boys, tall boys,
Tall boys, fine boys,
They feared the slaves;
That a slave has no saddle,
A servant has no shoes,
A poor child has a little colt.
I hear again, I respond;
What good is a horse to a slave,
A colt to a poor child,
A little shoe to a servant.
A wolf eats the slave's horse,
A bear breaks the poor colt.
He has no time to help.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The farm boy plows the mountain,
Turns out to the middle course,
The servant plowed by the swamp,
The evil children by the garden,
The hired boy by the willow.
Wait, let me say it again:
The servant got big lumps,
The orphan child got a bunch of lumps,
The hired boy got many lumps;
The farm boy got fine lumps.
From a servant came big men,
From a slave came a pursuer,
From a farm boy came a hired boy.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If only I were,
If only it were my doing,
Then I would know what I would do,
I would make the Germans into wooden shoes,
Gentlemen into oxen,
The Junkers into calves,
The Kubjas into cranes,
The farmhands into foundation piles.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
May God grant safe passage  
To get out of the manor house.  
From the clogs' building,  
From the slippers' room.  
I would not look back from the milestone,  
Nor would I turn my head from the dog's load;  
Where the pit of hell is visible,  
Where the devil's house is seen,  
May God grant safe passage,  
To get out of the manor house,  
From the clogs' building,  
From the slippers' room.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Rain, rain, little rain,  
Don't rain on me,  
I have no home dryers,  
No white shirt ironers,  
Rain on that poor German instead.  
He had home dryers,  
White shirt ironers,  
Fine shirt washers.  
If it were up to me,  
To do things differently,  
I would make the gentlemen plow,  
The ladies pick potatoes,  
The masters make manure,  
The mistresses mow hay.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Rain, rain, little rain,  
Don't rain on me,  
I have no home dryers,  
No white shirt ironers,  
Rain on that poor German instead.  
He had home dryers,  
White shirt ironers,  
Fine shirt washers.  
If it were up to me,  
To do things differently,  
I would make the gentlemen plow,  
The ladies pick potatoes,  
The masters make manure,  
The mistresses mow hay.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
What I do after I escape  
I rush to that ghost  
Come ghost, bring the horse  
Twist the yoke onto the cart  
Turn the tilt on the carriage  
The rider is the assistant farmer  
The guard is the old man  
These are the ones in front in hell.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I am a poor free woman.  
A little miserable woman.  
Please, take away the pot.  
Please do not put it down.  
I broke the corner piece,  
It cannot be put down! -  
I have a clay oven!  
I have a wooden road!  
The pot was still heated in the stove! -

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When I must leave the manor,  
The manor, the hell,  
I must leave from the mouth of death,  
Between the jaws of the lion,  
Behind the teeth of the serpent.  
The lions remain roaring,  
The serpents gnashing their teeth.  
I looked back from the grave,  
Turned my head from the cartload of dogs,  
Before the home gate.  
Let the home folk know  
Where the hell's pit lies.  
The devil’s house can be seen,  
The lynx's life is burning,  
The ghosts’ towers stand idle.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Yes, an empty stomach troubles us,
The hungry heart pinches,
The weak underbelly bends.
My dear old woman,
Look for help in the shed,
Bring patience from the room.
If there is no help in the shed,
No patience in the room,
Look for yarn in the chest,
From below, take some thread.
Let a wide belt be woven,
Tied with blue yarn,
Woven with wool yarn,
Stretched with linen yarn,
To satisfy my hunger,
To ease my poor belly.

label: Murelaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When I was still young,  
A knee-high little boy.  
At night I beat the manor's thigh,  
By day I plowed the manor fields.  
Where I stood, the walls were wet,  
Where I cried, the corners were wet.  
There dripped a little pond,  
A small fish lake grew.  
From there, the village herds drank,  
The village herds, big bulls,  
The spirits of the parish enlightened.

label: Murelaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When I was still young,  
A knee-high little boy.  
At night I beat the manor's thigh,  
By day I plowed the manor fields.  
Where I stood, the walls were wet,  
Where I cried, the corners were wet.  
There dripped a little pond,  
A small fish lake grew.  
From there, the village herds drank,  
The village herds, big bulls,  
The spirits of the parish enlightened.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If only it were my existence,
And secondly my doing,
Then I would know what I would do.
I would make gentlemen into bulls,
Kilts into checkerboards,
Headdresses into cranes,
Knights into jesters.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Why were our huts low,  
The thatched roofs meager,  
The reed rows foolish?  
Because our huts were low,  
The thatched roofs meager,  
As the manors were in the village,  
Golden nests in the pastures. –  
May God raise a strong wind,  
A strong wind, a north wind,  
To carry the manors away from the village,  
The golden nests from the pastures.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I am a poor free woman  
A tiny little woman  
A poor little sauna woman  
Surrounded by borders, a housewife  
I walk around a German woman  
Rowan berries in my pocket  
Juniper berries in my hand  
Little apples under my skirt  
I ask, please do not set  
A corner of my little corner  
My pot is out in the yard  
My broth is stirred slow  
The kettle is poured down  
My broth is on the fire  
My porridge is boiling  
I pile ashes in a scoop  
My own beginning is given  
A pole is shown by another  
From a peg a necklace is fashioned.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Wither, wither, little gentleman  
Lady with the golden crown  
Lift this chair by the window edge  
Step onto the window frame  
Look upon the poor parish  
How the parish is tormented  
The district is harassed  
The devil is made a bailiff  
The crow becomes the parish tormentor  
The doer predator on the piece  
The old crow on the spirit’s back  
What do I do before the day  
Or what do I do after the day  
That I throw to the devil  
Fill the devil’s sack  
Come you devil, bring the sack  
Come you devil to blow  
To take the intermediate.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I wouldn’t dare to tell a story  
I wouldn’t speak, I’m unfit  
I am not born among the great  
To fit into the rich man’s house  
I don’t have big boots' sheds  
Or tall corv’s ring sheds.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was diligent, I tended the flock  
I was a servant in Holland  
A shepherd boy from Leverland  
Oland made me a new suit  
A Leverland thick coat  
Lipidesta lapidesta  
From the sheep's skin, from the legs

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Master little master  
Mistress little mistress  
Now my year is over  
My weeks have been seen  
All the necessary things have been done  
Look for new slaves  
Try new servants  
Find new breadwinners.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, our poor beetle boys
When we get ourselves wet
Where do we dry ourselves
In the barn sauna down there
There we dry ourselves
What do we then poke into the stove
The shins of the woodsman, the thighs of the guard
Both the forearms of the farmhand
The thigh bones of the barn father
There we dry ourselves.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Away from Estonia, lords of Iru  
The rich of Kärmu are coming.  
Fingers to bites on the fingers,  
Handfuls of money,  
Shoulderpads of oak.

label: Laulud laulust  ; Murelaulud  ; Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I would sing, I would be laid to rest,  
I would be silent, I would be sworn,  
Otherwise I would sit, I am bored,  
Otherwise I would walk, I am sad.  
To whom I plant boredom,  
To whom I step sadness,  
To whom I complain of sorrowful minds,  
To whom do I humbly lower myself?  
To whom else, like a stone,  
To a stone at the church gate,  
On the stone, paper laid upon it.  
The stone does not understand a maiden’s words,  
The stone does not heed a maiden’s prayer.  
Little stone, little gold,  
Little limestone, little plump one,  
You might have slept in the sea,  
Cried on the seashore,  
Moved in the sea sand.  
Before you came to our land,  
Our land, our island,  
To torture us,  
To weary hand and arm;  
If we had rowans in the yard,  
Maples on our arms,  
Fingers crushed by currants,  
Then we would crack whips,  
We would endure the clubs.

label: Laulud laulust  ; Murelaulud  ; Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I would sing, I would be laid to rest,  
I would be silent, I would be sworn,  
Otherwise I would sit, I am bored,  
Otherwise I would walk, I am sad.  
To whom I plant boredom,  
To whom I step sadness,  
To whom I complain of sorrowful minds,  
To whom do I humbly lower myself?  
To whom else, like a stone,  
To a stone at the church gate,  
On the stone, paper laid upon it.  
The stone does not understand a maiden’s words,  
The stone does not heed a maiden’s prayer.  
Little stone, little gold,  
Little limestone, little plump one,  
You might have slept in the sea,  
Cried on the seashore,  
Moved in the sea sand.  
Before you came to our land,  
Our land, our island,  
To torture us,  
To weary hand and arm;  
If we had rowans in the yard,  
Maples on our arms,  
Fingers crushed by currants,  
Then we would crack whips,  
We would endure the clubs.

label: Laulud laulust  ; Murelaulud  ; Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:

Little stone, little gold,

You might have slept in the sea,

Moved in the sea sand.

If we had rowans in the yard,  
Maples on our arms,

Then we would crack whips,  
We would endure the clubs.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Perhaps the year of heaven should come,  
Let the rain fall from the year of the rod,  
Where will we dry ourselves?  
Let's go to the big leg,  
What will we put in the oven there?  
The lad's shanks, the bailiff's thighs,  
Both forearms of the farmhand,  
The rein-deer's chest pieces.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
A bit of this to praise,  
To understand this in time,  
When the servant is praised,  
His own child is blamed;  
In St. George's and St. John's months  
Is the time to praise the servant.  
Master, little master,  
Mistress, little mistress,  
Look for new servants,  
Try new herders,  
My years are finished,  
My weeks have been seen,  
The full days have been completed.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, I tended the herd
I threw into the Viru sisus,
Into the Livonian sheepfold.
Ollandeer made a new coat,
A wide coat of Livland
The coat was made of dog fur,
A coat of fish skin;
Of scraps, of patches,
Of old sheep skins,
Of mottled goat legs.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, come inside, little breeze,  
There are wind-makers indoors  
Inside are wall-trimmers  
Those who place the logs.  
Drive the winds towards the barn  
Turn the winds towards the chimney  
Take the key from under the eaves  
Or a twig from atop the slice,  
The bread bag is on the shelf,  
The cake bag is on the chimney.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Look where our place appears,  
That place in Siberia,  
The devil's home gleams,  
Hell's towers glare,  
From there a naked wretch  
An agan barks at it.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The hawk flies along the road,  
Eyes not on the field  
Like the dull eyes  
Of a spring goat.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Bite, bite dogs  
Do not dog together!  
But I will throw into the pear water  
Lift from the threshing water drain.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were my being,  
And secondly my doing,  
I would make the planks into sawteeth:  
The tubs I would make into cranes,  
The helper’s bench into a foundation,  
And the rake into a kilo of hay,  
The gentlemen I would put into ox-teeth:  
The lords I would put into hikes,  
The young men into chatter-teeth,  
The ladies into whip-teeth,  
Then I would go to the village to plow:  
A furrow for the widow in the meadow,  
Sawteeth in the threshing barn,  
Wide frosted ones in front,  
A golden yoke on the head,  
Hail as iron inside,  
Coiled braids of gold,  
And muddy ladies inside.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The lord had a mouse-gray one,  
The king had a golden-ear,  
My brother had a blood-red one.  
They wanted to breed in the summer,  
To comb on the copper comb,  
To groom with the golden comb.  
If they could not breed in the summer,  
If the copper comb was not used for combing,  
If the golden comb was not used for grooming,  
Then I was beaten with a whip!  
Pulled by a tarred rope!  
I, a poor man, began to stammer,  
A bluestocking man to slip away,  
A red-pants man to weave.  
Across the field, a narrow field,  
Sometimes along the road,  
Over Siim’s dung field,  
Over Kaie’s barley meadow,  
Over Andres’s ripe grain,  
The hayfork helped me out,  
The double-pole pushed from behind,  
The gateposts resisted me.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I do not have to go!  
I do not have to disappear, dear!  
Do not wander aimlessly!  
Do not leave the others behind like a grouse.  
Do not miss the sun,  
Do not miss the people's step,  
From better places,  
Here I leave this place,  
Leave this berry patch,  
I give the spots to others,  
I push the house to another,  
Let better ones come to the place,  
More honest ones in the stead.  
Let it be as I have been,  
Let them do as I have done.  
I ran to bring the fire to visit,  
I rushed to the water well.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I bring respect to my father,  
I bring respect to my mother,  
I bring respect to my brothers,  
I bring respect to my sister-in-law!  
Still, the brothers dig holes,  
The sisters-in-law nod.  
"Look, here comes the guest to eat:  
The guest to eat, the guest to drink,  
The one who sets the bowl in place.  
He ate from the bowl,  
He drank from the drinking vessel."  
What food did he get from that bowl,  
The bone was beneath, the skin was on top,  
A piece of lean meat.  
What drink did he get from those beans,  
The froth was beneath, the scum was on top,  
A drop of dark beer,  
A clear whirl to the middle of the glass.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Tui-tui brothers, tai-tai brothers,  
We drink at the tavern, haymakers.  
Where do we get money?  
We measure from the manor's granary,  
A scythe in front and a shovel in hand.  
Will we then become happy!  
Where do we go if we don’t get it,  
I don't want the earth below,  
Heaven high and also cold,  
Hell low and warm.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was born from pride,  
I speak of wealth,  
I await the proud,  
I lose the strong,  
I hang the rich on a hook.  
I break long poles into firewood,  
Large knitting needles into sticks,  
Thick tree roots into kindling.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Ulila proud, Ruila rich,  
Riisipere very rich,  
Kerna poor, Väätsa poor.  
Kelp sod eaten, tangud sold,  
Lentils going towards the town,  
Beans trailing behind,  
Buckwheat dancing at the back.  
Weights taken under the barn,  
Carding wool around the neck with a bag.  
Milk went in small drops,  
Butter taken with difficulty.  
Haystack went at dusk,  
Straw went in the evening.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a slave, I tended the herd,  
I was a servant of the nobleman,  
A sheep of Livonia,  
The nobleman built a new dwelling,  
Livonia a wide coat.  
Then I got to sleep in the forest,  
Under the sky I got to pasture,  
Under the spruce I got to bow,  
Down by the alder grove to slide.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Master, little master!  
Mistress, little mistress,  
Do justice to the man,  
Pay for the spirit’s labor,  
Give clothes to the honest,  
Pay wages better.  
If you don’t pay under the sky,  
You don’t pay wages on the threshing floor.  
In the village you pay to the sky,  
You pay before Mary,  
You go to the beggar’s tree,  
With cloth wide on your shoulder.  
“Take the clothes, little slave.”  
I immediately answer back:  
“I do not want to be a little servant,  
I do not want to be a little old woman.  
If you haven’t paid under the sky,  
Wages were not paid on the threshing floor,  
Now the little slave has:  
No longer need for wages in heaven.”

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Töölaulud
translation:
The house is awaited to be warmed up,  
The gate is awaited to be opened:  
Is the fire blower coming,  
Or the water carrier rolling in,  
The spoon rinsing,  
The trays shining,  
The churns greasing,  
The milk jug washing,  
The beer vessel decorating,  
The water bucket rolling,  
The pots standing up.  
I put the jugs on the shelf,  
The vessels on the kitchen roof,  
Buckets by the edge of the well,  
The churns white on the clothesline.  
When the fire carrier came to the village,  
To the old gentleman’s funeral,  
He thought the moon shining,  
The snow-white path spreading,  
The rainbow shimmering,  
The evening star glowing.  
The bucket tables flickered,  
The vessel handles glowed,  
The churn bottoms shone,  
The pot rods shimmered,  
The bath edges lightened,  
Here the daughter has done the work,  
The foster child has taken pains.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Töölaulud
translation:
The house is awaited to be warmed up,  
The gate is awaited to be opened:  
Is the fire blower coming,  
Or the water carrier rolling in,  
The spoon rinsing,  
The trays shining,  
The churns greasing,  
The milk jug washing,  
The beer vessel decorating,  
The water bucket rolling,  
The pots standing up.  
I put the jugs on the shelf,  
The vessels on the kitchen roof,  
Buckets by the edge of the well,  
The churns white on the clothesline.  
When the fire carrier came to the village,  
To the old gentleman’s funeral,  
He thought the moon shining,  
The snow-white path spreading,  
The rainbow shimmering,  
The evening star glowing.  
The bucket tables flickered,  
The vessel handles glowed,  
The churn bottoms shone,  
The pot rods shimmered,  
The bath edges lightened,  
Here the daughter has done the work,  
The foster child has taken pains.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Thank you to the family grandmother,  
Thank you to the family aunt,  
The kind little children of the family,  
The slender maids of the family:  
Do not refuse the poor,  
Do not deny the plea,  
Do not cut off a piece of bread,  
Do not refuse to carry the soup bowl,  
Do not deny a night's lodging.  
They helped me onto the stove,  
Laid me down on the bed frame.  
Thank you for the sick leg,  
The crooked heels,  
God helps the sick leg,  
I myself lift the healthy leg.  
The sick leg walks by the garden,  
The healthy leg walks by the road,  
I feel the leg faltering,  
The weak leg stumbling,  
I must step more carefully,  
Keep my head upright,  
So I do not fall on the uneven path.  
One cannot live unhappily,  
Without health to work.  
Those who have luck live,  
Those who have health do the work.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Worn, worn shoes,
Worn, worn laces,
I have a home with three pairs of shoes.
Tomorrow I will donate new ones,
Then I will make others;
Uncle's horsehide,
Aunt's doe hide,
The farmer's oxhide,
The son-in-law's calfhide,
Father's calfskin,
Old Käspre's bark hide.
Then I had a total of six pairs of shoes,
Altogether nine pairs of shoes.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
There was a little plant girl,  
She wants to get married,  
What shall we give with her?  
A sheep with two lambs,  
Do you agree with that?  
Are you satisfied with us?  
"No, I do not agree with that,  
I am not satisfied with you."  
What shall we give again?  
A pig with seven piglets,  
Do you agree with that?  
Are you satisfied with us?  
"No, I do not agree with that,  
I am not satisfied with you.  
I do not agree even with a cow,  
I will go and make my own way.  
My mind thinks about a horse,  
Always longing for a foal."

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
A German rides along the road  
Eyes burning on the field  
Just like the eyes of a mad wolf  
Eyes over the meadow and a patch of grass  
Eyes of a fiery goat  
Eyes of an autumn boar  
Eyes of a winter hare.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud laulust  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
I come to the barn, fox  
To the barley barn, squirrel  
To the oat barn, chicken  
To the rye barn, a little crow  
To the wheat barn, a little maiden  
The barn is not beaten with me  
The stake is not hit without the poor  
The spirits are drunk without me  
The breads are eaten secretly  
The barn keeper was a little boy  
He piles thick bundles  
Commands coughs to strike  
And to clap on top  
I will take the stake from the sleeve  
The rake from the wall of the thigh  
I must not sing the truth  
Nor announce the truth  
The barn keeper hears when I sing  
The ox wakes from the bed  
The lady from her pillows  
The girls from the linen  
They throw crackers from the stove  
Broken cups from the table  
Whose fault will be blamed  
Whose damage will be poured out  
My fault will be blamed  
My chicken’s damage will be poured.  
Oh you birds of the barn,  
Lady with a golden crown  
Young birds at the apple blossom  
Rise up onto the chair  
Step onto the windowsill  
How you are put to trouble  
How the little ones are tormented  
The smaller ones are twisted  
The stronger ones are pulled  
If it were my being  
To be my doing  
I would know what I would do  
I would make the birds alert  
The oxen into cranes  
The girls into bald heads  
The young men into talkers  
When we came from the road  
And we were wet  
Where do we dry ourselves?  
By the big barn door  
Near the barn oven  
The ox’s bones, the girls’ sleeves  
The barn keeper’s sternum  
Let us help both fields  
Our ox, a little gold  
The girl, a speckled little bird  
Little helper, a little meadow  
Let the children go to lunch  
Sisters to afternoon  
If not, I will swear  
The swine on your pigs  
The eagles on the chickens  
The timid on the eggs  
Disease on the women and children  
Others eat others’ turns  
Homefolk a third time  
Viisu folk a fifth time  
Koordi folk a sixth time  
Seidlas seven times  
Kaalepis eight times  
Öötlas nine times  
Kõisi folk ten times  
We still without a meal  
Larks without lunch  
Swallows without a snack  
Feathers without size  
See, we come to hunger  
Servants, young birds at the gate  
Empty sacks on the carriage  
I saw hunger beaten  
In front of the thigh on the spindle  
Chasing to the edge of the open field  
Behind the stack to the rowan  
Behind the room to the corridor  
Sister, little sister,  
Young maiden  
Sister Ella, I told first  
I forbade the cuckoo gold  
Do not run to the drunkard  
Do not to the lazy fellow at the table  
Go to the straw walker’s place

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
I did bitter work,  
Grumbling to ease my mind  
In the chambers of the stingy rogue.  
He does not feel bitter thanks,  
The wicked does not give help,  
The evil [man] two kopecks.  
Who says  
The scolding mouth from red stone?  
I take from between the poor child  
From the end of the poor slave child.  
He knows how to walk on fire,  
To rant without a chandelier,  
To write without a candle:  
Without a chandelier, he milks the cow,  
Without a candle, he strains the milk  
Night pain, moon pain,  
Playfellow’s head pain.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:

label: Laulud abielust  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I bow to my father,  
I bow to my mother,  
I bow and turn to my brothers,  
Yet the sisters grumble,  
The brothers' wives nod:  
No one is born to go in autumn,  
No one is allowed to go in spring:  
The rivers are deep in autumn,  
The waves were wide in spring,  
The apples fell into the stream,  
The mare stayed between the tufts of grass,  
The stallion ran into the waves.  
I bow and turn to my brothers,  
Yet the sisters grumble,  
The brothers' wives nod,  
Look where the big eater comes from,  
The big eater, the big drinker,  
The one who puts much in place,  
He eats up the plowshare while eating,  
He drinks the barrel while drinking.  
I think, I answer again:  
What can be eaten from the plowshare,  
What can be drunk from the barrel,  
The bones are underneath, the skin over it,  
A little chunk of lean meat;  
Yeast underneath, foam over it,  
A drop of clear beer.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
There were a few good days  
There was understanding in those times  
When the servant is praised  
And one’s own child is criticized  
In St. George’s month and in St. John’s month  
In May and in the month of juice  
Then the servant is praised  
And one’s own child is criticized

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our sturdy little ram  
Quirky, colorful little bird  
Let the children go to lunch  
Sisters to the evening hall  
Children ask for lunch  
Sisters wait for the evening  
The moon heats in front of the house  
The sieve edges towards the south  
If you don’t let them, don’t ask either  
I curse you to be a beggar  
Let the wolf come among your flock  
Let the bear come among your herd  
Let your pigs die  
Let the chickens lay in the well  
Let the sheep sleep in the barn.  
Let the oppressors die  
Let the stick-bearers disappear.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Word, word, little brother  
I got to keep the word  
Words like the Germans in Saaremaa  
Lords were in Hiiumaa  
True gentlemen in Pärnumaa,  
When the Germans came from Saaremaa  
Lords came from Hiiumaa  
True gentlemen from Pärnumaa  
Then yokes were put on the plow  
Harnesses were let loose from the plow  
The dark one was pulled up from the plow  
The reins were twisted on the head  
The yoke plows, the back is hunched  
The dark one pulls, the back creaks  
The reins pull, the head trembles  
The harness breaks the horns wide.  
Then came the bull’s biting  
Our black mooing  
Village's noisy screaming  
Mouths tied shut while eating  
Jawbones locked  
Logs were placed on the shoulders  
To step before the plow.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When we come home from work  
And we are wet  
Where do we dry ourselves  
Let's go to the manor's threshing floor  
What do we put in the oven there  
The son-in-law's cuffs, the smith's legs  
We help the bees both  
The master's thighs immediately dry.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The Hellish Mistress  
Says the work should feel good  
Oh come, let's try the work for an hour or two  
Should we put it to sleep on a pillow?  
But the work is measured in pieces  
A stick is thrown over the neck.  
The Hellish Mistress  
Pays the servant well  
Rewards the poor child's labor  
If payment is not given now  
Surely then you will pay in heaven  
Then you walk with your elbow in hand  
Marching to the gallows' shoulder  
To the clothes warehouse in the cellar  
To take wages, little servant  
Labor, poor little child  
I understand immediately, I respond  
I do not want to go to heaven  
If not paid now  
Where I tear ribs apart  
Break neck bones too  
Your big whip  
And the iron rod's bucket.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
We drink at the tavern, the errand boys
One on another’s money
If your money doesn’t pay
Then my money will pay
If my money doesn’t pay
Then the lady will pay at the cupboard
If the lady doesn’t pay at the cupboard
Then the master will pay at the barn
Our master has big barns
Big barns with new doors
New doors with tight locks
No keys can open them
I brought a skeleton key from the city
From the borough I brought a green iron
From the market I brought strong iron
I opened the tight locks
The bailiff ran to the master’s place
Our barns have already been robbed
The bailiff to the master Joan’s room
Our barns have already been robbed
To master Joan upstairs in the room
Our barns have already been robbed
The master came down with rope in hand
Joan’s room has leg restraints
They came to catch me
To capture me
I got down to the grey one
A gray ear, a little ear
Near the ear a dark little blueberry
I rode down to Riga city
There were three maidens in a row
In front was a maiden with a pearl necklace
Behind a maiden with a tin necklace
In the middle in a row a maiden with a paper necklace
I took the pearl necklace from the front
Did not take the tin necklace from behind
Nor the paper necklace from the middle
I took from the middle a poor child
In turn a slave in succession
Who knows how to weave cloth
To twist tin thread
To embroider with silver sewing
Those walk above with nika-naka
On top there is salla-salla
Floorboards trilla-tralla.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Töölaulud
translation:
The master was angry with me  
The mistress was loud with me  
I am not angry with anyone  
Nor with anyone excessively  
I drove the bulls well  
Gently yoked the mares  
I plowed the land shallowly  
I did not damage the grass roots  
Nor the roots of the thistles  
I scattered the willow without a family  
The willow had many without a family  
The thistle had many children  
The alder had many mourners  
The spruce had many listeners  
The birch had many criers.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Töölaulud
translation:

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Stop boasting for now.  
Stop arguing for now.  
This is not the right time to eat.  
This is not the right time to drink.  
The brushes eat from the cradle.  
The runes eat from the cradle straps.  
Men undress to the chest.  
Women squeal with their collarbones.  
Maids have their spitting nests.  
The women went towards the manor,  
Egg bunches in hand,  
A wheel of fat under the apron,  
They went to pray to the master,  
They went to pray to the lady.  
Oh, you doctor gentleman,  
You golden crown lady,  
Let us rise onto the chair,  
Get up on the hall platform,  
Step onto the window frame,  
Look upon the poor parish,  
How the parish is tormented,  
How the smaller ones are tortured,  
How the bigger ones are killed.  
The hands of the bigger ones die —  
The hands of the weaker ones grow tired,  
The longer fields wither —

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I am the sweaty servant of the lord  
Madam's ancient servant.  
Let the lord come outside as well  
I will immediately tell the lord too  
I complain to the dear lady.  
The lord brings beautiful beads  
The lady brings a rosy kerchief,  
Then I go through the villages  
All the village dogs bark  
And old women peer.  
I make the dogs bark:  
"Wait, wait, little storm,  
"Wait, little clever one,  
You will get a share from the wolf,  
Howl like a wolf,  
The forest beast is desperate,  
The boy will get a black bride.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Young girls of Kuldanukku  
Little maidens  
Rise up from the chair  
Stand up from the table  
Step onto the window frame  
Give way to the poor people  
When these people are troubled  
The little ones are tormented  
Those like fingers are scolded.  
Why do the maidens not tire?  
Strong horses grow weary  
Strong oxen grow weary.  
Why do the maidens not tire?

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Toi toi toi in progress  
Everything is fine  
The Tiumies spirit is shining  
The bag is ready shortly  
The dog trip is in the bag  
The cat trip is in the box  
The female donkey trip is in the mare.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Don’t go away, I am hurrying  
I give my place to others  
I hurry to another.  
Then tomorrow I will come to settle in  
Are there better places?  
There were no better places  
Only more famous spots.  
Spoons burnt in the stove  
Ladle handles bent.  
The stove asked for more fuel  
The room was swept hastily  
The bucket wants handling  
The fish cupboard is for the wiper  
The sheaf cries with the flail  
The jug was where I lifted it  
The bench was where I held it  
The table was where I stacked it.  
There were no better places  
More decent and honest  
Only more famous spots.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The devil dug in the spruce forest  
The demon thumped in the bush  
But they made a cartwheel shed  
With which to take the German hell  
Peasants up to heaven.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I would live for myself,  
I would work for others to know,  
I would walk with two hands;  
I would not let the village live,  
The village would not begin to settle;  
Oh, you jealous village  
Your elders are great witches,  
Let the little one live—  
The tiny one work,  
The small one walk humbly.  
Medicines were brought from Sweden,  
Salt was brought from Finland,  
p. 84 Put into our animal pen,  
The salt began to melt,  
The fresh ones began to sink,—  
Then the herd began to disappear,  
Pigs began to die,  
Cows began to lie down drowsily,  
The oxen began to bellow.  
Then I was left completely poor,  
Completely poor and alone,  
Then I became like a saint,  
A carrier of two sticks.  
Oh God Almighty,  
If only I were to the blacksmith's forge,  
The one who sticks under the pile,—  
May I not be the witch's eye needle,  
May I not be the potion of secret jealousy.—

Then I was left completely poor,  
Completely poor and alone,  
Then I became like a saint,  
A carrier of two sticks.  
Oh God Almighty,  
If only I were to the blacksmith's forge,  
The one who sticks under the pile,—  
May I not be the witch's eye needle,  
May I not be the potion of secret jealousy.—

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our girls, maidens,
Let us take counsel,
Let us go live with Uila,
Uila has a fine life,
Oh, those mothers-in-law of Uila,
Uila's mothers-in-law of hospitality,
Gentle cleaners,
Mighty washerwomen;
The oxen eat at the table,
The horses eat in the meadow,
The swine do not eat at the tables,
There is no rush with the horses,
Tapa parish is the prison parish,
That parish is the Siberian parish;
The oxen eat in the barn,
The horses eat in the rack,
Then the manor belongs to the village,
The gold nest to the cooperage.
If only I had my existence,
And it were for me to decide,
I would make the Germans ploughers,
I would make the young men talkers,
I would make the soldiers skippers,
The hunchbacks into cranes,
Then I would call the fine men together,
We would make fire from tinderbox,
From sweet berry trees,
From beautiful apple trees.
Then we would pile it up to burn,
To burn away the miserable,
To watch over the young master's house,
Then we would have eternal life,
Then the swine do not eat at the tables,
Nor is there rush with the horses,
The oxen eat at the table,
The horses eat in the meadow,
Fine men in the tin pen,
Spirits in the copper chamber.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
My year is running out,
My weeks are in my hand -
In the pocket is my little day.
I tasted summer’s mouth,
Under the water from the ambade (a type of plant or unclear word) -
Between bear’s paws,
Near the lion’s jaws.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Always without me  
How many times have you been sad  
That you never wore that shirt  
Which is for family celebrations  
For the host's daughter  
I wear plain sackcloth  
From an old fur coat  
I work until I am tired  
Mowing hay in the meadow  
Cutting rye in the field  
Gathering oats for a long time  
Barley in the grain bin  
Still I lack many things  
Salt for the eyes, bread for the mouth.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Looking for work as a servant  
Trying to catch a chicken  
If not to haul other mud  
To gather manure together  
To put the frame in place

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Töölaulud
translation:
I plowed very well  
Quickly yoked the oxen  
I set the oxen to plow  
Horses behind for dragging  
Carts behind for wheeling  
Cows behind for pulling  
I myself for driving  
Plow turned from earth to ditch  
The old red ox  
Turned the plow blade sharply  
The ditch stepped with a foot, struck a step  
The dark one pulled, the furrows trembled  
I plow very well through the fields  
The lady of the fields  
I did not ruin the roots of the grass  
I trampled even the horseman's heart  
The son was left without a father  
The thistle had large buds  
The son has a place without a father  
The horseman has wise children

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Töölaulud
translation:
I plowed very well  
Quickly yoked the oxen  
I set the oxen to plow  
Horses behind for dragging  
Carts behind for wheeling  
Cows behind for pulling  
I myself for driving  
Plow turned from earth to ditch  
The old red ox  
Turned the plow blade sharply  
The ditch stepped with a foot, struck a step  
The dark one pulled, the furrows trembled  
I plow very well through the fields  
The lady of the fields  
I did not ruin the roots of the grass  
I trampled even the horseman's heart  
The son was left without a father  
The thistle had large buds  
The son has a place without a father  
The horseman has wise children

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
A rich man dies in a swamp pit  
A money man in piles of money  
A farmer in a heap of straw  
A townsman on a tavern bench  
A manor lord in some place  
I die in a sheltered house  
A shelter by the stove's fire.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh we poor manor ghosts  
Poor journeymen second  
Foremen third  
Assistant spirits fourth  
Drunkards fifth  
Were driven to howl at night  
To march along the mud  
To walk across the desert  
Night pain, moon pain  
God’s fire pain  
Night pain we took to the threshold  
Moon pain we took to the piles  
Golden rakes in hand  
Sharp harrow handles

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The daughter is crying in the barn-time!  
It's not for nothing that the daughter cries.  
The daughter is having a quarrel  
And weighing the beams.  
The fire in the rooms does not go out,  
Nor the spark from the bedposts,  
Nor the woman from the young clothes,  
Nor the horse from the gray harness,  
Nor the spark from the bedposts.  

The barn is pregnant with gray,  
We have many stables in our yard,  
In every stable seven horses,  
They neigh and make faces.  
They all wanted payments,  
With joyous installments,  
On golden combs, with claws,  
With tender help and deeds.  
I could not pay them,  
Could not make joyous installments,  
Could not claw the golden comb,  
Could not tenderly do these deeds.  

Then they beat me with a whip,  
Pulled me with a tarred rope,  
But I, poor, got away,  
Dodging along the field.  
Then they caught me with a rope  
And chased me relentlessly,  
Look, the rope was a thousand fathoms long,  
And there were a hundred knots in the rope.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Laulud kodust ja lapsepõlvest  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The daughter is crying in the barn-time!  
It's not for nothing that the daughter cries.  
The daughter is having a quarrel  
And weighing the beams.  
The fire in the rooms does not go out,  
Nor the spark from the bedposts,  
Nor the woman from the young clothes,  
Nor the horse from the gray harness,  
Nor the spark from the bedposts.  

The barn is pregnant with gray,  
We have many stables in our yard,  
In every stable seven horses,  
They neigh and make faces.  
They all wanted payments,  
With joyous installments,  
On golden combs, with claws,  
With tender help and deeds.  
I could not pay them,
