label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Maiden daughter of the family  
Sleeps without a belt in bed  
Without pigs in my bed  
The old woman can bake bread  
In the oven, in the fireplace,  
She puts duck roast on top  
Eat now, dear, drink now, dear  
Then the old woman, dear Ingamaa  
Into the beautiful bed  
Into the right pillows  
Into the wider linens  
Boy, come to the brave one  
Now put your neck to the dear one.  
Oh that poor servant’s child  
She comes plowing to the village.  
Furrow in the wheel track for the young man  
Brisk young lads in front  
Sleds, sledges in the lap  
She herself is dirty to the top  
Chicken muddy up to the neck  
Head wet to the top  
But she was whipped with a twig  
Out of the oven’s ashes  
On top, she was hit with a rowan stick  
Oh that poor servant’s child.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The village invited me to eat,
To eat in the village, to drink in the village;
To live among the villagers.
I ate the bull while eating,
I drank from the barrel while drinking,
I used up a pile of hay. -
What did I get to eat from that bull?
Bone is underneath, skin is on top,
A little chunk of lean meat!
What can one drink from that barrel?
The yeast is at the bottom, foam is on top,
A little bit of dark beer.
What is to be used from the pile?
The stakes are underneath, beams on top,
The pile pole in the middle.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, we poor spirits of the parish,  
Makers of family day!  
Oh, our hands wear out,  
Oh, our feet grow tired:  
From walking the paths of the village,  
From trudging the spirit's way,  
From carrying the order bag!  
The village path is pointed,  
The spirit's path is arduous.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I saw the enemy herding,
Weaving along the field,
Along the field, across the field,
Along the forest path by the gardens,
Along the back of the old pig.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Now it is time to go home,  
Time urges us to walk:  
The moon is already above home,  
The smoke is already standing by the chimney,  
The rods are already leaning against the door,  
The rooster's crow should already be heard,  
The barrel is already wanted empty,  
The beer vat is cheerfully foaming,  
The butter churn shows the bottom of the house.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I am a poor freedman's wife  
A miserable sauna woman  
They pushed me away  
They trampled me down  
They destroyed my crops  
They spilled my milk  
They beat me themselves.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
"Bring fire and start boiling!"
The flame began to burn,
Then the pain began to come.
The mistress, the hellion,
Boils the shoes, dresses the straps,
Feeds the slave with shoes.
So the slave thought: chunks of ossi!
And the family thought: chunks of beatings!
These were the mistress's shoe trials.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Thus sing the boys of Lalli,  
The boys of Uuemõisa Oti:  
"Who is waiting for us at home?  
A cat is waiting for us at home,  
A dog is waiting for us at home.  
The cat wants to cover the bottom of the box,  
The dog, however, is waiting for scraps."

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
For I am truly sorry  
That my bride walks with a wreath,  
That she walks among others,  
A spirit with the poor people.  
And then she goes out by the manor,  
Often even plowing the furrows  
And digging the ditches.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I have certainly seen days of hunger,  
Some nights I was sleepless,  
Some days without lunch,  
At night I could not sleep,  
Could not lay my head on a pillow.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Our crooked little gold one  
Quirky spotted little bird  
Let the children go to lunch  
Little sisters to the evening  
The children ask for lunch  
The sisters wait for the evening,  
If you don't let them, don't ask,  
May it, may it be damned  
May the coercers die  
May the stick-bearers disappear  
Today one alone, tomorrow two,  
The day after tomorrow five or six  
In a week seven or eight  
Now the devil is placed crooked  
The eyes turn askew.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When we come back from work
And we are wet
Where do we dry ourselves?
Let's go to Rae's barn
There the spot is long ready
Sheets spread wide in the barn
Pillows beaten soft
There was straw up to the knees
Tangle up to the shin of the boot
Scab to the heel of the shoe
Lice to the ointment on the leg
Scabs walked with stems in their mouths
The louse went to the corner, the young one stayed on the back.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
If it were up to me  
And my doing as well  
I would surely know what to do  
I would put the gentlemen on the oxen  
The ladies behind the plows  
Kilts behind the earlocks  
German children in the stables  
The gentlemen on the sledge runners  
The gentleman ran, his head trembled  
The pants stayed with Saint George  
The buttons to Heikki in the corner

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
It’s raining by the rod’s length  
Fire and tar in the sky’s length  
A slave still must be  
Properly going soon  
Who will save us from shadows  
Heal from daily troubles  
Redeem from the snowfall  
Take us from the rain berries!  
The church in Juani was a little boy  
Peter a tall little tower  
He will save us from shadows  
Heal from daily troubles  
Redeem from the snowfall  
Take us from the rain berries.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Where the devil's house is  
Where the cursed place is  
Hell's windows are burning  
Ghosts and spirits are stirring  
Where the children play  
Where the daughters tylt  
Where the young women cry  
Better to dwell in hell  
Than in Tapa manor  
The Oxen do not eat  
The Runes fade away  
Men dress on their chests  
Girls go to work  
The Oxen groan with horns  
Men suffer in confession  
The Runes circle the chest  
Boys are on knees  
Women do housework  
Girls keep the sacred nests  
Unmarried women guard their feet!  
I praise the Brown Maiden  
Brown Maiden, Brown Lady  
Lowly lands  
Open lands  
The Brown estate goes its way:  
The smith like the moon inside  
The nobleman like God beside  
The estate like a copper plate in between. -  
But the Tapa estate goes its way:  
The smith like the devil inside  
The nobleman like Judas beside  
The estate like a prison cell in between!

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Not all home dogs  
Fathers work outdoors  
Brothers’ collars are iron chains  
What kind of hooliganism is done at the barn  
At the barn poles is shown  
At the manor thoughts are pondered  
Something is searched for  
Something is demanded  
Dogs are at home  
They smell the coming  
The scent of a spark reaches even here  
Swiftness brings it to the shoes.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Evening comes late,  
Lies low beside the day.  
Evening brings happiness,  
Dusk with a brother,  
Darkness with a shepherd,  
With whom shall I stay, little hen?  
Whose wing shall I be, little titmouse?  
On whose shoulder shall I rest, little apple?  

Evening brings straw to the barn,  
Dusk leads to sleep,  
Twilight wakes us up,  
Dawn separates us,  
Some to the manor, some home.  

I wander toward the manor,  
Swinging on the gate,  
Swaying by the smith's shop.  

I went roaming home,  
Calling out vainly,  
Heard a maiden crying,  
Calling out to the bachelor.  

I asked the maiden,  
Why do you cry, dear maiden?  
The maiden heard and answered:    
Why shouldn’t I cry, daughter of the youth,  
Wandering at the manor,  
Swinging on the gate,  
Swaying by the smith's shop,  
Amid the spirits.  

I fear being deceived by small shirts,  
Buried under coats,  
Covered by worn fur coats,  
Hidden by a white crane’s tail.  

I heard and answered back,  
I said in return:  
Not me, not me, dear maiden,  
Not me deceived by small shirts,  
Buried under coats,  
Covered by worn fur coats,  
Hidden by a white crane’s tail;  
I am hidden by a small sand,  
And buried by black earth,  
Covered by a worn church,  
Hidden by a white little tower.

Hidden by a white little tower.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Already past midnight  
The other half not yet gone  
Already roosters crowed once  
Hens clucked eight times  
Birds sang melodies.  
Now is the German eating time  
The young men’s drinking time  
The men’s sleeping time  
Our poor ones’ walking time.  
Our poor manor spirits  
Late masters’ spirits  
Spirits of the old lord  
Created to wander the night  
In the moonlight to wander  
To cut hay by night light  
To make haystacks in moonlight  
To carry large stacks to the swamp  
To the meadow thin cows  
To the field bright sheep  
To me the joyful little hills.  
Juhan has a strong cow  
Muaria a hefty heifer  
They carry large stacks from the swamp  
From the meadow thin cows  
From the field joyful little hills  
From the village high stacks.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Listen, dear gentleman  
Silver lace young lady  
Golden dolls young gentlemen  
Rise up from your chair  
Step onto the window frame  
Look at the poor parish  
How the parish is tormented  
The little ones are tortured  
The weaker ones are wasted  
Those the size of a finger are being killed.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
A slave was sought for work  
A servant was forced,  
When there was no other mud to carry  
To gather the dung.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud abielust
translation:
Hour, hour, little rain,  
Let it fall gently,  
Maybe it will turn into sleet,  
Pour rain from the rod,  
A serf must be,  
A serf must work,  
A serf is thought of with care,  
A wife of the man is pleased,  
A serf is praised,  
The man's wife is blamed,  
The tomcat wants, it will kill me,  
The tomcat cares, it lashes out,  
The tomcat thinks, it strikes!

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Laulud abielust
translation:

Let it fall gently,  
Maybe it will turn into sleet,  
Pour rain from the rod,  
A serf must be,  
A serf must work,  
A serf is thought of with care,  
A wife of the man is pleased,  
A serf is praised,  
The man's wife is blamed,  
The tomcat wants, it will kill me,  
The tomcat cares, it lashes out,  
The tomcat thinks, it strikes!

The tomcat thinks, it strikes!

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Master, little master,  
The little room was the little house.  
Anna the Estonian slave to eat,  
The slave feeds the bull to eat.  
The bull carries the straw to the field,  
The field swells to the edge of the swamp,  
The barn swells to the corner of the yard,  
And the purse swells to a pile of money.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Goodness gracious, brothers,
The master was harsh to me,
The mistress was loud to me.
I tended the oxen
Fit for their yokes.
I tended the oxen well,
I yoked the mares gently.
I plowed shallowly in the field,
I did not damage the grain roots.
The stallion did not stop his heart,
He did not touch the willow at home,
The stallion had large roots,
The mare had wise offspring,
The thorn bush had a crazy wife,
The willow had a better family.
The wolf ate my big oxen,
The wolf ate my big mares,
The bear ate the bridled horses.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Goodness gracious, brothers,
The master was harsh to me,
The mistress was loud to me.
I tended the oxen
Fit for their yokes.
I tended the oxen well,
I yoked the mares gently.
I plowed shallowly in the field,
I did not damage the grain roots.
The stallion did not stop his heart,
He did not touch the willow at home,
The stallion had large roots,
The mare had wise offspring,
The thorn bush had a crazy wife,
The willow had a better family.
The wolf ate my big oxen,
The wolf ate my big mares,
The bear ate the bridled horses.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, oh, Kaie Otsa,  
Otherwise a plump mistress,  
A kind caretaker of children.  
But your manners have long been heard,  
And your ways have been exposed.  
How do you feed your servant?  
You take the milk pail for yourself,  
And give the servant a water dipper.  
You pour from the milk pail,  
The servant pours from the water dipper,  
Dripping into the tar barrel.  
Oh, oh, Kaie Otsa!  
Otherwise a plump mistress,  
A kind caretaker of children.  
But your manners have long been heard,  
And your ways have been exposed.  
How do you pay your servant?  
You knit socks with a large needle.  
Gloves with a fuss,  
Ribbons in bad temper,  
Surely in the end you get blamed,  
In court days contested.  
Then you dance in the street of heaven  
Holding a long whip,  
Wearing broad cloth.  
There you call the servants:  
Come, servants, take your pay!  
The servant hears and replies:  
"If you didn’t pay me a wage,  
There’s no need for pay in heaven.  
If I did your work,  
Did the work, suffered hardships,  
Broken my neck bones,  
Dented my breast bones,  
Ribs were like handcuffs,  
Bladders between thumbs.  
If I asked for shoes,  
Barefoot I asked,  
I bent down to you deep!  
With your big buckets,  
Wide water buckets,  
Long milk pail."

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, that young gentleman of Ärtu,  
Miss Õbeportu,  
The young Lord of Kuldnukk,  
The German with the silk finger.  
Let us rise from the chair  
Let us rise from the table  
Let us step onto the window sill.  
Let us shout over the poor parish,  
Over the whole parish!  
How the parish is tormented,  
The parish is harassed!

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Tiny little birds,  
Let's go blossom, little flowers.  
At the cattle gathering, the hoofs.  
We are justified in blooming.  
At the cattle gathering, there is shouting!  
At the bloom, the beer is red.  
At the cattle gathering, the kvass is sour.  
Where are we going, the six of us,  
Over the field - five of us,  
Over the meadow, four of us.  
In front is a long boundary forest,  
Behind a wide board forest,  
In between, a maple tree forest,  
The sky is wide and open,  
The night is long and dark. –  
Young girls, young men:  
Let's go over the five fields,  
There sits a beautiful chamberlain,  
A white veiled chamber,  
The wind sweeps the chamber doors.  
There are many German houses,  
Many young gentlemen's houses,  
They wanted to make a bed there,  
To put pillows for sleeping.  
From there I secretly came out,  
A little sneaky – a little sneaking,  
I gave the door an elbow,  
Whistled a tune for the kvass.  
So that the doors wouldn't open,  
The birch twigs shouted,  
I ran home myself.  
Who met me on the way?  
Met were the Germans of Valga,  
Met were the old gentlemen of Valga.  
In front of them were burrows, little tunnels,  
Among them old hares –  
Behind them iron carts.  
In the middle, the handsome foxes.  
I asked for a whip to hit the dog,  
To tear the collar away. –  
They did not give a whip to hit the dog,  
Did not allow to tear the collar.  
Then I took a long rowan twig,  
A little bird cherry stick.  
At the front, I struck the burrows, the tunnels,  
Among them the old hares,  
From behind the iron carts.  
I ran home myself,  
I was not allowed to sleep at home,  
While I was at the feast –  
For two nights I slept on the oat straw.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Today is a day for fun and frolic  
Tomorrow one can sleep  
Tuesday is for going to work  
Saturday evening for coming from the fields  
I myself make fasting days  
I myself trudged through spirit days  
I myself make correction weeks!  
Spirit days are poor indeed  
Angry fasting weeks!  
Spirits torment us  
Fasting is enforced.  
Maidens are tormented  
Proper ones are peeled!  
Our steward is a harsh man,  
The bailiff has cruel hawk eyes  
Angry titmouse eyes.  
The bailiff has a crooked stick  
A coniferous tree – twiggy –  
The staff is made of thorny wood  
The whip’s handle is made of reed stalk  
The whip goes along the maiden’s waist  
Along the woman’s skin –  
Along the ploughmen’s sides.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, poor soul maid,
Whistled farm girl!
They whistle at me,
Regretting the herding.
The herding boy whistles at me,
The herder is an ill-tempered fellow!
The herding boy whistles, becoming smelly,
The herder calls him a little cat.
Why do they whistle at me,
Why do they often whistle?
Because I hit the herder's eyebrow,
The herding boy's nose.
When they hugged me,
They nuzzled gently.
They called me to the spruce grove,
To the spruce thicket - to the windfall.
But I did not go to the spruce grove,
To the spruce thicket, to the windfall.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, we beautiful gentlemen  
And our slender gentlemen  
Golden-doll young gentlemen  
Lace-pretty misses.  
Rise up onto the chair  
Step onto the window frame  
Look upon the poor parish  
Upon the entire parish  
How this parish is tormented.  
The parish is harassed  
Oxen still eat  
The weak horses are yoked  
Men dress in the chest.  
Oxen groan with their horns  
Horses paw at the chest.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Don’t rush away to the west,  
I will leave this place standing.  
I won’t give my place to anyone else,  
I won’t rush to another place.  
Let better ones come to the spot,  
More honorable ones take my place.  
More famous ones to the position,  
Those closest to the wall more respected.  
Then I will come back tomorrow  
To see if there are better ones in the place,  
More honorable ones in the position.  
More famous ones at the spot,  
Those nearest to the wall more respected?  
There were no better ones in the place.  
The next was where I left off,  
The chair was where I lifted it,  
The table was where I placed it,  
The spoons were burning on the stove,  
The ladle handles were heating,  
The room to be swept into the corners,  
The stove to be split into logs,  
The ember crying ashes,  
The stone cupboard to be cleaned,  
The water buckets to be carried.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
When I go home  
I step into the house  
I roll onto the wooden floor.  
Neither straws speak to me,  
Nor the beams tell stories –  
Speak back to the speaker.  
I begin to think  
The wheel’s gathering words  
What are you waiting for, servant,  
Or what are you guarding from the enemy children?  
Surely you will rise,  
Surely you will lean on the chair –  
Sit by the stove.  
Stick your butt on the bench.  
I heard and answered back  
Soon I responded:  
You cannot just rise up,  
You cannot just lean on the chair,  
Stick your butt on the bench.  
I have come to be a servant,  
A walkway walker,  
To handle family matters.  
I stir the porridge,  
I fetch the water bucket,  
I leap onto the neck collar.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
The axe did not tolerate a woman for me,  
The lord did not give good care,  
The sister did not help with the glove,  
The brother did not help with the brass,  
I took it in hand and got angry,  
I went to the grove of the leaf,  
There were three good helpers,  
One was the beautiful grey spirit of the sacred grove,  
The second was dear to the wilderness of the forest,  
The third was the field stallion of the grove,  
He even had a saddle on his back.  
I stepped onto the grey spirit of the sacred grove,  
Let the field stallion work,  
With the grey I plow from below,  
With the ears I plow from above during the day,  
Behind the sky is red,  
Then I strike the mark into the turf,  
Into the arrow of the wound,  
Across Riga's rod,  
It cracks, it creaks in the clouds,  
Who wants to chase after,  
I take the mark from the turf,  
From the arrow of the wound,  
From Riga's rod.  
It cracks, it creaks from the clouds,  
Then I drive down the weather.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Who is coughing in the spruce forest there  
Hammering there on the bark  
The devil is coughing in the spruce forest  
The fiend is hammering on the bark.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was a servant in Olland  
A shepherd boy in Leeverland  
Olland made a new hat  
Leeverland a wide coat  
Then I went on a marriage journey  
Fog ruined my new hat  
Rain ruined the sled letters  
That was the woman we got  
That was a woman to weave dough  
New Riga’s breastplate.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was the only slave in the world  
The stable boy was the second slave  
The orderly was the third slave  
The maiden in the manor was the fourth slave  
The distiller was the fifth slave  
The cooper in the manor was the sixth slave  
The little blacksmith was the seventh slave  
The herdsman was the eighth slave  
The night watchman was the ninth slave  
The plowman was the tenth slave.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Let's start, men, let's go  
Let's light up the pipes  
All the village ovens are burning  
Sauna stoves are smoking  
When I start to go  
Then sorrow begins to come  
Water rolls down from my eyes  
I feel sorrow over Anne  
Sorrow over Anne's children  
How should I go home?  
Answer by watching the wall  
The tune of home - everyone angry  
The home of six - everyone was mad  
The home of seven - all confused  
The home of eight - all jealous  
The home of nine - all bosses  
The home of ten - all were cold  
These will come after me  
The lower ones are time rods  
The upper ones are linden bark  
They fall on my eyes.  
One hundred years may pass  
One hundred years of rain may pour  
Still, I must go  
I do not care about the proud  
I do not heed the tall ones  
I do not boast proudly  
I tear apart the rich  
I was cursed by the dung lowly  
Slandered by the drinker  
Keeper of tavern benches  
Bartender of tavern doors  
Mover of tavern bells  
I was not a howler at doors  
Nor a mover of bells  
Nor keeper of tavern benches  
Nor a sleeper in the taverns.

label: Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:

Let's light up the pipes

Keeper of tavern benches  
Bartender of tavern doors  
Mover of tavern bells

Nor a mover of bells  
Nor keeper of tavern benches  
Nor a sleeper in the taverns.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Let's start going  
Home, to our waiting place  
To the gate view.  
Who is waiting for us at home?  
The dog is waiting for us at home  
The cat is chasing us behind  
The dog waits for puppies  
The cat for kittens in the box  
Evening comes unexpectedly  
Twilight comes uninvited  
The day goes down without hope  
What else do I worry about  
What harm do I regret  
Worrying breaks the mind  
Regretting breaks the neck  
It ruins the curly hair.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Oh, poor me, a servant woman  
A little wretched sauna woman  
I go around the mistress  
I go around the housekeeper  
I ask her for a pot  
I grumble for a corner of a cloth  
I have broth pushed away from me  
I have the beans taken away from me  
I have the dregs pushed away from me  
I spoon up ashes  
I beat myself up  
I brush away the little wretched cloth.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Mother Ella, dear granny!  
Heat the village's cold saunas,  
Heat the stove for the kitchen flower,  
Honor the whisk under the birch.  
Take me to the whisking in the sauna,  
And to the washing with a broom.  
Whisking strikes on the head like whip lashes,  
On the neck like cane blows,  
On the forearm like rod blows:  
What evil hump has struck,  
What harsh district chief has laid down,  
What bitter spite has troubled.

label: Murelaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was still very small,  
A knee-high little boy!  
At night I would beat the manor's thighs,  
By day I plowed the manor's fields.  
Where I cried – the corners were wet,  
Where I stood – the walls were wet,  
But there a little pond formed,  
A small fishpond grew.  
If it were up to my being,  
Up to my doing:  
I would turn the gentlemen into oxen,  
The young ladies into geese,  
The old ladies into hoe handles,  
The old gentlemen into broom handles;  
The bailiff, the steward, the assistant  
I would put at the very forefront of hell.  
The old night watchman,  
He is in hell before everyone else  
Inside the foremost cauldron.

label: Murelaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
I was still very small,  
A knee-high little boy!  
At night I would beat the manor's thighs,  
By day I plowed the manor's fields.  
Where I cried – the corners were wet,  
Where I stood – the walls were wet,  
But there a little pond formed,  
A small fishpond grew.  
If it were up to my being,  
Up to my doing:  
I would turn the gentlemen into oxen,  
The young ladies into geese,  
The old ladies into hoe handles,  
The old gentlemen into broom handles;  
The bailiff, the steward, the assistant  
I would put at the very forefront of hell.  
The old night watchman,  
He is in hell before everyone else  
Inside the foremost cauldron.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
My old Ella grandmother
Heard me coming home.
Väila
Behind the forest to the glade
Immediately she said to the daughter-flower:
Jump Anne, jump Kaie
Then take the gate from the prison
Close the doors with a ring
Home is coming a golden bird
A silk bird is coming inside
Then put the stove to heat
Heat the stove with caraway
Honor the whisk on the branch
Then go brush the branch
Quickly go to the well
The duck goes to wash its knees
The pig goes to wash the piglets
In hand are whip strokes
On the neck are stick blows
Whip harnesses between the belt
That the bad Kubjas struck
The chief of the village struck
The rough one knocked harshly
The rough one has a stone stick
The Junker has five stripes
Kubjas has six twists
Let's help him, the ambulance
It went along the path straight
Along the thin shoulder
Along the small arm.
***
Let it be, let me swear
Bitter death will come to the rough one
Ale death will come to the howler
Bad death will come to the swearer
A tongue was put on the stove
Teeth directly into the oven
Jaws to the edge of the oven.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Vao parish, troubled parish!  
Norra parish, weeping parish;  
Preedi parish, frozen parish.  
Oh, this dear Kapu parish.  
And this good Ärvit parish  
Love Aruküla.  
There on the instrument, the thigh is beaten,  
At the dance, the sole is stamped,  
At the song, the boards are struck,  
In Preedi, the whip is used to beat,  
At the beat, the boards are struck.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Maybe I could come from the year of heaven  
Turn into a rain of rods  
Let hail fall from the iron sky  
Still, one must be a slave  
A servant must really do  
Immediately start properly.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
So be it, so be it, I curse;
What do I wish upon the oppressor -
Shall I give to the letter carrier? -
Wolves upon his herd;
Buzzards upon his chickens,
Bears upon the entire flock! -
Sheep upon the forest herds.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Ellakene, little brother  
Don’t you become a drunkard  
Don’t become a farmhand  
Bitter death is for a drunkard  
Slow death is for a farmhand  
Fine death is for a swearer  
If he would die  
As if a stove was placed on the hearth  
Under the poor hay roof  
Teeth under the tongue.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Mother, dear little woman,  
Heating the stove in the kitchen,  
Grave—  
I take the torch to light the chaff,  
But the birch broom is left to simmer.  
I feel the whip blows on my palm,  
Blue marks on my back. –  
Oh, that poor Esna parish.  
Among other parishes;  
In the middle of the district:  
Whip strokes were heard in Laius,  
Whip lashes in Paide,  
Misery in Alutaguse,  
A girl’s crying over the meadow!  
I cried before, I cried yesterday,  
I cry again, I cry today!  
The eyebrows of Milla have dried up,  
The eyes have hardened and straightened,  
In the harsh village, between  
The graves of Esna parish?!

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
My tender old grandmother  
Heard me coming home,  
Was out searching.  
Behind the grove, singing,  
Immediately she said to her daughters:  
Jump Anne, leap Kaie,  
Take then the gate from the fence,  
Close the door tight—  
Home is coming the golden bird,  
Entering the silk bird.  
Then light the stove,  
Heat the stove with cumin,  
Prepare the birch whisk for the sauna,  
Then the birch whisk will begin to beat,  
Also the bones will begin to bathe,  
The duck will wash its knees,  
The mosquito will rinse its legs.  
In its hand are whip strokes,  
On its neck are club strokes,  
Whip folds under the belt—  
What the evil corporal struck,  
What the troop leader put on,  
The crooked one cursed wildly.  
In the crooked one’s hand is a stone stick,  
In the Junker’s, a five-striped one,  
On the corporal, a six-twisted one,  
On the aide, a toothy one:  
It went along the forehead,  
Along the thin shoulder,  
Along the slender arm.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
German children pages  
Gentlemen children, naughty children,  
Would give a nursing mother to endure  
The careless brats.  
To tend the wet nurse,  
To raise the housemaids.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
German children, squire children  
The lord's children are scoundrels  
The maiden's children are weaklings  
The whore has suckled milk  
The debauched whore has  
The peasant has wise children  
The old woman's milk is suckled  
The old man's cock is raised  
The cow has suckled.

label: Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest  ; Töölaulud
translation:
One of us doers  
We drink in the tavern until dawn  
We lick it wide and white  
Say a word if the purse listens to the word  
Our purse gives strength  
If the purse did not listen to the word  
The purse did not give strength  
Then we drink at the gentleman’s granary  
At the lady’s sturdy chest  
Then we go to start the aid  
To tear the lady’s chest  
The watchman happened to see  
The watchman ran to the young man  
The young man ran to the gentleman  
The gentleman steps towards the granary  
I step towards the gentleman  
Listen, dear gentleman  
Blue Stocking lady  
We drink at the tavern, godson  
Say a word if the purse listens to the word  
If the purse did not listen to the word  
The purse did not give strength  
Then we drank at the gentleman’s granary  
At the lady’s sturdy chest  
We came to start the aid  
To tear the lady’s chest  
The watchman happened to see  
The watchman ran to the young man  
The young man ran to the gentleman  
The gentleman steps towards the granary  
I step towards the gentleman  
Listen, dear gentleman  
Blue Stocking lady.  
Be quiet, you stable boy  
You do this mischief to us  
In summer you plow large fields  
In winter you chop much wood  
The nights are long, the flails beat the thighs  
The days are short, you chop wood  
I beat the threshing floor with joy  
The rains  
I beat the threshing floor, that I forget  
I sing a song, I worry about something else.
