label: Töölaulud
translation:
Flee, flee to the field  
The little plump one is coming out  
The field binders are coming  
There seem to be pasture walkers  
They bring sickles with them  
Hooked irons in hand.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field,  
If you do not end, I will leave you,  
Will I leave the cats to roam  
Or let the mouse jump,  
Whoever gets their sickle in front first,  
The bridegroom will row back to them.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let's go plowing to the village,  
A furrow ahead to Vainu,  
The oxen are yoked in front,  
Plows in hand in the smoke.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I take the little nasturtium  
A tiny pastel  
It weaves fabric, the fabric rustles  
He strokes with a swishing sound  
Lace ribbons rustle  
Floorboards tick-tack.

label: Töölaulud  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
I would ask for a family father  
I would ask for a family mother  
Who would hold a wedding in our field  
A wedding in the field, a gathering in the meadow  
Without killing this bull  
Who has seven heaves of ribs.  
Ten cubits around the flank  
A hundred cubits of back fat  
All his meat intact  
And his fat not wasted  
For he had not gone to the ditch,  
Gone to the ditch, nor seen the plow.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Little thighpap boy  
Put fat sheets on  
Ordered to beat the berry to the ground  
I beat the berry, wise to the ground  
I struck the floor with a bang  
The ceiling responded with a crash  
The ceiling beams rattled.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The field was long, I was small  
The field was wide, I was little  
The field does not end without singing  
The field is wide without haymaking  
I have no one to carry the grain  
To bend, to lean on a trio  
Then the little field would end  
The little field would diminish  
The field was long, I was small  
The field was wide, I was little.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Nest, nest, little hollow  
Take me, little boy  
But I know how to make fish traps  
Make fish traps, set nets.  
I can also put barriers in the river  
Then big whitefish came in  
Big whitefish, black backs  
Big, wide mouths at the end.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Kettle, kettle boil  
Cook, cook the porridge  
The men are coming from the forest  
The boys from chopping wood  
The children from picking chips.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
When the kangaroos went fishing  
Pot-bums to Peipus  
Then God gave good fishing  
Perches and pike heads  
Rudd good fishing.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Buzz, buzz the sickle  
Clatter, the curved iron  
Whoever reaches the sickle first  
From us will become a man  
Whoever falls under the sickle  
From us will die  
Whoever stops the sickle  
From us will grow old  
Whoever breaks the sickle  
Will become a whore.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Swing-swing little goat  
Laala-laala little lamb  
Swing little goat kid  
Other herds are far away  
Our herd is in the enclosure  
Other bulls are on the grass  
Our bulls are in the mud.  
Other mares are in the rye field  
Our mares are in the stubble field.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Master, little master  
Mistress, little mistress  
Raise your silky eyes  
Lift your hat  
For there in the golden field  
In the wide Ladevell field  
Without beating with a whip  
Without shooting the rose widely  
Without punishing with a club.  
Master, little master  
There are weddings in your field  
And calves in your yard  
He promised a bull for the wedding  
A verse for the calf  
He promised him ungreased  
Half a barrel of moonshine.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The field was pentagonal  
In each corner five stakes  
On each stake five rods  
On each rod six sheaves  
In each sheaf six ears.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
Chop, dear little clearing  
You have long been for chopping  
Every day people walked on you  
Every noon you were cut  
Every hour people came near  
Long ago I cooked chopped porridge  
Long ago chop or it melts  
Still chop, not yet cut  
To Attüma’s father  
To Attüma’s mother  
To feed and to water  
Well to send off  
Fed us with pork  
From the share of the kohioin  
To the tassel of the ox’s head  
What I promised to father  
What I promised to mother  
Tubs full, boxes full  
Children’s wide bellies full  
A thousand measures for the windy foot  
A hundred measures for the horn-stock  
Even more for father.

label: Töölaulud  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
Attüma father-in-law  
Attüma mother-in-law  
Who had held a wedding in the field  
A wedding in the field, stakes in the field  
There was a bull, but the bull  
Who had never been anywhere in the world  
Had plowed the furrow in the field  
And shaken the plowshare with its horn.

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Töölaulud
translation:
Hans was a sweet little boy  
Karel a dear little brother  
He made us a little playground  
A swing under the village  
A seesaw under the yard  
A dancing circle in the street  
Who went to swing on the swing  
To seesaw under the yard  
Ann was a diligent little girl  
Ann went to swing on the swing  
To seesaw under the yard  
To dance in the street  
The neck collars broke as well  
Broken, a mess were Anne's swings  
Ann went to the blacksmith's  
The little blacksmith, the fellow  
Take my swings and fix them  
Straighten the silver fittings  
You hear the blacksmith, answer back  
If you come to me  
To me, to my son  
Ann was a diligent little girl  
Anne hears, answers back  
I will not come to you  
To you, to your son  
The blacksmith's children were sullen  
Sooty, black-smudged children  
The blacksmith himself without soot  
The blacksmith’s wife black as the wall

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I am the son of a seaman  
Dear man, son of a fisherman  
Sit on the edge of the boat  
I will push you into the other sea  
I will drown you in the other well  
I will dress you in another fur coat.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let's go, my sisters  
Let's go, let's pull  
Let's hurry, let's row  
So that we reach the shore  
Paide appeared in the distance  
Narva seemed to be in the navel  
The laughing girls are coming  
The mockers of Riga seem to be coming  
Frosts of iron down the shoulder  
Mill irons around the waist  
Silver irons under the shoulder  
Gold irons under the ribs  
You will get a fiancé in Paide  
You will get Hindrek from Hiiumaa  
You will get Andres from Harjumaa  
Siim will bring you silk shoes  
I throw away the silk shoes  
Toomas brings fiery shoes  
I will put on Jaan’s shoes.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
Chop, dear little clearing  
I have long since been chopping you  
Long since chopped the unripe path  
Now I will begin to go to the meadow  
To plow the wilderness borderlands  
Fields unplanted in the meadow  
Fields unwalked in the wilderness  
Then I will begin to go to the meadow  
The low bushes called from the hill  
The beautiful ones called from the grove  
Come, girls, to pick  
To weave red wreaths  
I myself am weaving fields, little pig  
I myself am singing meadows, sheep  
I am edging the patchy land  
To become a giver of fish below  
To become a setter of pigs full of milk.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let's hurry, let's rush
So that we reach our destination
So that we sink our poles
So that we reach the tip of the island
The field's long bed
The wide border of the field
So I could get to the end, catch a squirrel
So I could get on the ice, catch a hare
At the end, one can drink beer
In the border, there is milk and bread
At the loading area, one can drink kvass
God knows, perhaps knows
Maybe the whips are in the border
Maybe the chain is in the loading area
An old man between the furrows
Maybe we will be whipped with the whips
Dogs will be fed on rose petals
At the dock, to be berated
At the harbor, to be hit.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
Now the ticks have teemed, barrels and bunches  
The broad ends of the ticks' children.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Uiut-tuiut hat  
Our grandma has many hats  
The sauna lady has a hundred hats  
The village lady has ten hats  
One was a nightcap  
The second a visiting hat  
The third was a church hat  
The fourth a hay mowing hat  
The fifth a rain hat  
The sixth a dry weather hat  
The seventh a pig feeding hat  
The eighth a herding hat  
The ninth a pride hat  
The tenth a village visiting hat.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Töölaulud
translation:
The man I would go to  
Whom I would go to  
I do not want to be a miller  
The miller's stone rumbles  
I do not want to be a farrier  
The farrier's bell rattles  
I do not want to be a blacksmith  
The blacksmith himself was black  
The blacksmith’s son was pitch-black  
I want to be a proper farmer  
Turning the black soil  
From there you can get rye bread  
Every day you can have your own bread  
On Sunday you can have rye bread.

label: Laulud noorrahva elust  ; Töölaulud
translation:
The man I would go to  
Whom I would go to  
I do not want to be a miller  
The miller's stone rumbles  
I do not want to be a farrier  
The farrier's bell rattles  
I do not want to be a blacksmith  
The blacksmith himself was black  
The blacksmith’s son was pitch-black  
I want to be a proper farmer  
Turning the black soil  
From there you can get rye bread  
Every day you can have your own bread  
On Sunday you can have rye bread.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
So let it be seen, let it be shown  
Whether what was promised was done  
Is there a sheep on the table  
With the lamb ending by the stove.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
How should I go home
Going under the eaves
Turning onto the roof ridge
Home is dark, everyone is angry
The home tune, everyone is mad
The home is angry and fallen
Starting words to grant
Bad words to cast
To whom else but to me
For the suffering daughter.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Home seven, all confused  
Home eight, all jealous  
Home nine, all superiors  
Home ten, all are cold  
All are cold sideboards  
Angry edge boards.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Surely here in the field there are armies  
Surely here in the field there are orphans  
Before the warm gleaming stove  
There is no line of poets in the field  
A line of poets, a line of singers  
Before the warm gleaming stove  
On the meadow gold there are fallers  
Those fallers are worn out  
Rollers of the field are tired  
Some have been carried away by rainstorms  
Or killed by winter cold  
Burned by summer drought  
Snow has lulled new ones  
Dew has bent the cold  
So let me say again  
They have not been carried away by rainstorms  
Nor killed by winter cold  
They have been taken by Viru boys  
Barked at by Harju boys  
With their golden wealth  
With the roots of beets  
With worn-out pennies.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Field, field, little field,  
At your edge, little tail.  
You have long been harming,  
And every midday you are cut,  
Every day people come upon you.  
Pigs have been seen, indeed seen,  
Whether it is allowed to come to the threshing floor,  
Whether there is a sheep on the table,  
Whether there is smoke of a piglet in the room,  
Smoke of aspen by the oven,  
Smoke of alder in front of the fire.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Sisters, dear ones,  
Let us walk with two hands,  
Two hands, ten fingers,  
So that we gather oats,  
So that we heap up islands,  
So that we sink into valleys.  
At the end, we have red beer,  
Milk cakes in powder.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
It is cruel to bow down,  
Evil to bend pain,  
To our evil crane.  
Ella under the rye ella  
Step forward, ellas  
Let the wide hand  
Find the lookout's Ojume  
We sow into the field garden.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let us finish this field  
Let us reduce this weed.  
Be vigilant, be tall,  
Be vigilant, our girls,  
We will gather the sheaves  
And store them in the field barn.  
Let us pull and draw,  
So that in the evening we  
Can get home from the field barn.  
Here we find smoke outside.  
In front, there is pot smoke,  
In the back room, stove smoke.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Eeli past irises,  
Mix-matched dog beds,  
Then rabbit beds!  
Left behind little plants,  
Left behind little plant stalks,  
Left behind little berries,  
Left behind little berry stalks.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Indeed, I am supposed to reap, but I cannot manage,  
I cannot make progress at the front,  
Nor can my little field escape,  
The grain residue is small,  
The oat residue is decreasing.  
Is my strength taken away,  
Or are my hands bound,  
My fingers tied and knotted.  
Indeed, I am supposed to reap, but I cannot manage,  
The sickle was bad, it does not gather.  
The sickle was made by an evil smith,  
The iron was hammered powerless.  
Soon I will start to curse,  
Take, devil, the smith's hands,  
The smith's hands and the smith's fingers.  
Otherwise, I risk burning the grain,  
Otherwise, wasting the iron.  
The fire consumed the wood, the fodder was spent,  
The piece of iron was wasted,  
The piece of steel spoiled;  
He made a faulty sickle,  
A sickle with seven serrations,  
My little garment, my little brother,  
Take it down to the spring,  
In hand a little golden device,  
Then ride to the smith in Viru,  
To the wise one behind.  
Make me a sharp sickle,  
Have a copper head cast,  
Front made smooth and bright,  
Back dripping with tin,  
Boil gold in the middle,  
Then I will reap, then I will manage,  
Gathering the gathered grain,  
Collecting the residue,  
Together clean the sheaves,  
Together the sheaf tops,  
Together the sheaf bottoms.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Surely I sow, but I do not have strength,  
I do not progress forward,  
My little field does not flourish,  
My grain field is diminishing,  
My oat field is shrinking.  
My strength has been taken,  
Or my hands have been tied,  
Fingers have been bound with thread,  
Fingers tied in knots.  
Summer heat has burned,  
Has brought down a rainstorm,  
Or maybe hail has struck,  
Or winter cold has killed,  
Cold dew has embraced,  
So let me say again,  
Hail has not struck,  
Winter cold has not killed,  
Snow has lulled new ones to sleep,  
Has not brought down a rainstorm,  
From here the young men of Viru have taken away,  
The young men of Arju have taken away.  
With their given money,  
With stove-copper coins,  
With deceptive pennies.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
We will finish this field,  
Reduce this open space,  
Plant seeds beneath the village,  
At the gate of the wide forest;  
So that I can go to the meadow,  
Walk in the hollow,  
Go red beneath the trees,  
To the alder grove on the linden leaf,  
Walk down the hill on the lowlands.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I wanted to go to the work party,  
To hold the barley wedding,  
To sow oats in the field.  
I have not been put on a wage  
Nor am I a buyer,  
Put on a salary for money.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let’s go with two hands,  
Two hands, ten fingernails,  
Roll five finger joints,  
Turn ten fingernails,  
So that we swim at the edges,  
So that we sink on the stakes.  
At the edge, you drink without oil,  
In the barn, milk and bread,  
From the blacksmith’s comes coarse bread,  
So let me say it again,  
Let me sing it back again.  
Maybe there is a whip in the barn,  
A stick between the blacksmith’s legs.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I will mow, I do not tire,  
I will wane, I do not lessen,  
My little field does not disappear,  
The grain field does not diminish,  
The oat field decreases as well.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
If my shoulders were made of apple wood,  
My forearms of maple,  
My fingers of currant wood,  
Then I would cut, then I would reach,  
Then I would pull, then I would decrease;  
Then I would progress steadily,  
And the barley field would diminish.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Now I have gathered this field,  
Bound this wide meadow,  
Layered this broad pasture.  
No hair was left struck by the wind,  
No grain lost to hail.  
Filled barrels, filled casks,  
Filled the wide stomachs of children,  
Filled my own little belly.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Rhubarb and rhubarb  
Hurry and rush  
So that we swim to the ends  
So that we sink into the marsh  
We take the long posts  
At the end there is red beer  
On the table there are milk pies  
On the island you are supposed to eat bread  
So let me say it again  
Let me sing the song back  
Maybe there is whip on the table  
Also rye on the oat field  
It goes along the waistline  
Along the thin shoulder  
Along the edges, along the back  
Across the open field  
What do I worry about in front of me  
For whom do I bear care  
If it is not my father who worries  
If it is not my mother who worries  
The head does not care for the boy  
Two brothers do not care.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Rise, serious sisters  
Hurry, foolish sisters  
So that we chase them away  
So that we trample down the threshing floor  
We buy a long post  
We’ll reach the island shore  
On the island one can eat bread  
In the garden there are curds and cream cakes  
But I hear, I reply  
Perhaps there is a whip in the garden  
And a rural barley field  
It goes along the waist  
Along the thin shoulder  
Along the edges, along the back  
Crosswise across the bare meadow.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Shrink, little clearing  
Come to an end, little island  
You have long been eaten  
Every noon you were cut  
Every day people walked over you  
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
So that the hair won't be beaten by the wind  
Nor a flock shaken by the rain  
Nor a handful beaten by the cloud  
Now it has all been gathered  
The field reduced  
Now we run out of midday bread  
The sandwiches fall apart  
The little dung beetles stay for a long time.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The vegetable garden is already visible  
The hedge is turning green  
Beer mugs in the porter  
Milk pies in the garden bed.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Spinner, spinner, little spindle  
Spinner, spindle of twisted yarn  
The comb does not tolerate tangled yarn  
The maid pulls  
The mistress tightens  
A taut umbrella  
A drooping windshade.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Uiut, tuiut, a doll's hat  
Our granny has many hats  
The village granny has ten hats  
One was a nightcap  
The second was a daytime hat  
The third was a visiting hat  
The fourth was a hay mowing hat  
The fifth was a seed sowing hat  
The sixth was a stack animal hat  
The seventh was a pig feeding hat  
The eighth was a shepherd's hat  
The ninth was a kitchen hat  
The tenth was a fodder plowing hat.

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Töölaulud
translation:
Eat, cattle, blue cattle  
Drink, cattle, red cattle  
Lie down, cattle, to rest  
For a little while to nap  
To cool down a bit  
To rest during the heat  
Until I set the pig ropes  
Weave grandmother’s apron strings  
I will make a belt with embroidery  
Eat, cattle, blue cattle  
Drink, cattle, red cattle  
Lie down, cattle, to rest  
To rest during the heat  
The herdsman watched the cattle  
The pasture to go seek  
In the foggy morning  
In the rough dew  
On the cloudy days  
Here there is no livestock manure  
No ringing in the pasture bell  
Now the herdsmen have vanished  
Have scattered to the edges  
Into the large swamp  
Into the dense bush  
Into the thick forest.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The front advance  
The retreat to the other side  
Until on the meadow bundles of straw  
By the forest edge, the barley heads.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little patch  
Already long ago you were plowed  
Every noon you are watered lukewarm  
You are swayed by the wind  
Rocked by the cruel world  
Howling in the gullies  
Thriving in the kuiks  
Nodding on the heads  
Spinning stones on the goat  
Fields swirling on the moose.

label: Töölaulud  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
The master wanted to organize a work party  
He killed that ox  
That ox, a big ox  
Who had carried the yoke over the field before  
But had not plowed the field thoroughly.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I will cut, but I cannot manage  
My strength has been taken away  
Or are my hands tied  
Are my fingers bound behind my back.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The end came when boiling
When everything was gathered together
You were already being beaten long ago
Every day they came at you
Every noon at boiling
Every hour they came near.

label: Töölaulud  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
I am the son of a seaman  
A handsome man, son of a fisherman  
Let it be, let it be, little brother  
Let the servants be bought  
The fishermen's boys traded  
Who comes rowing with the tide  
Rowing, demanding  
Fishing for victory  
I push another man into the sea  
I drown another new fellow  
I dip another man’s coat  
I sit on the edge of the boat  
With a new catch  
I pick with full strength  
The bigger ones I put in my mouth  
The smaller ones I throw back into the water  
From them grew little fish,  
Big bream, backs black  
Wide salmon, tails twitching  
Ants is not considered a man  
Pike is not considered a fish  
Roach is not fed  
Salmon gets bread crumbs  
But when Ants takes a wife  
Then Ants is counted a man  
Pike is counted a fish  
Roach is fed  
Salmon gets bread crumbs.

label: Töölaulud  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
I am the son of a seaman  
A handsome man, son of a fisherman  
Let it be, let it be, little brother  
Let the servants be bought  
The fishermen's boys traded  
Who comes rowing with the tide  
Rowing, demanding  
Fishing for victory  
I push another man into the sea  
I drown another new fellow  
I dip another man’s coat  
I sit on the edge of the boat  
With a new catch  
I pick with full strength  
The bigger ones I put in my mouth  
The smaller ones I throw back into the water  
From them grew little fish,  
Big bream, backs black  
Wide salmon, tails twitching  
Ants is not considered a man  
Pike is not considered a fish  
Roach is not fed  
Salmon gets bread crumbs  
But when Ants takes a wife  
Then Ants is counted a man  
Pike is counted a fish  
Roach is fed  
Salmon gets bread crumbs.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The village's golden young men  
The parish's fair-haired boys  
Some were among the manor boys  
Among them also boys from Harju  
Do not make the girls laugh  
Do not mock the village girls  
Do not bully Keedikaela  
Do not curse Sõlgekaela  
You have not won the girls  
The other half unaware  
Perhaps they do not get the girl  
Who cannot bring home the firewood  
Cannot help with the stove  
Cannot place it by the beam  
Cannot save the bed's edge  
Who brings the wool to the comb  
The locks to the wise girl  
Puts on the wool sack on his back  
Pulls on the lock sack.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little grain  
You've long been worked on  
Every day you were tread upon  
Every hour people came to you  
If you do not end with the weeds  
You will not be gathered together  
Then let the wind sway you  
Let the swallows walk on you  
Let the harrows roar.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let's finish this field  
Let's reduce this gap  
You've long been troublesome  
Every day they came after you  
Every noon was lukewarm.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Rise up, housewives  
Get up, householders  
Rise to milk the cows  
To send out the herd  
Our herd to the pasture  
The village herd to the distant place  
Our bulls to the mud  
The village bulls to the grassland  
Our oxen to the stackyard  
The village oxen to the hayfield  
Our stallions to the stackyard  
The village stallions to the ryefield.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Eat, cattle, blue, cattle  
Drink, cattle, red, cattle  
Lie down, cattle, to breathe  
For a rest, to rest  
For an hour, to doze  
Until I set the pig's collar  
Fastening the shoelace  
Weaving the apron string on the maiden  
Setting the sharp-edged  
Sticking the pear-edged  
But I raise the curled one  
I touch the cat's paw.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Our granny has several hats  
The sauna lady has a hundred pieces  
The tavern lady has ten pieces  
One was a chimney sweep's hat  
The second a stove keeper's hat  
The third a pile of animals' hat  
The fourth a hunting capture hat.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Rise up, young people  
Get up, elders of the family  
Rise to milk the cows  
To send the herd out  
Mundel’s herd to the distant fields  
Our herd to the pasture  
Munde’s young bulls on the grass  
Our young bulls on the mud  
Munde’s trotting steers on the pasture  
Our steers to the pasture  
Rise up, young people  
Rise to beat the thigh  
The sieve is already standing by the edge  
The moon in front of our home  
Our sieve stands on the dung heap  
Poles against the white fence  
The black thigh is debated  
The pole-striker is argued with  
The black thigh is not a good one  
Nor the loud pole-striker  
Reiepapp was a tender brother  
Who placed thick layers  
Set to the height of a sow  
The width of a foot sole  
Ordered Malle to hit to the ground  
I stepped forward inch by inch  
From the want-to-wall titsa-tatsa  
Dear Reiepapp  
Do not leave the thigh unbeaten.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
How should I go home  
To step outside  
At home everyone is angry  
At home everyone hates  
At home everyone is confused  
At home everyone is jealous  
At home everyone is above  
At home everyone is less feminine  
They all come at me  
They all rain on my eyes  
Words of blessings are given  
Bad words are thrown  
Angry words are cast down  
To whom else but to me  
Everyone must suffer dearly  
Poor, do not resist  
Grandmother's angry, grandfather's angry  
Louder, the elder is angry  
I roll the words up  
Press them to my belt  
Grandmother's angry, grandfather's angry  
Louder, the elder is angry.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
We mourn and finish  
We finish this field  
We reduce this crop  
Without beating with a whip  
Without striking the playful one  
God knows this  
Dear little Antsukene thinks  
Maybe the whip is in the meadow  
A stick on the edge of the island  
Today we are beaten with a whip  
Iron is pried from the pink  
I wanted to come to the communal work  
I wanted the communals without oil  
To rest a few days  
Two days on the rocky crust  
I did not come because of food  
Nor ran because of drinking  
I fled because of the fish  
Not because of warm beer  
I came to do beauty  
To hold a girl's party  
To joke with women  
The girl has a party to hold  
To the women a farmer's bride  
Let me speak frankly  
I speak to twisted iron  
The scythe wears out the stork  
Iron limits the length of the field.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Rise up, young people  
Wake up, heads of families  
Rise up to beat thighs  
To stretch bonds  
To pile up stacks  
The sieve is already standing by the edge  
The moon stands in front of the house  
The stars in front of our stairs.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Herdsmen and starvers  
Are eating the manor's black ox  
The dark ox of Tõrevere.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Hello room, where I came  
Hello thigh, where I travel  
Hello room of deer fur  
Room ceiling of ship fur  
Walls of woodpecker tails  
Thigh is beaten with joy  
Ceilings of capercailie song  
Joyfully the thigh is beaten  
In dance, the foot is stomped  
In song, the ceilings are struck.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Master, little master  
Mistress, little mistress  
Promised to hold weddings in the field  
Promised to hold charms in the meadow  
Promised a bull for the wedding  
A verse for the charms  
We shall see, we shall find out  
Whether there is a bull for the wedding  
A verse for the charms  
If there is no bull for the wedding  
There is no verse for the charms  
Let it be, let it be, I complain  
Let it be gloomy at home  
Yawning by the hearth.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The field was long, the maidens young  
The wide field, the children weak  
The field brings long entwining  
The wide field, broad clearing  
The ends run into the ditch  
The corners under the meadow spring  
The field was long, the maidens young  
The wide field, the children weak  
Surely I reap, but I have no strength  
I do not understand what is wrong with me  
Has my strength run out  
Or have my hands been bound  
The fingers tied behind my back  
Surely I reap, but I have no strength.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
You have long been plowed  
Every midday warmed up  
Every day walked over you  
You will see, you will see,  
Whether it is allowed to come inside  
Whether there is a sheep on the glow  
There is aspen smoke by the stove  
There is aspen smoke by the glow  
Whether there is a crackle of embers inside.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
How evil it is to bow down  
To bend the head to wickedness  
To your evil crane  
Ella alle at the flax field  
Step forward, flax stalks  
Let the sickle grab hold  
We will reach the sacred grove  
We will get the field demon.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Sisters, dear ones  
Let them walk on two hands  
Two hands, ten nails  
So we row the rafts  
So we build the islands  
So we sink the copper  
At the end we have red beer  
In the pantry, milk cakes.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Rise up, villagers  
Drive away the foremen  
Rise to milk the cows  
Send the herd out  
The village herd to the distance  
Our herd in the barnyard  
Village bulls in the grass  
Our bulls in the mud  
Village horses in the rye  
Our horses in the meadow  
The pig went to the mustard garden  
The chicken went to the hemp garden  
The rooster went to the golden kale garden  
The cow went to Ruuge rye field  
The pig ate mustard seeds  
The chicken ate hemp seeds  
The rooster ate golden kale seeds  
The cow ate Ruuge rye grains.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The wind comes, the bonfire sings  
The girl makes a cake, I go to the herd  
I keep the old woman's sheep  
I protect the old man's spotted cattle  
In front of God's house  
Behind Marie's room  
Between the creator's barns  
Go to the herd, blue herd  
Put (them) to the herd, red herd  
Let the herd rest  
Let them rest for a moment  
To wither into a little scrap  
Until I tie the leg bands  
Fastening the shoe laces  
Weaving the old woman's apron strings  
Beating the bark straps  
I drive in the old scatterings  
I create the old woman's swirls  
I knit cat paw shapes.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Evening comes, arrives late  
Darkness begins to take over  
Evening brings straw to the cabin  
Dusk puts to sleep  
Twilight wakes up  
Spreads white wide  
Dawns scatters haphazardly  
One here, another there  
Onto some manor's yard  
Onto a couple of manor fields  
Fairly even those few  
That are on the manor's fields  
Five were in front to go  
Six followed the stoat  
Seven were whipping sail  
Eight were punishing  
Nine were calling out  
Ten were thinking close  
Forehead passes down along my spine  
Properly along the shoulder  
Along the edges on the back with straw  
So my envelope jumps  
Spreads wide with a slap  
Half patch crackles

label: Töölaulud
translation:
A blacksmith is hammered from the thigh  
Disputed at the hand of the rod  
A good blacksmith cannot be made from black  
The loud rod tester  
Nor an excessive rafter striker  
The thigh smith was a young boy  
He struck the rafters thick  
Set the pigsty to height  
And the blade to the width of a foot.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
How should I go home
Home to the cruel message
To the worse prayer
Bad things happen at home
Words are given
Bad words are thrown
Master, little master
Mistress, little mistress
Hears me coming home
To be out of town
Singing behind in the forest
Jump, Eltse, leap, Kaie
Turn the gates around
Lock the doors tight
Hear where the eagle-hawk falls
The fierce titmouse pecks
Now comes the great eater home
Great eater, great drinker
The one who sets the barrel in place
The barrel eats even his food
He doesn’t eat his food
The barrel drinks even his drink
I hear, I respond
What food do I have from the bull
And what drink from the barrel
There is shit inside, the skin is on
Sometimes the hoof is in the middle
What drink do I have from the barrel
Foam is on top, yeast is below
A little beer in the middle
Master, little master
Mistress, little mistress
You walk to the village in heaven
Holding a loaf of bread
And a can of herrings in your hands
Come eat, poor spirits
The most miserable serfs
That was yours, what was mine.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
How should I go home
Go home and beat the thigh
I have a bad partner
Puts heavy loads on the rook
Sets the sow to the height
Commands to hit the berry to the ground
To the ground, to the top of the wreath
Anklet hits the bottom
If I don’t hit the berry to the ground
Anklet hits the bottom
The village matar beats to the ground
Algu knocks to the bottom.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
You have long been getting shabby  
Every afternoon turning lukewarm  
Every morning turning faded  
Every hour more was added  
Every day you were walked over  
Buzz, buzz, sickle  
Ring, bent iron  
So sings the lazy woman  
Sings the lazy woman's daughter  
If I were to till a field  
Eeke lee eessa  
At the little gate's mouth  
I would swiftly cut the grain  
Two sheaves for the dew  
Three sheaves for the dawn gold.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I mow hay, the forest rustles  
I take a story, the forest snaps  
The tune trembles out  
Touching the spruce’s branch on the heath  
Stopping by the seven bogs.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
We cut mighty whisks  
We make better birch twigs  
So that the whisk can be raised at the pond  
Raised at the pond, raised at the mother-in-law’s  
I am a famous pile maker  
A dear hakki planter  
A maker of buckle fates  
I make big piles in the swamp  
Mighty heaps on the field  
Slender ones obtained for the meadow.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
o, o, o, o, o, o, o, o, o, o
o, o, o, o, o, o, o, o

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
End our lukewarmness  
End what is to be gathered  
Every day it was walked upon  
Every morning a portion  
Every hour someone came near  
End, end, little field  
Rise, serious sisters  
Hurry, foolish sisters  
So that we may reach the end  
We may get to the tip of the island  
At the end we will have barley cake  
Sandwiches, the table is set  
For us it seems like a garden plot  
The edges are turning green  
The aspens stand straight and blue  
The spruces shine golden  
The birches grey green  
Who has the linen shirt  
The aspen has the linen shirt  
Who has the fur coat  
The birch has the fur coat  
Who has the golden hat  
The spruce has the golden hat  
Who has the grey stockings  
The birch has the grey stockings.  
End, end, little field

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Sizzle, sizzle, little cricket  
Clever rattling little knife  
Whoever gets ahead of the sickle  
That one will have a groom in return  
Who among us will die  
Who among us will become a man  
Who among us will become a slave  
Who among us will become a wench.  
Who must wear a lace cap  
One of us must wear a lace cap  
Who wears the dear apron  
One of us wears the dear apron.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
There is no food offered here  
Not even a little is offered  
I take it in hand and get angry  
I raise a wind, a whirlwind of mood  
I throw the sickle here and there  
Scatter the ash far and wide  
I throw the gnats all over  
I myself go to the father’s home  
I myself go to the mother’s home  
There I am given food  
Offered without asking  
Fat and pork  
Fish and chicken eggs.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
End when the field is cut  
Come to an end when gathering  
You have long been to the cutter  
Long been to the cutter’s sickle  
If you do not end when cutting  
You will not come to an end when gathering  
Then you will remain upright, growing  
Growing upright, going to the herd.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field
Come to an end, little island
There are slender breads on the island
In the garden bed are cream cakes.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, hell to the field  
Diminish, devil, out  
Long ago was the beginning  
Long ago the beginning became stale  
We cut the mighty sheaves  
We put strong binders.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Sizzle sizzle little sickle  
Rattle rattle curved little iron  
Whoever reaches the sickle first  
Gets the bridegroom first  
Whoever reaches the sickle sideways  
Will die a wanderer  
Whoever reaches the sickle head-on  
Will wander wearily among the heaps.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
Let's finish this field  
Let's reduce this side  
Fill the barrels, fill the sacks  
Fill the empty corners of the shelves  
Fill the empty folds of the bag  
Fill my own belly  
Fill the wide ends of the children.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Comes lazy, wanders lazy  
Goes lazy, shines lazy  
The lazy one does not advance  
Nor does his little field run away.  
End my little field  
You run out of my hope.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
When we run out of cutting  
The little island ends  
Then our lunch bread runs out  
Slices flow apart  
Beneficial slices last long.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
In Otsa we can drink beer
On the island we can eat bread
On the island the bread is tough.

label: Töölaulud  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
Thank you to the father of the family  
Thank you to the mother of the family  
Who has held a field celebration  
The field celebration brought out the poles  
Did not kill the ox  
That had never walked in the world  
Had plowed beyond the furrow.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
A shepherd loses his flock  
Drives me searching  
In the misty morning  
In the gray dawn  
On the harsh dew  
The fog spoils the new moon  
The dew on the furry coat.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Töölaulud
translation:
Shine, shine little sun  
Brightly little world  
Keep shining on us  
Don't shine on the shore  
The shoremen eat fish  
We poor ones eat water bread  
Tiny milk bread  
Thin beer bread  
Small kvass bread.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Töölaulud
translation:
Shine, shine little sun  
Brightly little world  
Keep shining on us  
Don't shine on the shore  
The shoremen eat fish  
We poor ones eat water bread  
Tiny milk bread  
Thin beer bread  
Small kvass bread.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Lift and pull  
Hurry and row  
Lift after the truth  
Take after the anger  
So that we reach the goal  
And arrive at the tip of the island  
Into the long field’s border  
Into the wide open expanse  
At the end, we have red beer  
In the border are milk loaves  
Sandwiches in between.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
Diminish, diminish, go away  
For you have long been cut down  
Long ago cut down, dried out  
Every day they walked on you  
Every hour they came to you  
Come, come, little wind  
Blessing, dear Creator’s wind  
Take the water from the grain  
The dew from the oat stalk  
Rust from the top of the grass  
So that I may reap a sheaf of grain  
Two sheaves with dew  
Three sheaves with the dawn.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
Long ago you were already being cut  
Everyone came to you every hour.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let's finish this field  
Let's reduce this crop  
You've long been bitter  
Every hour lukewarm.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
How should I go home  
A stranger to a stranger’s door  
The heart is anxious inside  
A knife is in the corner of the hand  
A spear is directly in the head  
Cuts bread, leads the head  
Raises the belt, scolds me  
Gives food, barks  
How should I eat  
How should I drink  
The mouth eats, the heart worries  
The eyes roll with water  
The eyebrows drop with water  
The table sinks with water.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let's finish this field,  
Reduce the wide gap,  
So that the herd can be taken out,  
The field can be grazed by piglets,  
The meadow stacked with sheep,  
A stone turned by the goat,  
The mountain played with the stallion.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Cottages, pastures  
Meadows, hayfields  
Our herds are lost  
Into the thick willow thicket  
Into the pale aspen grove  
Into the ugly spruce forest  
Into the soft pine woods  
Where one does not hear the rooster  
Nor the crying voice of a young child  
Nor the lazy bird’s singing voice.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Hut, shepherds  
Sheep in the hay  
Our flock has disappeared  
Into the thick willow  
Into the pale aspen grove  
Into the ugly spruce forest  
Into the fragrant pine forest  
Where you cannot hear the crow’s call  
Nor the cry of a young child  
Nor the lazy bird’s singing voice.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The shepherd had a hard life  
Water below, water above  
Water between the toes  
Large dirty cow dung  
Wide sheep droppings  
Shepherd boys like chicks  
Eat gold and drink gold  
Put gold in their purses  
Silver over their shoulders  
Copper between their toes.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Töölaulud
translation:
Roll, roll, little day  
Roll because of the day’s evening  
Because of the rich’s money  
Because of our poor’s toil  
Kings have plenty of gold  
The rich have plenty of money  
But we poor have plenty of toil  
There is no time to roll in the day  
The day scorches the husbandman's head  
The long maiden’s head is bound  
But Andres, the poor boy  
Go bring a blessing from the sea  
But Andres did not take the word  
He went slowly, little by little  
Along the tiny road  
Along the lowland earth  
Along the rolling water  
Along the flickering flame  
The lazy one waits for evening  
The sluggish day rolling  
I wait for the morning  
For another day to rise  
For the third dawn to glow  
Evening comes without waiting  
Twilight comes without warning  
Evening brings straw to the barn  
Twilight takes to sleep  
The dark one wakes us up  
Dawn sends us to far heaven  
White spreads apart  
The day scatters us helter-skelter.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Töölaulud
translation:

The dark one wakes us up  
Dawn sends us to far heaven  
White spreads apart  
The day scatters us helter-skelter.

label: Töölaulud  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
A hundred rains, to be able to sleep  
If a storm comes, I would doze  
The slave can sleep a little  
But the gap puts one to sleep  
Still, leaning against the walls  
An ax on the shoulder of a crow  
A prayer on the foundation beam.  
A hundred rains to Harju County  
Take the rain towards Vigala  
Do not rain on our land  
In Harju, the men were black  
The boys were little flames  
The children were little lambs  
In our land, the men were white  
Men were white, women were white  
The boys had golden hair  
The children were cowslips  
The girls were fire-beautiful.

label: Töölaulud  ; Looduslaulud

Do not rain on our land

The boys were little flames  
The children were little lambs  
In our land, the men were white  
Men were white, women were white  
The boys had golden hair  
The children were cowslips  
The girls were fire-beautiful.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud laulust
translation:
Hiss, hiss, little sickles,  
rattle, rattle, crooked irons.  
Whoever's sickle gets here first,  
that one the bridegroom demands to come;  
let's go singing through the forest,  
whose voice is heard far away,  
that one the bridegroom buys a hat for.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I would have plowed, dear brothers  
Dear brothers, strong bulls  
Then the crop would have grown in the stack  
The crop in the stack, in the barnyard  
To exchange a little grain.  
Here have plowed, frozen,  
Harrowed, furrowed  
Inseminated the piglets.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Hostesses, tall women  
Farm women, wise women  
Those women, stubborn women  
Eat cream, drink milk  
Gave butter to the butter-bearers  
Cream to the village herders  
In the evening the servant returns home  
Then those weeds are picked  
Thistles are searched for  
Mallow is boiled  
The servant eats, the heart aches  
The jugs pour waves  
Eyebrows drop golden drops.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud ühiskondlikest vahekordadest
translation:
Hostesses, tall women  
Farm women, wise women  
Those women, stubborn women  
Eat cream, drink milk  
Gave butter to the butter-bearers  
Cream to the village herders  
In the evening the servant returns home  
Then those weeds are picked  
Thistles are searched for  
Mallow is boiled  
The servant eats, the heart aches  
The jugs pour waves  
Eyebrows drop golden drops.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I was called a farm boy  
Father won’t give a truss  
Brother won’t open the barn  
Sister won’t put on the silver ring  
I went down to the hayfield  
But I made a horse out of haystack

label: Töölaulud
translation:
But I sing, I spread my thighs  
The other thigh is spread in the opposite way.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I went to the barn in Riga  
to the Paluvere yard.  
I had a bad partner,  
Kai didn’t quite manage it,  
Mari didn’t mix well.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
What took you so long at home  
You nursed little mice  
You raised little kittens  
You carried puppies to pee  
The puppy peed on the stove  
It pooped on the rye stalk

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I had a wide belt woven  
Edges well inscribed  
In the middle, nice belt patterns  
At the edge, pea patterns  
Inside, I made upright letters.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Some thought me a manor lord  
Some thought me a barn keeper  
I am a humble blacksmith apprentice  
A servant who works with iron  
In my mouth I melt iron  
On my tongue I forge it together  
My teeth are bellows tongues  
My jawbones are bellows boards  
My lips are the bellows holders.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
We finish this field  
Below the village, below the manor  
Below the village, the sowing ridge  
From the manor field, a piece of meadow  
Then I’ll start going to the clearing  
Walking towards the wilderness  
Pieces of the clearing unstarted  
Pieces of the wilderness unwalked.

label: Pulmalaulud  ; Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Our master organizes a work party  
In great warmth  
In more beautiful weather  
On a hotter day  
Full loads are brought  
We were given wide wagons  
Everyone drank out of desire  
The fiddle here calls a dance tune  
Lift your foot, white stockings  
Wait here in the manner of the dance  
Dust won't destroy us  
If you are willing to be alert  
And you want to behave wisely  
Before choosing the painful  
Clean the room, clear the tables  
Goods under the windows  
Wash the cups upside down  
Loads are brought in rows  
Jugs to the kitchen doorstep  
Windows facing the garden fence  
Tomorrow the bailiff comes to the village  
The old German comes to the manor  
He thinks of the moon shining  
The evening star softening  
The morning star paling  
Our cups upside down  
Loads to the edge of the room  
Jugs to the kitchen doorstep  
Windows facing the garden fence  
I went to a wedding somewhere  
I travelled to seven saes (seven settlements)  
I can’t give away what I  
Got from this bride’s hand  
I got a belt with five stripes  
Gloves with scratch marks  
Should I hide it under the bench  
Or should I let it fall under the table  
Or take it home in a bag  
Things the girl forgot at home  
Father-in-law’s shirts, mother-in-law’s shirts  
Every glove is torn  
Ragged garments  
These the girl forgot at home.  
Hey now, brother dear  
Drive home to the barnfront  
Try the key in the kitchen door  
Right at the place of the door  
Hold on, boy  
Give time, green willow  
The horse gallops well  
The lad beats the head with the whip  
The father-in-law stabs the ox in the field  
Go, go men  
Already the moon is in front of the house  
The star is over the granary roof  
Poles against the light  
Go, go men  
There are many cousins  
There are aunts filling the barn  
They flew in large numbers  
Now the crowd begins to wander  
The group is scattered  
The crowd disappears  
Let’s go quickly to Finland  
For a year, we go to Harju County  
For the winter, not behind the Russians.

Try the key in the kitchen door  
Right at the place of the door  
Hold on, boy  
Give time, green willow  
The horse gallops well  
The lad beats the head with the whip  
The father-in-law stabs the ox in the field  
Go, go men  
Already the moon is in front of the house  
The star is over the granary roof  
Poles against the light  
Go, go men  
There are many cousins  
There are aunts filling the barn  
They flew in large numbers  
Now the crowd begins to wander  
The group is scattered  
The crowd disappears  
Let’s go quickly to Finland  
For a year, we go to Harju County  
For the winter, not behind the Russians.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Young men of Paluküla  
They neither plow nor sow  
They do not cultivate the land  
Give me a garden edge full  
Leave it full of footprints  
Where I sow cumin  
Sometimes I reap sulfur  
The village smells of cumin  
The field smells of sulfur  
Young men of Vodja parish  
They neither plow nor sow  
They are not harsh plowers  
Shaking loud plows  
Young men of Palu parish  
Put behind whatever they want  
German plows or lord’s harrows  
These are harsh plowers  
Shaking loud plows

label: Töölaulud
translation:
They made me dig a hole  
They put me to chop willow  
I am muddy up to my penis  
Until the time of Kesavesi  
Ai-oi, it is white outside  
The crooked home on the back of the woman  
A man on the back of the girl  
A crow on the back of the spirit child

label: Töölaulud
translation:
At first by Lake Endla  
There we stayed behind Mai  
I had to go home  
I set off, it started raining  
I had to go to Pille  
But I fell asleep at Malle's  
Because I became a wreck  
From wandering the herd so long  
At night I guarded father's horses  
By day I protected brother's herd  
To the river mouth, to the lake mouth  
To the five gray rye fields  
To the shore of the six wells  
Among the German troops  
The Germans are cruel, the lands are barren  
Every year the barley is ruined  
Every month the oats are meager  
Kubjas blamed the fences  
Kubjas called to the house

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let's go cutting in the field  
We cut the sheaves swiftly  
We set the drags properly  
Sisters, dear ones  
Maidens, young ladies  
Let us lay hands on the broad field  
On the finer patches  
On the slender branches  
So that we reap the ends  
So that we reach the islands  
To the long edge of the field  
To the wide expanse of the meadow  
At the edge, pull the oiled ropes  
In the patch, we have milk and bread  
Master, little master  
Mistress, little mistress  
Give what you promised  
Make true what you vowed  
You promised to have a wedding in the field  
A wedding in the field, spars in the meadow  
You promised for the field wedding  
Spars for the verse trail.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud laulust  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
There was a field without an owner  
There was a meadow without a master  
Master, little master  
Mistress, young mistress  
Give the servant good food  
Food for the servant, food for the ox  
The servant feeds the ox  
I will slaughter the bull for a wedding  
Bars of the meadow's fence  
There was no crowd of poets  
Poets, singers  
The room was dark, the people feeble  
I alone the beauty maker  
Joking among the neighbors  
The rye head seemed to appear  
The wheat from the ear would come out  
The barley ear is turning green  
The oat ear covers the ground  
What are you singing if you don't understand  
You are singing pig’s messages  
Servant's dreams  
Old gold’s custom mother  
I sing in a bare-headed way  
A tuft was on the other ear

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud laulust  ; Looduslaulud
translation:
There was a field without an owner  
There was a meadow without a master  
Master, little master  
Mistress, young mistress  
Give the servant good food  
Food for the servant, food for the ox

Bars of the meadow's fence  
There was no crowd of poets

The room was dark, the people feeble

Joking among the neighbors

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let's set the injections one by one
Get ready to start working
Let's begin here in the field
We will surely bend over
We scribble on the sailboard
Wondering when we will reach the end of the field
The field was triangular
In each corner three bundles
In each bundle three sheaves
In each sheaf three stalks
In each stalk three heads
In each head three grains.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Dear little brother  
Make me a little wheelbarrow  
So I can roll to the barn  
To the stove in winter's warmth  
A man would be welcome  
A sweetheart, a dear one  
Neither my father forbids me  
Nor my mother forbids me  
Grandmother told me to take a man  
Grandfather called me a good girl  
To do better work  
To help in the fields  
If only after one good crop  
A sled would be made from raw spruce  
To plow the field with a hump  
To harrow well with a hop  
A sled for windy weather  
To plow the field with a harrow  
To sow seed diligently.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The old woman told me to spin  
But the old man said to comb the flax  
I did not want to spin  
I wanted to comb the flax  
I took the wool to Virk  
The flax to the wise woman  
I gave Aru Kärt to handle  
Kärt was quick at spinning  
Diligent at treading the wick  
Spinning nights and spinning days  
Could not fill the fine shirt  
Nor fill the loincloth  
There still showed a bare bottom  
A smooth little shit hole.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Up, up, village girls  
Get up, dairymaids  
Rise to milk the cows  
Send the herd out  
Other herds graze in the pasture  
Our herd grazes in the yard  
Other heifers on the grass  
Our heifers in the mud

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Grandmother told me not to spin
Grandfather told me not to card wool
I did not feel like spinning
Did not want to card wool
I brought the fleece to a brisk hand
The carding went to a wise woman
The village man pays the wages
The woman cleans up the spinning tools

label: Töölaulud
translation:
We finish this field  
We reduce this ridge  
So that no hair is beaten by the wind  
No flock is scattered by the rain  
No hail is rolled by the storm.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The niece with the long tail  
Long-tail, lazily lying down  
Went out to the field to cut  
Cut a branch, cut two  
Brought them under the beam  
She herself began to blossom  
Listen, young men of the village  
Here lies a girl  
And a maiden four inches tall  
From her mouth rises the scent of death  
From her nose four-pronged  
From her ears six-twisted  
Oh oh oh oh oh dear mother  
Why don’t you let me try your daughter?

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Together, together, a bit of cream  
Ready, ready, a bit of butter  
Midadi-mödadi into the mounds  
Let it come from the sky, let the basket go  
For my daughter’s happiness  
For the master, into the pot  
For the shepherd boy, into the corner of the box  
For the servant, into another corner

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Who among us will die  
The sickle will fall down  
Who among you will become a whore  
The sickle will break apart

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let's finish this field  
Let's reduce this wasteland  
So we can start on the arable land  
With fields unworked  
With fields in the meadow unharvested  
Let's hasten, let's pull  
Let's hurry, let's break the rye  
Let's hasten, serious sisters  
Hurry, foolish people  
So that we move forward  
So that we sow the islands  
Maybe the whip is on the firewood wagon  
The hammer is on the grazing land  
It strikes along my side  
Along the edge of the ridge field.  
Let's hasten, serious sisters  
Hurry, foolish people  
So that we reach the goal.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The Kodasema gentleman gives volunteer work  
That kills him with volunteer work  
Let it kill him with volunteer work  
That last year played on the lawn  
Spring played on the hayfield  
Winter danced in the stable stuff  
That kills him with volunteer work  
When we go to the road  
There is the rustling of boots  
The whistling of clog shoes  
The Esna gentleman gives volunteer work  
That kills him with volunteer work  
That last year stank on the lawn  
Spring stank on the hayfield  
Winter stank in the stable stuff  
That kills him with volunteer work  
When they go to the road  
There is the whistling of scythes  
There is the rattling of rakes  
The leather of harnesses rustling.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Grandmother is making a cake, I am leading the herd  
I am taking care of grandmother's ewes  
I protect grandfather's piebald herd  
Eat the herd, blue herd  
Drink the herd, red herd  
Grandmother's herd for breathing  
For rest time for resting  
For a while to nap  
To faint into a little bundle  
Until I tie the leg straps  
Fastening the shoelaces  
I close the buckle strap  
I make the cover stitches  
I sew in the frame stitches  
I lay in the table legs  
Table legs, children's letters  
Crosses, foolish letters

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Rise up, villagers  
Get up, heads of families,  
Rise to milk the cows  
To send the herd out  
Mundi's bulls to the grass  
Our bulls to the meadow  
Mundi's herd to the pasture  
Our herd to the cattle pen

label: Töölaulud
translation:
We cut the mighty weeds  
We make the meadows better  
The stacks are more numerous  
So that we can bring them ahead  
So we can get to the tip of the island  
To the long edge of the field  
Maybe to the edge of the whip too  
A scythe to the handle strap  
It strikes along my waist  
Along the edge against my back  
Exactly along the shoulder.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Little Eve, little boil  
Boiled a hot broth  
Burned the servant’s mouth  
The long mustache of the family son.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I sailed the Viru five times  
I left the lake after four  
I didn’t find among my own  
A girl from the four visits  
I send ships, I mend ships  
I send ships to Saaremaa  
Half the ships to Pohlamaa  
Empty ships to Turkey  
From there I bring a bride with a side lock  
Piia with a straight neck  
She knows how to walk with a fire  
How to roar without a chandelier  
How to write without a ring  
Without a chandelier she milks the cow  
Without a ring she strains the milk  
Night for pain, honor for pain  
For the headache of the crowned bull  
For God’s star pain.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Evening comes, dear sisters  
Evening brings beer goods  
Twilight brings wool goods  
Evening brings straw for the oven  
Twilight puts to sleep  
The cock wakes up  
Dawn scatters bits and pieces  
White spreads out  
Some of us are sent to manor work  
Some to the manor's courtyard.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
The field does not end at the frost  
The wide meadows of the field  
Every day was spent on you  
Every noon in the frost  
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The field was triangular  
In each corner three stalks  
In each stalk three stalklets  
In each stalklet three ears  
In each ear three heads  
In each head three grains  
In each grain three kernels  
Let's finish this field  
Let's reduce this narrow passage  
So that we may head to the river's end  
So that we can reach the island  
At the end, we have waiting pieces  
In the garden, milk bread  
On the island, slender loaves.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
Spindle, spindle, little maiden  
Who owns the spindles, little maiden  
Is it for father, or mother  
Is it for father-in-law, or mother-in-law  
Is it for the thaw or for the frost  
Is it for the humpbacked thaw  
Or for the funny spike  
Not for father, not for mother  
Not for father-in-law, not for mother-in-law  
Not for the thaw, not for the frost  
Not for the humpbacked thaw  
Not for the funny spike  
The spindle is for oneself  
For one’s own fine beard  
For one’s dear companion as well  
Let’s exchange the young man  
Who was sharp and rolling  
The silver needle weakener  
Drool, drool, little needle  
Drool, little needle’s eye  
Jump, round needle  
Leap, square needle  
Roll, five-sided

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks

Not for father, not for mother  
Not for father-in-law, not for mother-in-law  
Not for the thaw, not for the frost  
Not for the humpbacked thaw  
Not for the funny spike  
The spindle is for oneself  
For one’s own fine beard  
For one’s dear companion as well  
Let’s exchange the young man

The silver needle weakener  
Drool, drool, little needle  
Drool, little needle’s eye  
Jump, round needle  
Leap, square needle  
Roll, five-sided

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I started making a sled  
I made one in a month, I made two  
In a week, I made four in four months  
In five days, five in five months  
Then the sled was finished  
I took it to Eide to examine  
To old Eide to look at  
Then I rode through the villages  
Through the village street  
The man shines, the horse shines  
The pavement of the stone street thunders  
A dog barks at the bend of the linden tree  
From there I got maple slats  
From there I got linden bark with a groove  
The linden tree was between the villages

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I rowed, I reached across the sea  
I stepped on the ship, I went to Finland  
Yes, I went to Finland  
For the winter behind the Russians

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Rehe, I beat the ceiling of joy  
I struck the ceilings with a singing roof  
Beaten by Rehte, I  
Was not struck by hard work  
A humpback came from the sack  
From the crooked Kiima tavern  
The hat was on the head and the glove on the hand  
The hand was totally out of hands  
We receive love payment  
You do not know loyalty!  
The field was sideways in front  
The handle was flatly on the roof  
He went to piss on the spruce  
To the juniper’s farting place  
The spruce twig went to the crotch  
The ground went sideways into the ass.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Eat for the herd, drink for the herd,  
Lie down for the herd to rest,  
For rest, to take a break,  
To become a little calm.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
You have long been ready  
Every south is lukewarm  
Every south was added  
Everyone felt the pull  
Now the end has come  
Now you have become an island.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Let's finish this field  
Let's reduce the width of the row,  
Fill the barrels, fill the tons,  
Let the corners of the barn be filled,  
The bags of the harvest bundles.  
I thank this day,  
Tomorrow until mid-morning,  
To the father of the Attüma family,  
To the mother of the Attüma family.  
To feed, to give drink,  
To set the wide table,  
To slaughter this ox  
Which has not been through half the weather,  
Which has plowed half a knee of the field,  
A thousand vats of thick hunch,  
A hundred vats of long horn,  
Ten cubits of side ribs.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I will cut, but I cannot manage  
I will whistle whistles  
And I will put a pinch of salt  
As if my strength is taken  
A pig is tied on the back  
When the hands are bound.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I will reap, I do have the strength  
I will bind the sheaves  
And I will tend the threshing barn  
My strength has been taken  
Or my hands have been tied  
The sickle was sharp, but did not gather  
The grain was sparse, I did not manage  
If I had done it for my brother  
Then the grain would have grown to the belt  
Grain to the belt, necks of oats.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Men of Kireve, men of Kareve  
Build bridges over the swamps  
Dams upon the mountains  
Then the wife won’t wet her feet  
The wife won’t wet her feet, the ox won’t graze  
The pig won’t enter the boot shed  
Are your shoe laces not twisted  
Is your tail standing well

label: Pulmalaulud  ; Töölaulud
translation:
Little sister, dear one,  
Maiden, young girl,  
If you bother to be diligent  
And you want to be wise,  
Be an eager morning worker  
To watch before dawn,  
Wash the chamber pots clean,  
Put the buckets by the well edge,  
The ladles on the kitchen doorstep.  
The gentlemen from Münt passed by,  
The gentlemen from Kapu passed by,  
They thought the moon was shining,  
For we had shining ladle handles,  
Gleaming table edges,  
And sparkling bucket handles.

label: Pulmalaulud  ; Töölaulud
translation:
Little sister, dear one,  
Maiden, young girl,  
If you bother to be diligent  
And you want to be wise,  
Be an eager morning worker  
To watch before dawn,  
Wash the chamber pots clean,  
Put the buckets by the well edge,  
The ladles on the kitchen doorstep.  
The gentlemen from Münt passed by,  
The gentlemen from Kapu passed by,  
They thought the moon was shining,  
For we had shining ladle handles,  
Gleaming table edges,  
And sparkling bucket handles.

label: Pulmalaulud  ; Töölaulud
translation:
If you’re willing to be awake  
And you want to be wise  
Be a diligent morning worker  
Early before dawn  
Under the window sill  
Bring water while coming  
Wash the cups thoroughly  
Put the jugs under the room’s eaves  
Pots on the kitchen doorstep  
Windowsills by the garden fence  
Tomorrow the wagoner comes to the village  
The old man comes with wine  
He thinks about the moon shining  
He thinks about the day reddening  
About the morning star glowing  
About the dawn star turning yellow  
Our cups thoroughly  
Jugs under the room’s eaves  
Pots on the kitchen doorstep  
Windowsills by the garden fence

label: Pulmalaulud  ; Töölaulud
translation:
If you’re willing to be awake  
And you want to be wise  
Be a diligent morning worker  
Early before dawn  
Under the window sill

Put the jugs under the room’s eaves

Windowsills by the garden fence  
Tomorrow the wagoner comes to the village  
The old man comes with wine  
He thinks about the moon shining

About the dawn star turning yellow  
Our cups thoroughly  
Jugs under the room’s eaves  
Pots on the kitchen doorstep  
Windowsills by the garden fence

label: Lüroeepilised laulud  ; Töölaulud  ; Laulud abielust  ; Murelaulud
translation:
Ann was a heifer calf  
Keedi a charming young girl  
She went to jump into the barn  
Offered to take a swing  
Between stones on the threshing floor  
Anne’s beads broke  
Anne’s ribbon broke  
Ann went to her brother’s request  
My dear little brother  
Take my ribbons and fix them  
Sew the purse shut  
Renew the edges of the clasp  
Ann did not take, Ann did not go  
She went herself alone  
Along the road, very quietly  
Across the land, very humbly  
Ann went to the blacksmith’s request  
Blacksmith, such a man  
Take my ribbons and fix them  
Sew the purse shut  
Renew the edges of the clasp  
The blacksmith wanted, Ann was ready  
The blacksmith’s children hissed  
The blacksmith himself was without black  
The blacksmith’s wife was the blackest of all  
I thought to become the manor’s man  
I thought to become the aide man  
A serf would become a woman of the manor freely  
The other side of the farmhand  
What was under the care of the serf?  
Under the whims of the laborers  
The fire was in the room without a lock  
The well was uncovered on the hill  
The pigsty was without a door  
It was under the care of the serf  
Under the whims of the laborers  
May it not exist  
May it not be born  
Who bears the crown?  
May the coachman put on a coat  
Where was the crown’s home?  
Where did the coachman sleep at night?  
Where was the fire that lit the room?  
The hundred walls overgrown with moss  
Water rolled logs  
Mist covered the doors in front  
Dew tied onto the roof.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The owl was hooting
Rein's chest was bumpy
The coat was made of fish skin
The shirt was made from the scales of a zander
The pants were made from rooster feathers.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Oh-oh-oh-oh, uncle  
Why didn't you take me as your boy  
But I know how to make fishing traps  
To write horns on a bull  
I was strong to go to the herd  
To go to the herd, to watch the cattle  
Uncle was diligent in giving food  
Aunt was kind in cooking food  
The bag was put on like a cart.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
The old woman makes a cake, I go to the herd  
I look after the old woman’s sheep  
I protect the old man’s spotted herd  
Eat the herd, the blue herd  
Drink the herd, the red herd  
Lay the herd down to rest  
For a moment to rest  
For a while to nap  
To fade into a little crumb  
Until I tie the pig’s leg lace  
I fasten the shoe lace  
I tie the strap lace  
I make inside embroidery  
I make inside quilt embroidery  
I create embroidery with a story  
Table legs children’s embroidery  
Clovers of foolish embroidery

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Up, up, village women  
Up to milk the cows  
To send the herd outside  
To the other herd far away  
Our herd in the enclosure  
Our stallions in the rye field  
Our bulls in the bull's horn  
You have home in chains  
Horses are in chains  
Sheep in the barn, doors locked.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
The girl is growing up  
Long ago they started on you  
Long ago they cut you with a dull blade.

label: Töölaulud  ; Lüroeepilised laulud
translation:
But our gentlemen were good  
Our ladies are better  
The young gentlemen pecked at gold  
Silver and sparkly prelates  
They held a wedding out in the field  
A communal work party behind the fence  
There they slaughtered the bull  
That had never been seen in the world  
Worked the field for a hundred years  
Plowed for ten years.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
End, end, little field  
Come to an end, little island  
Let's finish this field  
Let's reduce the wide gap.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Together, together, little cream  
Could be God's little fruit  
Let it come from heaven, let it go to heaven  
Bits for the family  
A needle for the witch's pimple  
A cat for the jealous eye  
I myself will eat the butter or

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Buzz, buzz, little scythe  
Clink, clank, little curved iron  
Whoever reaches the scythe first  
The grim reaper will reach them sooner.

label: Töölaulud
translation: Rye bread between the beams

label: Töölaulud
translation:
I, Mihkel, son of Maie,
Kala Jaan, son of Kaie,
I decided to set a fish trap,
Decided to put the trap in the river;
I placed the trap's mouth towards the manor,
The trap's tail towards the lord.
Then God gave fish.
Then there was roach visible to the eye,
A fishing buffet to taste with the tongue.
Then the father could chew,
The mother could smack her lips.
From there, the thief got throat butter,
From the thigh father got breast butter.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Master, little master!  
Mistress, little mistress!  
Maybe you are angry about this!  
Because it stayed on the head of the field.  
To feed on the pig’s tail.  
I will bring the piglets to the pen  
The old pigs to rule  
The piglet picks a handful of field  
The old sow a basketful  
The young pigs a handful of scraps.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to the Creator to tell a story,
To Marija to the dining place,
Now the Creator is telling a story,
Marija is going to the table.
I take one, I take two,
I begin to take the third,
What do I find under one?
I find an eagle under one,
What is under the eagle's wing?
A cradle under the eagle's wing.
What is on the corner of the cradle?
A bone was on the corner of the cradle.
What will be made from that bone?
A room will be made for Toomas.
A shed will be built for Ants,
A house was built for Martin,
Logs were placed for Peeter,
A cradle was made for Juhan.
What is inside the sheds?
Beds inside the sheds.
What is under the beds?
Straw under the beds.
What is on the straw?
Linen on the straw,
What is on the linen?
Pillows on the linen,
What is on the pillows?
Four young maidens:
One wears a silk headband
Another weaves a golden belt,
The third weaves a silver belt,
The fourth cries for her young man.

label: Töölaulud
translation:
Ladies and gentlemen.
Let us build bridges over the swamps,
Barriers over the rivers.
Wide lids on the waves,
So that a woman does not wet her feet,
A woman’s feet, not to be caught.
A dove does not get stuck in a block of ice,
A rooster does not crow for gold,
A pig does not follow a silk ribbon.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks
translation:
The horse with the gray brother,  
The horse with the gray decorated meadow,  
Both legs white.  
The harness is written in gold,  
The tail silkily braided,  
The nose neatly trimmed,  
His head placed in a halter,  
Ears in golden rings,  
He went to the forest for the first time.  
He brought six spruces, six ells,  
From the juniper, eight ells,  
A hundred ells of alder,  
Fifty sturdy firs,  
A thousand ells of oak timber.  
He began to build the road,  
Built the road to Riga,  
On the oak’s curled leaf,  
On the berry stalk of the house,  
Made a pile on the wind,  
On the bare spot of the sauna,  
Sat himself by the corner,  
Took a stick on his knee.  
There then passed the noblemen of Sõlge,  
The noblemen of Münd passed by,  
Many gentlemen roamed,  
Many ladies roamed.  
Some began to wonder:  
Is this Lihula town,  
Or is this the rope church,  
Or is this the peaceful shore?  
I hear and reply:  
Oh you foolish guessers,  
Oh you mad and silly!  
If this were Lihula town,  
Then it would be made of flesh.  
If this were the peaceful shore,  
Then it would be built of peace.  
Or if this were the rope church,  
It would be twisted from ropes.  
This is the road of my brother,  
Sandi brother’s little sauna,  
The bad brother’s little shed,  
The dark brother’s little room.

label: Töölaulud  ; Laulud noorrahva elust
translation:
Let's go to the meadow story,  
To Marija to the hayfield.  
Golden rakes in hand,  
Silver rake handles.  
I took one rake, I took two.  
I’m about to take the third,  
I found an eagle under one rake,  
Among the haystacks.  
What is under the eagle’s wing?  
A swallow under the eagle’s wing.  
Where did the swallow come from,  
Came and settled there.  
To chop brushwood for the barn,  
But the swallow flew away.  
What is inside the barns?  
Beds inside the barns.  
What is under the beds?  
Pillows under the beds!  
What is on the pillows?  
Four young maidens,  
One is crying for a young man,  
Another is weaving a silver belt,  
The third is making a golden wreath;  
The fourth is crying for her father and mother.

label: Looduslaulud  ; Laulud meelelahutamiseks  ; Laulud abielust  ; Töölaulud
translation:
I went to a plowing ride,  
To let down the wide furrow,  
I myself rode along the heath,  
The dogs rode along the marsh,  
The shafts along the road tracks.  
I heard the shaft creaking,  
I heard the duck splashing.  
I took the duck from the shaft,  
The hawk from the shaft, from the noose.  
I took the duck to Paide,  
The hawk there I gave away.  
From Paide I got money in coins.  
I bought a new little instrument.  
I started to play the instrument.  
Father heard the instrument sound,  
Father started to do oiling.  
Mother heard the instrument sound,  
Mother started knocking the bark,  
Started to make brandy.  
Brothers heard the instrument sound,  
Brothers went to draw water.  
Sister heard the instrument sound,  
Sister went to fetch salt.  
The girl heard the instrument sound,  
The girl smiled at the young men:  
Here comes the instrument home,  
Takes my instrument to bed,  
Takes the kannel to the room,  
Calls the morning awake.  
Takes the instrument, starts to play,  
On the kannel’s room threshold.  
Get up, girl, milk the cow,  
Get up, boy, drive the cattle.  
Other cattle to the far field,  
Our cattle to the cattle pen,  
Other calves to the grass,  
Our calves to the mud.  
Other bulls to the hayfield,  
Our bulls to the meadow.  
Other mares to the rye field,  
Our mares to the shafts.  
Grandmother hears, again she hears:  
Oh my daughter-in-law, my dear,  
My daughter-in-law with the apron!  
Don’t leave the instrument without care,  
The kannel to live six hundred years,  
The instrument walks the straw road,  
Sings on the straw plank end;  
The kannel in the straw chamber.  
There it keeps the harlots,  
Carries the women of ill repute,  
Takes the sinner to the doorstep,  
Gives too much to the strumpet.
